These results demonstrate the feasibility of ISAC operation for low-cost devices with constrained RF hardware.

title: Dynamic Virtual Heat Fields for Void-Avoidance Opportunistic Routing in UASNs
authors: Shen’ao Tu, Yougan Chen, Yihao Zhao, Xuchen Wang, Yanhan Dong, Yi Tao, Xiaomei Xu
abstract: Underwater Acoustic Sensor Networks (UASNs) have attracted considerable attention due to their broad application prospects. However, the challenges of limited battery replacement and unstable communication in underwater environments make it difficult for traditional routing protocols to balance stability and energy consumption under dynamic topologies. To address these challenges, we propose a Dynamic Virtual Heat Field-based Void-Avoidance Opportunistic Routing (DVHVOR) protocol. Inspired by the diffusion of heat from sources to cooler regions in nature, the protocol constructs a virtual heat field to guide efficient data forwarding without relying on precise three-dimensional localization. Specifically, it integrates hop count, residual energy, and depth information into a heat-value metric. The Sink periodically broadcasts packets, forming a dynamic and adaptive virtual heat field across the network. During data transmission, each node selects the neighbor with the highest heat value as the relay and employs a concurrent forwarding mechanism to improve both energy efficiency and adaptability to topology changes. Compared with conventional greedy routing strategies, the virtual heat field provides stable and tunable multi-path guidance.Simulation results demonstrate that, relative to existing protocols, the proposed approach reduces the variance of residual energy by an average of 35.9%, decreases transmission delay by approximately 10.9%, and achieves superior performance across multiple metrics including network lifetime, thereby validating its feasibility and advantages in dynamic and network sparse scenarios.

title: Quantum Federated Gradient Aggregation using the Parameter-Shift Rule
authors: Jaehyun Chung, Chaemoon Im, Soohyun Park, Joongheon Kim, Wonjun Lee
abstract: Spurred by the limited availability of quantum resources, known as qubits, in recent quantum computers, quantum federated learning (QFL) is drawing attention. Due to its ability to fully utilize distributed qubits, QFL is suitable for developing quantum algorithms. QFL achieves local quantum gradients using the parameter-shift rule (P-S rule) and aggregates them, effectively coping with the limited number of qubits in each quantum computer. However, realizing QFL remains challenging due to the characteristics of the P-S rule, which requires two forward passes to compute the quantum gradients in each local quantum computer. These challenges become even more severe when the aggregation of the local quantum gradients occurs under heterogeneous channel conditions and data distributions. Motivated by this, this paper proposes the joint P-S rule, which eliminates the aggregation process in QFL and instead directly achieves the global quantum gradients. Furthermore, this paper proposes joint efficient quantum federated learning (Joint EQFL) that leverages successive interference cancellation and divergence-based clustering for achieving stability under heterogeneous channel conditions and robustness to heterogeneous data distributions. This paper analyzes the convergence and corroborates the superiority of Joint EQFL.

title: Fully-distributed Prescribed-time Consensus of High-order Integrator Multi-agent Systems: An Adaptive Protocol with Matching Gain Convergence
authors: Tao Yu, Yong Chen, Xin Gong, Zhiguang Feng, Tingwen Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal and Information Processing over Networks
abstract: This work focuses on the design of fully-distributed prescribed-time consensus protocols for first-order to higher order integrator multi-agent systems. An adaptive gain with a convergence rate that matches the prescribed-time protocol is proposed. Then, first-order to higher-order zero-error fully distributed prescribed-time consensus protocols are designed by replacing the parameters that need to be large enough in the traditional prescribed-time protocol with this adaptive gain. Considering different application scenarios, the edge-based version of the above adaptive gain is further designed, which can ensure that the graph is still balanced after adding adaptive gains to the weights. A special Lyapunov function is constructed to prove that the state and the adaptive gain have a prescribed time convergence. Finally, the effectiveness of the above protocols is verified by a UAV formation experiment.

title: Exploring Pre-Service Teachers&#x2019; Intention to Use Artificial Intelligence in Education: A Structural Equation Modeling Approach Based on UTAUT2_supp1-3677289.pdf
authors: Juan Victoria-Maldonado
abstract: The growing relevance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in education necessitates a better understanding of its acceptance among future educators. This study investigates the factors influencing pre-service teachers' intention to use AI, employing the UTAUT2 model extended with sociodemographic moderators. A cross-sectional quantitative design was applied to a sample of 908 undergraduate students from Early Childhood and Primary Education programs in Andalusia, Spain. Structural equation modeling results reveal that performance expectancy, effort expectancy, and habitual use are significant predictors of behavioral intention toward AI use. In contrast, the proposed moderating effects of gender and academic year were found to be non-significant. Findings highlight the pivotal role of habitual engagement with AI while questioning the effectiveness of current curricular approaches in promoting its pedagogical use, as academic progression showed no moderating influence. The study emphasizes the need for targeted training and curricular updates to foster meaningful AI integration in teacher education.

title: Exploring Pre-Service Teachers’ Intention to Use Artificial Intelligence in Education: A Structural Equation Modeling Approach Based on UTAUT2
authors: Juan José Victoria Maldonado, Santiago Alonso García, Alejandro Martínez Menendez, Manuel Enrique Lorenzo Martín
publish: IEEE Revista Iberoamericana de Tecnologias del Aprendizaje
abstract: The growing relevance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in education necessitates a better understanding of its acceptance among future educators. This study investigates the factors influencing pre-service teachers' intention to use AI, employing the UTAUT2 model extended with sociodemographic moderators. A cross-sectional quantitative design was applied to a sample of 908 undergraduate students from Early Childhood and Primary Education programs in Andalusia, Spain. Structural equation modeling results reveal that performance expectancy, effort expectancy, and habitual use are significant predictors of behavioral intention toward AI use. In contrast, the proposed moderating effects of gender and academic year were found to be non-significant. Findings highlight the pivotal role of habitual engagement with AI while questioning the effectiveness of current curricular approaches in promoting its pedagogical use, as academic progression showed no moderating influence. The study emphasizes the need for targeted training and curricular updates to foster meaningful AI integration in teacher education.

title: FilmShots: A Fast Film Shot Boundary Detection Based on Dilated Conv3D and Attention
authors: Chunfang Li, Kun Wang, Yuchen Pei, Yalv Fan, Zhetao Guo, Yushi Shen, Tongtong Zheng, Yuhe Hu, Fei Zhang
abstract: Shot boundary detection (SBD) is one of the representative tasks for computational film studies. Unlike television and short videos, movies present potential challenges, including dark scene, internal subtitles, occlusions, quick motions, flashes, color shifts, and low-resolution. This paper introduces a training data generation approach using random shot lengths to increase the positive samples and provides a 100-min public test set from 10 films. We propose a new end-to-end network FilmShots, which integrates temporal dilated convolutions to achieve a longer receptive field and attention mechanisms to enhance algorithm accuracy. The approach separately employs three techniques: Non-Local blocks (FilmShotNL) to capture long-range dependencies, Discrete Cosine Transform (FilmShotNLDCT) to reduce the computational complexity of spatiotemporal Non-Local blocks, and Conv1D (FilmShotECA) to compute channel attention weights. Additionally, this paper enhances interpretability by visualizing receptive fields of dilated Conv3D and attention operation. Experiments demonstrate that FilmShots surpasses both TransNetV2 and AutoShot in F1 scores, achieving the best performance currently available.

title: Simultaneous Reflection and Transmission in Metasurfaces: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Ahmed Ali Umar, Farid Zubir, Mohd Fairus Mohd Yusoff, Muhammad Inam Abbasi, Shaharil Mohd Shah, Arshad Karimbu Vallappil, Maher Assaad, Noorlindawaty Md Jizat
abstract: The ability of metasurfaces to manipulate electromagnetic waves has drawn much attention especially with its ability to implement multifunctional structures such as simultaneous reflection and transmission. This paper discusses the fundamental principles and key design metrics such as bandwidth performance, efficiency, polarization control, frequency, and angular stability, which describe performance capabilities and limitations of metasurfaces. A wide operational bandwidth is indeed a requisite for multiple applications; while efficiency allows for energy efficient operations and optimal throughput. Additionally, polarization control and angular stability improves the performance of the metasurface, especially for dynamic environments and next generation communication systems. However, these metasurfaces have some design limitations; for example, trade-off between bandwidth and angular stability, and independent control of reflected and transmitted waves. This paper outlines these challenges and offers avenues for further innovation toward fully realizing the potential of metasurfaces in next-generation technologies.

title: Deep Residual Feature-Enhanced DenseNet for Automated Age-related Macular Degeneration Detection in OCT Images
authors: C. Valvilori., Nithishkumar R, Ajay K
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This work proposes an optimized deep learning framework for the automated identification of Age-related Macular Degeneration (AMD) using DenseNet-121 from Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) images. The approach undergoes a thorough preprocessing pipeline: grayscale conversion, resizing, and denoising; this is done to enhance the quality of images for the extraction of robust features. This model utilizes the pre-trained architecture of DenseNet-121 and finetunes it to classify OCT images as either normal or abnormal with very high accuracy. This addresses one of the biggest challenges in the early detection of AMD. Another novelty of this work is the inclusion of an end-to-end deployment using Streamlit, allowing for real-time predictions on uploaded OCT images without requiring further retraining; thus, it bridges the gap between work and clinical usability. Experimental evaluations have been performed which show that the model reaches efficient convergence during training and is reliable on unseen data. This work underlines a practical and scalable solution for ophthalmic image analysis, joining advanced deep learning techniques with an interactive interface for immediate diagnostic support.

title: Research on Regression Model of Fire Occurrence Probability Based on Bayesian Theorem for Meteorological Data
authors: Xiaochuan Wu, Zhenpeng An, Yitian Qiu, Di Zhang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Exploring the regression relationship between meteorological data and fire occurrence has always been a research hotspot in the field of fire risk analysis. Based on hourly meteorological data, this paper collected and organized 3,910 fire data throughout the year of 2023 in a certain area, as well as a total of 8,760 hourly data of temperature, humidity, air pressure, precipitation, ground wind speed and wind direction throughout the year. By using the basic statistical data sorted out through the Bayesian formula and combining meteorological elements such as temperature, humidity and wind speed, researchers established curve models and multivariate linear models to quantitatively explain the relationship between these meteorological elements and urban fire occurrence.

title: SCR-YOLO: An Efficient UAV Object Detection Framework Based on Spatial-Channel Reconstruction and Statistical Metric Learning
authors: Xiuquan Yue, Tianshi Zhang, Hanyu Deng, Shiwei Wang, Junying Yuan, Haishan Chen, Haoran Deng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Social Network Systems (AISNS)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) imagery has emerged as a pivotal data source for intelligent social governance applications, such as urban traffic monitoring and crowd analysis. However, object detection in high-resolution aerial views faces severe challenges, particularly for tiny targets (e.g., pedestrians and vehicles). Most detectors suffer from computational redundancy in irrelevant background regions and gradient instability when localizing discrete tiny objects, primarily due to extreme background noise and the limitations of traditional geometric metrics in handling minute scale variations. To address these methodological and mathematical challenges, this paper proposes SCR-YOLO, an efficient detection framework based on structural optimization of YOLOv8. First, we design a novel Spatial-Channel Reconstruction C2f (SCR-C2f) module by integrating SCConv to replace the second standard convolution in the bottleneck unit. By integrating a redundancy-reducing topology, SCR-C2f effectively suppresses substantial background noise and refines the features of sparse target entities. Second, we introduce a collaborative regression strategy integrating Normalized Gaussian Wasserstein Distance (NWD). By modeling bounding boxes as 2D Gaussian distributions, this statistical metric mitigates the sensitivity and instability issues inherent in traditional IoU-based geometric metrics when processing tiny objects. Experimental results on the challenging VisDrone2019 benchmark demonstrate that our method significantly improves mAP50 by 5.6% and mAP50–95 by 1.2%, reaching 30.9% and 16.8%, respectively,with a real-time inference speed of 115 FPS on an NVIDIA RTX A4000 GPU. Consequently, the proposed method holds significant potential for deployment in intelligent social governance systems and resource-constrained UAV edge networks.

title: A Stochastic Computing-Based Architecture Design for Orthogonal Time Frequency Space Message Passing Detector
authors: Dingjie Yao, Zhisong Bie
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This paper reviews the application of the Message Passing Algorithm (MPA) in Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) systems. While achieving near-optimal detection performance, its high computational complexity severely constrains practical implementation. To overcome this complexity bottleneck, we propose an innovative MPA detection architecture based on the stochastic computing paradigm. This design maps the algorithm's most computationally intensive operations to the stochastic computing domain, fully leveraging SC's unique advantages in probabilistic processing. Theoretical analysis indicates the scheme's potential for significantly reducing hardware overhead. Simulation results further verify that the proposed stochastic MPA architecture maintains performance comparable to conventional binary approaches. This work provides a promising low-complexity detection solution for OTFS systems, and its hardware-friendly features offer a new technical pathway for designing future high-mobility communication terminals, holding substantial engineering reference value.

title: DeepGynScan: AI-Powered Cervical Lesion Detection from Colposcopy Images for Early Cervical Cancer Screening
authors: Krishna Kumar R, Pratheebha G, Manasavarshini G D, Nivas S, Sathya D, Mohamme Rahamadullah
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Cervical cancer is one of the leading causes of cancer-related deaths among women globally and is highly prevalent in areas where there is limited access to specialist care and diagnostic infrastructure. Current screening methods like Pap smear cytology and colposcopy are highly subjective and rely on expert interpretation, which can introduce variability and delay in diagnosis. This work discusses DeepGynScan, an AI-driven framework using the EfficientNetB0 architecture for automated classification of cervical lesions into normal, low-grade, and high-grade categories. The system was trained on the publicly available SIPaKMeD cytology dataset and obtained an accuracy of 93% with a precision, recall, and F1-score of 0.93, 0.94, and 0.93, respectively. A web-based diagnostic platform integrated using FastAPI and React enables real-time image upload, inference, and automatic generation of a clinical report. Although the model is currently trained on cytology images different from the macroscopic colposcopy views, the proposed work mainly stands at the feasibility-stage investigation to showcase that lightweight deep-learning systems are capable of performing assessments of cervical lesions. Future work will focus on training and validation with large-scale annotated colposcopy datasets to reduce domain shift and further support clinical deployment.

title: Integrating Artificial Intelligence and Social Network Systems to Enhance Contracting Efficiency
authors: Haiyan Sally Xie, Swetha Yallapu, Pranshoo Solanki, Catherine Shi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Social Network Systems (AISNS)
abstract: Traditional contracting processes often experience delays, miscommunication, and inefficiencies due to fragmented information and limited predictive capabilities. This paper proposes a framework to enhance transparency, accelerate decision-making, and optimize resource allocation throughout the contracting lifecycle. In this architecture, Legal-BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) extracts domain-specific semantic representations from legal documents, which are then processed by a Large Language Model for contextual reasoning and predictive analytics. Simultaneously, a Knowledge Graph organizes contractual entities into a structured relational map, enabling real-time query, compliance checks, and interpretability. The methodology combines Artificial Intelligence (AI) models for contract risk assessment with social network analysis to identify key influencers and streamline stakeholder engagement. The survey findings indicate that respondents expressed strong agreement (M=4.58, SD=1.23, and a 95% CI of [4.12, 5.04]) with the predictive analytics contributions of reduced negotiation time, improved compliance, and stronger relational governance. The paper offers practical implications for industries seeking agile and data-driven contracting solutions, supporting the emerging discourse on digital transformation in contract management.

title: Fusing Communication and Computation for 3D Tensor Parallel Training of Large Models
authors: Binwen Luo
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: As large language models scale rapidly, single-device training becomes infeasible due to computational and memory constraints, making distributed training essential. However, it introduces significant communication overhead and redundant activation computation, limiting scalability. This paper presents a 3D tensor-parallel framework based on communication-computation fusion. The initial distribution of input activations is parallelized to reduce bandwidth contention, while the AllReduce operation is equivalently decomposed and reordered with local computation to remove redundant activation costs without increasing communication volume. The method achieves operator-level fusion without additional synchronization and employs a cross-layer transposed partition for consistency and deployability. Experiments on multi-GPU clusters show notable improvements in throughput and parallel efficiency, enabling scalable and convergence-stable training of large-scale models.

title: Daily Rainfall Forecasting using LSTM-MLP with Spatial Embeddings
authors: V Asha, Arpana Prasad, M T Vasumathi, Abhinav Reddy R, Abhishek G H, Avinash Manik Kamble
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Daily rainfall forecast is critical in agriculture, emergency rainfall analysis, and urban planning. However, rainfall forecast remains a challenge because of dynamic changes in rain-forecasting models. The article proposes a combination model with a blend of three advantages, including an LSTM model to predict latest trends in rain, an MLP model to examine present rain information, and a spatial embedding model to grasp rain information in different geographical sites. With the Weather AUS dataset, which contains information relevant to rainfall in different stations in Australia, the model performed very well with a 68.82% accuracy level and an ROC AUC of 0.77. The model performed well in discriminating rainy days from non-rainy days, which is important in early rainfall warning or forecast systems. The model had a stable training environment with a controlled validation loss during rainfall forecast. A combination of space, time, and table information in this model enables better rainfall forecast sites with a suitable model implementation strategy.

title: Lightweight Blockchain-Based Framework for Credit Risk Assessment and Experimental Analysis
authors: Jiayi Tian, Chuan Li
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Traditional centralized credit risk assessment systems suffer from limited transparency and vulnerability to tampering, which undermines the reliability of financial data management. To address this issue, this paper proposes a lightweight blockchain-based framework that uses a hash-based storage and verification mechanism to achieve decentralized integrity protection for key credit data, while integrating logistic regression and random forest models for credit risk prediction. Experimental results on the UCI credit card dataset show that the proposed framework maintains high prediction accuracy and significantly reduces blockchain storage and operational overhead, demonstrating an effective balance between security, efficiency, and model performance.

title: Security Integration and Risk Management in Modern DevOps: Tools, Frameworks, and Emerging Trends
authors: Manjula Sanjay Koti, Ankit Kumar, J Rajeshwari, S. Satheesh Kumar, Khalid Anjum, Gautam S Languti
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: The emergence of DevOps has pushed teams to release updates more quickly than ever before, genuinely revolutionizing the speed of software delivery. Our study delves deeply into this important issue by examining how strong security measures, also known as DevSecOps, can be seamlessly integrated into DevOps procedures. We have combed through the most recent scholarly research to create a comprehensive picture of the frameworks, tools, and approaches that effectively integrate security into each stage of the software development process. Our analysis shows that there is an urgent need for automated security testing, cutting-edge risk models like the Preference Ranking Organization Method for Enrichment Evaluation (PROMETHEE), and cutting-edge solutions like blockchain to produce reliable audit trails. Future developments are probably going to concentrate on deeper governance integration, AIpowered threat intelligence, and the creation of standardized security maturity models for big businesses.

title: Human Factors In Cyber Incident Response: Mitigating Biases, Enhancing Communication, And Optimizing Tool Usability
authors: Harchi Anas, Hicham Toumi, Mohamed Talea
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This paper examines how important human characteristics affect the capability of teams managing cyber incidents. Three important components are emphasized: the impact of cognitive biases (CB) on decision-making, the interaction of communication in response teams, and the usefulness of security tools. We conduct a meticulous literature review alongside case study evaluations to pinpoint the difficulties in achieving effective incident response (IR). Our findings indicate that usual, technology-centered tactics often ignore the substantial ramifications of human mistakes and CB. We propose an integrated framework combining structured debiasing techniques, adaptive communication protocols, and cognitive load-optimized interfaces. The framework integrates with existing open-source platforms, providing practical solutions for organizations seeking to enhance IR capabilities through human-centered design. This paper presents a conceptual framework synthesized from extensive literature review and case analysis, establishing a foundation for future empirical validation in operational IR environments.

title: Adversarial Bandit Learning Assisted Online Optimization for Digital Twin Placement and Update in End-Edge-Cloud Collaboration
authors: Yuye Yang, Changyan Yi, Haifeng Zhu, Shimin Gong, Jiawen Kang, Dusit Niyato
abstract: Digital twin (DT) is envisioned not only to perform the high-fidelity virtual representation of its corresponding physical entity (PE), but also to serve as an active agent delivering diverse types of sophisticated services. This paper studies an end-edge-cloud collaborative DT placement and update framework. Specifically, we consider that DTs are dynamically placed across edge servers (ESs) via migration following their paired PEs' potential mobility, while being supported by real-time data fetched from the cloud center and user ends. On top of this, we emphasize a unique feature that DTs should also be continually updated capturing the uncertain evolutions for both personalized service ability improvement and versatile service ability maintenance, where the personalization is improved by utilizing the experiential knowledge from the cloud center and their corresponding PEs, and the versatility is maintained by integrating pre-stored profiles. To maximize the long-term system-wide average weighted quality-of-service (QoS) in handling all types of PEs' service requests under the stringent system cost constraint, we formulate an online problem to jointly optimize DT migrations, service priorities towards various request types, and all related DT updating strategies. To address underlying difficulties, we propose a novel adversarial bandit learning assisted online optimization approach, called ARBOK. We first leverage the Lyapunov decomposition method to transform the long-term problem into multiple instant ones, each of which is further decoupled into two correlated subproblems. For solving one subproblem with a bilinear structure, we develop a McCormick envelopes based algorithm (MO-EL). Besides, we design an extended adversarial combinatorial multi-armed bandit algorithm (AC-BL) to tackle the other subproblem, which constructs a super arm set to resolve the issue of excessively large decision space and employs a robust scheme to handle the inherent uncertainty and non-stationarity in each super arm's loss function. We integrate both algorithms seamlessly into ARBOK and alternately execute them till the convergence. Theoretical analysis and extensive simulations show the effectiveness of the introduced dynamic DT placement and continual update framework, demonstrating that ARBOK can converge to the asymptotic optimum within a polynomial-time complexity while outperforming counterparts.

title: DiffPixelFormer: Differential Pixel-Aware Transformer for RGB-D Indoor Scene Segmentation
authors: Yan Gong, Jianli Lu, Yongsheng Gao, Jie Zhao, Xiaojuan Zhang, Susanto Rahardja
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Indoor semantic segmentation is fundamental to computer vision and robotics, supporting applications such as autonomous navigation, augmented reality, and smart environments. Although RGB-D fusion leverages complementary appearance and geometric cues, existing methods often depend on computationally intensive cross-attention mechanisms and insufficiently model intra- and inter-modal feature relationships, resulting in imprecise feature alignment and limited discriminative representation. To address these challenges, we propose DiffPixelFormer, a differential pixel-aware Transformer for RGB-D indoor scene segmentation that simultaneously enhances intra-modal representations and models inter-modal interactions. At its core, the Intra-Inter Modal Interaction Block (IIMIB) captures intra-modal long-range dependencies via self-attention and models inter-modal interactions with the Differential–Shared Inter-Modal (DSIM) module to disentangle modality-specific and shared cues, enabling fine-grained, pixel-level cross-modal alignment. Furthermore, a dynamic fusion strategy balances modality contributions and fully exploits RGB-D information according to scene characteristics. Extensive experiments on the SUN RGB-D and NYUDv2 benchmarks demonstrate that DiffPixelFormer-L achieves mIoU scores of 54.28% and 59.95%, outperforming DFormer-L by 1.78% and 2.75%, respectively. Moreover, its effectiveness is further validated on the large-scale ScanNetv2 dataset, indicating strong generalization capability. Code is available at https://github.com/gongyan1/DiffPixelFormer.

title: Independent PPO-based Robust and Scalable Three-dimensional Configuration for Green Massive MIMO Networks
authors: Qichen Wang, Shuai Zhang, Mustafa Ozger, Cicek Cavdar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: In green massive MIMO networks, reducing power consumption (PC) while ensuring user quality of service (QoS) is critical for sustainable operation. To this end, we propose a robust and scalable reinforcement learning framework based on independent proximal policy optimization (IPPO), enabling intelligent base station (BS) control through a three-dimensional configuration of antenna activation, sleep mode transitions, and user offloading. Compared to a non-learning simple energy-saving policy, our proposed IPPO algorithm achieves approximately a 20.3% reduction in PC and a 49% improvement in energy efficiency (EE). In addition, it demonstrates significantly faster convergence and better scalability than multi-agent PPO (MAPPO), reducing convergence time by approximately 75% with 49 BSs and by around 90% with 81 BSs.

title: LED Pouch Motor: Wavelength Selective Wireless Actuation of Dyed Liquid-to-Gas Phase Change Actuators Using LEDs
authors: Kazuki Yamaura, Hiroki Ishizuka, Takefumi Hiraki
abstract: Untethered soft actuators that operate without physical connections to external power sources or control system cables are an active area of research for their potential applications in human-robot interaction, haptic devices, and 4D printing due to their wide range of motion and high degrees of freedom. Among these actuators, liquid-to-gas phase change actuators have a simple structure and can generate higher output force using photothermal conversion. In this study, we propose a novel control method for untethered soft actuators by dyeing the low-boiling-point liquids used in liquid-to-gas phase change actuators. The dyed low-boiling-point liquids exhibit different light absorption spectra matching their color, enabling wavelength-selective actuation using light sources with corresponding wavelengths. We evaluated and discussed this control method in terms of the relationship between irradiance and output, actuation speed, and continuous operation. Additionally, we prototyped a robotic arm to demonstrate selective motion according to the light color.

title: Rethinking Cross-Layer Dependency in MPQ: Sparsification with PSD Framework
authors: Hyesung Lee, Minkyu Jung, Kyungrak Son
abstract: Mixed-precision quantization (MPQ) is a vital technique for deep learning model compression. In IQP-based MPQ, the sensitivity matrix capturing cross-layer dependencies is often not positive semi-definite (PSD), and the conventional eigenvalue-clipping correction introduces ordering distortion that can lead to suboptimal bit allocations. In this paper, we propose a novel MPQ framework using Shift-PSD, a structure-preserving PSD correction method combined with block-level sparsification. Our approach provably eliminates ordering distortion while preserving the exact optimal solutions, simultaneously achieving lightweight computation. Specifically, our two-stage framework first sparsifies the sensitivity matrix to remove noisy long-range interactions, and then enforces the PSD property via a spectral shift that adds only a constant offset over the feasible set. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves superior accuracy with lower computational cost (BitOps) in the highly compressed region.

title: A Magnetic Capsule for Navigation and Multi-targeted Sampling in the Gastrointestinal Tract_supp3-3677072.mp4
authors: Yu Sun
abstract: Untethered capsules are capable of entering the gastrointestinal (GI) tract and collecting fluid samples containing microbial communities from specific locations, facilitating the study of chronic diseases. However, existing sampling capsules are designed for single-site sampling, making it challenging to gather samples from multiple targets. This paper reports a magnetic-driven capsule for multiple sampling within the GI tract and an on-demand magnetic-triggered fluid sampling strategy. The capsule consists of a body, a magnetic-triggered negative pressure unit, and a reservoir unit. Composed of an elastic membrane and Magnet I, the negative pressure unit controls pressure change inside the capsule cavity on demand to pump the sample by switching the magnetic field, while the embedded Magnet I also enables real-time magnetic localization for regional targeting and position tracking. The reservoir unit integrates three sampling papers for fluid absorption, two waterproof layers that maintain contamination levels below 25% to ensure reliable multi-site sampling, and a rotating arm embedded with Magnet II for posture adjustment of the sampling paper. The pumping and storage performance of the capsule was systematically evaluated and optimized. Meanwhile, the capsule, actuated by an external magnetic field, was evaluated for its active locomotion performance. Finally, the feasibility of using the capsule to perform active navigation and multi-target sampling in a porcine intestine was validated via ex vivo experiments.

title: EASNet: An Adaptive Edge-Aware 3D Medical Image Segmentation Network Based on the Swin Architecture
authors: Chengjie Chen, Jiang Wu, Liang Wang
abstract: To address the challenge of segmenting organs and tumors in medical images caused by low pixel contrast, this study proposes an Edge-Aware Self-Adaptive Network (EASNet), a 3D medical image segmentation network that integrates adaptive weight matching and attention mechanisms.The proposed method consists of three main components. First, the encoder combines a Swin Transformer with downsampling operations to achieve multi-scale feature extraction and global context modeling. Second, an AdaptiveWeight Matching (AWM) module dynamically adjusts the feature weights between the encoder and decoder based on channel characteristics, enabling efficient feature fusion. Third, the decoder incorporates a Hybrid Attention Convolution (HAC) module with both channel and spatial attention mechanisms to enhance the interaction between high-level and low-level features. Experimental results demonstrate that EASNet outperforms existing methods in terms of the Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) on the BTCV and BraTS 2021 datasets, achieving a DSC of 88.65% on the Synapse dataset and a low 95th-percentile Hausdorff Distance (HD95) of 7.76. These results indicate that the proposed method exhibits superior performance in both multi-organ segmentation and brain tumor segmentation tasks.

title: HAAD: A Heuristic Ant Algorithm for Defending Against Website Fingerprinting Attacks
authors: Pingfan Li, Kun Xie, Ruotian Xie, Pengcheng Zhao, Jiajun He, Jigang Wen, Wei Liang, Yong Xie
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Networking
abstract: None

title: ADA-Net: Avalanche Differential Attention Network with Covariance Correlation Differential Transformer for Multimodal Snow Avalanche Susceptibility Mapping_supp1-3676659.docx
authors: Yuan-Jun Jiang
abstract: Multimodal data integration plays a critical role in accurately mapping snow avalanche susceptibility, yet existing models struggle to capture the complex nonlinear interactions among optical imagery, SAR backscatter, snowpack measurements, and meteorological variables. Traditional statistical and shallow machine learning (ML) approaches often rely on handcrafted features and linear assumptions, while recent deep learning (DL) methods employ simplistic fusion strategies that overlook higher-order dependencies and cross-modal discrepancies. To address these limitations, this study introduces a novel multimodal DL framework combining a Covariance-Correlation Differential Transformer (CCDT) for second-order feature extraction with an Avalanche Differential Attention Network (ADA-Net) for targeted cross-modal fusion. CCDT constructs differential covariance correlation matrices to reveal subtle interdependencies across 17 topographic, snowpack, and meteorological inputs, and ADA-Net’s Siamese transformer architecture amplifies critical discrepancies between modalities. Evaluated on a curated dataset of 150 avalanche events along China’s G217 and G218 highways in the Central Tianshan Mountains, our CCDT-ADA-Net framework achieves superior predictive performance (ROC-AUC = 0.9903, F1 = 0.96) and spatial accuracy, successfully classifying 97.4% of historical avalanche release areas within its high-susceptibility zone. It significantly outperforms benchmark models, including Vision Transformer, ConvLSTM, RNN, HybridMLP, and TabNet. Permutation importance analysis confirms snow density, elevation, and snow depth as dominant risk factors. This scalable, interpretable approach offers practical utility for operational avalanche forecasting and infrastructure protection in high-mountain regions.

title: IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Energy Publication Information

title: EventChat: Implementation and user-centric evaluation of a large language model-driven conversational recommender system for exploring leisure events in an SME context

title: Alzheimer’s Disease Prediction from MRI Images using a Hybrid CNN–SVM Model
authors: K. Maheswari, K. Shilpa, Bayyaram Bhargavi, Bandi Bharath Raj, Badavath Sai Charan, Punyaban Patel
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease is an aggressive, progressively worsening condition that results in the loss of memory and intellectual functions. Henceforth, prompt recognition of Alzheimer’s disease is going to play an important role in making sure that the patient receives adequate treatment. Brain Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is a noninvasive method typically used to illustrate the presence of anatomical alterations associated with the disease in individuals. Nonetheless, interpreting the images remains a resource-intensive and subjective task that heavily relies on the evaluations of the experts involved. The swiftly growing amount of imaging data produced in healthcare has enabled the development of automated and intelligent diagnostic systems that are progressively being embraced in the quest for more precise and reliable disease diagnosis. This study presents a hybrid approach for forecasting Alzheimer's disease through the use of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) alongside Support Vector Machines (SVM). The CNN model's role is to independently identify deep features from MRI images, while the SVM classifier makes the final decision based on the features obtained. The contemporary hybrid approach combines deep learning for feature extraction with a robust machine-learning classifier, leading to improved prediction accuracy; nevertheless, it maintains the same level of reliability, making it a dependable resource for decision-making in the early diagnosis of Alzheimer’s disease

title: Energy-Aware Routing Protocol for Wireless Sensor Networks using Reinforced Sparrow Search Optimization (RSSO)
authors: Ashokkumar Muniraj, Aarthi.V.P.M.B., S.Ramya, D.Sharmiladevi, P.Gomathi, N.Mahendran
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are severely constrained by limited battery energy, making energy-efficient routing a critical factor in extending network lifetime and ensuring reliable data delivery. This paper proposes a novel energy-aware routing protocol based on Reinforced Sparrow Search Optimization (RSSO), which enhances the conventional Sparrow Search Algorithm by integrating reinforcement-based adaptive learning to dynamically balance exploration and exploitation. RSSO optimizes both cluster head selection and multi-hop routing by jointly considering residual energy, communication cost, and transmission distance, thereby achieving balanced energy consumption and reduced routing overhead. The reinforcement mechanism enables continuous adaptation of control parameters in response to network conditions, preventing premature convergence and uneven energy depletion. Extensive MATLAB-based simulations were conducted under identical stopping criteria and repeated runs to ensure statistical reliability. Performance evaluation against HHO, EWOA, and HGW-SSO demonstrates that RSSO significantly improves network lifetime, packet delivery ratio, energy efficiency, and throughput, while exhibiting lower variance and tighter confidence intervals across multiple runs. The results confirm that RSSO provides a scalable, statistically robust, and adaptive routing solution suitable for both homogeneous and heterogeneous WSN deployments.

title: Improved Dung Beetle Algorithm for Odor Source Localization using Fuzzy Inference
authors: Kumar Gaurav
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: This paper presents an improved Dung beetle algorithm (IDBA) for odor source localization (OSL) using a fuzzy inference method. The algorithm was evaluated in a simulation platform using laminar flow. The simulated mobile robot was used to perform experiments. The IDBA was compared with the Dung beetle algorithm (DBA) and found to be efficient in the given conditions. The overall distance travelled by the mobile agent was reduced by 6% to 35%, which highlights the efficiency of the algorithm. Moreover, the offset angle obtained from the proposed algorithm is also smaller compared to the conventional approach.

title: Data-Driven Prediction of Average Torque, Phase Resistance, and Coil Turns in 6/4 Switched Reluctance Motors Using ANN and EMDLAB
authors: Nasrin Majlesi, Ali Jamali-Fard, Tohid Sharifi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: In this paper, a data-driven approach based on an artificial neural network (ANN) with high accuracy was developed to predict the average torque, phase resistance, and number of coil turns of a switched reluctance motor (SRM). An initial 6/4 SRM model was designed in ANSYS Maxwell, and to reduce the computational cost of finite-element analysis, the open-source EMDLAB software was used to generate the data required for the ANN. A total of 1,000 SRM samples with variations in stator pole arc angle, rotor pole arc angle, and stator inner diameter were designed and simulated in EMDLAB. These three geometric parameters were used as the input data to the ANN so that it could simultaneously predict the three important outputs, including average torque, phase resistance, and number of coil turns. Evaluation of the model on the test data demonstrates the model’s ability to generalize to new data. The trained network achieves a coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) close to one for all outputs, and the residual error is very small. This surrogate model significantly reduces the time required for multi-objective optimization. The results show that the proposed model is a reliable substitute for time-consuming simulations and can be effectively used in the design and optimization processes of SRM machines.

title: Optimization and Enhancing the Torque Profile of a Novel Double-Layer Radial-Flux Magnetic Gear Using a DOE Method
authors: Mohammad Mardani, Mahdi Abolghasemi, Aghil Ghaheri, Seyyed Ebrahim Afjei
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: The growing demand for high-efficiency and low-maintenance power transmission systems has accelerated interest in magnetic gearing technologies. Compared with conventional mechanical gears, magnetic gears offer silent operation, improved reliability, and inherent overload protection, making them highly attractive for modern transportation and renewable energy applications. This paper presents the design, analysis, and optimization of a novel Double-Layer Radial-Flux Magnetic Gear (DLRFMG) aimed at improving torque density and reducing torque ripple. The proposed structure employs two torque conversion layers to achieve a higher gear ratio and torque transmission capability. To optimize the performance, the Taguchi method was applied with an L27 orthogonal array, enabling multi-objective optimization of maximum applicable torque and torque ripple across all rotors with minimal experiments count. The results demonstrate significant improvements, with maximum torque enhanced by up to 72.45% and torque ripple reduced by more than 60% compared with the initial design. Analysis of variance (ANOVA) further identified the dominant influence with their corresponding percentages of the most effective control factors. The optimized DLRFMG exhibits superior torque characteristics, making it a promising solution for applications such as electric bicycles, transportation systems, and renewable energy devices.

title: A Lightweight and Example-Free Class-Agnostic Counting Network
authors: Hao Li, Jianhui Guo, Weiyu Sun, Yabo Zhao
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: In current class-agnostic counting research, models commonly suffer from reliance on external exemplars and high computational complexity, which limit their application in resource-constrained scenarios such as edge computing. Therefore, exploring lightweight counting methods that require no exemplar priors and enable efficient inference has become a key direction for promoting technological deployment. Toward addressing the limitations of existing class-agnostic counting methods in external exemplar dependency and model complexity, we propose LECNet, a lightweight and example-free class-agnostic counting network. The network adopts an encoder-decoder architecture, using MobileViTv2-S as the backbone encoder to extract multi-level features with local details and global context. Building upon this architecture, a pseudo-exemplar generation module integrating dual channel-spatial focus attention and adaptive pooling is designed, which autonomously extracts representative visual features from a single input image without relying on manually annotated exemplars. To enhance the semantic relevance between backbone features and pseudo-exemplar features, this paper introduces a bidirectional attention fusion module to improve the model's ability to discriminate and localize target regions. The decoder employs a lightweight design to progressively reconstruct high-resolution density maps, achieving end-to-end precise counting. Experiments on the class-agnostic FSC-147 dataset demonstrate that LECNet maintains competitive counting accuracy while substantially reducing both parameter count and computational overhead. With only 5.4M parameters and 2.8G FLOPs, it achieves an MAE of 18.26, striking an excellent balance between accuracy and efficiency. https://drive.google.com/file/d/1ymDYrGs9DSRicfZbSCDiOu0ikGDh5k6S/view?usp=sharing provides access to the FSC-147 dataset.

title: Supervised Machine Learning for Detecting Unreliable Online Reviews
authors: UndraLeela Gayathri, N. Sreenivasa Rao, Segu Mounika, JuturuBhavya Sri, Dasari Poojitha, M. Surya Bhupal Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Product ratings on the internet significantly influence the buying behavior of consumers, yet the increasing number of fake or deceptive reviews is still a major problem for both consumers and retailers. This study proposes a machine-learning pipeline that is supervised and automated for the classification of the reviews into genuine and false based on both the textual and linguistic features. The whole process started with the pre-processing of a total of 40,432 product reviews that had undergone text cleaning, normalization, and removal of noise, and then each review was assigned a binary credibility label. The TF-IDF vectorization was used for cleaning the text and, with a total of 5,000 features, the numerical representation was achieved. Logistic Regression and Random Forest classifiers were trained and their performance was assessed in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score metrics. The models that have been trained can now be applied to predict the real-time credibility of reviews thus helping the platform gain more trust and consumers in their decision-making.

title: Design of Waist Exoskeleton Based on STM32
authors: Pengfei Jiang, Jinliang Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Handling process is an indispensable part of the production process. To reduce the physical burden on manual handlers, this paper designs a waist exoskeleton system with STM32 as its core. The system determines the current human movement state using a 6-axis attitude sensor module, combined with angular velocity from the motor encoder and information on motor rotation angle. Leveraging the real-time motor movement angle and angular velocity values obtained by the encoder, it controls the motor to output time-varying power, thereby assisting the wearer in their movements. Through bending load experiments and walking load experiments, it is proven that the system can effectively analyze and process the feedback information from the sensors and motor encoder, and can control the motor to assist the human body in bending and walking movements.

title: Adaptive Masking DWI Denoising Algorithm Guided by b0 Images
authors: Huarui Wang, Linhai Wu, Zeyu Deng, Lihui Wang, Yueming Zhu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Denoising diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) is crucial for early disease diagnosis, yet existing self-supervised methods often over-smooth structures, are affected by spatially correlated noise, and depend heavily on the number of diffusion directions, resulting in degraded performance under sparse sampling. We propose a b0-guided self-supervised DWI denoising method that integrates double-shift region partitioning and region-adaptive masking. A texture-measurement matrix is first computed from the b<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> image using double-shift region partitioning, enabling robust separation of flat and textured voxels while suppressing uncertain regions. Then, region-adaptive masking assigns different mask ratios to different region types, balancing noise decorrelation and structure/texture preservation. Unlike approaches relying on angular redundancy, our masking paradigm exploits spatial redundancy, reducing sensitivity to diffusion-direction count and improving directional robustness. Across three noise models (Gaussian, Rician, Nc-Chi) and multiple direction settings in both simulation and ablation studies, the proposed method consistently achieves superior PSNR, SSIM, and RMSE, and experiments on real datasets further validate its effectiveness and robustness.

title: Research on Accurate Traceability and Prediction Method of Power Failure Events of Smart Meters Based on Spatio-Temporal Big Data
authors: Xuan Kong, Rundan Zhang, Leiyang Zhao, He Cui, Yunze Li
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Aiming at the challenge of extreme weather and new energy access to the traditional power grid fault handling mode, this paper proposes an accurate traceability and prediction method of power outage events of smart meters based on spatio-temporal big data. A physical constraint-spatio-temporal fusion-dynamic prediction framework (PSTF-Pred) is constructed. Firstly, the power grid structure is transformed into a graph neural network calculation model at the physical constraint level, and the weighted adjacency matrix is obtained through the electrical distance correction to reflect the real physical characteristics of the power grid topology. Then, in the spatio-temporal fusion layer, Spatio-Temporal Graph Attention Network (ST-GAT) is used to combine the power grid topology and time series data, and the time characteristics are extracted by the Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and the importance relationship between nodes is dynamically learned through the spatial attention mechanism, thus effectively solving the problem of spatial over-smoothness. Finally, the dynamic prediction layer uses the fractal multi-scale model to quantify the fault propagation characteristics, and evaluates the long-range dependence by calculating the Hurst index of voltage series. Combined with short-term, medium-term and long-term prediction mechanisms, LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) time series prediction, weather data fusion and survival analysis based on fractal parameters are used to predict the power grid fault risk respectively. The experiment is based on the data of 120,000 smart meters collected by a provincial power grid from 2021 to 2023. The results show that PSTF-Pred is significantly superior to the comparison method in three indicators: average positioning error, typhoon scene error and the ratio of error exceeding 2 km, and is also significantly lower than the comparison method in the mean absolute error (MASE) of multi-scale prediction, and it is more robust in extreme scenarios.

title: Efficient and Precise Cheat Detection with Deep Learning
authors: Ilhame Khabbachi, Ghalia Mdaghri-Alaoui, Abdelhamid Zouhair
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: The digital transformation of education has accelerated significantly, driven in large part by the global health crisis of 2020. While this shift to online learning has opened new pedagogical opportunities, it has also raised pressing concerns about the authenticity of remote assessments. Educational institutions worldwide now face a key challenge: ensuring the fairness and credibility of examinations in fully digital environments—a challenge that grows more urgent as online education becomes a permanent fixture. In response, this research introduces an innovative, AI-powered solution designed to detect non-compliant behaviors during online assessments. The proposed system integrates real-time video monitoring with automated behavioral analysis to identify and deter academic misconduct. Using a student’s standard computer webcam, continuous video streams are captured and analyzed through advanced deep learning algorithms, combining object detection, classification models, and tailored data preprocessing to maximize detection accuracy. This approach contributes to safeguarding academic integrity in a rapidly evolving educational landscape while upholding the principles of fairness and transparency fundamental to effective evaluation.

title: Resource Slicing for Co-existence of eMBB and URLLC in Multi-RIS Aided Networks
authors: Bhagawat Adhikari, Ahmed Shaharyar Khwaja, Muhammad Jaseemuddin, Alagan Anpalagan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: In this paper, a two-stage solution for multiplexing of Enhanced Mobile Broadband (eMBB) and Ultra-reliable Low Latency Communication (URLLC) is provided in multireconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) aided downlink wireless networks. An Alternating Optimization (AO) technique is proposed to maximize the eMBB sum rate as a first-stage solution. In the second stage, a matching-based heuristic algorithm is used to minimize the eMBB rate loss caused by the punctured Resource Blocks (RBs) in the URLLC transmission. The proposed solution is evaluated through extensive simulations with various scenarios including different URLLC packet sizes, number of URLLC users and the eMBB threshold rates. The proposed solution with optimized RIS phase shift provides a smaller eMBB rate loss compared to the solution with random phase shift in the various scenarios mentioned above.

title: Improvement Method of Convolutional Neural Network Model in Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Ziwei Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Medical image segmentation is of great significance for disease diagnosis and treatment. However, the existing convolutional neural network (CNN) model has problems such as insufficient accuracy when processing complex medical images. This paper proposes an improved CNN model, constructs a multi-scale feature fusion module based on dilated convolution, introduces a channel and space joint attention mechanism, and designs a hybrid loss function. Experiments are conducted on the BraTS brain tumor dataset and the ISIC skin lesion dataset, and the model performance is evaluated by indicators such as Dice coefficient and IoU. The results show that the Dice coefficient of the improved model on the BraTS dataset is 0.895, which is 7.2% higher than U-Net, and the IoU value is 0.821, which is 6.1% higher, on the ISIC dataset, the Dice coefficient is 0.861, which is 6.3% higher than DeepLabv3 +, and the IoU is 0.791, which is 5.7% higher. Ablation experiments show that the improved modules work together significantly, effectively enhancing the model's ability to identify and segment the target area, and providing more accurate technical support for medical image-assisted diagnosis.

title: Design and Implementation of Predictive Location Analytics using Google Maps Data and Machine Learning
authors: Chillangi Manasa, Devanga Dharani Lakshmi, D. Venkatesh, M. Mallikarjuna Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Predictive location analytics is vital as a component of intelligent mobility, transportation planning, and city decisions. The given paper represents a proposal of a single machine-learning system, which will use Google Maps APIs, traffic data, and the POI distributions to predict the travel time, the 3D congestion, and the hotspots of demand. In that methodology, it combines spatial preprocessing based on geohashing, temporal window modeling, POI density modeling, and a hybrid prediction architecture based on Gradient Boosted Trees (GBT) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. The combination of these two approaches (LSTM and GBT) is shown to be effective in predicting the travel-time in Google Maps with high accuracy by incorporating nonlinear temporal dependencies and estimating the intensity of spatial hotspots, respectively. The reliability of the system is established through graphs and statistical findings which show that the system reduces the errors by a large margin compared to the baseline models. The suggested architecture offers a well-grounded scalable architecture of predictive mobility analytics within smart-city settings.

title: Advanced Deep Learning Algorithms: Architectures, Mathematics, Applications and Open Challenges
authors: Lobna El Harrouchi, Hanae Moussaoui, Mounir Karmoudi, Nabil El Akkad
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Deep learning has revolutionized artificial intelligence, but with very sensitive systems that exploit minute patterns inside data, able to solve impossible problems with fine nuance beyond anything that is even comprehensible by humans just a few years ago. The objective of this survey is to present an update on the state-of-the-art architectures, learning methods and computational techniques for GANs using relevant literature as source of information. We investigate Convolutional (CNNs), Recurrent (RNNs) and Long-Short-Term Memory networks, Transformers and attention mechanisms, Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), diffusion models, Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) and meta-learning. Theoretical backgrounds, mathematical definitions and training process are provided for each family, followed by association of their pros and cons to typical application domains as well as popular benchmarks. We also discuss cross-cutting issues interpretability, robustness, efficiency, ethics and privacy and summarize emerging research directions. To connect theory with practice, we add a comparative summary, case studies, and notes on implementation. Our aim with this work is to provide guidance to researchers, practitioners, and policy makers looking to responsibly harness advanced deep learning in practice.

title: Optical Performance and Environmental Durability of Radiation-Resistant Polarization-Maintaining Er/Yb Co-Doped Fiber in High-Power Optical Amplifiers for Space Communications
authors: Hiroki Kobayashi, Masatoshi Mitaki, Ryoichi Nishimura, Hideo Shiratani, Kentaro Ichii, Yasutoshi Takada
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: This report presents the optical performance and environmental durability of a radiation-resistant polarization-maintaining Er/Yb co-doped fiber (EYDF) developed by Fujikura Ltd. for space communications. The EYDF features a double cladding structure with a PANDA (polarization-maintaining and absorption-reducing) design, employing germanium doping for radiation resistance and phosphorus co-doping to improve Yb-to-Er energy transfer efficiency. To verify the reliable operation of this EYDF as a high-power optical amplifier (HPOA) in space environments, Nikon Corp. assembled and evaluated an amplifier incorporating this EYDF. The HPOA achieved an output power exceeding 30 W with ratios of signal to amplified spontaneous emission exceeding 37 dB and demonstrated stable operation for over 400 h. The polarization extinction ratio consistently exceeded 17 dB during continuous 10 W operation under temperature cycling from 25 °C to 45 °C, demonstrating robustness against temperature variations. High-temperature durability tests caused no significant changes in optical characteristics. These results demonstrate that the radiation-resistant EYDF is suitable for space-based optical communication systems.

title: Multi-Modal Medical Image Fusion using Guided Filtering and Multi-Scale Anisotropic Diffusion
authors: Dev Kumar Chaudhary, Prabhishek Singh, Achyut Shankar
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Multi-modal medical image fusion (M3IF) is an important field of medical image enhancement. It will enhance the examination of medical outcomes. The proposed method is an optimized M3IF (M3IF-GF-MLAD). Guided Filtering (GF) and Anisotropic Diffusion (AD) filtering are used in this technique. The GF method decomposes the input image into base layers (BLs) and detail layers (DLs). Both BLs and DLs are fused using an adaptive weighted strategy to maintain the equal information contribution of each modality. An edge-preserving AD filtering method is implemented as post-processing refinement for sharp edges. M3IF-GF-MLAD uses conductance function for edge preservation and less blurring. The Weickert diffusion model is integrated to boost contrast and for maintaining anatomical structures. M3IF-GF-MLAD is compared with conventional fusion methods, and it surpasses all of them. As M3IF-GF-MLAD offers better results so it has the capability to solution for medical imaging applications.

title: Application of Machine Learning-Based Multi-Source Heterogeneous Information Fusion in Moving Target Tracking
authors: Shunjie Lin, Weiwei Geng, Hanhua Cao, Tianyu Zheng
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Multisource heterogeneous fusion with machine learning still lacks geometric–physical hard constraints, interpretable uncertainty estimates, and out-of-distribution robustness. Existing approaches have not explicitly embedded geometric residuals, physics-conservation constraints, or interpretable uncertainty into the network loss, allowing systematic errors to be smoothed and concealed, which introduces potential risks in real-world deployment. This paper propose DynaToken-LT, which incorporates a delay-compensation mechanism into the attention computation. The algorithm was designed to take into account the temporal characteristics of sensor streams, thereby enhancing its applicability. In the experiment, the situation where the target temporarily leaves the field of view was simulated, and the tracking performance under conditions of sensor data loss and extreme motion was tested. Experiments demonstrate that it achieves robust, low-ID-swap, and low-computation multisource fusion tracking for moving objects.

title: GAN-based Synthetic Data Augmentation to Address Class Imbalance in RetinaMNIST Dataset
authors: Sai Harsha Gangari, Jithendra Katta, Nikhil Reddy Kodumuru, Balakrishana Reddy Ragannagari, Bhagavath Sai Darapureddy, Sai Bharath Reddy Lattupalli
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Medical imaging datasets often suffer from significant class imbalance, limiting the effectiveness of deep learning models in identifying rare conditions. In this study, we focus on the RetinaMNIST dataset, a benchmark for retinal disease classification, where minority classes are underrepresented. To address this, we trained a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) enhanced with residual connections on samples from the minority Class 4 to generate synthetic retinal images. These generated images were combined with the original training set to up sample the minority class. A VGG19-based Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) was trained on both the original and augmented datasets to assess performance improvements. Results show that augmenting the training data with GAN-generated samples significantly improves minority class recall and overall model robustness, highlighting the potential of residual GAN architectures and deep feature extractors like VGG19 in medical image augmentation tasks.

title: Genetic Algorithm-Based Placement and Resource Allocation for Flying Base Stations
authors: Fady A. Abouelghit, Karim G. Seddik
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are emerging as key components in next-generation wireless networks, offering adaptable and scalable solutions for dynamic communication environments. However, their effective deployment requires careful optimization of both spatial positioning and resource allocation. This paper addresses the joint problem of three-dimensional placement and transmit power configuration for Flying Base Stations (FBSs) operating in the presence of Macro Base Stations (MBSs). The problem is formulated as a Mixed-Integer Nonlinear Programming (MINLP) task, a class known for its computational intractability. To tackle this complexity, we propose a genetic algorithm-based framework that simultaneously optimizes spatial coordinates, power levels, and activation status for single and multiple FBS scenarios. Unlike conventional approaches that decouple these dimensions or limit their focus to isolated deployments, our methodology integrates spatial positioning, power allocation, multi-FBS coordination, and interference management with MBSs into a unified optimization framework. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves nearoptimal user coverage and significantly faster convergence compared to a bounded exhaustive search baseline, confirming its effectiveness and scalability in complex deployments involving multiple FBSs and MBSs.

title: Parking Path Planning Improvement Strategy Based on AI and Functional Safety
authors: Dan Li, Yunlei Zhang, Lijuan Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address the potential safety risks of traditional parking path planning strategies under electrical/electronic faults, this paper proposes an AI-enhanced functional safetyimproved parking path planning strategy. By defining the functional safety boundaries during the parking process and combining an arc-line-arc method optimized with cubic polynomial curves, a phased fault detection safety mechanism for perpendicular parking is designed. When a wheel steering angle fault is detected in the previous phase, functional safety boundary indicators are first selected as inputs for the AI model. By leveraging the nonlinear relationship learned through reinforcement learning between fault scenarios and path planning equation parameters, the path parameters are dynamically adjusted, forming an AI-enhanced functional safety-improved parking path planning strategy. The proposed parking algorithm was validated through Simulink/CarSim cosimulation. A comparison between the improved and nonimproved strategies shows that when the faulty steering angle is 10 degrees, the improved path planning strategy effectively avoids collisions, demonstrating superior parking safety. Furthermore, the improved strategy results in a smaller variation amplitude of the yaw rate during movement, indicating better driving stability.

title: Learned Image Compression with Frequency Feature Interaction and Non-local Cross-similarity Prior
authors: Jian Wang, Qiang Ling
publish: ACM Transactions on Multimedia Computing, Communications, and Applications
abstract: None

title: Fall Detection Framework - A Study of Optimized Neural Network and Ensemble Classifiers with Feature Explainability
authors: Roopa B Hegde, Pradyumna G R, P Dhakshak
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Falls constitute a leading cause of injury specially among the elderly population, which necessitates automated high precision systems. The present study proposes an automated fall detection system integrating edge device with explainable machine learning models. Tri-axial accelerometer data from smartphone representing six activities namely fall, jump, run, sit, sleep and walk was acquired, and spectral features were extracted using a 4000 ms window to capture movement patterns. Top twenty significant features were selected employing ‘ReliefF’ algorithm to train Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Neural Network classifiers, optimized using Bayesian optimization for hyperparameter tuning. The optimized Neural Network outperformed achieving accuracy of 99.14% and a recall of 98.26%. Further, explainable AI was employed to validate the models for reliability and clinical trust on relevant features. These findings indicate that spectral feature extraction combined with optimized machine learning models are effective, for real-time fall detection and suitable for edge deployment.

title: Integrated Edge-based Fault Analytics and Auto-Reclosing Control for Smart Three-Phase Distribution Systems
authors: M Muthukumaran, Prithive S, Sriram R, Lohitha G, Sonika S
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Traditional relays persist in holding back dependable fault management with their poor discrimination of momentary disturbances from permanent faults, lack of fine-grained phase-wise analysis, and failure to provide a uniform cloud-based visibility, especially in three-phase distribution systems. This gap creates the need for an edge-compatible, low-cost protection network architecture with real-time event-type classification and automated response mechanisms as well as remote monitoring. The designed scheme utilizes an ESP32 controller for the analysis of PT and CT readings with instant magnitude retrieval, while a constant threshold of 50 A is used to classify L-G, L-L, and three-phase faults. A persistence time of 0.05 s is used to eliminate transitory signals from sustained errors. For non-damageable faults, the schedule initiates a three-shot auto-reclosing operation, whilst for faults that do not clear, there is a transfer to lockout of appropriate quantities. MATLAB/Simulink simulation results show that all expected waveforms (voltage sag and inrush) at 0.04 s match the specified logic implemented in the program. Random hardware tests further validate consistent fault detection, transient fault restoration, and permanent fault lockout. Phase and fault recordings are transmitted to ThingSpeak every minute when a phase voltage is present, fault codes (0-3) or temporary/permanent event counts: see MATLAB analytics for verification of data availability. A practical defense and sensing layer for smart-grid/industrial/academic deployments, the system platform is also a scalable substrate for further penetration into higher voltage deployments, predictive maintenance, and more robust IoT communication stacks.

title: ConYOLO-SLAM: A Robust Dynamic SLAM System via Semantic Perception and Temporal Consistency
authors: Wen Liu, Le Miao, Xinsen Li, Zhongliang Deng
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Moving objects in dynamic environments severely undermine the static world assumption of visual SLAM systems, leading to feature mismatches and pose estimation drift. Existing semantic SLAM methods overly rely on the accuracy of single-frame segmentation networks, making them prone to failure when the network experiences missed detections or noisy segmentation boundaries. To address this issue, this paper proposes ConYOLO-SLAM, a robust dynamic SLAM system that integrates semantic awareness with temporal consistency. First, the system integrates the YOLOv8 semantic segmentation network to provide pixel-level semantic prior information for front-end feature extraction, identifying potential dynamic regions within the scene. To address the uncertainty inherent in single-frame semantic inference, we introduce a sliding-window-based temporal consistency feature filtering mechanism. This mechanism constructs a temporal buffer for feature point states and performs multi-frame validation of dynamic attributes through geometric position correlation, retaining only temporally persistent dynamic features. Experimental results on public datasets demonstrate that this approach effectively suppresses dynamic object interference, achieving significant improvements in localization accuracy and robustness in dynamic scenes compared to ORB-SLAM3.

title: Development of an Embedded Machine Learning Model for Predictive Fault Diagnosis and Energy Optimization in Electric Vehicles
authors: R.Nidhya, G.Muthuram, A.Karthikeyan, V.Ellappan
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The increasing complexity of powertrains of electric vehicles (EV) requires the incorporation of intelligent mechanisms on fault prediction and energy management. Conventional diagnostic and energy control methods do not offer real-time information or predictability, and their reliability is lower with increased cost of operation. This study builds a built-in machine learning (ML) system that can perform predictive fault diagnostics and dynamic optimum energy regulation of EVs. The experiment used real and simulated EV data sets of different driving conditions, loads, and environmental parameters. Several ML models such as the Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks were trained to identify malfunctions in the battery and motor subsystems. The LSTM-based model had the best overall performance with an accuracy of prediction of fault of 96.8 %, precision of 95.4 %, recall of 94.9%, and energy optimization efficiency improvement of 12.6% as compared to baseline control strategies. The model had a computation latency improvement of 18.3% which proved its applicability in embedded implementation. The designed embedded ML system is efficient in predictive maintenance and adaptive energy management of EVs.

title: Transmission Duty Cycle Derivation Using AI/ML Algorithm from Telemetry Data
authors: Rakesh Kushwaha, Hiralkumar Patel
abstract: This paper presents an innovative methodology for generating transmission duty cycles for drivetrain evaluation, combining Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) techniques. Traditional methods rely on limited physical tests and deterministic models that fail to account for real-world driving complexities. The proposed framework uses large-scale vehicle telemetry data, AI-based pre-processing, and a combination of supervised and unsupervised ML algorithms to generate more accurate duty cycles. These algorithms include clustering, Principal Component Analysis (PCA), and neural networks. Additionally, reinforcement learning and predictive models are employed to simulate transmission responses under varied, unseen usage conditions. The methodology enhances the development of both conventional and electrified powertrains, providing proactive duty cycle optimization for advanced vehicle architectures, such as Automated Manual Transmission (AMT), Dual Clutch Transmission (DCT), and Electric Drive Units (EDUs).
