The integration of AI/ML-based strategies significantly reduces development costs and time while aligning with the Industry 4.0 paradigm and digital twin frameworks.

title: Real-time optical turbulence and wind profile monitoring with FEELINGS optical ground station
authors: Florian Quatresooz, Cyril Petit, Aurélie Bonnefois, Gaël Kermarrec, David Alaluf, Nicolas Védrenne
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: Future free-space optical communication links be-tween the ground and satellites will require systematic on-site monitoring of atmospheric turbulence to ensure reliable operation. To address this need, ONERA’s FEELINGS optical ground station has been equipped with a real-time turbulence monitoring pipeline, capable of retrieving refractive index structure parameter and wind profile information along the line of sight during optical links. The pipeline is first validated under controlled turbulent conditions before being applied to measurements collected during GEO-feeder links with the TELEO payload in September 2024. The retrieved turbulence conditions are then compared with numerical weather prediction simulations and used to assess the expected link performance.

title: Ground-to-GEO Bidirectional Optical Links between TELEO and various OGS: In Orbit Test Result, Experimental Comparison with Numerical Models for power budget and telecom link margins
authors: Sylvain Poulenard, Alvaro Jose Pascual-Gracia, Jean Frédéric Chouteau, Thibault Marduel, Frédéric Lacoste, Géraldine Artaud, Julien Sommer, Bouchra Benammar, Thierry Lanz, Etienne Samain, Loïc Eymar, Baptiste Jans, Alain Thomas, Nicolas Védrenne, Aurélie Montmerle-Bonnefois, Cyril Petit
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: TELEO is the Airbus Defence and Space demonstrator dedicated to in-orbit testing of cross atmospheric optical uplink and downlink, designed and implemented in the frame of the CNES DYSCO project. The payload, equipped with high-frequency irradiance metrology, offers the possibility of fine characterization of uplink irradiance statistical properties in the presence of adaptive optics pre-compensation. Connected to optical ground station with high-frequency metrology of irradiance and injected power, the overall system is unique to consolidate know-how on optical link budget. This work presents In-Orbit Demonstration (IOD) results and then focuses on a comparison between Airbus OneWave software output with IOD data from FrOGS beacon, from FEELINGS pre-compensated uplink, from FEELINGS corrected downlink.

title: Structure-Aware Decoding Mechanisms for Complex Entity Extraction with Large-Scale Language Models
authors: Zhimin Qiu, Di Wu, Feng Liu, Yuxiao Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: This paper proposes a structure-aware decoding method based on large language models to address the difficulty of traditional approaches in maintaining both semantic integrity and structural consistency in nested and overlapping entity extraction tasks. The method introduces a candidate span generation mechanism and structured attention modeling to achieve unified modeling of entity boundaries, hierarchical relationships, and cross-dependencies. The model first uses a pretrained language model to obtain context-aware semantic representations, then captures multi-granular entity span features through candidate representation combinations, and introduces hierarchical structural constraints during decoding to ensure consistency between semantics and structure. To enhance stability in complex scenarios, the model jointly optimizes classification loss and structural consistency loss, maintaining high recognition accuracy under multi-entity co-occurrence and long-sentence dependency conditions. Experiments conducted on the ACE 2005 dataset demonstrate significant improvements in Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-Score, particularly in nested and overlapping entity recognition, where the model shows stronger boundary localization and structural modeling capability. This study verifies the effectiveness of structure-aware decoding in complex semantic extraction tasks, provides a new perspective for developing language models with hierarchical understanding, and establishes a methodological foundation for high-precision information extraction.

title: Accelerating Multimodal Large Language Model Inference through Visual Feature Compression and Decoupled Architectures
authors: Xusheng Li, Zonghai Zhu, Li Feng, Huanlai Xing
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Multimodal large models face two major efficiency bottlenecks during inference: high memory consumption and serialized execution flow. This paper proposes an inference acceleration method that integrates visual feature compression with vision–language decoupling, comprising a Text-guided Image Feature Dimensionality Reduction (TG-IFDR) mechanism and a Visual–Language Decoupled architecture (VLBridge). TG-IFDR dynamically filters visual features based on textual semantics, reducing visual KV cache by approximately 40%, while VLBridge restructures the inference pipeline to enable parallel execution of visual preprocessing and language generation. Experiments on the MSCOCO image captioning task demonstrate that the proposed method achieves significant acceleration across four mainstream models: LLaVA, BLIP-2, CogVLM2, and Qwen-VL. Using VLBridge alone increases generation throughput to 9–10× that of the baseline, and when combined with TG-IFDR, overall inference speed reaches 15–20× the baseline, delivering an order-of-magnitude acceleration. Although feature compression introduces controllable prefill latency and minor semantic degradation, this overhead can be effectively amortized in long-sequence generation scenarios. The method maintains acceptable semantic consistency while substantially improving inference efficiency, offering a feasible solution for efficient deployment of multimodal large models in real-time interactive and resource-constrained scenarios. Future work will focus on learnable visual feature selection and cross-modal attention sparsification to further optimize the trade-off between accuracy and efficiency.

title: Modification of rotor lamination stacking of 425kW induction motor
authors: Arsalan Ajdani, Pouyan Shahabi, Meysam Davoud Abadi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: High-performance induction motors employ laminated rotor core designs to prevent undesirable eddy currents and minimize power loss. During the manufacturing of these laminated rotor cores, geometrical tolerances play a crucial role in maintaining proper stacking pressure. This case study focuses on determining the optimal geometrical tolerances for a 425-kW induction motor core lamination. Multi-step non-linear finite element simulations are conducted to obtain the optimal parameters for the core, fixture, and press plate design.

title: Triptych AI - Innovation - Cybersecurity: Challenges and Opportunities for Financial Services: The Case of Moroccan Banks
authors: Hammani Safae, El Bayane Adnane, El Mouaffaq Fadoua
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This “The future of banking belongs to those who can integrate technology without losing customer trust” (Gomber, Koch & Siering, 2017). This statement illustrates the current dynamics of financial services, where artificial intelligence (AI), innovation, and cybersecurity constitute an essential strategic triad.This raises a key question: how can the integration of artificial intelligence and innovation improve the performance and competitiveness of banking financial services while maintaining cybersecurity? This question refers not only to the search for a balance between efficiency and security, but also to the need for a sustainable development model for banking services in a digitalized context. In the case of Morocco, this topic is of particular interest. The Moroccan banking sector, one of the most developed in Africa (World Bank, 2021), is increasingly investing in digitalization and fintech solutions. Major banks such as Attijariwafa Bank, Bank of Africa, and Crédit Agricole du Maroc have initiated major digital transformation programs, integrating AI into customer relations, fraud detection, and credit scoring.

title: FOP: A Personalized Federated Learning Framework for CLIP Integrating LoRA and Differential Privacy
authors: Zepeng Li
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: To address the problems faced by cross-modal pre-trained models such as CLIP in federated learning scenarios—high computational overhead of full-parameter fine-tuning, high risk of privacy leakage, and insufficient personalized adaptation caused by data heterogeneity—this study proposes a personalized federated learning method (FOP) that integrates low-rank adaptation (LoRA), differential privacy (DP), and global-local optimized interpolation. The FOP framework adopts a dual-prompt mechanism and dynamically balances global consensus and local features through the interpolation coefficient λ. It uses LoRA to reduce the dimensionality of prompt parameters, combines the Gaussian mechanism with gradient L2 norm clipping, and achieves privacy protection. Experiments conducted on heterogeneous datasets verify that the FOP framework outperforms existing methods such as CoOp and FedOTP in all scenarios, fully demonstrating its effectiveness in the privacy-utility trade-off and personalized adaptation.

title: An Image Recognition Method Based on Multi-Scale Wavelet Transform Convolution and Convolutional Block Attention
authors: Yuhan Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: With the rapid development of digital media and artificial intelligence, intelligent image recognition has become a key link between the physical and digital worlds. Although convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have achieved strong performance in visual tasks, they still struggle with high-frequency detail loss, background interference, and hyperparameter sensitivity in fine-grained and complex-scene classification. To address these limitations, this paper proposes MSWT- CBAMFusionNet, a hybrid framework that combines multi-scale wavelet transform convolution with channel-spatial attention. The MSWT module jointly enhances fine-grained texture information in the spatial and frequency domains, while the CBAM-enhanced ResNet-50 backbone adaptively recalibrates features to suppress background noise. Furthermore, an improved Whale Optimization Algorithm (IWOA) is introduced to automatically tune key hyperparameters. Experiments on the CUB-200-2011 and Rice Leaf Disease datasets demonstrate that the proposed model achieves superior fine-grained feature extraction, stronger robustness in complex environments, and overall improved classification accuracy. Ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of each core component.

title: A Forged Speech Detection Method Based on a Hybrid Attention Mechanism
authors: Jing Yin, Peidong Luo
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: To address the security threats posed by deepfake audio and the limited generalization capabilities of existing detection methods, this paper proposes a synthetic speech detection approach incorporating a hybrid attention mechanism. This approach constructs a dual-branch network architecture. The front-end processes LFCC features and CQT spectrograms, while the back-end employs a ResNet18 backbone with an embedded CBAM module. Additionally, an Involution operator is introduced to adaptively capture feature correlations across multiple positions and scales. Combined with a Cross-Attention mechanism, this enables deep interaction and fusion of dualbranch features. Experimental results on the ASVspoof 2019 LA and ASVspoof 2021 LA datasets demonstrate that compared to baseline models, the proposed method achieves significant reductions in both the Equal Error Rate (EER) and the Minimum Tandem Detection Cost Function (min t-DCF) metrics. This effectively validates the feasibility and superiority of the proposed approach.

title: A Fast Analytical Method for No-Load Air Gap Flux Density and Voltage Estimation in Large Synchronous Generators
authors: Farshad Kiani, Hamed Tahanian
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: A rapid and efficient analytical approach is presented in this paper for the estimation of air-gap flux density and no-load induced voltage in large cylindrical-rotor synchronous generators. The approach begins by assuming a linear magnetic circuit and neglecting the effects of rotor and stator slotting. These factors are then progressively incorporated to improve model accuracy, including the influence of slotting and magnetic saturation on the air gap flux waveform. Unlike the Finite Element Method (FEM), the proposed analytical method offers significantly reduced computation time while maintaining acceptable accuracy. Comparative studies with FEM results show a low deviation in air gap flux density and RMS voltage estimation.

title: New Input-to-State Stability Results by Revisiting Vector Halanay's Inequality
authors: Frederic Mazenc, Michael Malisoff
abstract: We study vector versions of Halanay's inequality. We provide an input-to-state stability result. Even in the scalar case, we provide tighter bounds than those that were available in the literature. Our key new techniques involve finding componentwise positive vectors in the null spaces of suitable matrices. These matrices are expressible as weighted sums involving the coefficient matrices that occur in the vector versions of Halanay's inequality. We allow time-varying coefficients in our Halanay decay conditions. Other key tools to obtain our tighter bounds include novel applications of the Perron-Frobenius Theorem.

title: Self-Adapting Federated Continual Learning With Gradient Causal Constraint for Fault Propagation Path Identification
authors: Kai Zhong, Xiang Peng, Zhenhua Fan, Darong Huang, Shixiang Lu
abstract: In modern industrial scenarios, federated learning (FL) has been widely adopted due to its advantages in privacy preservation and distributed modeling. However, existing FL approaches rely on a single update paradigm, which significantly hampers communication efficiency. Moreover, the server preserves historical aggregation states to model causal propagation across training rounds, instead of performing memoryless per-round aggregation. For this end, we propose a self-adapting federated continual learning with gradient causal constraint (GCC-SFCL). First, the selection of synchronous or asynchronous communication across FL rounds is reformulated as continual learning task, where an experience accumulation mechanism records historical aggregation states to model long-term temporal dependencies among client updates. Based on the aggregated context, we further design a self-adapting scheduling strategy driven by multidimensional client state information, enabling the server to dynamically select optimal aggregation. Furthermore, a causality-steering gradient constraint is introduced to explicitly regulate gradient updates, improving the interpretability of the causal model and allowing the capture of fault propagation paths. Experimental results of a simulation case and the real-world case demonstrate that GCC-SFCL significantly improves not only communication efficiency but also the accuracy of fault propagation path identification.

title: A High-sensitive and Fast-response MXene/GO Pressure Sensor for Low Pressure Sensing and Dynamic Threshold Voltage Regulation
authors: Siwei Sun, Ye Zheng, Debo Wang
abstract: In order to achieve the higher sensitivity and faster response, a MXene/GO piezoresistive pressure sensor with bilayer PDMS microstructures is proposed, in which MXene (Ti<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>T<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>) and GO dispersion are mixed in a volume ratio of 1:4. The composite exhibits a synergistic effect, ensuring high conductivity and excellent mechanical strength. Moreover, the bilayer hemisphere microstructures effectively increase the contact area between pressure-sensitive layers and establish abundant conductive paths under pressure. The measurement results show that the sensor achieves extraordinarily high sensitivity (21.65 kPa<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>) within the low-pressure range (0~1.25 kPa), remarkably fast response time (4.9 ms) and recovery time (7.3 ms), low hysteresis (3.6%), and minimal repeatability error (3.9%). Furthermore, it is innovatively integrated into the voltage comparison circuit as an adjustable resistance. By switching three loading conditions, dynamic threshold voltage regulation is completed, resulting in the output signal demonstrating different duty cycles. Therefore, this work provides a novel sensing module for voltage comparison circuits, enabling immediate and easier-to-access threshold voltage regulation.

title: Spatio-Temporal Series Prediction Based on Multi-Objective Evolutionary Deep Convolutional Echo State Networks
authors: Meiling Xu, Lixin Tang, Lu Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Accurate prediction of spatio-temporal series remains a challenging task due to complex spatial dependencies, temporal dynamics, and factors such as data sparsity, nonlinearity, and high dimensionality. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel framework which integrates a spatio-temporal interpolated deep convolutional echo state network with an improved multi-objective evolutionary algorithm. The model integrates cubic spline interpolation to enhance temporal resolution, a spatial attention mechanism to capture inter-location correlations, and a convolution-based deep echo state network to efficiently model spatio-temporal patterns. A multi-objective evolutionary algorithm is then employed to jointly optimize model hyper-parameters with two objectives of maximizing prediction accuracy and minimizing model complexity. To further improve efficiency, a topological data analysis-based strategy is used to select a representative and diverse subset of candidate solutions, reducing computational cost without compromising optimization quality. Experiments on two real-world meteorological datasets demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves superior prediction accuracy across single and multiple horizons. Ablation study further confirms the importance of each architecture component and the effectiveness of the proposed optimization strategy. The results validate the potential of the proposed model as an efficient tool for real-world spatio-temporal forecasting tasks.

title: A Phaseless Source Reconstruction Method Based on Two-Stage Physics-Informed Neural Network
authors: Zhao Feng, Shijian Zheng, Xiaoting Yuan, Shaoqiu Xiao, Hao Chen, Yin Li
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this work, a phaseless equivalent source reconstruction method based on a two-stage physics-informed neural network (PINN) is proposed. Unlike conventional dipole-based equivalent source artificial neural networks that rely on Green's functions, the proposed approach is capable of recovering the phase information of the equivalent sources while simultaneously predicting both near-field magnitude and phase. By leveraging the physical mapping relationship between equivalent sources and near-field magnitudes, the proposed framework enables the joint reconstruction of the magnitude and phase of the equivalent sources. Moreover, through a phase-information constraint, it effectively alleviates the multi-solution problem inherent in phase retrieval. Compared with traditional iterative phase-retrieval algorithm, the proposed method exhibits superior performance in both magnitude prediction accuracy and phase recovery capability. Two numerical examples are provided to validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach. Simulation results show that for a 2×2 patch antenna array and a microstrip line on glass-base substrate, the method achieves magnitude prediction errors of 0.7% and 2%, respectively, while recovering relatively accurate phase distributions. These results indicate that the proposed method is well suited for near-field measurement, as well as antenna analysis and design applications.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: IEEE Computer Society: Unleash Your Potential
authors: N/A

title: Robust and Accurate LiDAR SLAM in Complex Environments via Intensity-Enhanced Features
authors: Yuqing Chen, Shuoshuo Huang, Yanyang Wang, Huosheng Hu
abstract: LiDAR-based Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) is a critical component in robotics and autonomous driving. Traditional SLAM systems relying solely on geometric features often struggle in environments with sparse or repetitive structures. To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel LiDAR SLAM framework that integrates intensity features with a dynamic weighting strategy to enhance both robustness and accuracy. Key contributions include an intensity-based curvature metric for feature extraction, an adaptive fusion scheme that combines geometric and intensity cues, and a Height-Intensity-Density Scan Context (HIDSC) descriptor for improved loop closure detection. Additionally, a factor graph-based optimization approach is employed to jointly optimize multimodal data. Experiments on the KITTI odometry benchmark, the TIERS dataset, and our custom campus dataset show that our method consistently achieves lower trajectory errors and more stable performance than representative baselines, including FAST-LIO2, LIO-SAM, Point-LIO, IGE-LIO, and Intensity-SLAM.

title: Smartphone Applications in Environmental, Biological, and Pharmaceutical Colorimetric Analysis: A Review
authors: Rimadani Pratiwi, Fadia Fatiha, Primadini Ayesha Nugraha, Ifa Latifah
abstract: Rapid technological advancements have brought about a revolution in various fields, including chemical analysis. The features of smartphones, including high-resolution cameras, reliable processing capacity, and robust connectivity, have repurposed them into an effective analytical platform. This development enables the utilization of smartphone components to perform colorimetric measurements on-site, in real-time, and at a low cost. Techniques such as Digital Image Colorimetry (DIC) leverage the smartphone camera to measure the color intensity of a chemical reaction and convert it into quantitative data utilizing the RGB (Red, Green, Blue) model, alongside other color model such as CMYK (Cyan, Magenta, Yellow, Black), and HSV (Hue, Saturation, Value). This review examines the progress and recent methodological developments in the field of smartphone-based colorimetric analysis, covering the environmental monitoring, biological samples, and pharmaceutical drugs. The study investigates the influence of smartphone camera specifications on the accuracy and sensitivity of the analytical results, while also discussing its inherent advantages and limitations.

title: Smart Predict and Optimize: A Tutorial on End-to-End Optimization Resource Allocation for IoT Networks
authors: Xiaoyu Song, Kunsheng Wang, Kwan-Wu Chin, Changlin Yang
abstract: The operators and nodes of Internet of things (IoT) networks often perform resource allocation, where they may solve a mathematical program to compute the optimal allocation of resources or decision(s), e.g., energy usage of devices, using sensed data as parameter values. Further, these parameter values may be stored/predicted using a neural network given one or more features, e.g., historical energy arrival rates at nodes. In this respect, to date, there are no IoT specific tutorials or works that focus on the smart predict and optimize (SPO) resource allocation framework. This paper thus fills this gap. Briefly, it shows the workflow of SPO, which involves using a machine learning model and a mathematical program to compute decisions. A key feature of SPO is that unlike prior approaches that only aim to predict the parameter values of a mathematical program, it maps the features of a system to decisions in an end-to-end manner. This paper also outlines various potential IoT applications that would benefit from SPO. It then presents a case study where SPO is used to decide task offloading from a device that has to contend with other users in a WiFi network. Lastly, it outlines some future research directions.

title: Secure performance of STAR-RIS aided networks with a full-duplex active eavesdropper
authors: Ping Yang, Liang Yang, Weichao Kuang, Shanjin Wang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: Unlike prior works assuming perfect attack detection, this paper investigates the physical layer security of a simultaneous transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS) aided network under a full-duplex active eavesdropper in the more realistic scenario where its jamming remains undetected by the legitimate transmitter. Assuming Nakagami-m fading channels, we first derive the probability density functions (PDFs) of the signal-to-interference ratios (SIRs) at both the legitimate user and the eavesdropper. Then, using the PDFs, we obtain novel closed-form expressions for key secrecy metrics, including the probability of nonzero secrecy capacity (PNSC), secrecy outage probability (SOP), and average secrecy capacity (ASC). Moreover, the asymptotic PNSC, SOP, and ASC at high signal-to noise ratio (SNR) are also derived. Based on those asymptotic expressions, we present compact expressions for several key performance indicators: 1) the secrecy diversity order and the secrecy array gain of the SOP, and 2) the high SNR slope and the high SNR power offset of the ASC. Results demonstrate that, compared to the passive eavesdropper scheme, the active jamming attack carried out by Eve will not cause any loss in secrecy diversity gain and high SNR slope, but only result in the loss of secrecy array gain and high SNR power offset. Finally, we verify our analytical results via simulations.

title: A Comprehensive Study on GDPR-Oriented Analysis of Privacy Policies: Taxonomy, Corpus and GDPR Concept Classifiers
authors: Peng Tang, Xin Li, Yuxin Chen, Weidong Qiu, Haochen Mei, Allison Holmes, Fenghua Li, Shujun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Machine learning (ML) based classifiers that take a privacy policy as the input and predict relevant concepts are useful in different applications such as (semi-)automated compliance analysis against requirements of a specific data protection law such as the EU GDPR. Although many researchers have studied ML-based privacy policy concept classifiers, we observed multiple research gaps, e.g., the lack of a more complete GDPR taxonomy and the less consideration of hierarchical information in privacy policies. To fill such research gaps, we produced a more complete GDPR-oriented privacy policy concept taxonomy, constructed the first privacy policy corpus with explicitly hierarchical information at three levels, and conducted the most comprehensive performance evaluation study of GDPR concept classifiers for privacy policies, cover many aspects that have not been studied systematically. Our work led to multiple findings and insights, including the usefulness of considering hierarchical contextual features and different hierarchical structures, the observation that a “one size fits all” approach may not work, the reduced performance of such classifiers on our newly constructed corpus especially after the first level, and the necessity to split the training and testing sets by documents.

title: Energy-Efficient Radioisotope Identification Using Spiking Neuromorphic Systems
authors: Felix Wang, Mark Plagge, Yang Ho, Michael C. Krygier, Craig M. Vineyard, Aaron J. Hill
abstract: This work presents the development and benchmarking of energy-efficient spiking Radioisotope Identification (RIID) algorithms on neuromorphic hardware, with applications to high-performance, ultra-low-power nuclear material detection. We develop a template-based matching algorithm based on sparse coding and the Locally Competitive Algorithm (LCA) that approximates state-of-the-art RIID methods such as those found in the widely known Gamma Detector Response and Analysis Software (GADRAS). We additionally evaluate simple machine learning (ML) models for RIID which are trained through the Python RadioIsotope IDentification (PyRIID) software. These algorithms are implemented as spiking neural networks (SNNs) and deployed on neuromorphic hardware platforms, including Intel Loihi 2, SpiNNaker2, and the BrainChip Akida accelerator. To quantify performance, we evaluate these algorithms against both synthetic and real-world datasets as well as compare against traditional RIID algorithms through GADRAS. The synthetic dataset was generated through PyRIID and consists of 25 isotopes across 124 shielding configurations, and the real-world dataset consists of roughly 200 test samples spanning 7 different isotopes of interest (<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">133</sup>Ba, <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">226</sup>Ra, <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">235</sup>U, <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">232</sup>Th, <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">238</sup>U, <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">239</sup>Pu, <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">131</sup>I). We also measure energy efficiency of these algorithms through power profiling on the neuromorphic hardware. Significantly, our neuromorphic implementations consumed less than 1 mJ per inference, representing dramatic orders of magnitude reduction in energy consumption compared to traditional ARM-based GADRAS deployments. Our results also demonstrated the robustness of our developed LCA-based approach compared to a multi-layer perceptron ML model, which excelled on synthetic data and known noise models but failed to generalize to challenging real-world data. In contrast to the GADRAS baseline performance (F1 score) of 0.623, the ML-based model only achieved 0.466, whereas the LCA-based implementation was more closely matched at 0.579. Together, these findings underscore the potential of spiking neuromorphic hardware as a transformative technology for advancing RIID implementations, offering robust, energy-efficient, and field-deployable solutions for real-world applications such as real-time nuclear material detection.

title: Low-Carbon Scheduling for Power-Hydrogen Integrated Energy System Using Large Language Model Enhanced Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Mingde Lin, Tao Wu, Kaigui Xie
abstract: Power-hydrogen integrated energy systems (PH-IESs) are emerging as a promising solution to achieve net-zero carbon emissions. However, the large-scale integration of uncertain renewable energy sources (RESs) and flexible demand-side resources (e.g., hydrogen electrolysis) pose great challenges in the real-time low-carbon scheduling of PH-IESs. This paper proposes a novel source-demand coordinated low-carbon scheduling framework for PH-IESs, empowered by deep reinforcement learning (DRL) and large language model (LLM) techniques. The proposed framework systematically integrates source-side generation with demand-side flexible hydrogen loads to improve carbon reduction and economic efficiency. An LLM-enhanced carbon flow pattern recognition and inference mechanism is developed to enable accurate predictions of real-time carbon intensity under complex, high-dimensional operating conditions. An LLM-enhanced DRL algorithm is then proposed to directly generate optimal scheduling strategies for the flexible demand-side resources, which can overcome computational bottlenecks in real-time decision-making. Numerical simulations on two PH-IESs are conducted to demonstrate the effectiveness of integrating DRL and LLM techniques for intelligent, efficient, and low-carbon operation of PH-IESs.

title: Information-Theoretic Bounds on Transmission Rates for Integrated Communication and Navigation Systems
authors: Jiyang Liu, Xiaomei Tang, Feixue Wang, Xin Chen, Wenbo Xu
abstract: The emergence of 6G space-air-ground integrated networks imposes stringent requirements on resource utilization and spectral efficiency, positioning integrated communication and navigation (ICAN) as a promising enabling technology. However, existing ICAN architectures lack a unified theoretical foundation. Communication is typically characterized by the Shannon capacity, whereas navigation is evaluated via the Cramér-Rao lower bound. As a result, the two functions are still treated independently, leading to suboptimal system design and resource allocation. To bridge this gap, this paper proposes a unified framework that quantifies positioning performance through a positioning information rate (PIR) derived from differential entropy and the Fisher information matrix. This formulation enables ICAN performance to be assessed in a common information-theoretic domain. Under this paradigm, we conduct a comparative study of representative ICAN schemes and develop a corresponding resource allocation optimization framework. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed PIR metric is consistent with conventional metrics while accurately capturing system performance under a unified one-dimensional metric, thereby facilitating direct and simultaneous optimization of communication and navigation functions without separate performance evaluations.

title: PNProRL: Self-Supervised Neural Relighting via Photometric Perception and Progressive Optimization_supp1-3677109.pdf
authors: You Yang
abstract: Portrait relighting shows great potential in photography, film, and AR by simulating diverse lighting effects. Existing state-of-the-art methods often rely on expensive paired OLAT or synthetic data, which limits scalability. Moreover, accurately modeling the interaction between physics-guided rendering, neural rendering, and real-world remains challenging. To address these issues, we propose a novel multi-stage self-supervised relighting framework. It progressively refines intrinsic scene properties via a simple-to-complex training strategy, removing the need for expensive paired data while adapting to various lighting conditions. One core design introduces a novel pre-training method approach using diverse shading-based masking for self-reconstruction, which improves the model's perception of complex lighting variations. Furthermore, we introduce two perceptual modules that leverage the linear superposition of light to narrow the gap between physics-guided and neural rendering, and better align relit results with real-world observations. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our unified framework achieves new state-of-the-art performance in portrait relighting, surpassing recent methods in photorealism, synthesis quality, and identity preservation. It provides a practical paradigm for high-fidelity relighting under diverse lighting.

title: Dust to Tower: Prior-Driven Coarse-to-Fine Photo-Realistic Scene Reconstruction from Sparse Uncalibrated Images
authors: Xudong Cai, Yongcai Wang, Zhaoxin Fan, Haoran Deng, Shuo Wang, Wanting Li, Deying Li, Lun Luo, Minhang Wang, Hongyuan Zhang, Xuelong Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Photo-realistic scene reconstruction from sparseview, uncalibrated images is highly required in practice. Although some successes have been made, existing methods are either Sparse-View but require accurate camera parameters (i.e., intrinsic and extrinsic), or SfM-free but need densely captured images. This paper proposes Dust to Tower (D2T), a novel coarse-to-fine framework to address the coupled difficulty. The key idea is to explicitly narrow down the solution space and then introduce reliable supervision at novel viewpoints without resorting to expensive diffusion-based view synthesis. To do this, we first introduce a Coarse Construction Module (CCM) which exploits a fastMulti-View Stereo model to initialize a 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) and recover initial camera poses. To refine the 3D model at novel viewpoints, we introduce Confidence- Aware Depth Alignment (CADA), which aligns a monocular inverse-depth prior to the reliable regions of the coarse depth using DUSt3R confidence, producing sharp and scale-consistent depth maps for accurate warping. We further propose Warped Image-Guided Inpainting (WIGI), which converts the accurate warped views into multi-view-consistent pseudo supervision via elaborate warping and inpainting process. Experiments on three benchmark datasets show that D2T achieves superior novel view synthesis quality and pose accuracy over ten representative baselines, while keeping high efficiency. Code will be publicly available.

title: Understanding the Effects of Projectors in Knowledge Distillation
authors: Yudong Chen, Sen Wang, Jiajun Liu, Xuwei Xu, Frank de Hoog, Brano Kusy, Zi Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Conventionally, during the knowledge distillation process (e.g., feature distillation), an additional projector is often required to perform feature transformation due to the dimension mismatch between the teacher and the student networks. Interestingly, we discovered that even if the student and the teacher have the same feature dimensions, adding a projector still helps to improve the distillation performance. In addition, projectors even improve logit distillation if we add them to the architecture too. Inspired by these surprising findings and the general lack of understanding of the projectors in the knowledge distillation process from existing literature, this paper investigates the implicit role that projectors play, but so far been overlooked. Our empirical study shows that the student with a projector (1) obtains a better trade-off between the training accuracy and the testing accuracy compared to the student without a projector when it has the same feature dimensions as the teacher, (2) better preserves its similarity to the teacher beyond shallow and numeric resemblance, from the view of Centered Kernel Alignment (CKA) [1], and (3) avoids being over-confident [2] as the teacher does at the testing phase. Motivated by the positive effects of projectors, we propose a projector ensemble-based feature distillation method to further improve distillation performance. Despite the simplicity of the proposed strategy, empirical results from the evaluation of classification tasks on benchmark datasets demonstrate the superior classification performance of our method on a broad range of teacher-student pairs and verify, from the aspects of CKA and model calibration that the student's features are of improved quality with the projector ensemble design. Code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/chenyd7/PEFD</uri>.

title: Global dependency modeling target identification method based on time-frequency feature constraints
authors: Xunqian Tong, Tongyu Nie, Jun Lin, Yuqi Wang, Kangcheng Bin, Xiaopu Zhang, Xintong Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: In complex interference environments, vibration sensing systems are crucial for monitoring micro-vibration anomalies, but in the case of noise interference and partial data loss, the target features will be overwhelmed, making it difficult for data-driven deep learning models to maintain stable recognition performance under multi-scene conditions. To solve this problem, we propose a time-frequency constrained global dependency (TFGD) model. The model consists of three parts. First, feature extraction is performed by convolutional neural network and long short-term memory module. Secondly, a Transformer coding module based on self-attention mechanism is introduced to establish global dependencies in feature sequences. Most importantly, to solve the key challenges of robust classification due to the lack of low-frequency information and sample class imbalance in noise and vibration data, the logarithmic power spectrum and phase spread spectrum are embedded as physical prior information loss functions. Finally, experimental evaluation of three different datasets, including noise interference scenarios and sample imbalances, showed that the recognition accuracy exceeded 95%, and the standard deviation of the accuracy was only 0.24%, confirming its effectiveness and application potential in complex environments.

title: Advanced OTP Based EV Charging and Maintenance Assistive System
authors: Baby Shalini V, Dhanush K, Leakhana R, Sukanya G, S Balaji
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The rapidly accelerating global transition towards electric vehicles highlights the critical necessity for cutting-edge charging innovations that tackle issues of accessibility and safety. This paper introduces an OTP-based charging system that can offer vehicle-to-vehicle wireless charging, coupled with GPS tracking and smart fault detection. The system proposed facilitates EV users in low battery emergencies to call for help through a secure website where OTP authentication confirms system integrity. After being authenticated, the system employs geo-location information to locate the user's vehicle and sends out mobile charging vehicles. Charging automatically initiates as the EV reaches the point in question, reducing reliance on static infrastructure. At the same time, the system uses voltage and current sensors to check charging behavior in real time. By processing current-voltage characteristics, the system can check for anomalies like overcurrent, undervoltage, and abnormal charging cycles and prevents unsafe charging practices. This method increases the convenience of the user, facilitates charging ease, and provides safety to batteries, ultimately leading to the mass appeal and reliability of EVs.

title: Preliminary results of a space-qualified polarization tracking system for QKD space systems using a genetic algorithm
authors: A. Jimenez-Girela, D. Merino-Perez, P. Garcia-Parejo, A. Crespo-Ramos, E. Gonzalez Del Campo, D. Rodríguez de Llera González, A. Álvarez-Herrero
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: We present a closed-loop polarization tracking system (PTS) designed to compensate polarization perturbations in real time for satellite-based QKD links. The system employs a liquid crystal polarization control device (PCD) and a genetic algorithm that optimizes the driving voltages to minimize the detected intensity, thereby restoring the input polarization state. Experimental validation under static and dynamic perturbations demonstrates the capability of the PTS to maintain high polarization extinction ratios and low polarization-induced QBER. These results confirm the feasibility of implementing closed-loop, real-time polarization correction in dynamic free-space quantum communication scenarios.

title: Research Based on Machine Learning Technology for Enhancing Consumer Behavior Prediction Models
authors: Jing Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This article systematically evaluate machine-learning models for consumer-behavior prediction across retail, e-commerce and subscription platforms. On 14 industry datasets (2.3 M sessions), CatBoost attains 0.934 precision, 0.935 recall and 0.985 ROC-AUC with 23% fewer features than XGBoost; both outperform SVM (0.886 recall) and RF (0.787 accuracy). A deep-learning session-level model reaches 0.952 F1 but requires <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6 \times$</tex> training time. Production A/B tests show 30% stock-out reduction, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 5}$</tex>% CTR uplift and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 8}$</tex>% churn decrease. Interpretable trees remain preferred for regulated domains, whereas gradient-boosting and neural nets scale best for high-dimensional multi-modal data.

title: Resource Optimization in Computer Networks using Differential Evolution Algorithm
authors: Sandeep Kumar Sunori, Chitresh Bhatia, Pradeep Juneja
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The intensive development of computer networks has made the necessity of effective and intelligent resource allocation systems extremely high. Conventional optimization techniques very often fail to deal with the nonlinear relation and multiobjective trade-offs between the network resources including bandwidth, CPU allocation, memory usage, power consumption, and buffer capacity. To solve these issues, this paper outlines a Differential Evolution (DE)-based resource optimization model to computer networks. The model designed uses MATLAB to allow the dynamic interaction of network parameters and the allocation to maximize throughput and minimize the operational cost. The algorithm will make repeated adjustments to a population of solutions by using mutation, crossover and selection to arrive at an optimal resource configuration. The analysis of the simulation reveals that this algorithm provides a balanced use of the resources, and an enhanced optimization efficiency. MATLAB has been used in the simulation, visualization and quantitative assessment of the algorithm performance.

title: A Comprehensive Analysis of Artificial Intelligence-Based Drug Side Effect Prediction Techniques
authors: Naga Padmaja Yenireddy, G Sri Lakshmi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Predicting drug side effects before they reach patients is one of the most important challenges in modern healthcare. Many recent studies have used different artificial intelligence methods to identify possible harmful reactions, but their performance varies widely because they rely on different types of data, learning techniques, and evaluation settings. This work presents a detailed review of these approaches to understand how well they perform, where they succeed, and where they fall short. The analysis covers models based on chemical structures, similarity networks, machine-learning classifiers, deep-learning architectures, and graph-based frameworks. The review shows that while these methods can detect common side effects with reasonable accuracy, they often struggle with rare or complex reactions due to limited data, narrow feature representation, and high computational demands. The study also highlights gaps in coverage across different disorder groups and points out the need for models that combine chemical, biological, and clinical information. This comprehensive assessment helps outline the strengths and weaknesses of current techniques and offers guidance for building more reliable and efficient drug side-effect prediction systems in the future.

title: Comparative Analysis of Bayesian Optimization and Neural Network-Based Adaptive PID Tuning for Complex Control Systems
authors: Carson Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Although Proportional Integral Derivative (PID) controllers are often seen as the gold standard for control engineering, providing robust and efficient control for many scenarios, they hinge on classical tuning processes such as the Ziegler-Nichols method and often struggle with nonlinear, time varying, and multi-loop environments. As a result, recent research has seen the rise of adaptive tuning techniques that result in enhanced performance and added situational robustness. This paper analyzes two such proposals: the first proposes a Bayesian Optimization (BO) based framework for automatic tuning in industrial processes with multiple inputs and multiple outputs and takes advantage of an iterative approach, while the second proposes a back propagation trained neural networks to tune a PID controller continuously in real time for motion control in intelligent vehicles. Both papers analyze the limits of using classical PID tuning methods, the performance metrics used within each respective experiment, experimental results achieved, as well as real world implications of the findings. While both proposals showed considerable improvements in their respective metrics in comparison to the controls tuned using classical methods, it is notable that they are each focused on different situations and thus have different strengths and weaknesses as a result. Ultimately, the study's comparison results in the suggestion for a hybrid of the two, yielding a combination of the advantages within each strategy.

title: Three Pooling Strategies for ViT → GPT-2 Image Captioning
authors: Qi Zhuo
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This paper compares CLS, mean, and attention pooling within a unified ViT-GPT-2 image captioning pipeline. A Vision Transformer (ViT) encoder produces patch embeddings; each pooling variant compresses them into a single conditioning vector mapped to GPT-2 through a linear layer. All other components, training hyperparameters, and decoding are held constant to isolate pooling effects. Experiments on a Flickr30k subset use AdamW, schedules up to 40 epochs, and greedy decoding. We report BLEU-1-4, ROUGE-L, METEOR, and CIDEr. Results show CLS converges fastest and performs best under short training (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 15$</tex> epochs), but overfits with longer schedules, decreasing test scores. Attention pooling improves and generalizes better, yielding the strongest higher-order BLEU and CIDEr. Mean pooling remains consistently weaker across metrics. Ablations confirm that gains are attributable to the pooling stage rather than decoder tuning. We conclude that attention pooling is preferable when robustness matters, whereas CLS is attractive for tight budgets; simple mean pooling is discouraged.

title: Research on Crop Pest Recognition Based on Improved YOLOv8
authors: Xiaofang Wu, JingChen Wu, ShiYu Zhang, YingLi Wen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of numerous types, complex information of crop pests and low efficiency of traditional manual monitoring methods, this paper proposes an improved crop pest recognition method based on YOLOv8. Three optimization strategies are adopted: modifying the input size, adding the SE (Squeeze-and-Excitation) attention mechanism to the detection head, and adding the ECA (Efficient Channel Attention) attention mechanism to the backbone network. A dataset containing 2,470 images of ten common crop pests (including mole cricket, sugar beet weevil, fall armyworm) is constructed for model training and validation. Experimental results show that the improved model achieves significant performance improvement: the precision of the original YOLOv8 model is 80%, while the precisions of the models optimized by the three strategies reach $82 \%, 92 \%$ and 83.5% respectively. The model integrated with the SE attention mechanism exhibits the best performance, with fast inference speed and strong robustness, providing an effective technical solution for rapid and accurate identification of crop pests.

title: Design and Implementation of a Wav2Lip-based Digital Human Framework for Intelligent Power Customer Service
authors: Qiao Xueming, Qiao Linfei, Du Nianjiang, Gong Baoning, Xiao Linwei, Yu Anqi, Han Dong
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In the customer service domain of the power industry, we propose an intelligent digital human framework developed on an optimized Wav2Lip model. The framework integrates multimodal interaction, speech-driven lip synchronization, semantic understanding, and dynamic decision-making technologies to transform traditional customer service processes. By leveraging these capabilities, the digital human not only achieves accurate user intent recognition but also exhibits natural and human-like interaction behaviors, thereby improving user experience and engagement. Experimental evaluations on multiple key indicators demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed system. Results show that the framework achieves high speech recognition accuracy, fast response times, and timely feedback generation, highlighting its potential for practical deployment in intelligent power customer service scenarios.

title: An AI-Driven Fusion Approach for Multilingual Retrieval via Weighted Greedy Subset Selection
authors: Rui Wang, Bin Zeng, Jian Kang, Hui Shu Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: With the rapid expansion of multilingual digital resources, multilingual information retrieval (MLIR) has gained significant importance in information science. While fusing results from multiple retrieval systems can enhance MLIR performance, integrating too many systems leads to computational inefficiency and information redundancy, challenging the balance between effectiveness and cost. To address this, we propose an AI-driven Multilingual Retrieval Fusion (AI-MLRF) method, which incorporates advanced natural language processing techniques and a weighted greedy subset selection strategy to optimize the fusion process. AI-MLRF consists of four core modules: semantic performance evaluation using cross-lingual pre-trained models, diversity measurement based on contrastive learning, weighted greedy subset selection, and attention-based result fusion. Experimental evaluations on benchmark datasets demonstrate that AI-MLRF outperforms state-of-the-art fusion methods, showing substantial improvements in retrieval accuracy and adaptability to multilingual scenarios. The method provides a transparent and efficient solution for multilingual retrieval result fusion, with potential applications in cross-border collaboration and academic research.

title: Predictive Modeling Framework for Early Identification of Hypothyroidism among Women
authors: Akshita Sharma, Vijeta Sharma, Rahul Sachdeva, Deeptanshu Jha
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: The rising cases of Hypothyroidism, especially among Women, have generated an urgent need for the effective detection of hypothyroidism using various personal and clinical thyroid data of patients. Hypothyroidism is a common disorder directly linked to the functioning of the thyroid gland, which is necessary for production of hormones that control the metabolism of body, its growth, and overall development. It is often under-diagnosed due to its non-specific symptoms. Early and accurate detection is essential to prevent long-term complications, yet conventional diagnostic practices face limitations in efficiency and scalability. The present paper compares the effectiveness of several classification approaches using clinical thyroid function data in order to assess supervised machine learning (ML) models specifically tailored for the detection and of hypothyroidism in women. The results show that Random Forest outperforms the other approaches achieving an accuracy of 96.8%. The proposed model may be used by medical practitioners to enhance the decision making and for early medication of patients so as to avoid related complications of hypothyroidism among women.

title: Real Time IoT and Machine Learning Based Monitoring System for Bedridden Patients
authors: Sarika J, Sanjana N, Rakshitha S, Praveen Kumar KC
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Monitoring bedridden patients during their recovery often requires constant attention to notice any significant changes in their health and provide the necessary action on time. Traditional monitoring relies almost entirely on manual observation and any deficiencies in staff could delay necessary actions and reduce their effectiveness. This paper describes the development of a Real-Time Continuous Health Monitoring System for bedridden patients utilizing IoT and Machine Learning. The monitoring system is designed with multiple biomedical sensors, including heart rate, temperature, oxygen saturation and motion sensors, interfaced with an IoT driven microcontroller allowing real-time collection and transmission of data to a cloud platform. Then, the collected data is analyzed using machine learning algorithms to detect anomalies and predict risk for health deterioration. In the event of an abnormal finding, alerts are automatically forwarded to the clinicians or family members of the patient mobile applications. This intelligent system will eliminate the possibility of human error and allow for 24/7 monitoring of bedridden patients which will allow for early detection of health deterioration and shortening of response times.

title: Asymmetric Protection-Based S-SFC Deployment for LLM-Inference-Enabled Networks
authors: Hengyu Jin, Danyang Zheng, Huanlai Xing, Xiaojun Cao
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In emerging LLM-driven edge computing scenarios (e.g., edge robotics), service function chains (SFCs) must be deployed with both security and reliability guarantees. To enhance security, security-aware service functions (S-SFs) are employed, forming a security-aware SFC (S-SFC). To ensure reliability, service providers typically use dedicated backup SFCs to protect the primary one. However, no existing works jointly consider SFC security and reliability in the deployment process. To address this gap, we propose the concept of asymmetric protection for S-SFCs, allowing the primary and backup chains to differ in terms of order, version, and SF types to reduce resource overhead. We formally define the problem of Securityaware Service Function Chaining, Embedding, and Protecting (SSFCEP) and prove its NP-hardness. To efficiently solve S-SFCEP, we propose the Near-Source SFC Deployment (NS-SD) algorithm. Extensive simulations demonstrate that NS-SD reduces resource cost by up to 56% (1.8× savings) compared to state-of-the-art benchmarks, while achieving faster runtime.

title: Optimal Design for Enhanced Torque and Efficiency of a Dual-Stator Dual-Rotor Axial-Flux Switching PM Motor for Lightweight EVs
authors: Mohammad Farahzadi, Mohammadreza Naeimi, Amir Ebrahimi Shohani, Fariba Farrokh, Mohammad Sedigh Toulabi, Karim Abbaszadeh
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This paper presents the design and optimization of a dual-stator, dual-rotor axial-flux switching permanent magnet (AFSPM) motor aimed at enhancing torque and efficiency for lightweight electric vehicle (EV) applications. A Taguchi-based optimization strategy is employed to systematically improve torque characteristics and overall performance. The proposed AFSPM motor features dual stators with 12 poles on each side and an internal 10-pole dual-rotor configuration. Phase-group concentrated windings are adopted to enhance fault-tolerant capability. The back electromotive force (EMF), cogging torque, average torque, torque ripple, and losses are analyzed using a three-dimensional finite element method (3D-FEM) to evaluate the AFSPM motor’s electromagnetic performance. A comparative study with existing AFSPM motor designs in the literature is conducted to validate the proposed structure’s superior torque density and efficiency. The optimized model demonstrates significant reductions in cogging torque and torque ripple, confirming that the proposed AFSPM motor is a promising candidate for efficient lightweight EV propulsion systems.

title: YOLOv11n-RDF: A Detail-Enhanced YOLOv11n with Low-Level Feature Fusion for Damper Detection
authors: Weiwei Zou, Can Chen
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: To address the challenge of detecting vibration dampers on transmission lines during UAV inspections, where complex backgrounds and their small size impede performance, this paper proposes an improved detection model, YOLOv11nRDF. The model introduces a Receptive Field-based Coordinated Attention (RFCA) mechanism to suppress background noise. It also integrates a new P2 detection head with a Detail Guided Block (DGB) and a Feature Refine Module (FRM) to significantly enhance the capture of high-resolution details and the lossless fusion of multi-scale features, thereby effectively boosting perception accuracy for these dampers. To validate our model, we first constructed a dataset of transmission line dampers covering diverse and complex real-world scenarios. Experimental results show that YOLOv11n-RDF outperforms existing mainstream models, achieving a remarkable 8.6 percentage point increase on the mAP@.5:.95 metric, which measures precise localization. The model strikes an excellent balance between accuracy and efficiency, offering a robust technical solution for achieving highly reliable automated monitoring of damper conditions.

title: STM32-Based Smart Humidifier with Enhanced PID Algorithm
authors: Shimin Zhang, Xueting Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This study proposes an enhanced incremental PID algorithm for smart humidity control, employing a third-order recursive parameter self-tuning framework with sliding time window strategy. It dynamically adjusts PID coefficients, reducing steady-state error from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 1 0 \% \text{RH}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 2. 6 \% \text{RH}$</tex> (74%) improvement) at 50% RH target. Experimental results show 4.3% overshoot (vs 23.7%) and 84s settling time, maintaining <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 3.5 \% \text{RH}$</tex> accuracy under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 10 \% \text{RH}$</tex> disturbances. A cloud-edge architecture enables 280 ms response via JavaScriptencapsulated mobile apps, forming closed-loop control. Statistical analysis confirms significant superiority over commercial products (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p}<0.01$</tex>) in precision and dynamics, offering a scalable solution for cost-effective smart environment devices through algorithm optimization and lightweight communication protocols.

title: A New Energy Benchmark Electricity Price Model Based on System Cost Optimization
authors: Xudong Jia, Xiaodong Chen, Xuyao Xiao, Zhe Xu, Jiang Wang, Zhaoyuan Wu, Ming Zhou
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: In response to the demand for market-oriented pricing reform of new energy electricity, this paper proposes a benchmark electricity price model based on system cost optimization. The model quantifies the overall system cost savings resulting from the integration of new energy sources—including thermal power generation costs, environmental costs, and energy storage costs—to establish a dynamic pricing mechanism. Specifically, the benchmark price is determined by the reduction in system cost per unit of newly installed capacity. Using a provincial power grid as a case study, empirical results show that the benchmark price for wind power is 0.308 ¥/kWh, higher than that of solar PV, which stands at 0.21 ¥/kWh. The primary reason for this difference is that wind power can replace high-cost power generation units and alleviate supply shortages during critical periods, whereas solar output coincides with midday peak load, limiting its marginal substitution effect.

title: Research on the Optimization of Engineering Task Based on the Assignment Model
authors: Bo Zhong, Yingchang Duan, Yuxin Bai, Weikang Peng
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: In response to the practical needs of multi-equipment and multi-personnel collaboration for multi-task stages in engineering operations, this study establishes an assignment optimization design model targeting task completion efficiency. The model comprehensively considers factors such as multi-skilled personnel, multi-functional equipment, and different rest periods of human and equipment, provides corresponding constraint conditions. Given the characteristics of large feasible-solution space and long computation time for this optimization problem, the Virtual Equipment Method is adopted to decompose multi-functional equipment into combinations of single-functional equipment. Additionally, a rapid model-solving approach based on task cycles is designed. The results of the case analysis demonstrate that this assignment model can optimize and achieve the best personnel and equipment combinations in multi-stage tasks. Furthermore, by decomposing multi-functional equipment and incorporating task cycles, the feasible solution space can be significantly reduced, thus greatly improves computational efficiency.

title: Erasure Code Enhanced UAV Swarm Mission Planning for Critical Data Collection
authors: Haitao Wang, Zhuyu Liu, Zheng Dong, Zhi Liu, Yong Zhou, Jinhua Li
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: We consider a critical data collection mission undertaken by an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) swarm in a complex environment where each UAV is subject to the risk of being randomly damaged. Generally, to enhance data collection efficiency, each UAV is responsible for collecting data in a specific area with minimum or even no overlap and then carrying each copy back to the data center. Once a UAV is destroyed, the information collected by this particular UAV cannot be recovered by the data center. To deal with this problem, we employ erasure codes to combat random UAV loss. More specifically, the original information such as the remote sensing data for a certain area is encoded through the utilization of erasure codes, and then the UAV swarm collects data in accordance with the encoded data to achieve fault tolerance. We propose three distinct encoding schemes corresponding to the three scenarios of fault tolerance of 1, fault tolerance of 2, and fault tolerance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 3$</tex>, and design an optimized erasure code in the case of fault tolerance of 1, which significantly reduces the data collection duration. Simulation results demonstrate that with the aid of erasure codes, the probability of completing the data collection task has been substantially enhanced. Under the same fault-tolerance requirements, the number of UAVs required by the proposed method is much smaller than that of the backup-based method.

title: IRS Deployment Under Practical Channel Model: Joint Position and Rotation Optimization
authors: De Xu, Tao Wang, Nuocheng Yang, Xinxin He, Changchuan Yin, Changsheng You, Haonan Tong
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In this paper, we study the deployment strategy of intelligent reflecting surfaces (IRS) for coverage extension, where the IRS is used to establish a cascaded link between the access point and users. Existing works have mostly adopted simplified channel models that consider only propagation path loss, overlooking critical IRS reception and reflection factors. To address this issue, we propose a practical channel model where the reception and reflection factors are explicitly derived based on electromagnetic field theory. Based on this channel model, we formulate an average achievable rate maximization problem over multiple users by jointly optimizing the position and rotation of the IRS. Due to the high-dimensional and coupled variable space of the formulated problem, we adopt a particle swarm optimization (PSO)-based algorithm, which efficiently obtains a near-optimal solution with significantly reduced computational complexity compared to exhaustive search. Numerical results demonstrate that our proposed scheme improves the achievable rate by at least 34.2% compared to conventional deployment strategies.

title: A FedAvg-Based Federated Learning Framework for Brain Tumor Recognition Using Multi-Modal MRI Data
authors: Jixuan Le
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Brain tumor poses a severe global healthcare challenge, with high mortality and low cure rates primarily attributed to diagnostic delays and high misdiagnosis rates in Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)-based detection. While artificial intelligence (AI) techniques—such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), transformer models, and hybrid architectures like VSMU-Net—have achieved promising results (e.g., Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.98 in 3D tumor segmentation), their real-world clinical application is hindered by “data silos” caused by privacy regulations (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR) and uneven data quality. Federated Learning (FL) emerges as a potential solution, but existing FL studies in brain tumor recognition either use small-scale simulated data or ignore the 3D structural characteristics of MRI sequences. To address this gap, this study proposes a FedAvg-based FL framework for brain tumor recognition using multi-modal MRI data. The framework enables collaborative training across 5 simulated medical centers without sharing raw data, adopting a CNN model optimized for medical image features. Experimental results show the global model converges steadily over 10 communication rounds, achieving 89.7% accuracy and 0.2536 loss on the test set, with 90.2% tumor-specific accuracy and 89.1% normal-specific accuracy. This work validates FL's feasibility in balancing privacy preservation and detection performance, providing a practical framework for collaborative medical AI research.

title: An Intelligent Personalized Learning Recommendation Algorithm Based on Deep Learning for Student Learning Behavior
authors: Jiahui Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: To address the challenges of traditional recommendation algorithms in educational settings, which often underutilize multi-source data and struggle to capture dynamic demand, this study proposes a personalized learning recommendation algorithm based on multimodal attention fusion and LSTM. First, a multi-source data input system is constructed, preprocessed with anomaly cleaning, feature normalization, and temporal reconstruction. A CNN is then used to extract multimodal features of learning resources. An LSTM is used to model students' temporal learning behaviors, and a dynamic attention mechanism is designed for adaptive feature fusion. Finally, a two-layer fully connected network is used to predict student resource preferences. Experiments were conducted on a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5, 0 0 0}$</tex> junior high school students and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> resources from a domestic K12 platform (January to June 2024). The training/validation/test set was split in a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7: 2: 1$</tex> ratio, and the algorithm was compared with six other algorithms, including traditional CF and a single LSTM. Results show that the proposed algorithm achieves Precision@5 of 0.82 and Recall@15 of 0.86, representing improvements of 13.9 % and 13.2 % respectively compared to the NCF. The MAE and RMSE are 0.085 and 0.108, respectively, reducing them by 24.1 % and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 5. 5 \%}$</tex> compared to a single LSTM algorithm. Training time is 8.5 hours, and the recommendation response time is 0.3 seconds, achieving a balance between accuracy and efficiency, meeting the needs of personalized recommendations in online education.

title: Construction of Japanese Text Mining Model Using Natural Language Processing Technology
authors: Xiaolu Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of complex grammar and semantic understanding of Japanese text, this paper proposes a Japanese text mining algorithm that integrates multimodal information with a dual attention mechanism and constructs a corresponding model. The algorithm captures the semantic association of Japanese sentences through the intra-text attention mechanism, realizes the interactive fusion of multimodal information such as text and image through the inter-modal attention mechanism, and optimizes information fusion with an adaptive weighting strategy. The experiment uses Japanese news, novels, academic papers and other types of data sets, and compares them with Text CNN, LSTM and other models with text classification and sentiment analysis as tasks. The results show that in the text classification task, the accuracy of the model in this paper is 92.3 % in the news corpus, which is 11.2 % higher than Text CNN and 8.7% higher than LSTM; the accuracy on the novel data set is 89.7 %, which is 6.8 % higher than the average of the comparison model. In the sentiment analysis task, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{F 1}$</tex> value of the model in the news corpus reaches 89.6 %, which is 7.5 % higher than the average of the comparison model. Ablation experiments show that removing the intra-text, inter-modal attention mechanism and fusion strategy will reduce the model performance by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.2 \%, 4.8 \%$</tex> and 3.3 % respectively, verifying the effectiveness of the algorithm and proving that the multimodal and dual attention mechanisms significantly improve the model's ability to process Japanese text.

title: MCP-YOLO: Lightweight Detection Method for Pointer Instruments
authors: Feiqing Zhang, Longqing Fang, Zhenyu Yin, Fulong Xu, Weibin Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In order to solve the deployment difficulties caused by the high computational complexity and large number of parameters of traditional models in the detection task of pointer instruments, a lightweight improvement scheme MCP-YOLO based on the YOLOv8s network is proposed. First, the lightweight backbone MobileNetV4 is used to reduce the number of parameters of the model. Secondly, a Lightweight Token Mixer(LTM) is designed. By integrating the new interaction mode of spatial and channel attention mechanisms, complex operations such as matrix multiplication and Softmax in traditional methods are avoided. Finally, a pointer instrument special dataset DTS1500 containing 1500 samples is constructed. Experimental data show that compared with the baseline model YOLOv8s, the GFLOPs and Parameters of the improved model on the validation set are reduced by 31.0% and 10.0% respectively, and the mAP50: 95 is improved by 1.3 percentage points. This lightweight design significantly improves the applicability of the model on mobile terminals and edge computing devices, and can effectively meet the engineering needs of real-time detection of pointer instruments in industrial sites.

title: A Comparative Study of Stacked Ensemble and SVM Models in Breast Cancer Classification
authors: S. Nithya Selvakumari
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The research evaluates Support Vector Machine (SVM) alongside a Stacked Ensemble Model for their diagnostic use in breast cancer through direct comparison. The two models learned from classification data consisting of malignant and benign tumor patterns to determine their performance capabilities. An SVM model achieved successful detection of malignant cases with 90.69 % accuracy alongside precision at 1.00 and recall of 78.95 % combined with an F1-score value of 88.23 %. Layered / stacking an ensemble learning algorithm that uses meta-model to stack or layer-wise stack many classification, or regression models together. The level- 0 predictions given in base models are fed into a more comprehensive model (level-1 or meta-model) which produces the end prediction produced the best results which delivered 94.18 % accuracy along with 0.96 precision and 91.67 % recall and 93.75% F1-score. The experimental findings from these results demonstrate ensemble learning delivers improved performance through stable operation while using generalized algorithms than independent SVM classification models. These acquired data points validate that ensemble methods enhance diagnostic support systems for breast cancer detection through optimal optimization features. The enhanced performance of the stacked ensemble model directly supports global efforts to combat life-threatening diseases by providing reliable, technology-driven healthcare solutions.

title: Research on Contour Error Prediction of Five-Axis CNC Machine Tools Based on CLA-Net
authors: Xiyang Zhang, Juntao Li, Yi Hu, Yanqing Zhao, Di Wu, Zeng Tao
