publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Contour error, primarily induced by the coupling of multi-axis dynamics and servo lag, is a critical factor limiting the machining accuracy of computer numerical control (CNC) machine tools during complex surface machining. Traditional theoretical modeling methods based on dynamics and parameter identification are difficult to cope with complex nonlinear errors. To address the challenges, this study proposes a data-driven deep learning method to achieve high-precision prediction of contour errors. In this paper, the VMC2100B vertical machining center is used as the experimental platform, and the high-speed dynamic coordinate data acquisition research is carried out with the help of dynamic coordinate acquisition technology, which provides strong data support for the training of deep learning model. A hybrid deep learning prediction model named CLA-Net (CNN-LSTMAttention Network) was constructed: 1D Convolutional Neural Networks (1D-CNN) were employed to extract local spatial features, Long Short-Term Memory networks (LSTM) were used to learn long-term temporal dependencies, and an Attention was introduced to dynamically focus on critical historical information. The experimental results show that the proposed CLA-Net model can control the mean square error (MSE) at about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 ~ \mu m}$</tex>, which verifies the effectiveness and superiority of the model in the prediction task. This study provides a new solution for the contour error prediction task of five-axis machine tools.

title: AI Powered Network Intrusion Detection System Using Machine Learning
authors: M Prasanna Venkatesh, Nithish Kumar V, S Saraswathi, K Abinaya
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This study presents a machine-learning-based Network Intrusion Detection System (NIDS) designed to overcome the limitations of traditional signature-based IDS, such as low accuracy and poor real-time performance. The proposed system evaluates multiple classification algorithms, including Random Forest, AdaBoost, XGBoost, and Logistic Regression, for identifying abnormal network traffic patterns. Real-time testing shows an accuracy of 0.92 with a false alarm rate of 0.05 between 08:00-09:00, and an accuracy of 0.90 with a false alarm rate of 0.06 between 09:00-10:00. The average detection latency remained below 55 ms across all intervals. Keywords- network intrusion detection, machine learning, real-time monitoring, anomaly detection.

title: Message from General Chair
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)

title: Inter-Turn Short Circuit Fault Diagnosis in PMSM Using a Temporal Convolutional Network with Attention Mechanism and Parametric ReLU
authors: Na Wu, Yixiao Zhang, Yao Meng, Xingzhen Bai
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: Permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) are key components in modern industrial systems, and their operational reliability is critical to overall system stability— particularly in demanding applications such as mining roadheaders. Among early-stage electrical failures, inter-turn short circuit (ITSC) faults are common and can lead to significant motor degradation or catastrophic failures if not detected promptly. To address the challenges of detecting weak fault signatures under harsh conditions and varying fault parameters, this paper designs a fault diagnosis method based on a temporal convolutional network with attention mechanism and parametric ReLU (TCN-AP). A PMSM fault simulation model is developed to emulate various fault scenarios and generate corresponding training data. The TCN-AP model is then used to identify the fault phase and shorted turn ratio. Simulation results demonstrate that, under varying fault resistance conditions, the designed TCN-AP method achieves a diagnostic accuracy of 97.41% and enables more efficient early fault detection compared with other deep learning methods.

title: Application of dual-objective optimization algorithm in logistics route planning
authors: Fangziqiang Zhang, Tao Jiang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: This paper analyzes the core conflict between delivery timeliness and operating costs in logistics route planning. Drawing on the principles of constrained dual-objective optimization models, this paper constructs and implements a dual-objective optimization model for minimizing delivery time and cost, using multi-delivery point routing within an urban area as a scenario. The model is then solved in Python. It uses itertools to enumerate routes, calculate objective function values, and verify constraints. This model quantifies the trade-off between time and cost, providing flexible and actionable decision support for logistics scheduling. It is scalable to accommodate practical operational requirements such as multiple delivery points and dynamic constraints.

title: Calibration and uncertainty analysis of mode conversion loss parameters of balanced twisted pair wire
authors: Lei Yu, Yunling Wang, Yibin Wan
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: Balanced twisted-pair transmission lines stand as critical media for data communication, finding widespread application in high-speed data transmission scenarios such as buildings, data centers, and factories. They enable the transmission of voice, data, images, and other forms of information to target nodes within a network, demonstrating exceptional anti-interference performance. With the rapid progress of information technology, high-speed data transmission has set more rigorous requirements for frequency bands and anti-interference capabilities. In noisy environments, the common-mode and differential-mode signals of balanced twisted-pair transmission lines are susceptible to interference from external sources such as industrial noise, resulting in imbalanced Ethernet transmission signals, which may give rise to potential bit errors and data retransmissions. This paper elaborates on the calibration method for the Equal Level Transverse Conversion Transfer Loss parameter, conducts a comparative analysis of measurement results obtained from network analyzers and cable analyzer, and assesses the uncertainty of these measurements, thereby providing technical support for relevant metrological technology research.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)

title: Learning Based Online Tracking Algorithms for Marine Litter in Multibeam Water Column Images
authors: Pedro Alves Guedes, Hugo Miguel Silva, Sen Wang
abstract: Marine litter is a growing environmental threat, with severe ecological and socio-economic impacts. Most monitoring strategies rely on optical sensors to detect surface pollution, however these approaches fail to capture submerged plastics dispersed throughout the water column. Multibeam acoustic imaging offers a complementary solution, but the scarcity of annotated sonar datasets and the high noise levels of acoustic imagery make automated detection and tracking particularly challenging. This study presents a comparative evaluation of deep learning based multi-object tracking (MOT) algorithms applied to water column acoustic data. Pre-trained YOLOv8 detectors were integrated with tracking-by-detection frameworks including BoT-SORT, OC-SORT, ByteTrack, and DeepOC-SORT. Performance was assessed across acoustic frequencies and preprocessing strategies using standard MOT metrics. Results show that adaptive Gaussian thresholding and opening morphology improved robustness at lower frequencies (950 kHz and 1200 kHz), while unprocessed inputs proved more resilient to severe clutter at 1400 kHz. BoostTrack and ByteTrack achieved the most consistent tracking, effectively managing intermittent detections to maximise MOTA and IDF1. In contrast, OC-SORT underperformed, struggling with fragmented sonar trajectories. Furthermore, while efficient Nano models dominated at lower frequencies, Medium models were required under higher noise. These findings demonstrate the feasibility of applying MOT methods to sonar-based litter monitoring. Future work will explore unsupervised learning approaches to leverage intrinsic sonar data structure, reduce annotation needs, and enable scalable marine litter tracking.

title: A Tri-Band Frequency Selective Surface based on Multilayer Concentric Slot Geometries
authors: Yael Jasso-Martinez, Claudia E. Perez-Martinez, Rosalba Martinez-Lopez, Jose I. Martinez-Lopez, Lourdes Martinez-Lopez
abstract: This paper presents a frequency selective surface (FSS) composed of three cascaded layers, each consisting of unit cells with distinct concentric slot geometries. The concentric geometries corresponding of a square, an octagon, and a circular ring are designed to generate three passbands at the -3 dB level, centered at 6.10 GHz, 8.88 GHz, and 11.8 GHz, with corresponding fractional bandwidths of 29.3%, 8.11%, and 11.9%. This makes the structure suitable for applications in the C and X frequency bands. The proposed configuration exploits the progressive reduction of perimeter among the square, octagon, and circular geometries to achieve multiple resonances within a compact concentric structure, while the progressive separation between the resonant paths helps to reduce mutual coupling and improve frequency selectivity. The design accounts for the mutual coupling introduced by the close spacing between the concentric geometries. An equivalent circuit model is developed to describe the behavior of the FSS, comprising three cascaded sections connected through transmission lines. Each section contains three nested RLC resonant circuits with mutual inductive coupling, representing the interaction and combined response of the three geometries. To validate the simulated results, a prototype was fabricated and tested. Each layer contains 121 unit cells arranged in an 11 × 11 array. The experimental results show good agreement with both the full-wave simulations and the equivalent circuit model. The FSS demonstrates good angular stability for oblique incidence, maintaining good performance for elevation angles up to 65°.

title: IEEE Electron Device Letters Publication Information
authors: N/A

title: Industrial Processes Fault Diagnosis Method Based on Expert System-Guided Neural Network Decision-Space Sparsification
authors: Min Yin, Youqing Wang, Wei Yu, David Wilson, Xin Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: Industrial fault diagnosis (FD) often faces challenges due to the scarcity of labeled data and the inability of rule-based systems to handle high-dimensional nonlinearities. This study proposes expert system-assisted neural networks (ES-Nets), a novel hybrid framework featuring ES-guided decision-space sparsification to bridge symbolic reasoning with neural networks (NNs). Unlike traditional data-driven models, this knowledge-preconditioned architecture embeds domain-specific logic into a comprehensive knowledge base before training. Specifically, the optimization process constrains the gradient descent trajectory to a knowledge-consistent subspace, effectively regularizing the parameter updates based on symbolic expert logic rather than purely on statistical gradients. Advanced embedding techniques pre-configure the model, enabling early operational performance and significantly reducing training requirements. Validation on the Tennessee Eastman (TE) process and a real-world petrochemical plant case demonstrates the framework’s superiority in accuracy and operational efficiency. This study provides an efficient and interpretable solution, facilitating effective human–machine collaboration in complex industrial environments.

title: Commutation Failure Prediction for LCC-HVDC Based on CNN-LSTM-Attention Neural Network
authors: Jian Liu, Liyu Meng, Sheng Lin, Lei Liu, Jingbo Zhao, Zhengyou He
abstract: Commutation failure (CF) can cause DC current surges, impaired system transmission power and even leading to HVDC blocking. The prediction of CF combined with early control triggering is beneficial for avoiding CFs. However, current analyses of CF identification mainly focus on real-time detection or prediction based on limited factors, which are insufficient for reliable CF prediction. Therefore, a CF prediction method for the line-commutated converter high-voltage direct current (LCC HVDC) system based on CNN-LSTM-Attention is proposed. Firstly, key indicators that represent the commutation state are selected by analyzing the factors that influencing CF. Secondly, a CF prediction model is constructed based on CNN-LSTM Attention. Additionally, the input data sampling principle is clarified and the commutation state discrimination component based on DC current variation is built for the model training. On this basis, a method for predicting CF is proposed. Finally, the accuracy and efficiency of the proposed method is validated based on the CIGRE LCC-HVDC Benchmark system and the Bin-Jin HVDC system in China.

title: Research on Elastic Protection Methods of Generalized End Nodes in Information Networks
authors: Yunjie Gu, Jing Wang, Shijie Wang, Yanbin Li, Jianxing Chen, Huaidong Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: The trend of modernization of information networks is in full swing, however the continuous enrichment of network extensions has also introduced new security risks. The current information network security protection methods have problems such as limited applicability, poor scalability, and high resource consumption, making it difficult to match the current scale of information development and construction. In response to the appeal issue, this article conducts research on elastic protection methods applicable to information networks. Adhering to the concept of “elastic security protection” of “adaptability to defense scenarios”, “scalability of defense capabilities”, and “efficiency of defense methods”, we have established a basic framework of protection technology that can cover the generalized end nodes of information networks. We propose a security protection elastic adaptation method for complex battlefield environments, integrating protection functions and means based on access control and extended by monitoring, warning, risk assessment, etc. Research has shown that the elastic protection method designed in this article has higher attack blocking rate and lower false alarm rate. Filling the blind spots of information network security protection, and endowing defense with elastic expansion capabilities.

title: Intelligent Traffic Accident Detection System using Deep Learning Fusion Techniques
authors: Ajay Kumar, Sanjay Roka, Aditya Joshi, Manoj Diwakar, Neeraj Kumar Pandey, Prabhishek Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Video surveillance traffic incident detection is an important part of Intelligent Transportation Systems, as it allows for the quick response to emergency incidents and enhances the safety of road users. The use of fixed cameras to monitor traffic creates several problems for traffic incident detection, including vehicle occlusions; changes in lighting, the complexity of how vehicles interact with each other, and the need to represent both sudden collisions and gradual change in traffic flow over time. While deep learning techniques have shown success in this area, most methods have focused on short-term event modelling and do not have the capacity to capture long-term event dependencies seen in real-world roadways. To address these challenges, an intelligent traffic incident detection framework using a deep fusion architecture is introduced. The spatial feature extraction is performed at the frame level using a ResNet50 backbone. Temporal dependencies on successive frames are captured by using transformer-based encoder. Sequential information of motion over time is refined using a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network; thus, providing a temporal-coherence component of sequencing. The intelligent traffic accident detection framework was tested against the Traffic Accident Detection Dataset, built from highway surveillance camera video footage. The evaluation of the framework produced a classification accuracy of 97.93% (with equal precision and recall for accident and normal traffic classes) in realistic surveillance scenarios.

title: Internet of Things Applications in Classroom Environments: A Bibliometric and Thematic Analysis
authors: Mark Anthony A. Castro, John Paul P. Miranda, Christian N. Pineda, Dorothy Joy M. Tongol, Nicholas D. Alonzo, Aaron John C. Alegre
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: This study analyzes the research landscape on IoT applications in classroom environments through bibliometric and text-mining techniques. The dataset contains 919 documents published from 2011 to 2026 and shows a steady rise in scientific output, strong co-authorship, and broad participation across 256 sources and 2,838 authors, which indicates growing scholarly engagement with IoT-enabled learning spaces. The analysis identifies the most productive authors, institutions, and countries and highlights influential works that shaped the field through contributions to environmental sensing, face recognition, adaptive instruction, occupancy analytics, and digital learning systems. Annual publication trends show rapid growth from 2016 to 2024, with prominent activity in engineering, computing, and education venues that support research on smart classrooms, sensor networks, and AI-supported instruction. Furthermore, the text-mining analysis uses Latent Dirichlet Allocation and reveals three dominant themes: IoT-enabled classroom operations, smart learning environments and educational applications, and intelligent educational technologies that support data-driven teaching.

title: Design of an Efficient Tobacco Foreign Object Detection Model Based on YOLOv3 Architecture
authors: Tianhou Yang, Mengshi Fan, Chengshun Sun, Maozhe Qin, Chengkun Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: A tobacco foreign object detection model based on the YOLOv3 architecture was designed, integrating multiscale feature extraction and real-time inference mechanisms. Experiments conducted across multiple industrial scenarios achieved detection accuracy exceeding 99% and inference speeds reaching 41 FPS. This solution is suitable for real-time production line inspection, significantly enhancing quality control efficiency.

title: An Analysis of Integrated Face Recognition Attendance Analytics
authors: P.Pandiaraja, Pattidi Megha Chaitanya, Katakam Nithin Kumar, C.Selvarathi, Charles Prabu V, S.Jagan
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: In schools and other organizations, attendance control is extremely important in terms of discipline and accountability. The use of traditional methods, including manual recording in the registers or RFID-based solutions is usually ineffective, time- consuming, and subject to human error. Face recognition has emerged as a strong option of automating the check-in system in the presence of an exceptionally high rate of computer vision and artificial intelligence growth. The literature review has shown that use of biometric technologies like fingerprint or face recognition is the basis of many modern attendance systems. Although these methods have helped to enhance precision, they are however affected by the highly populated area, changing illumination or network-depending structures. Other systems do not work when the biometric sensors do not work or when the internet connection is poor resulting in incomplete records of attendance. The identified gap in the current research is the absence of a powerful, hybrid system that can effectively operate in the context of high traffic and guarantee real-time data fine-tuning even in the situations when network conditions vary. Also, the current models tend to be mobile unfriendly with regard to live monitoring and have difficulties in managing attendance at the large scale. In order to address these shortcomings, the proposed work proposes an Attendance Management System based on Face Recognition with Python and Open CV which incorporates Haar Cascade Classifier to detect faces and Local Binary Pattern Histogram (LBPH) algorithm to recognize faces. The system has automatic features of detecting and identifying individuals by a web camera, attendance marking and storing the data safely in a CSV database. It also offers a Tkinter based Graphical User Interface (GUI) that is simple to use to register, train, and monitor. The method offers real time attendance taking, reduces human intervention, and eradicates the chances of proxy attendance. The findings indicate that the system under normal lighting conditions has high recognition accuracy (approximately 95%-97%) and an average response time of less than two seconds. It is scalable, reliable and user-friendly making it applicable in deployment in schools, colleges and organizations. To sum up, the offered approach manages to fill the gap between the traditional and modern attendance systems by incorporating both the accuracy, speed, and real-time availability, and further improvement can be provided by adding the deep learning or the cloud-based synchronization to even ease the processes.

title: DETU-Net: An Improved TransUNet with Dual-Branch for Right Ventricle MRI Segmentation
authors: Junchi Lu, Yu Fu, Bing Xu, Qingdi Li, Changhao Li, Haibo Yang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases related to the right ventricle have a high mortality and disability rate worldwide. Accurately segmenting the right ventricle from cardiac magnetic resonance imaging is essential for the diagnosis and analysis of cardiac diseases. However, owing to the intricate crescentshaped geometry of the structure, blurry boundary features, and significant shape variation from the apex to the base, automatic segmentation of the RV still faces tremendous challenges. Existing methods still lack accuracy in dealing with the complexity and deformability of such structures. Therefore, we propose a DETUNet model to segment right ventrilce, which mainly consists of two components: the dual-branch module and the ExTransformer module. The Dual-branch module employs a parallel branch structure to capture detailed information, thus enabling the extraction of the edge features of RV. ExTransformer enhances the long-range dependencies of the model, not only to focus on its own global features, but also to enhance the modeling of RV regions with structural discontinuities or large morphological variations in the form of cross-slices. Comparative experiments are conducted on both RVSC and ACDC datasets. The results on the RVSC dataset show that DETU-Net achieved a mean Dice on the epicardium and endocardium of 0.88 and 0.91, which demonstrates that our segmentation approach outperforms representative approaches.

title: Measuring Multimodal Transportation Network Resilience Using Curvature-Core Decomposition and Flow Inequality Metrics
authors: Guillaume Bouleux, Giacomo Kahn, Aurélie Charles
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Control, Decision and Information Technologies (CoDIT)
abstract: Production, health, transport and goods systems have been severely affected by recent health crises, and continue to be so in the face of current climatic and geopolitical disruptions. In this unstable context, it is essential not only to ensure their robustness, but also to reinforce their resilience. Many works in the literature deal with the robustness of systems, but very few focus on proposals related to resilience. We therefore propose to approach this concept within the framework of a multimodal transport network modeled by a multilayer network. We develop a resilience strategy based on the curvature-core decomposition of the network and compare it with a random decision strategy and a decision strategy based on maximum transport capacities. The results show that the strategy based on the curvature-core of the multilayer network enables a better decision to be made, avoiding congestion and link saturation during reconstruction, and finally enabling a balance in the distribution of flows.

title: A Multi-Source Data-Driven Hybrid Model for Predicting Crop Yield under Varying Climatic Conditions
authors: Premkumar Duraisamy, Sriharani R, Harishh D V, Jones Martin J
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Crop yield prediction is essential for enhancing food security and promoting sustainable agriculture, especially under varying climatic conditions. This work introduces a multi-source datadriven hybrid model that integrates structured soilweather parameters with unstructured satellite imagery to achieve superior prediction accuracy. Structured data comprising soil nutrients, rainfall, temperature, and humidity are analyzed using the XGBoost algorithm, known for its robustness in handling complex non-linear interactions and feature importance analysis. Simultaneously, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are employed to extract spatial and vegetation features from NDVI and RGB satellite images, capturing crop health variations across regions. Experimental datasets were obtained from FAO, MOSPI, and NASA Harvest (Radiant Earth MLHub). The hybrid XGBoost-CNN framework demonstrated outstanding predictive performance, achieving an overall accuracy of 96.5%, outperforming individual models such as Random Forest (94.1 %) and LSTM (93.7 %). This work highlights the effectiveness of combining multi-source agricultural data with hybrid AI techniques to enhance yield forecasting accuracy, strengthen climate resilience, and support informed decision-making in precision agriculture.

title: Research on Intelligent Facility Visualization System Design and Emergency Management Based on Internet of Things Technology
authors: Yunjie Sun, Xuesong Wang, Lina Xue, Zhipeng Yao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Edge Computing (ICDSEC)
abstract: In the construction and development of modern cities, emergency management can complete the prevention and treatment work through the intelligent facility visualization system, effectively control the loss of personnel and property, and provide technical support for the creation of smart cities. With the continuous development of the Internet of Things technology and three-dimensional visualization technology, the design of intelligent facility visualization system and emergency management system has become possible, and domestic and foreign scholars have put forward a number of issues on these contents. Therefore, after understanding the current research status of intelligent emergency management system and measures, this paper deeply discusses the key technologies and application principles of intelligent facility visualization system, and proposes effective measures for emergency management combined with practical cases, in order to provide reference for the design of intelligent facility visualization system and emergency management research in China.

title: Defect Diagnosis and Analysis Method of Sewage System Based on SWMM and Flow Monitoring Data
authors: Yangjun Zhou, Yufei Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Edge Computing (ICDSEC)
abstract: It is established in the article that a method for identifying and locating the defects of the sewage system based on flow monitoring. An optimization locating model is established to optimize the distribution of groundwater infiltration at the nodes in the sewage system and realize the locating and diagnosis of the defects in the sewage system. The content is to establish the optimization and locating model of the sewage system defects by coupling secondary development technology and scientific algorithm with SWMM. Taking the minimum error of RMSE between the measured flow values and the simulated flow values of the target nodes as the objective function, the PSO optimization algorithm is used to find the most suitable node groundwater infiltration scheme, so as to realize the localization and diagnosis of defects in the sewage system under the limited flow monitoring data.

title: Weakly Supervised and Knowledge-Constrained Multi-Source Learning for Building Fire Safety Risk Assessment
authors: Ao Xing, Di Zhang, Zhenpeng An
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenges of limited labeled samples and heterogeneous multi-source data fusion in building fire safety risk assessment. A weakly supervised and knowledge-constrained multi-source learning framework is proposed to enable label-efficient and interpretable risk modeling. The framework employs a Feature-Level Completion Model (FLCM) to impute missing attributes across buildings based on similarity relationships at the feature level and integrates an LSTM - MLP hybrid architecture to jointly fuse temporal and static features. Rule-based soft labels and self-supervised deviation detection form a unified learning objective, while knowledge constraints enhance model interpretability and stability. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model significantly outperforms traditional machine learning methods in expert-ranking consistency, providing a scalable and practical approach for intelligent building fire safety assessment and decision support.

title: A Stackelberg Game Model for Incentivizing Civil Aviation Data Sharing
authors: Meng Yue, Bolin Chen, Yuze Tian
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: The rapid expansion of civil aviation has generated vast and complex data, making accurate valuation and effective sharing difficult. Traditional single-dimensional methods are inadequate in multi-source environments, reducing data owners' willingness to share. To address this, we propose a Stackelberg game-based framework with data owners as leaders to maximize their utility. A multidimensional valuation approach replaces traditional intermediaries, enabling precise assessment. The framework consists of two stages: the first optimizes data source selection under complex multi-source scenarios, while the second applies dynamic pricing strategies to incentivize bulk transactions. This dual-stage design improves both valuation accuracy and sharing efficiency. Simulation results show that, compared with the bargaining model, the Stackelberg model increases data owners' utility by 26.11% and overall system efficiency by 52.88%, demonstrating enhanced willingness to share and improved system performance in the civil aviation data ecosystem.

title: Graph Neural Network-Based Method for Demand-Side Response Resource Regulation in Power Systems
authors: Yafei Ji, Ling Ling, Ting Zhang, Runming Dai, Xuming Shen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Edge Computing (ICDSEC)
abstract: Industrial enterprises consume substantial amounts of electricity and possess significant load regulation potential. Load-side demand response is a key approach for flexibility adjustment in distribution networks and optimal dispatch of demand-side resources. To fully leverage the adjustable capacity of these resources, a power demand-side response resource control method based on a graph neural network model is proposed. First, an uncertainty model for renewable energy output is constructed using fuzzy sets based on electrical distance, and a demand response baseline model that accounts for renewable energy forecast errors is reconstructed. Similar-day data are selected for calibration, and a neural network model is employed to rapidly estimate load power. By relying on the small dataset generated from calibration, the data required for neural network training is reduced, effectively lowering the structural complexity and training time of the neural network, thereby enabling rapid model response. A hierarchical clustering algorithm is applied to cluster the load curves of industrial enterprises, and the minimum safe load for each enterprise is calculated and compared within time windows to identify their adjustable load capacity. Finally, using electricity consumption data from industrial enterprises in a specific region, it is verified that the proposed strategy can enhance and optimize the regulation of power demand-side response resources.

title: A Lightweight LSTM-Based Model for Sequential Movie Recommendation
authors: Aya Lyousfi, Khadija Salih Alj, Kenza Bouqdir, El Habib Nfaoui
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Sequential recommendation systems aim to learn from user interaction data to determine which item a user is most likely to engage with next. While Transformer-based models such as BERT4Rec have achieved state-of-the-art performance, they often rely on complex architectures and auxiliary metadata, such as genre embeddings, which increase computational overhead and limit applicability in real-world settings. This paper introduces LSTM4Rec, a lightweight sequential recommender system that leverages a two-layer Long Short-Term Memory network trained solely on user watch sequences without any side information. Despite its simplicity, LSTM4Rec achieves an NDCG@10 of 0.98 and Hit@10 of 99% on the MovieLens 1M dataset, surpassing a genre-enhanced BERT4Rec implementation (NDCG @ 10: 0.7689, Hit@10: 95.07%). The results demonstrate that deep bidirectional attention mechanisms are not strictly necessary for high-quality sequence modeling, and that compact, interpretable models like LSTM4Rec remain highly competitive, especially in resource-constrained environments.

title: A Prediction Framework Incorporating Support Vector Regression and Random Forest Models
authors: Yiyang Wang, Tingyu Lin, Zihang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In this study, a hybrid prediction framework integrating support vector regression (SVR) model and random forest model is constructed. Firstly, the support vector regression (SVR) model is used to transform the problem of seeking optimal regression hyperplane into a quadratic convex programming problem solution, capturing the nonlinear distribution law of the data, and realising the prediction of continuous-type variables in small-sample and high-dimension scenarios. Meanwhile, the Random Forest model is used to select the characteristic quantities such as quantity and change trend for specific outcome prediction, and the Spearman correlation coefficient is applied to carry out two correlation analyses in order to explore the intrinsic connection between different categories of data. With the multi-model collaboration and multi-method comprehensive analysis, the framework effectively enhances the analysis and prediction ability of data, and shows good universality and robustness in related data processing tasks, providing an effective methodological reference for similar researches.

title: A Multi-Label Fake News Detection Framework Using Undersampling and Transformer-Based Deep Learning Models
authors: Pratyush Ranjan Mohapatra, Jayadeep Pakala, Nagagopiraju Vullam, Vunnava Dinesh Babu, Naladi Ram Babu, P. Chandra Sekhar Reddy
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The rapid spread of misleading and fabricated information across social media platforms has intensified the need for robust fake news detection systems. Traditional binary classification methods are insufficient for real-world scenarios where news items often receive multiple credibility judgments. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a multi-label fake news detection framework that integrates undersampling strategies with advanced transformer-based deep learning models. The dataset used in this study consists of tweets annotated with multiple credibility ratings, enabling multi-label classification through annotator-based label vectors. Two undersampling techniques—One-vs-Rest (OVR) and MultiLabel Random Undersampling (MLRU)—are employed to mitigate the effects of label imbalance and improve detection of minority credibility classes. Classical machine-learning algorithms, deep learning architectures, and transformer models including BERT and RoBERTa are evaluated on both undersampled datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that transformer models consistently outperform traditional approaches, with RoBERTa achieving the highest performance across all evaluation metrics. Furthermore, the application of MLRU significantly improves class-wise recall in minority categories. These findings highlight the effectiveness of combining undersampling with transformer architectures for multi-label fake news identification in highly imbalanced realworld settings.

title: Advanced Machine Learning with Feature Aggregation based Detection and Classification of Cardiac Arrhythmia on Electrocardiograms
authors: Majed Balkheer, Reda Salama, Mahmoud Ragab, Ashis Kumer Biswas
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: As we age, our blood vessels exhibit signs of deterioration, often leading to cardiovascular diseases. Arrhythmia is a common early symptom, and its timely detection is vital for reducing mortality. Automatic arrhythmia detection, heavily reliant on machine learning (ML), has gained significant attention. Using sensors, it captures real-time electrocardiogram (ECG) data, which ML models analyze to classify arrhythmias. However, body movements and environmental factors often disturb ECG signals, introducing noise, missing samples, and unbalanced data distributions that hinder detection. This study introduces a novel Classification of Cardiac Arrhythmia Using Multi-Technique Feature Fusion and Deep Hybrid Neural Networks (CCAMFF-DHNN) approach for ECG-based clinical diagnosis. The main objective is to establish the CCAMFF-DHNN system as a reliable tool for real-time cardiac arrhythmia analysis in clinical settings. Initially, the CCAMFF-DHNN method applies comprehensive preprocessing, including interpolation for signal regularization, noise removal, standardization for consistent scaling, and data oversampling. Subsequently, it employs a fusion of feature selection techniques—principal component analysis (PCA), recursive feature elimination (RFE), chi-square test, and mutual information (MI)—to extract the most informative features from the high-dimensional ECG data. Finally, a hybrid deep neural network combining a convolutional neural network with a long short-term memory (LSTM)-driven denoising autoencoder (CL-DAE) performs arrhythmia classification. The proposed system undergoes extensive experimental evaluation, with outcomes assessed under various performance metrics. Simulation results highlight the superiority of the CCAMFF-DHNN approach compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: Prenatal Heart Diagnosis Using Convolutional Neural Networks: A Web-Integrated Approach (PrenatalNet)
authors: Surabhi Kakade, Meghraj Ranaware, Shreyash Jadhav, Ishika Kale, Roshan Madake, Saurav Maghade
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Congenital heart disease (CHD) is one of the most common birth defects, and pre-prenatal diagnosis plays an important role in the planning of timely medical intervention. This article presents PrenatalNet, a fixed nervous network (CNN) based video processing framework designed to help with automatic identification of CHD from the fetal ultrasound video. The system integrates an intensive teaching model into a user-friendly web application, able to upload ultrasound videos to doctors and receive real-time clinical predictions. Backend achieves inputs from each video frame using advance exercise models, predicting overlay notes, and the highest frequency provides an anotat output video with predicted results. The system was built with OpenCV and Flush and originally ran with Google Colab. The system indicates how to use learning on diagnosis before delivery can be used effectively. This work provides a scalable and cheap solution for A-nhen's foster heart evaluation, especially with low resolution or distance applications in the health care system.

title: Real-time Explainable Deep Learning for Intrusion Detection System in Wireless Sensor Network using Edge Computing
authors: Vinod Kumar, Amit Kumar, Saurabh Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) have become a key element of edge computing systems today and can be used in healthcare and industrial automation, as well as smart cities, but due to their limited resources, they have become a major target of advanced cyberattacks. The scalability and flexibility of traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDSs) can be a challenge, whereas deep learning (DL)-based IDSs, even though more accurate, are often considered black-box models, making it difficult to trust and use them. This paper will resolve these constraints by proposing XEdge-IDS, an explainable deep learning-based IDS that can be deployed as edges in WSNs. Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and one-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (CNN-1D) were trained and tested on the UNSW-NB15 dataset using the hyperparameter optimization to guarantee efficiency in the conditions of resource allocation limitation. The CNN-1D performed the best among them with 94.38% accuracy and better precision, recall, and F1-score than MLP and LSTM. To mitigate the problem of the lack of interpretability, Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME) and SHapley Additive explanations (SHAP) were used, which identified packet- and protocol-level characteristics as strong features of malicious traffic, including sttl, dttl, and ct_state_ttl. These insights increase the model transparency and the confidence of the administrator and make the IDS operational in real-time in limited WSN settings. The results support the claim that explainable deep learning is not merely an accuracy-improving detection method, but also a practice that offers applicability in a real-world context, and a potential route toward ensuring the safety of future-generation WSNs in edge computing environments.

title: Smart Jamming via Downlink Control Information Sniffing and its Countermeasures
authors: Shao-Di Wang, Heng-Wei Lou, Chang-Long Wang, Feng Zhou
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the susceptibility of the physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH) to a novel smart jamming threat in fifth-generation (5G) new radio (NR). In this attack, an intelligent adversary first acquires resource allocation details by monitoring the downlink control information (DCI), and subsequently launches a targeted and stealthy jamming that severely undermines PUSCH data transmission. To defend against such smart jamming via DCI sniffing (SJ-DCIS), we propose an innovative suppression method by leveraging the DCI-scheduled subset identification and PUSCH resource reconstruction. The proposed method exploits distinguishable spatial characteristics specifically, variations in control channel element utilization and resource block group allocation patterns between legitimate users and the SJ-DCIS attacker. By identifying and filtering out malicious components while preserving the integrity of targeted transmissions, our method effectively mitigates the impact of SJ-DCIS. Simulation results are presented to validate the performance and demonstrate the efficacy of the developed defense method.

title: Deep CNN-Based U-Net Framework for Accurate Human Detection in Complex Through-the-Wall Scenarios
authors: Zakeen Ahmad, Chen Peng
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Through-the-wall radar imaging (TWRI) enables critical tasks such as search and rescue, surveillance, and health monitoring. However, strong wall reflections often obscure weak human signatures. This work presents a simulation-driven framework for detecting stationary and breathing individuals at low frequencies behind walls. Synthetic radar datasets were generated in MATLAB, modeling multiple wall types and clutter conditions. A convolutional neural network (CNN)-based U-Net was developed, trained, and validated to suppress wall clutter and enhance target visibility. The model effectively distinguishes human responses from dominant wall echoes, yielding over 30 dB improvement in signal-to-clutter ratio. Tests on synthetic and emulated radar data further confirm its ability to recover weak target echoes while maintaining real-time throughput within FPGA power limits. Results demonstrate the robustness and efficiency of the proposed deep learning approach, establishing a strong foundation for embedded implementation and practical field deployment in complex through-wall scenarios.

title: HyperMap: AI-Guided Manifold Exploration for High-Dimensional Optimization
authors: Gaurav Vishnu Londhe, Muthuraman Subbiah, R.Suresh, Shaik Reddi Khasim, Vivek Kothuru, M.Baritha Begum
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: High dimensional optimization is important in engineering, artificial intelligence, and scientific computing but inefficient, slow convergent, and intractable with traditional algorithms like a Bayesian Optimization, Particle Swarm Optimization, Deep Reinforcement Optimization, and Gradient-Free Evolutionary Search. It is suggested in this paper that HyperMap is a new AI-assisted optimization, which combines latent manifold learning, reinforcement-based adaptive optimization, and iterative policy unraveling to effectively address high-dimensional optimization problems. HyperMap can dynamically reduce redundancy and structural correlations in dimensions and dynamically balance exploration/exploitation in order to attain high-quality convergence, optimal gaps, and better computational efficiency. These dimensional experiments indicate that HyperMap is more effective than base-case solutions, with an average Optimality Gap of 12.0, average Convergence Rate of 237 iterations and enhanced exploration diversity (0.57) and can provide an average Expected Reward Improvement of 23.7. The above findings verify that the framework is being scaled, can be generalized and it can be used to offer a real time and actionable optimization solutions to highly dimensional tasks.

title: Data-Driven Energy Prediction and Dynamic Model Selection for Green AI Systems
authors: Nikita Thakur, Rohit Bajaj
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The rapid evolution of Deep Learning (DL) models is accompanied by a significant increase in power consumption, which in turn raises sustainability issues in the context of modern Artificial Intelligence (AI). The contribution of this research is a new dynamic energy-aware framework that combines predictive modeling with adaptive deployment to produce an energy-efficient computation. The applied system uses datasets to predict model-level energy consumption through the deployment of Machine Learning (ML) algorithms such as Random Forest (RF), Gradient Boosting (GB), and Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM). Subsequently, a dynamic model selection process determines the best model by considering the trade-off between energy cost and accuracy. As a result, the framework proves its effectiveness in the sustainable management of models by reducing energy consumption by as much as 27.6% while still achieving a high level of accuracy 94.8%. This research also makes a notable contribution to the development of Green AI by offering a scalable, data-driven solution that increases operational-efficiency alongside performance. Moreover, this method paves the way for the environmentally friendly training and deployment of AI models in both data centers and edge environments.

title: Data Science Approaches for Global Renewable Energy Forecasting: Analysis of Sustainable Energy Data (2000–2020)
authors: Shivam Chaurasiya
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The transition to renewable energy is vital to mitigating climate change and achieving Sustainable Development Goal 7 (SDG 7) on affordable and clean energy. Accurate forecasting of renewable energy adoption is critical for policymakers, researchers, and energy planners to design effective strategies for sustainable development. This study presents a comprehensive data science framework that benchmarks statistical, machine learning (ML), and deep learning (DL) models using the Global Data on Sustainable Energy (2000–2020) dataset. Baseline models, including Linear Regression (LR) and AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA), provide interpretable foundations but demonstrate limited forecasting accuracy. Classical ML models such as Random Forest (RF) and XGBoost significantly improve performance by capturing non-linear feature interactions. DL approaches, particularly Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and CNN–LSTM hybrids, further enhance predictive capacity by modeling temporal dependencies across two decades of global data. To introduce novelty, hybrid ARIMA–XGBoost and Transformer-based architectures are employed, yielding superior results with an overall accuracy of 96.7% and a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) below 5%. Comparative analysis demonstrates that while statistical models offer interpretability, hybrid DL approaches deliver state-of-the-art forecasting accuracy. The findings underscore the importance of advanced data-driven methods for global energy forecasting, providing actionable insights to guide renewable energy transitions, carbon reduction strategies, and evidence-based energy policy formulation.

title: Intelligent Dispatching Resource Optimization Configuration for Power Equipment Production Command
authors: Bei Li, Yuhuan Jiang, Haixin Luo, Siyuan Liu, Junfeng Wei
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This paper proposes a solution based on an improved multi-objective sparrow search algorithm for the problem of optimal configuration of power equipment production dispatching resources. The algorithm balances the multi-objective relationship through a dynamic weight adjustment mechanism, and uses an adaptive step size strategy and an elite reverse learning strategy to improve search efficiency and accuracy. In the experimental design, based on actual production data and simulation data, traditional genetic algorithms and particle swarm optimization algorithms are selected as comparison algorithms, and the optimal solution quality, convergence speed, solution set diversity and other indicators are used for evaluation. The experimental results show that the improved algorithm has a 32<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">%</sup> increase in convergence speed compared with the traditional genetic algorithm and a 28% increase compared with the particle swarm optimization algorithm; in terms of optimal solution quality, the production cycle is shortened by an average of 18%, and the resource utilization rate is increased by an average of 22%; in terms of solution set diversity, the hypervolume index is increased by an average of 25% compared with the comparison algorithm. This algorithm provides an effective way to optimize the configuration of intelligent dispatching resources for power equipment production command.

title: Research on Furniture Design Based on Knowledge Graph Evolution
authors: Zeqing Li, Suoling Wang, Qunye Gu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This study employs Citespace to conduct a visualization analysis of 995 Chinese and English literature sources published between 2015 and 2025, based on knowledge graph theory. It constructs co-occurrence networks and evolutionary pathways to reveal the knowledge structure of AI-driven smart furniture design. The research highlights the critical role of knowledge graphs in semantic understanding, personalized recommendations, and context awareness, and demonstrates their effectiveness in enhancing user experience through the case study of a smart sofa.

title: Research on Innovative Software Architecture Design and System Performance Improvement Based on Intelligent Optimization Algorithm
authors: Zhu Lijin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This study focuses on designing innovative intelligent optimization algorithms and applying them to software architecture design to improve system performance. The algorithm is inspired by the cooperative behavior of biological groups and integrates dynamic adaptive search strategies and multi-dimensional information interaction mechanisms. By building a mathematical model, its operating principle is explained in detail. In the software architecture design model, the performance goals are clearly defined as the system response time is controlled within 5 milliseconds and the throughput is 10,000 transactions per second, while considering the 8GB limit of hardware memory, CPU computing power constraints and compatibility with existing systems. The experiment was built in a hardware environment equipped with an Intel Core i7 processor and 16GB of memory, using Python language and related frameworks, and using a data set containing tens of millions of transaction records collected from the e-commerce business system. Small, medium and large e-commerce system architecture scenarios are set for comparative experiments, and the architecture design scheme based on the innovative algorithm is compared with traditional algorithms and classic design methods. The results show that the innovative algorithm shortens the average system response time by 30%, increases the throughput by 2.5 times, and improves resource utilization by 20%.

title: ElbowHack: Video-Assisted PIN Inference Through Elbow-Forearm Dynamics Analysis
authors: Bo Yin, Yuefeng Chen, Hongbo Liu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Personal Identification Number(PIN), as a mainstream method for identity verification and payment authorization, has become a prime target for malicious attacks, leading to significant economic and privacy losses. However, with the increasing awareness of security, people now deliberately cover their hand movements when entering PIN to protect their privacy, leaving attackers with minimal side-channel information to exploit. In this paper, we present a novel video-assisted PIN inference system, ElbowHack, which leverages video captured from behind the victim to launch the attack. Specifically, to address scenarios where attackers cannot capture hand or wrist data because of visual occlusion, we utilize the subtle spatiotemporal information of elbow joints and the upper forearm contours to infer the entered PIN code. We develop an attack system that identifies the click time of each keystrokes, reconstructs fingertip coordinates using elbow joint positions and arm contour dynamics, and infers the entered PIN with inferred fingertip coordinates. with extensive experiments. ElbowHack demonstrates an average accuracy of over 73 % on the first attempt and over 81.3 % after 3 attempts.

title: Transfer Learning-based Culvert Silting Condition Classification
authors: Hemanth R. Madduri, Jun Zheng, Claudia Wilson, Isabel Morris, Wesley Cook
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Culverts are essential structures that channel water across or alongside roads and other infrastructure. Their primary function is to facilitate proper drainage, preventing water accumulation, stagnation, erosion, and potential flooding that could compromise structural stability and longevity. Regular inspections and maintenance are crucial as culverts deteriorate over time. However, traditional manual inspections often involve stringent conditions, making them hazardous, time-consuming and labor-intensive, especially in culverts with poor structural integrity, limited visibility, or confined spaces. These challenges highlight the need for predictive models to automate the inspection process, improving efficiency, accuracy, and safety. In this paper, we investigated the use of transfer learning to automatically classify culvert silting conditions based on image data. Transfer learning leverages a model pretrained with a large dataset for a specific task and applies it to a new, related task, significantly reducing training time and computational resources. Our approach employs pretrained convolutional neural network (CNN) models, including DenseNet-121, EfficientNetV2-Small, ResNet-50, and VGG11, to extract features from culvert images. The extracted features are then fed into a custom classification head to predict silting conditions. We evaluated model performance using a culvert image dataset collected in the state of New Mexico, categorizing silting conditions into three levels: low (<10%), medium (10%-60%), and high (>60%). Our results show that the model using EfficientNet as the feature extractor achieved the highest classification performance. Additionally, the customized classification head significantly outperformed traditional machine learning classifiers, including Support Vector Classifier (SVC), Random Forest, and XGBoost.

title: Bridging Visual and Linguistic Intelligence for Chest X-rays: A Comprehensive Review of ViTs and LLM Synergies
authors: Ismail Hossain, Mridul Banik
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: The integration of Vision Transformers (ViTs) and Large Language Models (LLMs) in chest X-ray analysis has emerged as a promising solution to address the growing challenges in radiology, including increasing diagnostic workloads and the need for timely, accurate interpretations. This systematic review examines the recent advancements in ViTs-LLM hybrid systems, exploring their architectural innovations, multimodal fusion strategies, and application in automated report generation. A comprehensive search of databases such as Google Scholar, PubMed, and IEEE Xplore was conducted to identify studies published between 2018 and 2025, focusing on ViTs-LLM integration, performance metrics, and clinical validation. Key findings highlight that ViTs-LLM models significantly improve diagnostic accuracy, with a 15% improvement in pneumonia detection compared to traditional CNN-based models. These systems also excel at producing clinically relevant reports, achieving a 93% alignment rate with clinician-generated reports. Research demonstrates that ViTs-LLM hybrid models reduce diagnostic errors, enhance radiology workflow efficiency, and support clinical decision-making by offering real-time assistance. However, challenges related to computational complexity, data biases, and regulatory approval remain, posing barriers to widespread clinical adoption. Future directions include optimizing these models for real-time deployment, addressing ethical concerns, and integrating them into clinical settings with minimal disruption to existing workflows. The review points out the opportunity for ViTs-LLM systems to enhance both diagnostic performance and patient care, offering a transformative tool for the future of radiology.

title: Threshold-Based Memory Resource Allocation for Entanglement Distribution in Quantum Communication Networks
authors: Zihao Wang, Yuhang Liu, Xiaosong Yu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Efficient quantum memory management is a critical challenge for large-scale entanglement distribution networks, where the probabilistic nature of entanglement generation and finite memory coherence time often cause asynchronous link completion and severe resource blocking. To address this problem, we propose a Threshold-Driven Dual-Mode Allocation (TDDMA) scheme that adaptively switches between pre-allocation and ondemand allocation according to a configurable success-probability threshold. The algorithm dynamically classifies links into highreliability and low-reliability categories. High-success-probability links are pre-allocated at the beginning of each attempt to guarantee continuity, while soft links allocate memory only when entanglement generation succeeds, releasing unused memories immediately after failure. This dual-mode mechanism effectively balances reliability and utilization by minimizing idle occupation without compromising end-to-end success probability. We develop a simulation framework on 64 -node grid and ring topologies to evaluate the proposed scheme. Results show that TDDMA achieves up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 5. 7 \%}$</tex> lower blocking probability and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 - 8 \%}$</tex> higher effective memory utilization compared with the static End-to-End Reservation Allocation (EERA) baseline. Moreover, TDDMA demonstrates robust adaptability under varying traffic loads and link success distributions, maintaining stable performance even near network saturation.

title: Decoding Optimization of Spatially Coupled Polar Codes Based on Improved State Monitoring
authors: Mingqiang Su, Shiyao Li, Qiufang Lian, Miao Wu, Zijun Lu, Lin Zhou
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Polar codes face challenges of insufficient polarization and limited decoding performance in short code-length applications, and existing decoding algorithms struggle to achieve an ideal balance between performance and complexity. To address this issue, this paper proposes a decoding optimization scheme for spatially coupled polar codes based on an improved state monitoring decoding (ISMD). This scheme adopts a spatially coupled structure to enhance the polarization effect and achieves efficient decoding by combining fixed-iteration soft cancellation (SCAN) decoding. The ISMD mechanism adaptively saves the optimal soft information state by monitoring the variation of the average probability of errors in real time during the decoding process. It then makes a hard decision based on this optimal state, which effectively avoids the performance degradation problem in iterative decoding. The simulation results show that the proposed scheme can effectively improve the decoding performance of spatially coupled polar codes with short code lengths.

title: DiffFreq: Emphasizing Important Frequencies and Equal Learning of All Frequencies for Time Series Generation
authors: Xuncan Xiao, Yi Zhu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Seasonal-trend decomposition is pivotal for time series analysis, yet existing frequency-domain learning methods face significant limitations. Approaches focusing solely on high-amplitude frequencies fail to capture subtle but critical variations encoded in low-amplitude components. Conversely, methods treating all frequencies equally often under-learn high-amplitude patterns, leading to suboptimal decomposition and generation quality. To overcome issues, we propose DiffFreq, a novel diffusion-based framework for time series generation. DiffFreq uniquely combines equal learning of all frequencies with adaptive emphasis on important frequencies, enabling a more comprehensive and balanced seasonal decomposition. This dual strategy is realized through specially designed components including Patch Attention and Adaptive Weighting Model, integrated within Transformer layers enhanced with rotational position encodings. Extensive experiments on eight challenging benchmarks demonstrate that DiffFreq consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance, significantly enhancing generation quality. Specifically, DiffFreq reduces the average correlation score metric by 41% and the average Context-FID metric by 19% compared to existing methods, confirming its superior fidelity to real data patterns.

title: Visualization Analysis of Sound Wave Interference through Computer Simulation
authors: Hui Meng, Yi Wu, Xinrong Guo, Huili Dai
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: This article focuses on the visual analysis of acoustic interference phenomenon through computer simulation. In view of the limitations of traditional methods to study acoustic interference, an algorithm model based on finite difference method is constructed with the help of powerful computer simulation functions. The model divides grids in two-dimensional space, sets key parameters, and simulates sound wave propagation and interference according to discrete iterative formula of wave equation. Through experiments, a virtual two-dimensional experimental environment is built by using COMSOL Multiphysics software, and different sound source parameters are set to verify the model. The results show that under different experimental conditions, the maximum relative error between the simulated constructive interference sound pressure value and the theoretical value is 2%, and the maximum relative error of the destructive interference sound pressure value is 5%. This proves that the algorithm model can accurately simulate the phenomenon of acoustic interference, provide an effective tool for acoustic interference research and related engineering applications, and enhance the understanding and application ability of the internal mechanism of acoustic interference.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning Framework for Early Diagnosis of Chronic Liver Disease
authors: T V N L Aswini, Kamesh Sonti, Purnima K Sharma, A V N Kumara Swamy, T J V Subrahmanyeswara Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Chronic liver disease (CLD) is a severe global health concern that is frequently recognized only in the late stages due to limitations in standard diagnostic approaches. Reducing mortality and improving patient outcomes require early, accurate, and non-invasive CLD prediction. For the early diagnosis of CLD, this study offers a novel hybrid machine learning (ML) framework that combines Extreme Gradient Boosting and K-Nearest Neighbors. A dataset with demographic, lifestyle, and biochemical information for 1,700 people was examined. With an accuracy of 98%, precision of 0.96, recall of 0.95, and an AUC of 0.99, the suggested hybrid model performed better than any other. These results demonstrate how hybrid ensemble models can improve prediction accuracy, provide a dependable non-invasive method for early CLD screening, and open the door for sophisticated healthcare decision-support systems. Notably, the suggested paradigm shows potential for early-age screening as well, considering the rising prevalence of pediatric and adolescent CLD associated with obesity and lifestyle modifications.

title: Research on OFDM Subcarrier Extraction and Reconstruction Method Based on Multi-Rate Filtering for 5G Radar
authors: Peiyuan Hui, Chenghu Cao, Haisheng Huang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: To address the high-fidelity extraction of a single subcarrier from a wideband Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) signal, this paper proposes a multi-rate filtering chain comprising down-conversion, anti-alias filtering, decimation, narrowband low-pass filtering, and up-conversion, combined with normalized cross-correlation for delay estimation. Under a high sampling rate of 122.88 MHz, two stages of Kaiser-window Finite Impulse Response (FIR) filters are designed to reduce computational complexity while effectively suppressing adjacent-channel interference (ACS). The method preserves the inherent delay and Doppler characteristics of the target subcarrier and enables high-precision delay measurement. Simulation results show that, compared with direct full-rate processing, the proposed scheme reduces the back-end computational load by more than an order of magnitude while keeping the delay-estimation error below 5% of one full-rate sampling interval. Furthermore, extensive simulations under various signal - to - noise ratio (SNR) conditions confirm the robustness of the proposed method. Even in the presence of strong adjacent - channel interference and random delay - Doppler shifts, the two-stage Kaiser - window filtering structure preserves the amplitude and phase integrity of the target subcarrier and provides stable delay estimates. The multi-rate implementation also facilitates real-time processing, making the technique well suited to high-bandwidth communication, passive radar sensing, and other applications that require accurate timing information while operating with limited computational resources. These results demonstrate that the proposed approach offers a practical and scalable solution for high-fidelity OFDM subcarrier extraction and precise delay estimation in wideband systems.

title: AI-Driven Analysis of Social Media Influence on Stock Market Trends
authors: Arpit Bhatia, Lakshay Khatri, Sagar Bhandari, Parul Gandhi
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The fast emergence of social media sites has changed the way financial information is conveyed, and this could affect the trends of the stock markets, though the exact effect is under-researched. This paper examines the connection between sentiment in social media and the stock market patterns, that is, the influence of online discussions on investor actions. We analyse a large amount of social media data through the application of AI-based natural language processing and sentiment analysis, and juxtapose this with historical stock market trends. In our approach, we combine machine learning to discover the sentiment trends and match them to the market changes. The results indicate that the social media sentiment has a substantial impact on short-term market volatility, especially in high-profile corporate events, but the impact of social media sentiment on longer-term trends is not as strong. Positive sentiment rises can be the forerunners of short-lived price increases, whereas short-term price drops can be aggravated by negative sentiment. We come to the conclusion that social media is a strong yet short-lived force in market dynamics, and we should not underestimate the fact that investors and regulators must evaluate the role of social media in the formation of financial markets.

title: IEEE App
authors: N/A

title: RBD-Net: Enhancing Binocular Depth Accuracy with Robust Multi-Scale Neural Networks
authors: Xin Zhang, Yuqi Song
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Depth estimation is critical for applications such as autonomous driving and robotics, enabling these systems to perceive and interact with their environments effectively. Binocular depth estimation, which utilizes stereo image pairs to derive depth information, is a popular approach due to its potential for accuracy. However, the performance of existing methods is highly dependent on ideal conditions, focusing predominantly on scenarios involving perfectly aligned devices and optimal imaging conditions. These methods often fail to address real-world challenges like variable lighting, camera misalignments, and environmental noise, limiting their practical utility in non-ideal or dynamic settings. To address these limitations, this paper introduces RBD-Net (Robust Binocular Depth-Net), a sophisticated convolutional neural network designed to robustly estimate depth from stereo images under diverse and challenging conditions. RBD-Net integrates a noise-reduction module that proactively mitigates the impact of noise and disturbances, thereby enhancing the reliability of depth predictions. The model employs a multi-scale approach to capture detailed depth features across various resolutions and incorporates a novel loss function tailored to improve precision and robustness in varying environments. Extensive testing on the KITTI 2015 and KITTI 2012 datasets demonstrates that RBD-Net not only maintains but also significantly enhances the robustness and accuracy of depth estimations compared to existing methods. Our findings confirm RBD-Net’s superior performance, establishing it as a formidable tool for achieving high-fidelity depth perception across a broad range of operational scenarios, making it highly suitable for advanced computer vision applications where environmental adaptability and accuracy are paramount.

title: A DC-Transformer Driven Multi-Source Dynamic Thresholding Algorithm for Spatiotemporal Pollution Early Warning
authors: BoLin Qi, Xiong Yang, Hongliang Wang, Jian Wu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Accurate early warning of air pollution is not only a key technical challenge in urban environmental management but also an important task for sustainable development. Traditional fixed-threshold methods, constrained by static national standards, fail to capture the spatiotemporal dynamics of environmental pollution and therefore often lead to false alarms and missed detections. To address this limitation, we propose a DC-Transformer-based dynamic thresholding framework that models threshold determination as an adaptive and evolving process. The framework integrates multi-source information, including real-time monitoring data, short-term sequence predictions, and long-term seasonal patterns, and generates context-aware dynamic warning thresholds through a weighted combination. Within this framework, diffusion convolution captures spatial dependencies among monitoring stations, while the Transformer network models complex nonlinear temporal evolution, jointly enabling fine-grained prediction of pollution dynamics. A tunable weighting mechanism further enhances flexibility while preserving interpretability. Experiments on multi-station hourly datasets demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms fixed-threshold strategies in terms of accuracy, recall, and F1-score, effectively reducing missed detections while controlling false alarms. Overall, this study provides an efficient and scalable solution for dynamic pollutant early warning, offering both theoretical contributions and practical value for intelligent urban environmental management.

title: Research on Power Grid Investment Decision Model Based on Multi-objective Optimization Algorithm
authors: Chen Ye, Han Jiang, Yaogang Luo
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Power grid investment decision-making is faced with the conflict and trade-off between economic, reliability and environmental protection objectives. In order to solve this problem, this paper constructs a multi-objective optimization model, which comprehensively considers the economic, reliability and environmental protection objectives of power grid investment, and introduces NSGA-II as the solution algorithm. The model optimizes the investment decision of power grid by minimizing the investment and operation cost, maximizing the reliability of power supply and minimizing the environmental impact. Through case analysis, this paper shows the application effect of the model in the actual power grid upgrading investment, and verifies its effectiveness in providing a variety of trade-off schemes and meeting different decision-making needs. The research results show that the model can provide a more scientific and reasonable solution for power grid investment decision-making and contribute to the sustainable development of power industry.

title: ICSPED 2026 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)

title: Quantum Computing-Driven Integration of Multimodal Biometrics for Enhanced Authentication and Security
authors: Richa Sharma, Charanjit Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Biometric authentication systems have come a long way in terms of accuracy and reliability, but they still suffer from computational efficiency and security issues constraints. This paper, examines the fusion of quantum computing with multimodal biometric authentication in order to improve the rate of recognition, the processing rate and it’s resistance to adversarial attacks. We work with quantum machine learning models like Variational Quantum Classifiers (VQC), and Quantum Neural Networks (QNN), to process fingerprint, iris, facial recognition datasets. Detailed comparison with conventional schemes indicates the advantage over the classical counterparts in terms of the authentication accuracy and the computation efficiency, respectively. Quantum-enhanced biometric security is verified by experimental results.

title: Performance Analysis of GDOP-Based Satellite Selection Methods for LEO Constellations
authors: Bohan Zhang, Aijun Liu, Kegang Pan, Xiaohu Liang, Xin Lin, Fan Zhang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Low earth orbit (LEO) satellite constellation systems possess the potential to deliver integrated communication and navigation (ICAN) services with wider coverage, higher accuracy, and stronger reliability. However, the increase in visible satellites elevates observational data redundancy and substantially increases the computational load. In this context, efficient satellite selection algorithms serve to mitigate this issue and enhance overall system performance.
