This paper systematically elaborates on the principles of various traditional satellite selection algorithms, integrates the advantages of different approaches to propose an improved heuristic selection strategy, and conducts a comprehensive comparative performance analysis of five algorithms in a LEO navigation environment. Simulation results clearly reveal the strengths and weaknesses of each method. The proposed algorithm achieves a geometric dilution of precision (GDOP) value comparable to that of the exhaustive search method, while significantly outperforming it in computational efficiency, making it more suitable for addressing satellite selection challenges in LEO navigation constellation systems.

title: Enhancing Secondary-Level Polymer Chemistry Learning with Gamified Augmented Reality: Development and Evaluation of Gameful Experience and Usability
authors: Fadhlina Izzah Saman, Fadilah Ezlina Shahbudin, Muhammad Syaril Rusmizi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Polymer chemistry, a topic in the Malaysian secondary school curriculum, is often taught using traditional 2D methods that might hinder student understanding because of its abstract nature. Many students find it difficult to visualize complex molecular structures, leading to low interest and engagement. This paper presents the development of Poly-AR, a gamified augmented reality mobile application designed to enhance the learning experience of polymer chemistry for Form 5 students, and its usability and gameful experience evaluations by the target users. The application utilizes 3D AR models and also gamification elements to assist students in visualizing the polymer structures and also in their interaction with the learning material. A preliminary study involving 42 respondents highlights the challenges faced by learners and teachers, which Poly-AR aims to address. The usability and gameful experience of the application were evaluated, with results of 5.78 out of 7 point average mean value for gameful experience, and 4.60 out of 5 point average mean value for usability, indicating high levels of efficiency, effectiveness, satisfaction, and engagement, providing a modern educational tool to support ubiquitous, engaging learning.

title: Route-Assisted Peer Model Training among Connected Vehicles under Different Radio Capabilities
authors: Han Zheng, Mengjing Liu, Fan Ye, Yuanyuan Yang
publish: ACM Transactions on Internet of Things
abstract: None

title: Semantic Frame Recognition for Chinese Literary Metaphor Constructions: Model, Corpus, and Applications
authors: Hui Yang, Hui Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Literary metaphors in Chinese (e.g., simile, metaphor, and borrowing-based tropes) are core carriers of aesthetic meaning. Existing computational approaches typically focus on shallow pattern matching or generic metaphor detection, and rarely model the constructional nature, semantic deviation, and frame-level mappings that drive literary interpretation. This paper proposes a semantic-frame-based computational framework for Chinese literary metaphor constructions. We formalize literary metaphors as form-meaning-function pairings, design a three-layer annotation scheme, and build the Chinese Literary Figurative Construction Corpus (CLFCC) with 5,000 manually annotated instances from modern Chinese novels, prose, and poetry. On top of CLFCC, we develop a two-stage hybrid system: Stage-1 uses a pretrained language model enhanced with construction-aware features to detect metaphoric sentences and classify trope types; Stage-2 performs prompt-based frame parsing and mapping extraction with knowledge-guided normalization using HowNet and Chinese FrameNet. Experiments show that our system achieves an F1-score of 0.86 on metaphor identification, and reaches 0.72 accuracy on source/target frame recognition and 0.58 F1 on fine-grained mapping extraction, outperforming rule-based and fine-tuned PLM baselines. The proposed corpus and interpretable frame-mapping outputs support computational stylistics and digital humanities at scale.

title: Position Aided Beam Tracking: Real-World Assessment for Future Beam Prediction
authors: João Morais, Ahmed Alkhateeb
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: We evaluate a beam tracking method based on machine learning in a real-world mm Wave dataset and compare a position-based approach with a beam-based approach. The beam-based approach outperforms position most of the time. But we noted that the position-based approach prevails for predictions far into the future, i.e. at least half a second ahead of time. Furthermore, the position-based approach provides more accurate predictions as beams get wider. Additionally, we assess both approaches on learning windows of different sizes, i.e. from how far into the past we gather information to perform the beam predictions. Our tests with real-world data show that larger learning windows appear to mitigate the effect of addictive random position noise, suggesting that leveraging more information can compensate for lower-grade hardware. Plus, since position-based beam tracking completely avoids beam training overheads, the proposed position-based approach appears an attractive complement to minimizing the overhead of current beam tracking solutions.

title: A Robust Data Provenance Method for Multi-hop Networks Based on Recursive Chained Fingerprinting and Soft-Decision Decoding
authors: Huiting Hou, Ming Lv, Minshi Chen, Jia Zheng, Jie Cheng, Jia Liu, Shiquan Xu, Yuhan Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: The dynamic dissemination of sensitive documents across multi-hop networks necessitates rigorous data provenance. However, existing methods face significant challenges: traditional audit logs rely on centralized trust, while robust watermarking suffers from "lineage obliviousness" and the inherent "cliff effect" of error correction codes (ECC). To address these limitations, this paper proposes a robust data provenance method for multi-hop networks. First, a recursive chained fingerprinting protocol is designed to cryptographically bind consecutive transmission nodes, establishing an immutable and recursive propagation lineage. Second, to overcome the physical robustness limit, a hybrid decoding strategy combining algebraic decoding with soft-decision decoding is introduced. By utilizing the global registry as side information, this method transforms the decoding problem into a high-dimensional nearest neighbor search, effectively mitigating the "cliff effect". Experimental results show a traceability success rate of over 98% under extreme conditions (20-hop chains, 6,000 nodes, and 30% BER), providing a resilient solution for securing digital assets in untrusted environments.

title: Green Link – A Vehicle-to-Vehicle Charging Exchange for Green Mobility
authors: Sreekumar U, Abhijit B Mulay, Singilesu Jagan
abstract: This paper presents Green Link, an innovative vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) charging exchange system designed to address the limitations of current electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure. The system enables direct peer-to-peer energy transfer between EVs through a hybrid architecture supporting both plug-in and wireless charging modes. Green Link incorporates bidirectional onboard chargers, high-frequency inverters, and advanced communication modules to facilitate secure and efficient energy transfer. The wireless mode utilizes resonant inductive wireless power transfer (WPT) technology operating at 85 kHz with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>90 \%$</tex> efficiency. Simulation results demonstrate the system's effectiveness in supplementing existing charging infrastructure, particularly in remote locations and emergency scenarios. The system achieves overall efficiency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>93 \%$</tex> in plug-in mode and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>85 \%$</tex> in wireless mode while maintaining compliance with IEEE 2030.1.1 and SAE J2954 standards.

title: 3D Reconstruction of Drone Images for Highway Monitoring
authors: Weizhe Wang, Jinhua Liu, Haitong Li, Guangli Li, Hongliang Liu, Huimin Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: The patch based multi view Iterative reconstruction (PMVS) algorithm based on panel is used to generate a 3D model, so that when monitoring the highway settlement, highway slope settlement and surrounding buildings, there are holes in the point cloud model at the weak texture. Therefore, it is proposed to improve the PMVS algorithm for Iterative reconstruction by using the improved Key. Net algorithm is used to complete feature extraction. After feature matching, the obtained matching pairs are used for SFM sparse reconstruction. Then, the sparse 3D points optimized using the de minimum and average strategy are adjusted for candidate points and normal vectors during subsequent panel reconstruction to generate a more complete road scene point cloud model. Comparison of point cloud models generated from three scenarios: highway, highway slope, and highway construction. The experimental results show that the improved PMVS algorithm in this paper can obtain more point clouds in weak texture regions, and in different highway scenarios, compared with the unmodified PMVS algorithm, the number of pixels in the model increases by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5. 4 3 \%} \boldsymbol{,} \mathbf{1 4. 8 2 \%} \boldsymbol{,} \mathbf{1 9. 2 3 \%}$</tex>, and 15.88 %, respectively. A more complete three-dimensional model of highway scenes can be obtained, serving highway monitoring tasks.

title: Displacement Measurement Method Integrating Kalman Filter and ViT Algorithm
authors: Chen Chen, Peng Li, Zhi Li, Peng Wu, Zuxiang Xu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: This article proposes a high-precision displacement measurement method that integrates Kalman filtering and ViT (Vision Transformer) algorithm. Real time displacement monitoring of engineering structures is of great significance for structural safety assessment and life prediction. Although traditional displacement monitoring technology based on computer vision (CV) has advantages such as non-contact measurement, easy equipment deployment, and low cost, its recognition precision is easily affected by environmental factors such as lighting changes, image resolution, and shooting frame rates, which limits the widespread application of this technology in practical engineering. In response to the above issues, this article innovatively designs a displacement measurement scheme that combines Kalman filtering and improved MobileViT algorithm. This method introduces two core modules, Convolutional Pooling Downsampling (CMP) and Multi Branch Residual Feature Fusion (MR-FF), based on the original MobileViT architecture. The CMP module effectively reduces the number of model parameters and computational complexity, while the MR-FF module reduces feature redundancy and prevents the loss of key feature information through a multi branch feature fusion mechanism, significantly improving the robustness of the algorithm. The results indicate that our method can still maintain high displacement measurement precision under complex environmental conditions.

title: Research on the Evolution and Technological Innovations of 5G Cellular Network Architecture Based on Digital Signal Processing
authors: Zhenxuan Xu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: With the continuous development of 5G technology, cellular network architecture is undergoing unprecedented transformation, with digital signal processing (DSP) technology playing a crucial role. 5G networks have not only achieved significant improvements in transmission speed, latency, and reliability but also provide support for various emerging applications such as smart cities, industrial internet, and autonomous driving. This paper explores the evolution of 5G cellular network architecture based on digital signal processing and its technological innovations. It first analyzes the limitations of traditional cellular network architectures, then discusses the key applications of digital signal processing in 5G, including how advanced signal processing methods such as OFDM and MIMO technology enhance signal quality and network efficiency. Through an analysis of technological innovations in 5G networks, this paper also summarizes the challenges faced, such as spectrum resource management and network security issues. Finally, this paper looks ahead to the potential of digital signal processing in 5G and its subsequent development, and proposes directions for further research. Research indicates that digital signal processing technology will continue to drive the efficient evolution of 5G networks, providing strong support for future communications technology innovation.

title: Green AI: Energy - Aware Framework for Sustainable Machine Learning
authors: S. Raja, S. Balamurugan, S. Praveen Raj, A. Devaranjani, R. Gowsika, A. P. Janani
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The energy-aware deep-learning framework allows the training and evaluation of a variety of convolutional neural network architectures on standard datasets and the concurrent assessment of their environmental impacts. Through the Code Carbon toolkit, the energy consumption, power consumption, and CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emissions during training are measured in real-time, giving an accurate account of the total carbon footprint of each convolutional neural network model. The modular trainer built in Py Torch provides support for architectures such as Simple CNN, Tiny Model, and Res Net as well as detailed metrics of accuracy, training time, and evaluation by class. In addition to the training process, a complete Green AI Dashboard developed using Stream lit is available to visualize the accuracy and energy trade-offs, eco-scores, carbon efficiencies, and equivalents in terms of environmental impacts, allowing for the intelligent comparison and sustainable decision-making of these network models. The proposed framework provides both performance evaluation based on accuracy and energy use as well as environmental impact assessment, thus promoting the development of more energy-efficient and sustainable AI systems; the system encourages model developers to choose models that balance accuracy with computational cost-effectiveness and environmental sustainability.

title: Application of Nonlinear Equation System Solutions in Robot Arms
authors: Kerui Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: From initial laboratory explorations to large-scale applications, robots have not only achieved significant technological breakthroughs but have also demonstrated extensive application potential across various sectors of society. Among these advancements, the robotic arm plays a crucial role, necessitating in-depth analysis of both forward and inverse kinematics via solving nonlinear equations. Numerous methods exist for analyzing multi-degree-of-freedom robotic arms, including the construction of coordinate systems using Denavit-Hartenberg (D-H) notation [1]. This paper examines various solution methods for nonlinear equations in robotic arms kinematics, including Newton's method, the secant method, the parallel line method, and the homotopy method. Additionally, MATLAB is utilized for analysis to provide a clearer understanding of the specific steps, advantages, and disadvantages associated with each method. This study offers valuable insights into the application of robotic arms in solving systems of nonlinear equations.

title: Long-Horizon Set Prediction for Multi-Object Tracking via Temporal Instance Queries
authors: Haokun Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Multi-Object Tracking (MOT) faces significant challenges in maintaining consistent identities, particularly under occlusions, due to the reliance of conventional methods on short-term, hand-crafted association modules. While Transformer-based trackers integrate temporal context, they often suffer from a limited temporal horizon and instability caused by dynamic query propagation. To address these issues, we propose AssociaTR, an end-to-end Transformer framework that directly outputs complete object trajectories. Its core innovation is an instance-centric decoder, where each query is responsible for predicting the entire trajectory of a single object. This is enabled by a factorized attention module that enables longer temporal horizon and captures long-range dependencies. We also introduce a stable query propagation strategy that transform joint tracking-detection to pure object detection task for online data association, ensuring training stability and fixed inference complexity. On the MOT17 benchmark, AssociaTR demonstrates superior association capability, achieving an AssA of 63.4% and IDF1 of 74.7% with significantly reduced identity switches, validating its effectiveness for long-term association. The overall HOTA and MOTA are primarily limited by detection performance on small-scale datasets, highlighting the framework’s strength in identity preservation.

title: Data Privacy Protection and Security Access Control Mechanism in Cloud Computing Environment
authors: Xin Guo, Mingxing Zhu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: With the growth of cloud computing technology, users can use public computing resources to complete big data analysis tasks such as machine learning at low cost and high efficiency. However, while cloud computing provides users with convenient storage and sharing services, it also faces increasingly severe user data security issues. Although traditional ciphertext policy attribute based encryption schemes can provide fine-grained access control for data sharing services in cloud environments, the plaintext attributes contained in their access policies can easily lead to the leakage of privacy and sensitive information, making it difficult to meet the needs of modern data security. This article focuses on the research of data privacy protection and security access control mechanisms in cloud computing environments, and proposes a system architecture that integrates privacy protection and access control. The system consists of a data privacy management module, an access control module, a security authentication module, and an audit log module. Each module works together to effectively protect data privacy and finely manage access permissions. The results indicate that the system significantly improves the flexibility and efficiency of access control while ensuring data privacy and security.

title: Advanced Deep Learning Techniques for Detecting Anomalous Human Behavior Patterns
authors: K. Sumathi, V. Sharmila, Y. Rajendra Babu, L. Manjunath, S. P. Santhoshkumar, Saurabh Chandra
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The proliferation of security surveillance setups in both public and private spaces has made the need for advanced video analysis to have a clear edge in security and public safety. Detecting the abnormal human behavior on video streams, specifically in crowded locations due to the rare occurrence and unpredictable nature of such events (which usually appear as spatiotemporal deviation from normal activity), presents a challenging task. This paper proposes a deep learning based framework for a real-time abnormal behavior detection using a hybrid framework that combines 3D Convolutional Neural Networks and Temporal Transformers. The 3D CNN module captures the local spatiotemporal features between subsequent frames whereas the Temporal Transformer links the long-range temporal features to identify the minute behavioural anomalies. Compared to the classical object detection methods like YOLO, which mainly focuses on spatial characteristics, the above scheme can focus more on temporal modeling to accurately identify such complex and delayed abnormal behaviors as theft, attack, and traffic violation. Despite the continuous challenge in camera placement and scalability of this system, this paper demonstrate the effectiveness of this framework as the means to enable automated and context-aware anomaly detection for use in increasing public safety for video surveillance applications.

title: Lightweight Adaptive Differential Privacy for Federated Learning in Internet of Vehicles
authors: Shuju Yu, Yuchuan Fu, Changle Li, F. Richard Yu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: As the demand for data privacy and collaborative learning in the Internet of Vehicles becomes increasingly prominent, federated learning has emerged as a feasible distributed learning model. However, traditional federated learning faces high communication costs and weak privacy protection in the dynamic environment of the Internet of Vehicles. This paper proposes a lightweight federated differential privacy framework, which optimizes the aggregation strategy while introducing an adaptive noise injection mechanism and can protect user privacy while maintaining model performance. Experiments show that the proposed framework significantly reduces the success rate of member inference attacks on the NGSIM dataset while maintaining high prediction accuracy and convergence speed.

title: Design of Adaptive Impedance Control Algorithm for Underwater Manipulator Based on Environmental Interaction Modeling
authors: Xingchen Liu, Dong Zhang, Haonan Chen
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In this paper, an adaptive impedance control algorithm based on environmental interaction modeling is proposed to solve the problems of hydrodynamic interference, compliance interaction with seabed topography and sensor data noise faced by underwater manipulator in complex underwater environment. Firstly, combining the hydrodynamic theory with multi-sensor data, the interaction force between the manipulator and the environment is estimated in real time by using adaptive Kalman filter, and accurate environmental perception is realized. Then, a double-layer control architecture combining reinforcement learning (RL) and sliding mode control is designed. The upper RL strategy network dynamically adjusts impedance parameters according to environmental interaction information, and the lower sliding mode controller provides robust compensation to deal with system model uncertainty and external disturbance. The simulation and experimental results show that the algorithm has high contact force control accuracy, small overshoot and short stability time in the precise grasping task in still water environment. The trajectory tracking accuracy is high under the interference of constant water flow; The impedance parameters can be adjusted quickly and adaptively under the interference of pulsating water flow, showing good dynamic response and environmental adaptability; In the exploration of irregular terrain, it is flexible and adaptable to the unknown stiffness environment, which effectively improves the quality of underwater manipulator in many typical underwater tasks, including improving the success rate, shortening the average time, reducing the damage rate, etc., fully embodies its comprehensive advantages in control accuracy, dynamic adaptability and safety, and provides an effective method for intelligent and flexible control of underwater manipulator.

title: Performance Evaluation of Ensemble Machine Learning Techniques for Gold Silver Price Forecasting
authors: Mahendra Kumar Gourisaria, Aadi Poddar, Debolina Ghosh, Sonal Jain, Parthasarathi Pattnayak, Saurabh Bilgaiyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Gold and Silver Price Prediction has been a concern for many financial analytics and business owners. This becomes critically important due to the volatile nature of the precious metals. This study focuses on daily gold and silver price movements over the period 2013–2023. It engineers 74 technical indicators and time-based features using a data-driven forecasting approach while preventing data leakage by forecasting next-day closing prices. It uses eight machine learning models that were trained on the dataset: Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, Gradient Boosting, Ridge Regression, ElasticNet, AdaBoost, and Extra Trees. It was found out that linear regularized models, particularly ElasticNet and Ridge Regression, achieved better performance with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> scores of 0.966 and 0.965 for gold, and 0.957 and 0.954 for silver respectively. Tree-based ensemble methods underperformed, with Random Forest achieving R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.928 for gold and XGBoost reaching 0.889 for silver. They were calculated on evaluation metrices like RMSE, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and MAPE.

title: Embedded Vision System Using YOLOv8 on Jetson Orin NX
authors: Ahlem Maghzaoui, Emna Aridhi, Sadok Ben Yahia, Abdel Kader Mami
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Date-palm pruning is a hazardous and laborintensive task performed at heights among sharp fronds. This paper presents an embedded vision system to automate datepalm branch pruning by detecting branches and localizing precise cutting points using deep learning on an edge device. We propose a two-stage detect-to-cut pipeline: a YOLOv8 object detector identifies palm branches, and a YOLOv8-Pose model predicts keypoints corresponding to optimal cut locations. Both models are optimized for deployment on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX, enabling real-time inference on the edge. In experiments, the system achieves high accuracy (branch detection mAP@0.5 of 93.58%, and cut-point PCK@0.2 of 79.9%) while maintaining an inference speed of 5.56 FPS on the embedded device. The results demonstrate a viable solution for precision robotic pruning, combining state-of-the-art vision algorithms with efficient edge computing. The proposed system could improve safety and efficiency in agricultural robotics by enabling autonomous pruning of date palms in field conditions.

title: Integration of Computer Vision and Machine Learning Technologies for Accurate Classification of Objects in Images
authors: Karimov Sardor Ilkhom Ugli, Kholiknazarov Rashidjon Khomidjonovich, Ergashev Otabek Mirzapo’Latovich
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: This paper presents an in-depth analysis of modern object-based image classification methods based on the integration of VGG19, U-Net, and MobileNet deep learning architectures. Particular attention is paid to a comparative study of their structural and functional characteristics: the high ability to extract informative features in VGG19, the effectiveness of segmentation analysis in U-Net, and the computational compactness and energy efficiency of MobileNet. A comprehensive experiment was conducted on open datasets using key quality assessment metrics—Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-score. The results of the analysis confirmed that the combined use of these architectures provides an optimal balance between classification accuracy, processing speed, and computational performance.

title: A Lightweight Ship Object Detection Algorithm Based on Improved YOLOv5
authors: Houda Lu, Miyuan Zhang, Lin Su, Jia Tian, Chengcheng Han, Yuwen Tan
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of limited computing power of the platform and high requirements for model lightweight in the scenario of UAV-based maritime ship detection, this paper proposes a lightweight ship target detection algorithm fused with YOLOv5s and EfficientVit. Taking YOLOv5s as the basic framework, the algorithm introduces the lightweight feature extraction module of EfficientVit, which significantly reduces the model parameters and computational complexity while ensuring detection accuracy. Trained and validated on the public Seaships7000 ship dataset, the experimental results show that compared with the original YOLOv5s, the improved YOLOv5s-EfficientVit model maintains basically the same mAP@.5 index (0.989 vs 0.992), while the number of parameters decreases from 15.8×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sup>to7.89×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sup>, and the computational complexity (GFLOPs) reduces from 7.01 to 3.78, achieving a remarkable lightweight effect. This model provides a new solution for applications on low-computing-power platforms such as UAV maritime ship detection, and has good practical application value and versatility.

title: Application of AI-driven heterogeneous graph neural network in visual design element association mining and intelligent recommendation
authors: Hefan Fan
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Recommendation of visual design elements is crucial for enhancing design efficiency in fields such as advertising and UI/UX. However, existing methods face multiple limitations, including challenges in matching heterogeneous design elements, suboptimal association mining, and severe cold-start issues. This paper proposes an AI-driven heterogeneous graph neural network (HGNN) visual design element recommendation scheme integrated with computer vision technology to address these problems. The core innovations include: 1) A cross-modal heterogeneous node mapping module that combines visual and semantic features to unify the feature spaces of multi-type design elements (graphics, colors, fonts) and their attributes (style, purpose, scene). The adaptive association mining method employs a heterogeneous relationship attention mechanism to dynamically learn semantic and visual associations between elements. 3) A multi-granularity recommendation framework that balances accuracy and diversity. Experimental results from the public DesignNet dataset and real-world design platforms demonstrate that the proposed model improves Precision@10, Recall@10, and F1@10 metrics by 15.3%,18.7%, and 2.1% respectively compared to baseline methods. This research provides designers with an efficient intelligent tool, enabling the visual design workflow to evolve toward smarter automation.

title: Detection and Localization of Open-Circuit Fault in Two-Level Inverters Feeding PMSM
authors: Sara Misaghi, Hadi Afsharirad
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This paper introduces an innovative approach for detecting open-circuit failures (OCFs) in the Voltage Source Inverter (VSI) of a Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) drive. The system is modeled using the Mixed Logical Dynamic (MLD) framework, and fault detection is performed by analyzing the Current Residual Vector (CRV) and examining its positive and negative sequence components. A fault is identified when the CRV magnitude surpasses a specified criterion, and the faulty switch is determined according to the angular sector of the CRV. The proposed approach, due to its model-based nature, operates independently of the main controller, making it suitable for closed-loop systems. By monitoring the deviations between estimated and measured currents in the positive and negative sequences, the method is capable of accurately find and identify both single- and multiswitch faults. The validity of the proposed method has been confirmed through comprehensive MATLAB/Simulink simulations.

title: Neuro-Adaptive Firewalls: Integrating Deep Reinforcement Learning for Predictive Network Defence
authors: Jayabratha.S, T.Siva Kumar, K.Geethadevi, V.Jamuna, V.Kumararaja, Vikram N
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Conventional firewalls are based on fixed rules and signature recognition hence cannot resist dynamic and emerging cyber attacks. The proposed paper discusses Neuro-Adaptive Firewalls (NAF) an intelligent defence framework, which utilises Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL). The suggested system constantly gets knowledge about the network traffic, autonomously modifying its filtering rules to approximate and prevent the possible attacks before they take place. The NAF uses the model to dynamically respond to unknown threats with minimum human operator involvement by modelling network defence as a sequential decision-making problem. Simulations of the experiments prove that the accuracy of detection and the number of false positives are lower and better than those of traditional methods, which prove its feasibility in real-time and predictive network defence in large and heterogeneous environments.

title: Design and Implementation of a Mobile-Based Sun Outage Forecast System for Geostationary Satellite Communications
authors: Bo Zhao, Chuankai Cui, Ying Chen, Peng Wang, Jun Peng, Yi Dun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Geostationary satellite communication systems are vulnerable to sun outage interference around the spring and autumn equinoxes, causing link degradation or service interruption. This paper presents a mobile forecast solution for operations personnel. We develop an angular separation model between the Sun and the main lobe of the ground station antenna, and integrate it with a coarse-to-fine search strategy. The system runs on Android and provides configurable, visual sun outage predictions. Experiments show that the system completes full-year forecasts in seconds on typical mobile devices. Results align closely with published sun outage calendars. The approach offers practical value and supports operational decision-making in satellite communications.

title: Seasonal IoT-GNN: A Conceptual Framework for Context-Aware Handicraft Recommendation in Tourism Environments
authors: Khadija Kassabi, Fahd Kalloubi, Abdelhak Boulaalam
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: The handicraft sector represents both an economic pillar and a cultural heritage asset in tourism-dependent regions such as Morocco. While Internet of Things (IoT) technologies have been deployed for product traceability and authenticity, their potential for driving personalized, season-aware recommendations remains underexplored. This paper introduces Seasonal IoT-GNN, a conceptual framework that combines IoT-driven contextual data acquisition, graph-based modeling of heterogeneous relationships, and seasonal adaptation to improve handicraft product recommendations in tourism markets. The framework is presented as a layered architecture, supported by a workflow that spans data acquisition, preprocessing, seasonal segmentation, and recommendation delivery. Illustrative scenarios are discussed, highlighting the relevance of the approach in contexts such as the Medina of Fez, Ramadan markets, and summer festivals. Beyond its cultural and economic contributions, the framework opens new research avenues in Big Data integration, Explainable AI, and blockchain-enabled trust mechanisms, positioning Seasonal IoT-GNN as a promising step toward culturally sensitive, technology-driven smart tourism.

title: The Role of Intelligent Data Analysis Algorithms in Enhancing the Efficiency of Information Technologies and Automation
authors: Sadirova Khursanoy, Shakhrizod Ganiyeva, Khusanova Mokhira
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: In an era where the world is drowning in data, the real challenge isn’t just collecting information — it’s actually making it useful. This paper dives into how intelligent data analysis acts as the engine behind modern automation and IT efficiency. We look at Data Mining not just as a technical chore, but as the “Knowledge Discovery” process (KDD) that unearths the hidden patterns and trends buried in massive datasets. By using smart tools like the Levenshtein method and other advanced algorithms, we can transform raw, messy “Big Data” into clear, actionable insights for better decision-making. Ultimately, this study shows that integrating these intelligent algorithms isn’t just an upgrade; it’s a necessity. By doing so, organizations can build systems that are more accurate, productive, and adaptable. As we move further into the digital age, these analytical techniques will be the cornerstone of the next major leap in automation and industrial efficiency.

title: Research on Trajectory Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles Based on an Improved CNN and Bidirectional LSTM Model
authors: Jiayue Huang, Dongyan Ren
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional trajectory prediction models in complex traffic scenarios, such as insufficient spatiotemporal feature capture and the difficulty in balancing prediction accuracy with real-time performance, this study proposes a hybrid "Improved CNN-Bidirectional LSTM" prediction model. The model strengthens spatial feature extraction capabilities through an improved Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), captures long-term temporal motion dependencies using a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) network, and incorporates vehicle dynamics constraints to ensure physical plausibility of trajectories. Experiments demonstrate that the model achieves an Average Displacement Error (ADE) of 0.44 m and a Final Displacement Error (FDE) of 0.58 m, with an inference speed of 32 frames per second (FPS). The success rate for collision-free obstacle avoidance remains stable between 90% and 95%. The model outperforms traditional methods in accuracy, real-time capability, and robustness, providing reliable support for the practical implementation of autonomous driving trajectory prediction technology.

title: Adaptive Real-Time Intrusion Prevention Framework using Double DQN and Prioritized Experience Replay on Live Network Traffic Streams
authors: Vinay Mallikarjunaradhya, Tapankumar A. Kakani, Deepak Wahane, Nasmin Jiwani, Mohd Arshad
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The study proposes an Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) in real-time that exploits a Reinforcement Learning framework grounded in Double Deep Q-Networks (Double DQN) coupled with Prioritized Experience Replay to address the weaknesses of conventional static and signature-based security implementation. The proposed model is dynamically learned to attain the best preventive measures through continuous interaction with live traffic streams over the network and therefore, it makes adaptive and intelligent decisions. The system has shown a remarkable performance at the UNSW-NB15 dataset with good detection performances of 97% and a big lowering of the false-positive rates. It is designed such that it can quickly infer with low latency, and operate in real-time in high-speed and high-scale network conditions. The strength, stability and efficiency of the presented IPS are tested through extensive experimentation, such as classification measures, ROC-AUC analysis, convergence performance, latency performance and throughput performance. This paper proves the concept of Double DQN with Prioritized Replay and makes it a strong, scalable, and state-of-the-art solution to proactive countering of cyberattacks in contemporary network systems.

title: Research on Network Traffic Protocol Classification Based on an Improved Random Forest Model
authors: Yuyue Yan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Addressing protocol classification demands amid escalating network traffic volumes, conventional detection methods exhibit limited accuracy in encrypted traffic and categorically imbalanced datasets. Conventional random forest models struggle to precisely identify niche protocols due to data preprocessing deficiencies and category imbalance. This paper proposes an enhanced random forest model based on 394,137 real-world network traffic records: A dynamic balanced sampling strategy is introduced, adaptively setting the SMOTE oversampling neighbour count. Combined with random undersampling, this constructs a fully balanced training set, resolving the scarcity of minority protocol samples. A three-stage dynamic monitoring mechanism is designed to track, in real-time, the category distribution bias in the sub-training set, the concentration of top-level split features in the base decision tree, and the prediction performance on out-of-bag samples, thereby safeguarding the model's generalisation capability. Experimental results demonstrate that the improved model achieves macro-average precision (+27.2%), macro-average recall (+38.8%), and macro-average F1 score (+31.7%) over the baseline model, with niche protocol identification recall exceeding 40% improvement. This research provides a solution for real-time classification of large-scale network traffic and establishes a technical framework for protocol classification under imbalanced data conditions.

title: Simscape-Based Modeling and Analysis of a Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor and Its Control System
authors: Sun Chang, Fu Chao
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address issues such as complex configuration and high computational demands in co-simulation approaches that integrate Simulink-based control strategies with external permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) and actuator physical models, this paper proposes an integrated modeling method for PMSM and its control system based on Simscape. Targeting the operational requirements of an electronically controlled torque vectoring transfer case, a Simscape-based vector control model for the PMSM and a model of the transfer case system are developed. These models are seamlessly integrated with the physical plant model within the MATLAB/Simulink environment for co-simulation. Validation results demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively replace existing co-simulation processes, enabling rapid development and verification of control system functions without relying on physical prototypes, while eliminating the need for third-party software and complex interface configuration.

title: Simulation of Heating Performance in Electric Light Trucks Using AMESim
authors: Fancong Zeng, Rui Chen, Jiwen Cai, Qishen Yang, Jun Xiao, Fengyun Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: To address the issues of high heating energy consumption and severe range degradation in electric light trucks under low-temperature conditions, this paper proposes an integrated vehicle thermal management system architecture featuring heat pump technology with waste heat recovery functionality. Utilizing the AMESim platform, a one-dimensional vehicle thermal management model was established for both a pure PTC heating system and a heat pump waste heat recovery system. In order to ascertain the most efficient heating performance, system energy efficiency, and overall vehicle energy consumption, comparative analyses were conducted on the vehicles under dual operating conditions at -5°C. The findings of the simulation indicate that, whilst satisfying the heating requirements of the cabin and power battery, the heat pump with waste heat recovery considerably improves the overall efficiency of the system. The Coefficient of Performance (COP) has been shown to increase by 41.2%, whilst total heating energy consumption has been demonstrated to decrease by 12.97%.

title: AI-Enabled Cyber Incident Management System: APK Analysis, Phishing Detection, and Cyber Threat Reporting
authors: A.Abubakkar Siddiq, M.Gokul Sanjay, S.Gowdish, R.Sathya, K.Muthumanickam
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The rapid expansion of digital platforms has led to a significant increase in cyber incidents, including phishing attacks, malicious Android applications, and manipulated multimedia content, while existing cyber complaint portals largely rely on manual reporting and lack intelligent threat analysis and real-time guidance. This paper presents a unified AI-enabled cybersecurity portal designed for secure cyber incident reporting and automated threat assessment. The system employs a hybrid RSA–AES encryption framework with JWT-based session management and role-based access control to ensure confidentiality, integrity, and controlled access in accordance with the CIA triad, with all complaint records and digital evidence stored exclusively in encrypted form. Analytical modules are integrated for URL phishing detection, APK malware analysis, and CNN-based image and video forensics, enabling multimodal identification and severity classification of cyber threats. A centralized administrative dashboard supports structured incident handling through severity-level prioritization, while a guided security playbook provides users with contextual mitigation and awareness recommendations during complaint registration. By combining secure data handling, multimodal AI-driven analysis, and intelligent case management within a single platform, the proposed system enhances response efficiency, supports informed decision-making for authorities, and improves user-level cybersecurity awareness.

title: High-Gain 2×1 Rectangular Microstrip Patch Array with Double-Reflector DGS for Long-Range (LoRa) Communication
authors: Ilhami Pramaditya, Suthami Ariessaputra, Abdullah Zainuddin, Cahyo Mustiko Okta Muvianto, Sareh Malekpour
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)
abstract: Communication technology is developing rapidly, including the use of Internet of Things (IoT) technology for long-distance communication. One of the IoT modules currently being developed is the LoRa (Long Range) module, which requires an antenna to transmit data to the receiver because it uses radio waves. Gain is one indicator of an antenna's ability to operate optimally and extend its range, including for LoRa communication. Microstrip patch antennas are one of many types of antennas that have undergone significant Development. This antenna has relatively low gain, so modifications are needed to increase its gain. The use of an array antenna and a double Defective Ground Structure (DGS) reflector layer is an option for optimising microstrip antennas. This antenna configuration integrates a square DGS structure into the first reflector and uses the patch's conjugate for the second reflector. Based on the measurement result, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 1$</tex> Rectangular Microstrip Patch Array antenna operates at 915 MHz, with a gain of 4.96 dBi, a return loss of −20.59 dB, a VSWR of 1.21, and a bandwidth of 20 MHz. The resulting radiation pattern is directional.

title: Structure Learning of Time Series Chain Graph from Multivariate Time Series Data
authors: Huyang Xu, Yuanchen Fang, Nasser Fard, Yingju Pei
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Time series chain graphs are a class of probabilistic graphical models that incorporate both directed and undirected edges, extending their application to the temporal domain. This representation allows for the modeling of causal relationships and statistical dependencies among variables, as well as contemporaneous associations and sequential influences, making it a powerful tool for analyzing complex dynamic systems. Despite the promise of this approach, structure learning for time series chain graphs from dataset has yet to be fully developed. In this paper, we demonstrate that time series chain graphs satisfy the Andersson-Madigan-Perlman Markov property, and we propose a generic structure learning algorithm for these graphs based on Markov blanket discovery. Experimental results on synthetic datasets, generated from representative time series chain graph structures, indicate that the proposed algorithm effectively learns the underlying structure of time series chain graphs from high-dimensional multivariate time series data.

title: SOC-SOH Estimation Using Dual EKF Adaptive to Measurement Noise Covariance
authors: Bhagat Ram Labana, Kranthi K. Nidubrolu
abstract: Accurate estimation of the State of Charge (SOC) and State of Health (SOH) is critical for the safe and efficient operation of lithium ion batteries in electric vehicles and energy storage systems. However, measurement inaccuracies and noise degrade the accuracy of the estimate, especially under dynamic load conditions. This paper proposes a Dual Extended Kalman Filter (DEKF)-based approach that adaptively adjusts the Kalman gain based on the measured load current magnitude. The algorithm decouples SOC and SOH, allowing independent tuning of the estimation processes into two interacting filters and real-time correction of the model parameters. The simulation results under Hybrid Pulse Power Characterization(HPPC) and Randomized World Motorcycle Test Cycle(WMTC) driving profiles and noise scenarios demonstrate that the proposed method improves the estimation accuracy by reducing the SOC error below 2 % and the SOH drift below 2 %, even with high sensor noise (up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm \mathbf{5 m V}$</tex> voltage and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm \mathbf{1 \%}$</tex> current errors at the cell level). The proposed DEKF framework is computationally efficient and suitable for real-time on-board implementation.

title: Advancements in Gastric Cancer Detection: A Comprehensive Review of CNN-based Approaches
authors: Darsh Patel, Gordhan Jethava, Ankita Gandhi
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Gastric cancer is among the most common health conditions on earth and the highest rate of mortality among the causes of cancer. The early and accurate diagnosis plays a big role in increasing patient outcomes and the conventional diagnostic methods, such as endoscopy and biopsy are invasive, time consuming and may give room to the error of human beings. During the past several years, deep learning, and CNNs, in particular, have proven to be an efficient tool of medical image analysis in the framework of computer-aided diagnosis. The most recent trends in CNN-based gastric cancer detectors, segmenters and classifiers are critically summarized and categorized in this review. The article provides a review on 18 representative studies addressing the 4 classification of methodology that are CNN-based classification, segmentation-based approaches, hybrid approach, and ensemble approaches and Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)-based augmentation approaches. The comparative performance analysis shows that there has been a significant advancement in the accuracy of the diagnosis, yet there are still the issues of the low diversity of datasets, the high cost of computation, and the artificially low generalization. Integration of explainable AI, multi-mode imaging, lightweight architecture to be run in real-time, and large-scale clinical validation are the directions of research in the future. This survey is a generalized opinion of the current and the future state of detecting gastric cancer through application of deep learning.

title: AI-based Multi-Class Detection of Fetal Brain Abnormalities in Prenatal Imaging
authors: Aswin Kumar K, Helrenraj A, Abdhur Rahman A
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Early and accurate screening of both structural and congenital brain malformations is quite useful in the neurodevelopment and clinical intervention management. Other disorders include Arnold-Chiari malformation, arachnoid cysts, cerebellar hypoplasia, cisterna magna abnormalities, colpocephaly, encephalocele, holoprosencephaly, hydranencephaly, intracranial hemorrhage, intracranial tumors and the extent of ventriculomegaly which are quite hard to discover, particularly when a manual examination is done on the neuroimaging data. The proposed paper recommends an AI-powered, real-time, and diagnostic system in the shape of the YOLOv12 deep learning architecture integrated into a Flask-based web application. It is based on a model of the object detector called YOLOv12, which is used to localize brain abnormalities of prenatal and postnatal ultrasound or MRI images in the most fast and accurate way possible. YOLOv12 improves the model by introducing the following features: the features and attention modules are better represented, which enables high-quality classification of non-reliable and overlapping anomalies such as polencephaly or severe ventriculomegaly. The model is trained using a chosen set of images that are optimized using such metrics as mean Average Precision (mAP), precision, and recall. Its interface is a friendly web-based interface of Flask that allows clinicians to upload images of diagnostic imaging that are real-time processed. The system will give annotated images of image conditions identified, bounding boxes and confidence scores. It is a scalable, fast, and interpretable solution to the issue of assisting radiologists and neurologists in screening and early detection of severe brain abnormalities, that will be utilized to enhance the patient outcomes and efficiency of the entire clinic system.

title: Diagnosing Partial Demagnetization and Static Eccentricity in Surface Mounted Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor
authors: Mahdi Nikzad, Zahra Nasiri-Gheidari
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Fault diagnosis in Surface-Mounted Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (SPMSMs) is crucial for ensuring operational reliability in industrial and transportation systems. Among various failure modes, demagnetization and static eccentricity faults are two of the most common and potentially destructive conditions. Both faults distort the symmetry of the air-gap magnetic field, generating similar harmonic components in the electrical signals, which makes their discrimination challenging. This paper presents a comparative analysis of these fault types using three-dimensional finite element simulations. In the demagnetization fault model, the residual flux density of one rotor magnet is reduced by different levels, while in the static eccentricity model, the air-gap length is varied to represent different eccentricity severities. Stator current waveforms were obtained for both healthy and faulty conditions and analyzed using the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) to extract harmonic signatures and calculate the Total Harmonic Distortion (THD). The results show that while THD can indicate the occurrence of a fault, it cannot distinguish between the two fault types. To differentiate them, the 5th-to-3rd harmonic amplitude ratio should be used as a diagnostic indicator.

title: Research on a Precision-Intelligent Control Algorithm of Targeted Optogenetics for Calcium Imaging Image Calibration
authors: Jintao Zhang, Jiawei Li, Zhi Li, Jiayu Qiu, Yuxiao Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: In neuroscience research, calcium imaging technology can monitor neuronal activity in real time, but the image is easily disturbed by various factors, which affects the accuracy of subsequent analysis. This paper proposes a targeted optogenetics precision intelligent control algorithm for calcium imaging image calibration. The algorithm first preprocesses the original calcium imaging image, and adopts an improved non-rigid motion correction method based on local feature matching and deformation model to effectively correct the non-rigid displacement of the image and improve image stability. In terms of targeted optogenetics control, by constructing a deep neural network model and combining optogenetics experimental data, the complex mapping relationship between neuronal activity and light stimulation parameters is learned. The model is used to predict and optimize light stimulation parameters to achieve precise control of specific neuronal populations. Experimental simulation results show that on the standard calcium imaging dataset, the image calibration accuracy of the proposed algorithm is improved by 15% compared with the traditional NoRMCorre algorithm, and the image correlation coefficient after motion correction reaches an average of 0.92. In the targeted optogenetics control experiment, the light stimulation accuracy can be controlled within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>, which is 30% higher than the existing method, effectively enhancing the ability to control neuronal activity. This algorithm provides a more efficient and accurate tool for calcium imaging image analysis and targeted optogenetic research, which helps to deeply understand the function of neural circuits and the mechanism of nervous system diseases.

title: Breakdown of a Bit Commitment Protocol
authors: Anshul Singhal, Janis Nötzel
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Quantum Bit Commitment (QBC) is a foundational primitive in quantum cryptography, intended to enable secure commitments with strong information-theoretic guarantees. The protocol described in ‘Unconditionally secure multi-party quantum commitment scheme’, [1] published in Quantum information processing, 2018 Springer, uses coherent-state encoding and claims statistical hiding and computational binding. In this work, we identify a fundamental vulnerability in the hiding property of the protocol. Specifically, we demonstrate that an adversarial receiver applies Hadamard transformation to the incoming states, effectively changing the basis to enhance distinguishability between the two received messages. This linear optical strategy enables partial extraction of message without the use of quantum storage or entangled resources. We formally analyze the structure of the protocol and quantify the information leakage by computing the distinguishability between quantum state ensembles associated with different commitments. Our findings indicate that the protocol fails to maintain statistical hiding under general quantum adversarial models, raising concerns about its applicability in settings requiring strong concealment guarantees.

title: An Iterative Refinement Framework for Classical Chinese Poetry via Knowledge Graph and Sentiment Fusion
authors: Haolu Yu, Yonghua Zhu, Yilian Wu, Changlin Song, Xinglong Song
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Addressing the limitations of existing automatic generation models in Classical Chinese Poetry concerning strict metrical norms, semantic coherence, and the capacity for rhetorical refinement, this paper proposes an Iterative Refinement Framework for classical Chinese verse. The main contributions are:(1) Fine-grained Detection: A BERT-BiLSTM-CRF mechanism identifies "Wrong/Mismatched Words." (2) Dual Knowledge Enhancement: Re-generation fuses Knowledge Graph concepts and Affective Embeddings using soft/hard masking for targeted repair. (3) Iterative Loop: A "detection-masking-correction" cycle simulates human poetic 'Tui-Qiao' (deliberative polishing) until quality standards are met. Experiments show this method significantly outperforms baselines and LLMs (e.g., GPT-4) in metrical accuracy, semantic consistency, and overall artistry.

title: From Competency to Curiosity: Revolutionalizing IT Work
authors: Mo Nishiyama
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 ACM SIGUCCS Annual Conference
abstract: None

title: Attention-Emotion Assessment of ASD Children via Representation Learning based on Cross-Modal Disentanglement and Attention Alignment_supp1-3676489.pdf
authors: Dan Chen
abstract: Comprehensive cognitive-affective profiling for precision ASD assessment is hindered by inadequate computational modeling of emotion-behavior dynamics. This gap persists due to limited methods for reconciling EEG, eye-tracking, and facial expression modalities-critical for capturing ASD heterogeneity drivers. This study proposes the Cognitive-Affective Ability Assessment (CA3) framework, focusing on attention control in conjunction with valence-arousal, to address these limitations through: (1) Ability-Specific Neural Disentanglement extracting attention- and emotion-specific representations from EEG using ET and facial expression recordings as anchors; (2) Symmetrical Cross-Ability Alignment modeling contextual dependencies via a symmetric cross-attention mechanism; and (3) Uncertainty-Aware Ability Integration fusing per-ability predictions using Dirichlet modeling and Dempster-Shafer theory while quantifying confidence. Extensive experiments have been conducted to evaluate CA$^{3}$ vs. state-of-the-arts counterparts on the multimodel datasets (EEG, ET and facial expression recordings) with CCNU (27 ASD vs. 30 typically developing (TD) children) and BNU (49 ASD vs. 48 TD), and the results demonstrate that: 1) In the ASD cognitive-affective abilities assessment task, accuracy is improved by 2.7% for attention control and 2.5% for emotion perception, and by 3.5% for cognitive-affective abilities assessment, compared with current state-of-the-art methods. 2) For attention control and valence-arousal, composite ability gaps are measured between ASD and typically developing children: 15.6% and 19.9% for mild ASD, 28.2% and 39.1% for moderate ASD, and 44.4% and 61.3% for severe ASD, respectively. Overall, the framework effectively bridges computational assessment with clinically actionable insights, enabling robust decision-making through personalized cognitive-affective ASD profiles.

title: Geometry-Aware 3D Gaussian Representation for Real-Time Rendering of Large-Scale Scenes
authors: Haihong Xiao, Jianan Zou, Shuai Xing, Pengcheng Li, Wenxiong Kang Kang
abstract: Existing NeRF-based methods for reconstructing large-scale scenes face challenges in visual quality and rendering speed due to spectral biases and extensive sampling requirements. Recent 3DGS-based methods for real-time rendering of 3D objects and small scenes outperform NeRF, but several issues persist when extending these techniques to large-scale scenes. These include robust rendering in weak-texture areas, effective densification under memory constraints, finer detail rendering, and achieving natural lighting transitions. To address these, we introduce a geometry-aware 3DGS method for efficient real-time rendering of large scenes. First, we propose a geometry-guided anchor point initialization that reduces noise away from structural surfaces and generates new points in weak texture areas, particularly for large-scale datasets. We also present a structure-aware joint densification strategy combining surface- and curvature-based densification, ensuring Gaussian points are near structural surfaces and increasing density in low-curvature areas. Additionally, we propose a hash grid-assisted, viewpoint-sensitive feature enhancement scheme to improve detail rendering and natural lighting transitions. Our method achieves superior rendering quality compared to state-of-the-art methods while maintaining reasonable memory usage. Extensive experiments across 16 scenes, including 11 from five public datasets (MatrixCity-Aerial, Mill-19, Tanks & Temples, WHU, and UrbanScene3D) and five self-collected scenes from SCUT-CA and plateau regions, demonstrate its generalization capability. Codes are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/SCUT-BIP-Lab/Geo_gs</uri>.

title: Clinical Metadata Guided Limited-Angle CT Image Reconstruction
authors: Yu Shi, Shuyi Fan, Changsheng Fang, Shuo Han, Haodong Li, Li Zhou, Bahareh Morovati, Dayang Wang, Hengyong Yu
abstract: Limited-angle computed tomography (LACT) improves temporal resolution and reduces radiation dose, but suffers from severe artifacts due to missing projections. Clinical workflows record abundant patient- and acquisition-level metadata, yet such information remains underutilized in image reconstruction. To tackle the ill-posed LACT inverse problem, we propose a metadata-guided two-stage diffusion framework that leverages structured clinical contexts as semantic priors for robust reconstruction. In Stage-I, we learn a metadata-to-anatomy generative prior by conditioning a transformer-based diffusion model on clinical metadata (acquisition parameters, patient demographics, and diagnostic impressions), and sampling a coarse anatomical estimate from Gaussian noise. In Stage-II, a second conditional diffusion model performs coarse-to-fine refinement, using the Stage-I estimate as an image prior while re-injecting the same metadata to recover full-resolution anatomy. To preserve anatomical fidelity and suppress hallucinations, projection-domain data consistency is enforced periodically after denoising update via an ADMM-based solver. Experiments on the public multimodal CTRATE dataset demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms iterative, CNN-based, and diffusion-based baselines, with the greatest gains under severe truncation, e.g., up to 5.23%/11.21% higher SSIM/PSNR than the strongest metadata-free diffusion competitor at 90°. On real clinical cardiac CT, it yields coronary artery calcium scores closer to full-view references, indicating improved clinical utility. Furthermore, the proposed method is generalized to out-of-distribution angular ranges and projection geometries, and ablation results confirm complementary contributions from different metadata types under limited-angle conditions. Our results highlight clinical metadata as actionable semantic priors to synergize with physics-informed constraints to improve both reconstruction fidelity and clinical quantification in LACT.

title: Secure Control Information Transmission via RSMA for Low-Altitude Economy Networks
authors: Zhaoxin Feng, Huabing Lu, Weidang Lu, Zhaoyuan Shi, Nan Zhao, Dusit Niyato
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) have been applied to various tasks in the low-altitude economy (LAE) with the advantages of high mobility, low costs, and flexible deployment. However, due to the broadcast nature of wireless channels and the increasing number of UAVs, the security of UAV control information and the spectrum resource utilization face significant challenges and threats. Therefore, in this paper, we investigate the secrecy performance of UAV short-packet control information transmission networks based on rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA) with the presence of multiple eavesdroppers. Moreover, we consider and analyze the impacts of both imperfect channel state information (CSI) and successive interference cancellation (SIC) in a more realistic scenario. Considering both large-scale fading and Nakagami-m small-scale fading, the closed-form expression of the average effective secrecy sum rate is derived utilizing stochastic geometry and the Gauss-Chebyshev quadrature. Considering that the private stream can be concealed within the high-power common stream, an optimization problem is formulated to maximize the common rate by jointly optimizing the blocklength and power allocation coefficients to enhance security. The block coordinate descent (BCD) algorithm is adopted to solve this problem. Finally, simulation results demonstrate the accuracy of the analysis and the effectiveness of the proposed scheme.

title: Perceiving Weight in Augmented Reality using a Wearable Device with Minimal Haptic Feedback_supp2-3677013.mp4
authors: Tiange Zhang

title: Optimal Quantum Variational Circuits for Detecting False Data Injection Attacks on Transmission Substations
authors: Taha Hammadia, Ahmad Mohammad Saber, Marthe Kassouf, Deepa Kundur, Mohsen Khalaf
abstract: In digital substations, protective relays receive their measurements over vulnerable communication channels, which makes them susceptible to cyberattacks against substation operations. This paper introduces a novel Quantum Intrusion Detection System (QIDS) for detecting False Data Injection Attacks (FDIAs) against power transformer differential relays in digital substations. These FDIAS can manipulate the targeted relay's measurements, aiming to falsely trip the power transformer protected by the relay. The proposed QIDS leverages Quantum Variational Circuits (QVCs), a quantum machine learning circuit architecture with trainable parameters, that can be trained offline to distinguish manipulated relay measurements from those arising from faults. To optimize the QIDS's performance, a Hyper-Parameter Optimization (HPO) procedure, Tree-Structured Parzen Estimator, is utilized in this paper. We test the performance of the optimized QIDS under a comprehensive set of FDIA and fault scenarios generated in an OPAL-RT HYPERSIM environment. Our results confirm that the proposed QIDS can accurately detect malicious FDIA values without compromising the relay's protective dependability. The performance of the proposed QIDS is also evaluated under measurement noise, measurement errors, sophisticated attacks, and varying measurement window sizes and sampling rates, and is further compared against existing methods. Finally, the proposed solution is implemented in an online Hardware-in-the-Loop testbed, illustrating the practicality of the solution.

title: Weather Forecasting using Supervised Learning Techniques LSTM and XGBoost
authors: Pranali Kosamkar, Shambhavee Gune
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Weather prediction is a crucial aspect in many fields. It impacts the decisions made in day-to-day life. From farming to aviation, it is important to take into consideration meteorological factors and insights for accurate weather forecasting. Currently, AI, ML, and DL models are used alongside the traditional NWP (Numerical Weather Prediction), in order to increase the accuracy of prediction. Traditional NWP exhibits a low accuracy as compared to models with ML integration (0.1% increase in accuracy). This paper focuses on implementation of Deep Learning Model – LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) with different number of layers (1, 5, 10, 15) and Machine Learning Model - XGBoost to forecast certain factors of overall weather. Also, 2 different optimizers and activation functions (Adam, SGD, tanh, ReLu) are implemented in order to achieve a clear insight on which variation of LSTM yields more accuracy. It is observed that XGBoost (~73%) and one certain variation of LSTM (~72%) give the maximum accuracy. Even though many models are implemented till date for accurate weather forecasting, further research is required to analyze and create models that give high accuracy and perfect predictions.

title: Leveraging Social Interaction: Stroke Rehabilitation Using Extended Reality
authors: Bernardo Marques, Sérgio Oliveira, Inês Figueiredo, Paula Amorim, Mariana Leite, Carlos Ferreira, Samuel Silva, David Palricas, Paulo Dias, Beatriz Sousa Santos
abstract: Stroke survivors are often left with physical and cognitive limitations. Effective rehabilitation is essential to promote functional recovery and improve quality of life. However, existing approaches are limited by their repetitive/monotonous nature, which can lead to decreased motivation, emotional disengagement, and depression, as well as a lack of personalization and little to no social interaction. A five-month longitudinal study conducted at a rehabilitation center, employing a virtual reality supermarket, led to the creation of a research line focused on social interaction. Building on this, a broader extended reality rehabilitation framework was introduced, aimed at fostering motivation through multiuser experiences. We report initial results on healthcare professionals’ and stroke survivors’ acceptance, and explore the impact of collaborative versus competitive dynamics. Finally, we discuss the promising role of augmented reality for daily living environments.

title: Exploiting Foundation Models for Costless Open-vocabulary Semantic Segmentation of Optical Remote Sensing Images
authors: Bowei Ye, Tao Shao, Fei Su, Zhicheng Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Deep learning algorithms have driven substantial progress in remote sensing semantic segmentation. However, conventional approaches typically rely on predefined semantic categories, necessitating costly data annotation and model retraining when new classes are introduced. While large-scale vision-language models, such as CLIP, enable segmentation of arbitrary class with natural language guidance, their limited localization capability poses challenges for dense prediction tasks. This study investigates the potential of CLIP for semantic segmentation of optical remote sensing images and proposes LD-Seg, a novel training-free framework that optimizes localization ability and visual-textual alignment through dual representation refinement. Through systematic analysis, we identify anomalous “singularity” tokens in the CLIP visual encoder that aggregate global contextual information while disproportionately attracting attention from local patch tokens, thereby degrading spatial discriminability. To address this, we introduce a singularity feature repair (SFR) strategy that mitigates feature distortion by recalibrating these tokens. Furthermore, we develop a hierarchical semantic expansion (HSE) method to generate precise hierarchical text descriptions, enhancing cross-modal alignment. The SFR and HSE strategies complement existing methods, providing further improvements. Extensive experiments demonstrate that LDSeg achieves state-of-the-art performance, delivering mIoU improvements of 0.95% to 7.84% over existing methods. Code is available at https://github.com/yeelinsen/LDSeg.

title: Agentic AI as the Next Paradigm in PHM
authors: Farnoosh Naderkhani

title: Instagram as a Digital Media Platform for the Supreme Court's Public Relations: Public Reach, Comprehension, and Strategic Challenges
authors: Felicia Kora, Muslikhin Muslikhin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: This study explores the role of Instagram as a digital media platform for public communication by the Public Relations Division of the Supreme Court of the Republic of Indonesia (Humas MA-RI). As digital transformation reshapes institutional communication, Instagram has become a strategic tool to enhance transparency and deliver legal information in a visual and accessible format. Using Kent and Taylor's (1998) dialogic communication theory as an analytical lens, this research investigates how Instagram facilitates public reach, comprehension of legal content, and strategic interaction with the public. A qualitative case study method was used, involving semi-structured interviews with five relevant respondents from within and around the Supreme Court's public communication scope. Thematic analysis, based on Miles and Huberman's model, revealed that while Instagram is effective in disseminating information, two-way communication remains limited. Challenges include bureaucratic validation processes, content accuracy, and maintaining institutional neutrality. However, the potential for interactive and dialogic engagement exists, especially through features like comments, live sessions, and infographics. The study concludes that Instagram can serve not only as an information channel but also as a space for building public trust and participatory communication, provided it is managed with dialogic principles.

title: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Edge Computing (ICDSEC)
authors: N/A

title: AI-Powered Embedded Systems for Predictive Maintenance in Industrial IoT Devices
authors: Dr.G.Ashok Kumar, K.B. Shobana, Dr.Harikarthik S K, T. Venkatesh, B Chitra Lekha, Vasukidevi G
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The research develops an AI-backed embedded framework which operates for predictive maintenance tasks in Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) applications. Through its combination of lightweight machine learning models and edge-computing platforms the system facilitates immediate fault detection together with performance forecasting in industrial equipment. The approach operates with minimal delay while decreasing reliance on cloud services and supports maintenance planning before failures happen which results in longer equipment uptime and decreased costs. Testing of the proposed architecture commences by deploying it to limited-power devices which proved effective anomaly detection while using minimal power. This research establishes the capability of edge AI technologies to revolutionize current maintenance standards in smart manufacturing operations.

title: BioMuse – Living Art and Wellness: A Fusion of Technology and Bio-Art for Sustainability and Identity
authors: Rohini Sengupta, Rajanikanth Aluvalu, Mallellu Sai Prashanth
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: BioMuse is the name for a futuristic startup idea that re-explores art as a living, evolving system at the intersection of biology, technology and design. Unlike static décor, BioMuse art pieces use living organisms, generative processes, and bio-inspired eco friendly sustainable systems to create a dynamic artwork that can grow, adapt and interact with environment.
