Its domain expands across art, sustainability and education, translating experimental bio-art practices, once confined to labs, into accessible, everyday experience. BioMuse merges biology with design enabling customers to curate personalized [1]"living museums" in their homes, and offices making life itself a part of artwork. The main reason of project BioMuse is to stimulate dialogue and reflection around identity, ecology, and technology while contributing to promote sustainability and well-being through interactive living art pieces that connects people with living systems. BioMuse ultimately positions itself as a [2]"21<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">st</sup>-century model of living art", transforming usual everyday spaces into interactive, sustainable, and a reflective environment.

title: Demonstration of 8-kV SiC Gate Turn-Off Thyristors With High di/dt for Nanosecond Pulse Fast-Switching Applications
authors: Lingfeng Li, Juntao Li, Lianghui Li, Zhengxiang Liao, Xingliang Xu, Lin Zhang, Yinghao Meng, Yingjiang He, Zhiqiang Li
abstract: This work presents the design, fabrication, and characterization of a high di/dt silicon carbide (SiC) gate turn-off (GTO) thyristor optimized for fast operation in nanosecond pulsed-power applications. Analysis reveals that the current rise time during the turn-on process critically depends on carrier injection efficiency. Guided by this insight, the P-plus and N-base layers are carefully designed to improve the nanosecond unsaturated conductivity modulation. Optimal doping ranges (P-plus: $2.0\times 10^{{19}} \sim 1.0\times 10^{{20}}$ cm ${}^{-{3}}$ ; N-base: $2.0\times 10^{{16}} \sim 1.6\times 10^{{17}}$ cm ${}^{-{3}}$ ) are proposed with consideration of punchthrough and incomplete ionization. The accordingly optimized 8-kV GTO achieves a record di/dt of 82.7kA/( $\mu $ s $\cdot $ cm2) at a peak current density of 3.18kA/cm2 in a high-power pulse forming network, and the halved turn-on time (30.8ns) sets a new benchmark for SiC GTO pulse application. In addition, repetitive pulse operation is demonstrated for 2000pulses (20Hz, 100s) based on the above pulse condition. This study provides both a physical-level design approach and a promising solution for ultrafast pulse switching, propelling the nanosecond power pulse technology forward.

title: Analytical Model of Charge Transfer in Vertical Pinned Photodiode With Planar Transfer Gate for CMOS Image Sensors
authors: Jingmin Li, Quanmin Chen, Jiangtao Xu
abstract: Recently, the pinned photodiode (PPD) has transformed from a planar structure to a vertical structure with the shrinking of pixel size. An analytical model to describe the dynamic charge transfer process in vertical PPD is proposed in this article. Different from existing models, PPD capacitance ( ${C} _{\text {PPD}}$ ) is composed of four graded junction capacitances. The pinning process is described through a lateral depletion mechanism with consideration of sidewall junction effects on ${C} _{\text {PPD}}$ and full well capacity (FWC). Charge transfer behavior is characterized using the thermionic emission mechanism, incorporating potential variations of the PPD and potential barrier caused by clock feedthrough when the transfer gate (TG) is turned on. The agreement between the model predictions and measurement results demonstrates the accuracy of the proposed model in predicting potential barrier height and charge transfer efficiency (CTE). The proposed model enables a comprehensive analysis of vertical PPD and reveals the impacts of TG turn-on voltage ( ${V} _{\text {TG-on}}$ ), TG–PPD spacing, and TG-floating diffusion (FD) spacing on CTE.

title: A Novel Methodology for Implementing Planar Ultra-Wide Stopband SIW Bandpass Filters and Filtering Crossover
authors: Zhenghai Luo, Kang Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents a novel methodology for implementing a planar substrate-integrated waveguide (SIW) bandpass filter (BPF) with ultra-wide stopband based on the mode cancellation technique. To realize mode cancellation, a dual-probe feeding scheme featuring equal-amplitude, in-phase input and output signals is employed, superseding the conventional central feeding method. First, the electric and magnetic field distribution rules of various resonant modes are analytically derived to guide the placement of the input–output feeding ports and intercavity coupling windows, thus enabling effective mode cancellation and harmonic suppression. Second, a T-junction is incorporated at both the input and output ports to realize the dual probe feeding structure for mode cancellation. Moreover, the introduced T-junctions are fully integrated with the resonant cavity and provide additional suppression of higher-order harmonic modes without deteriorating the circuit performance. The proposed methodology is applicable not only to planar ultra-wide stopband BPFs but also to planar ultra-wide stopband filtering crossovers (FCRs) without intricate design procedures or degradation in device performance. Finally, the proposed methodology is validated through three planar examples, including asecond-order SIW BPF, a third-order SIW BPF, and a third-order SIW FCR. The simulated and measured results showcase that their stopbands are extended up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$4.1f_{0}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$40f_{0}+$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$40f_{0}+$</tex-math> </inline-formula> (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{0}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> is the operating center frequency), respectively, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed design approach.

title: Voltage-Dependent Multi-Stage Consecutive Self-Healing in Metallized Polypropylene Film Capacitors
authors: Yao Wu, Feipeng Wang, Guoliang Li, Yuteng Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Metallized polypropylene film capacitors (MPPFCs) are pivotal components utilized across a spectrum of applications, including electromagnetic ejection, aerospace, and DC power transmission. Understanding the self-healing (SH) mechanism of MPPFCs is challenging, necessitating a comprehensive grasp of the physicochemical processes involved in SH, alongside a deep understanding of the impact of MPPFCs’ multilayer winding structure on SH. In this paper, we present an analysis of the voltage-dependent characteristics of the SH mode of MPPFCs under DC voltage. We substantiated our findings through experimentation and proposed a voltage-dependent multi-stage consecutive self-healing (CSH) model based on MPPFCs’ SH behavior at varying voltage levels. Our investigation reveals that as the SH voltage increases, the SH mode transitions from single SH to multi-stage CSH. In the CSH mode, sub-SH events are temporally and physically coupled through arc plasma and energy transfer, with notably stronger coupling between the first two sub-SHs observed in the three-stage CSH compared to the two-stage CSH. This heightened coupling leads to more breakdown layers with a significant increase in SH time, SH energy, and electrode removal area, albeit accompanied by a substantial decrease in insulation resistance. This paper offers direct evidence that multi-stage CSH is identified as the dominant mechanism driving insulation resistance decay and structural damage under high voltage. This understanding serves as a crucial basis for condition monitoring and structural optimization of MPPFCs.

title: All-Fiber Multimodal Sensor for Dual-Gas and Spectral Co-Detection Based on Hollow-Core Photonic Bandgap Fiber
authors: Junhao Lin, Jifeng Li, Yujing Wu
abstract: Accurate, non-invasive measurement of greenhouse gases is crucial for environmental monitoring and sensor networks. Traditional gas sensors are limited in terms of sensitivity and spatial resolution, and thus accurate spatiotemporal mapping of gas concentrations is difficult. In this work, we present a fully fiber-integrated multimodal sensing system combining a HC-PBF gas detection module with a UV-excited spectral analysis module. This arrangement allows for simultaneous spatially resolved monitoring of vegetation spectral signals and greenhouse gases. The HC-PBF sensor with side-hole diffusion structure provides non-invasive gas sampling, and time-division multiplexed dual-wavelength detection measures methane and carbon dioxide with 1 s integration detection limits of 0.15 ppm and 14 ppm, respectively. The system provides high sensitivity, low interference, and spatial resolution, allowing real-time integration of gas concentration and spatial data. Rice paddy field experiments demonstrate the robustness and stability of the system under complex environmental conditions and indicate a potential correlation between vegetation fluorescence ratio parameters (F735/480 and F780/480) and gas emissions. These results demonstrate that the fully fiber-integrated approach is suitable for high-precision multimodal environmental sensing and can be applied to ecological monitoring and distributed fiber-optic sensor networks.

title: Four-set Hypergraphlets for Characterization of Directed Hypergraphs
authors: Heechan Moon, Hyunju Kim, Sunwoo Kim, Kijung Shin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: A directed hypergraph, which consists of nodes and hyperarcs, is a higher-order data structure that naturally models directional group interactions (e.g., chemical reactions of molecules). Although there have been extensive studies on local structures of (directed) graphs in the real world, those of directed hypergraphs remain unexplored. In this work, we focus on measurements, findings, and applications related to local structures of directed hypergraphs, and they together contribute to a systematic understanding of various real-world systems interconnected by directed group interactions. Our first contribution is to define 91 directed hypergraphlets (DHGs), which disjointly categorize directed connections and overlaps among four node sets that compose two incident hyperarcs. Our second contribution is to develop exact and approximate algorithms for counting the occurrences of each DHGs. Our last contribution is to characterize 11 real-world directed hypergraphs and individual hyperarcs in them using the occurrences of DHGs, which reveals clear domain-based local structural patterns. Our experiments demonstrate that our DHG-based characterization gives up to $12\%$ and $33\%$ better performances on hypergraph clustering and hyperarc prediction, respectively, than baseline characterization methods. Moreover, we show that CODA-A, which is our proposed approximate algorithm, is up to $36\times$ faster than its competitors with similar characterization quality.

title: The Architecture of Silence
authors: Fahim Kawsar

title: Transformation-Driven Hellinger Distance Framework for Electricity Theft Detection in Advanced Metering Infrastructure
authors: Sandeep Kumar Singh
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: The integration of sensing, computing, and communication technologies has improved the efficiency and flexibility of modern power grids. However, it has also increased the vulnerability of advanced metering infrastructure (AMI) to cyber-physical threats, particularly energy theft. This paper proposes a novel, transformation-driven detection framework based on the Hellinger Distance (HD) to identify anomalies in daily electricity consumption at the individual smart meter level. By applying log and antilog transformations to measurement variations, the method enhances distribution separability, improving detection accuracy. The HD metric is then used to quantify statistical deviations between historical and real-time consumption patterns. The proposed scheme operates without requiring system-wide observability or state estimation, making it lightweight and scalable. Experimental validation using real-world datasets from the Irish Social Science Data Archive (ISSDA) and the State Grid Corporation of China (SGCC) smart meter database demonstrates detection rates between 83% and 100% across multiple attack scenarios. Compared with existing statistical and ML-based methods, the proposed approach achieves higher detection rates while remaining computationally efficient, confirming its effectiveness and suitability for practical AMI deployments.

title: Data-Driven Hierarchical Decision-Making Modeling for Complex Industrial Processes
authors: Shengbo Hong, Wen Yu, Tianyou Chai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Accurate decision-making for complex industrial processes is essential for improving resource utilization and optimizing operating conditions. However, current decision work-flows operate within a hierarchical closed loop between the operations layer and the process-control layer. Cross-layer transmission of objectives and constraints and feedback coupling can degrade performance; the effect is pronounced under nonstationary conditions or when objectives or constraints change. To address these challenges, we propose the Bi-level Evolving Neural Networks (BENNs) framework with four components: (i) a bi-level neural architecture that explicitly models cross-layer information flow and constraint propagation; (ii) a graph neural network-based structural similarity assessment that reduces redundant evaluations to improve search quality; (iii) an online evolving network strategy that adapts rapidly to process dynamics without extensive retraining; and (iv) a convex-hull knee-point-based compromise selection that encodes engineering preferences. Experiments on real mineral processing data and on the Tennessee Eastman Process (TEP) show improvements over baselines in prediction accuracy, performance retention, and adaptation speed, demonstrating the potential of BENNs for complex industrial settings.

title: Dynamic Performance of Parallel Heterogeneous Grid-Forming Inverters During Islanding and Grid Resynchronization
authors: Zahra Ahmadimonfared, Stefan Eichner
abstract: Modern power systems with high inverter penetration face reduced inertia and increased frequency instability during disturbances, necessitating grid-forming inverters (GFMs) that emulate synchronous-machine dynamics. This study examines the parallel operation of a Virtual Synchronous Machine (VSM) controlled and a synchronverter-controlled GFM in a low-inertia microgrid across the full operational cycle of grid-connected, islanded, and resynchronization phases.Aunified simulation framework was developed to systematically evaluate the impact of coordinated variations in virtual inertia (H) and damping coefficient (Dp) on frequency dynamics, power sharing, and stability under power imbalance and grid-code-compliant reconnection conditions. The results demonstrate that a higher virtual inertia limits the rate of change of frequency and nadir after islanding, while increased damping enhances oscillation suppression and settling performance. During resynchronization, both GFM satisfy IEEE Std 1547–2018 [1] limits on voltage, frequency, and phase-angle differences, confirming stable interaction and seamless transition between the operating modes. These findings reveal the complementary strengths of heterogeneous VSM–synchronverter operation and the critical role of unified parameter tuning for robust microgrid performance in low-inertia environments.

title: Controllable End-to-End Neural Source-Filter Vocoder with Low-Dimensional Speech Parameters and Data-Augmentation
authors: Sumiharu Kobayashi, Tetsuo Kosaka, Takashi Nose
abstract: Recent neural vocoders synthesize speech waveforms from high-dimensional spectrograms, making it difficult to control individual speech parameters such as F0 and formants. Previous research has proposed a speech synthesis system that controls speech parameters by converting low-dimensional parameters into spectrograms that serve as input to a neural vocoder. However, the quality of the synthesized speech degrades because the generated spectral time series differs from the natural speech used to train the neural vocoder. In this study, we propose a controllable end-to-end neural vocoder that directly synthesizes speech waveforms from low-dimensional speech parameters. Furthermore, we aim to improve the synthesis quality during speech-parameter control by introducing a source–filter model and employing data augmentation. Experimental evaluations show that the source-filter-based method synthesizes speech with quality equal to or better than that of conventional approaches when controlling speech parameters. Additionally, data augmentation improved the robustness of F0 and formant controls. A demonstration of the proposed methods is available online (https://misumisumi.github.io/myprojects/end-to-end-neural-formant-synthesis).

title: Variable Memory Sequence Neural Network Based Data-Driven Predictive Control for Power Converters
authors: Tingjun Pan, Lin Qiu, Xing Liu, Youtong Fang, Jose Rodriguez
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Model predictive control (MPC) is an effective solution for handling nonlinearity and multi-objective problems in power converters. However, the conventional MPC method is prone to uncertainties caused by parameter variation and external disturbances. To address this challenge, in this article, a novel ultra-local (UL) model based variable memory sequence online learning predictive control scheme is proposed. To be specific, the original system model is replaced by a first-order UL model, where the disturbance term is estimated by a neural network with a variable-memory training architecture. This proposed variable memory training architecture, endowed with a combination of historical and current operating information, can fully leverage the temporal data to enhance the estimation accuracy of the disturbance term. In view of this merit, the improved UL model is further employed in the subsequent control process, thereby eliminating the need for an explicit model. Moreover, this proposal also incorporates the merits of a multi-vector mechanism, which allows for low current ripple, along with a fixed switching frequency. Finally, the stability analysis of the proposal is manifested, and the performance of the proposed method is validated for a three-level neutral point clamped converter through both simulation and experiment.

title: Unsupervised Deepfake Detection via Camera Source Clustering and Temporal-Spatial Features
authors: Zhuang Li, Chenxu Lu, Zahid Akhtar, Tong Qiao
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming diverse domains like cybersecurity, robotics, and healthcare but are also being misused for harmful purposes. A notable example is deep fakes that involve audio or visual content artificially generated or altered using deep neural networks. Current deepfake detection methods rely predominantly on supervised learning paradigm, which faces issues like poor interpretability and the impracticality of acquiring extensive labeled data. This paper proposes a novel unsupervised deepfake detection framework to overcome these limitations. By utilizing camera source clustering based on inherent noise patterns, the method decomposes large-scale deepfake clustering into smaller and more manageable problems. It further extracts and aggregates robust inter-frame and intra frame features to highlight temporal and spatial inconsistencies in deepfake videos. Extensive experiments validate that the proposed training-free framework achieves high detection accuracy and relevant generalization across multiple datasets, rivalling the training-based baselines. Moreover, the proposed scheme reduces reliance on labeled data, enhances interpretability, and paves the way for scalable and cost-effective deepfake detection solutions in real-world scenarios. Our source codes have been released at https://github.com/Q-bigbig/unsupervised-deepfake.

title: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters Information for Authors
authors: N/A

title:
BR
                    <sup>3</sup>
                    L: Rethinking Bilateral Reweighted Reconstruction Representation Learning for Unsupervised Hyperspectral Band Selection
authors: Guoxia Xu, Qi Wu, Yulong Sun, Lizhen Deng, Hu Zhu
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Unsupervised band selection aims to identify the most informative spectral bands from hyperspectral images, thereby reducing spectral redundancy while preserving essential spatial–spectral structures. However, existing methods often ignore global or local structural information and noise, leading to suboptimal band subsets. In this paper, we propose a bilateral reweighted reconstruction representation model that jointly leverages spatial and spectral priors for unsupervised band selection. To simultaneously exploit both spectral correlation and spatial structure, a reconstruction residual consistency constraint is introduced into superpixels, forcing residuals of pixels inside the same superpixel to converge toward the regional mean. Additionally, spatial adaptive weight matrices W<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</sub> and spectral adaptive weight matrices W<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">b</sub> are designed to coordinate the weight consistency of pixels and band dimensions through bilateral weighting terms, suppress noise and maintain edge structure. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms several representative algorithms in terms of noise suppression and edge preservation.

title: Collective Resonance in Dielectric Resonators and Application to Microfluidic Dielectric Spectroscopy
authors: Nikhil Kumar, Utpal Dey
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This work presents a novel technique to engineer collective resonance in a 2-D periodic array of spherical dielectric resonators sandwiched within a parallel metal plate configuration. To do so, a favorable choice of the Mie electric quadrupole (EQ) resonance mode in alumina dielectric spheres is used to generate a tightly coupled resonating array featuring a high quality factor and intense electric fields in the air-gaps between consecutive array elements. A microcapillary tube carrying the test fluid can be conveniently placed in the nonradiating field hotspots, thereby resulting in high-sensitivity dielectric spectroscopy. The microfluidic-based dielectric spectroscopy of water and commonly available chemicals is performed around 40 GHz. The collective resonance-based microfluidic sensor design is robust, easy-to-assemble, low-cost, features self-alignment, and is frequency-scalable up to sub-terahertz (THz) frequencies.

title: Research on Photovoltaic Prediction Algorithm Integrating LSTM and Transformer
authors: Haitao Zhao, Xin Li, Zhaohan Wang, Zhonglin Sun, Hongze Xu, Kongquan Yang, Liang Zheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Accurate prediction of photovoltaic power generation capacity was critical for power dispatch, power allocation and power plant construction in power grids and markets. This paper proposed a photovoltaic prediction method that combined the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) algorithm and the Transformer algorithm. To address the limitation of LSTM in handling time jumps or data gaps, the Transformer algorithm, which excelled at processing discontinuous time information, was introduced. This made the predicted photovoltaic power generation data more aligned with real-world conditions. Results showed that in the prediction model based on NWP meteorological data and power generation data, the LSTM-Transformer hybrid model outperformed the standalone LSTM model by 40.58%, 64.67%, 12.90% and 55.79% in RMSE, MSE, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> and MAE metrics, respectively, and outperformed the single Transformer model by 36.60%, 59.75%, 10.75% and 48.24%, respectively. In summary, the model that combined LSTM and Transformer could better meet the modern photovoltaic forecasting requirements.

title: Hybrid LSTM-GAT Model with k-Nearest Neighbors for Accurate Multi-Agent Vehicle Trajectory Prediction
authors: Chaymae Rami, Mohammed Ammar, Youness Bouhout, Ibrahim Ouahbi, Khalid El Makkaoui
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Understanding how vehicles behave in real traffic is still difficult, especially for autonomous driving and intelligent transportation systems. Roads are busy, drivers interact in many different ways, and the scene changes quickly, so predicting how a vehicle will move is not straightforward. Trajectory prediction is one way to anticipate future positions and reduce the risk of collisions, and it is now used in many contexts, such as motion planning for self-driving cars, and traffic management tools. In this work, we study a hybrid model that combines a Graph Attention Network (GAT) with a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network. Vehicle interactions are represented as a graph built with a k-nearest neighbors (kNN) procedure that selects the closest vehicles at each time step. The model is trained with a loss that mixes the Average Displacement Error (ADE), the Final Displacement Error (FDE), and an extra smoothness term so that the predicted trajectories stay realistic and evolve smoothly over time. On the NGSIM dataset, the trajectories remain stable over the 10 -step prediction horizon, which suggests that the model captures multi-agent interactions while keeping coherent motion patterns.

title: Artificial Intelligence Approaches for Autism Spectrum Disorder Diagnosis and Therapeutic Insights: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: V. Chithra, J. I. Sheeba, S. Pradeep Devaneyan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: It is crucial to manage autism spectrum disorder (ASD) through accurate diagnosis, followed by efficient rehabilitation. AI methodologies include conventional machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL). DL approaches are concentrating on neuroimaging-based methods. ML and DL are cutting-edge methodologies that researchers have advanced for recognising ASD using neural network (NN)-based approaches. This survey paper provides a comprehensive overview of ASD, covering screening methods, key features, underlying causes, and treatment options. Numerous screening models, comprising standardised diagnostic tools and behavioural evaluations, are inspected, together with their efficacy in early identification. The paper also addresses the multifactorial causes of ASD, encompassing genetic, neurological, and environmental impacts, and emphasises how these factors shape individualized therapeutic strategies. Existing classification and identification approaches are reviewed, concentrating on both conventional methodologies and developing AI-based models. A comparative study of these approaches shows that the AI-based method offers greater scalability and precision; however, challenges such as limited data and model interpretability remain. This survey concludes by highlighting the importance of integrating clinical expertise with sophisticated computational models to improve ASD diagnosis and treatment, thereby enhancing outcomes and quality of life for affected individuals. Ultimately, it presents significant challenges for automated recognition of ASD and proposes some future work.

title: Color Image Enhancement Using Ripple-Carry Approximate Adders
authors: Y. S. S Sriramam, Bala Sindhuri Kandula, Triveni Chintha, Dasari M N K Shankar, Vaddipalli Vinesh, Thoram Sai Ram Durga Ganesh
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: From medical diagnostics to embedded vision, the improved quality of images is vital as they have a direct effect on decision-making. The present work analyzes a histogram equalization algorithm that uses Ripple-Carry Adder (RCA) and Approximate Ripple-Carry Adders (ARCAs) using different approximate variants such as using ARCAs (A1-A12). The findings indicate that the ARCA using A2, A9 and A11, gives the best compromise between computational performance and perceptual quality, with good PSNR and SSIM values. Apart from this, the Probability Density Function (PDF) and Cumulative Distribution Function (CDF) indicate the effect of approximation on the intensity distribution. Comprehensively, the results suggest that approximation when carefully applied to LSB positions reduces hardware metrics with no apparent quality reduction and thus approximate computing is a feasible option when the application demands real-time color image enhancement under power constrained operations.

title: Web Spam Detection for the Evolving Web Graph Based on Inductive Learning
authors: Yuanyi Ma, Siyang Chen, Shuangwu Chen, Dong Jin, Feng Yang, Jian Tan
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: The dynamic and adversarial nature of the web presents significant challenges for web spam detection, as spammers continuously evolve their tactics in both content deception and link manipulation. To address this, we propose a novel Graph Neural Network (GNN)-based framework for robust web spam detection. A key innovation of our work is an inductive learning model that operates on subgraphs, enabling it to generalize effectively to previously unseen nodes and dynamically evolving web graphs. We evaluate our framework extensively on both fixed and dynamic web graph datasets. The results demonstrate its strong practical viability, high accuracy in spam identification, and superior ability to adapt to the continuously changing topology of the web. Our approach integrates the web page's content features with the topological information from link structures within a model. The results demonstrate its strong practical viability, high accuracy in spam identification, and superior ability to adapt to the continuous changes of the web graph.

title: MaskDiff-TAD: Masked Diffusion Trajectory Imputation for Anomaly Detection
authors: Qi Ouyang, Shuxin Liu, Ran Li, Junjie Zhang, Hongchang Chen, YanCheng Mu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Spatio-temporal trajectory anomaly detection is critical for applications like autonomous driving and traffic monitoring, yet faces challenges in capturing complex dependencies and localized anomalies. To address this, we proposes the MaskDiff-TAD framework. Its core innovation lies in introducing an alternating masking strategy that compels the model to learn deep spatio-temporal dependencies between trajectory segments and their context under partial observation conditions. Building upon this, the framework combines spatio-temporal Transformer layers with a conditional diffusion imputation mechanism to achieve robust reconstruction of missing trajectory segments, while exhibiting high sensitivity to subtle differences between normal and anomalous patterns. Ultimately, a multi-step voting function integrates reconstruction errors from different denoising steps during diffusion, enabling stable and adaptive anomaly inference. Experiments demonstrate that this approach significantly outperforms existing baselines on real-world datasets, achieving F1 scores of 0.933 and 0.945, thereby substantially enhancing the detection accuracy of fine-grained local anomalies.

title: Implementation of Hybrid model for Enhanced Crop Yield Prediction using Explainable AI for Precision Agriculture
authors: Sheetal Mavi, Neetu Mittal, Pradeepta Kumar Sarangi
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Crop yield prediction is important for global food security, sustainable farming, and efficient resource management in agriculture. Inaccuracy and inherent opacity are the main issue for accurate prediction. To solve the problems of deep learning models a hybrid model has been developed for better prediction. This research works to improve agricultural decision-making through advanced predictive modelling. In this research work SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) and Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping), have been employed. SHAP analysis helps us to understand the input feature impact of soil moisture at middle level growth and helps us to analyse the over fertilization that is no longer increasing the crop yield rather reducing it. This work shows integration of deep learning and explainable AI for data-driven decisions on irrigation process, usage of fertilizers, and strategies to improve the decision making of farmers and thus heading towards precision farming. A hybrid model that considers dynamic and static characteristics to forecast crop yields. The model employs Conv1D layers for finding short-term patterns and Bidirectional LSTM layers for long-term dependencies, along with dedicated dense layers for static features. The overall $\mathbf{R}^{\boldsymbol{\wedge}} \mathbf{2}$ is $\mathbf{0. 8 1}$ and $\mathbf{R}^{\boldsymbol{\wedge}} \mathbf{2}$ for specific crop types (wheat, soybean, corn, and rice) is always 0.91 and above, therefore outperforming models based on exclusive use of dynamic or static characteristics by a wide margin.

title: AI-Powered Labor Demand Forecasting: Advanced Deep Learning Architectures for Workforce Management
authors: Hao Zhang, Linjun Wei, Yangchun Hao
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This paper systematically evaluates the performance of deep learning models-including LSTM, CNN, Transformer, and XGBoost-in workforce demand forecasting. LSTM achieves a 1.5% MAPE and 26.5 RMSE in hourly forecasting, significantly outperforming traditional methods. The CNN-LSTM hybrid model reduces MAE by up to 70% in multi-period tasks. XGBoost optimized with TPE achieves 91% prediction accuracy in retail scenarios while reducing labor costs by 18%. Across healthcare, retail, and logistics cases, hybrid architectures incorporating attention mechanisms boost prediction accuracy by over 25% on average. The study confirms that deep learning-based AI forecasting systems demonstrate significant advantages in handling nonlinear, nonstationary labor data, providing a scalable technical pathway for intelligent workforce scheduling.

title: Time-current characteristics and tripping accuracy of main and backup relays in diverse fault scenarios
authors: Sultan Sh. Alanzi, Asmaa A. Alrushoud, Ahmad S. Saleh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: This paper presents an experimental investigation of fundamental protection relays applied to key power system components, including transformers, busbars, and induction motors. A programmable current-injection setup is used to emulate a wide range of abnormal conditions under both loaded and unloaded operation. The tested relays include differential, overcurrent, earth-fault, negative-sequence, over- and undervoltage, and overcurrent with autorecloser types. Results showed that careful, appropriate settings led to accurate and selective operation across all cases. Specifically, the differential relay tripped instantaneously for internal faults and remained secure for external disturbances. The overcurrent relay responded only to low-resistance faults, while the negative-sequence relay provided additional sensitivity to high-resistance unbalances. Earth-fault relays operated solely for earth-involved disturbances, and voltage relays correctly responded to deviations beyond ±10% of nominal. The autorecloser successfully restored supply during temporary disturbances and entered lockout mode for permanent conditions. Overall, the experiments confirm that carefully coordinated relay settings can maintain sensitivity, selectivity, dependability, and speed, offering a practical and reliable framework for protection testing and coordination studies in renewable-integrated, converter-dominated modern power networks.

title: AI-Enhanced Prediction of Respiratory Irritation From Biomass Combustion Byproducts: An Integrative Machine Learning Framework for Sustainable Bioenergy Systems
authors: Shehabul Alam, Ayush Biswas, Rehnuma Islam, Shah Samadur Rahman, Hasebul Hassan Chowdhury, Sudoy Kumer Ghosh
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Biomass burning has continued to be a part of renewable energy infrastructure, but due to incomplete oxidation, they produce fine particulate matter and volatile organic compounds that cause pulmonary oxidative stress in the exposed populations. The study is a dual-paradigm machine learning model that combines the frameworks of both the Random Forest and the Long Short-Term Memory to forecast respiratory health risks of bioaerosol exposure in biomassdependent rural areas. Environmental monitoring data (PM2.5 concentrations, nitrogen oxides, and volatile organic compounds) in time synchrony with the clinical data (inflammatory biomarkers) were integrated to predict against clinical biomarkers to create real-time risk stratification predictive models. Nitrogen oxides were the most important feature according to the importance analysis, which confirmed mechanistic connections between inefficiency in combustion and respiratory inflammation. Random Forest model was the best in the interpretability with low prediction bias whereas LSTM networks performed high-fidelity in their ability to model temporal exposure dynamics. This integrative framework closes the environmental engineering, medical diagnostics, and optimization of renewable energy, provides evidence-based opportunities in the implementation of clean cooking technology and feedstock processing strategies to balance energy access and respiratory health care in the sustainable changes of bioenergy.

title: Towards Vehicle-Infrastructure Cooperation: A Retina-Inspired Model for Low-Light Image Enhancement
authors: Fangzhou He, Jie Li, Xiaohui Li, Peixiang Zhang, Yi Xiang, Zhihua Qu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: The reliability of roadside visual perception in Vehicle-Infrastructure Cooperation (VIC) systems is severely challenged under low-light conditions. Images captured in such environments typically exhibit low signal-to-noise ratio, poor contrast, and significant noise, which substantially degrade the performance of vision-based tasks. While deep learning-based methods have shown promising results, they often suffer from computational complexity and limited interpretability. Inspired by Retinex theory, this paper proposes a lightweight decomposition-enhancement network that employs a center-surround structure for illumination estimation and reflectance separation. The network architecture incorporates a dedicated denoising module for reflectance restoration and a content-aware gamma correction mechanism for illumination adjustment. Extensive experiments validate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple benchmarks, outperforming existing approaches in both quantitative metrics and visual quality. The efficient design ensures practical deployment potential for edge devices in intelligent transportation systems.

title: Design of an Intelligent Home–School Communication Mini-Program Powered by Large Language Models: A Case Study of an AI Teaching Assistant
authors: Yuan Yao, Xu Tu, Chang Liu, Yaming Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Social Network Systems (AISNS)
abstract: This study explores the application mechanisms and interaction design innovations of Artificial Intelligence (AI) teaching assistants in home–school communication under the context of large language models. Based on a review of existing research and intelligent communication tools, it addresses challenges such as information fragmentation, heavy teacher workloads, and low feedback efficiency by leveraging the natural language understanding and generation capabilities of large language models. Using the “AI Teaching Assistant” mini-program as a case study, the research analyzes interaction processes, information integration, and intelligent response strategies. The findings indicate that, compared with traditional communication tools, AI teaching assistants based on large language models offer clear advantages in semantic understanding, contextualized feedback, and content generation, effectively reducing teachers’ cognitive and time burdens while improving the efficiency and quality of home–school communication.

title: Vectorized HD Map Construction Based on Efficient Kernelized Self-Attention
authors: Jiaming Liu, Yi Fang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Vectorized maps are crucial in autonomous driving, providing critical support for vehicle localization and planning. This paper aims to develop an efficient vectorized map construction architecture to enhance both accuracy and efficiency. To achieve this, we introduce an efficient SCC-kernel-based self-attention (ESSA) which exhibits antiinterference characteristics to address environmental disturbances in map data. ESSA module stabilizes feature extraction of map elements, expands receptive fields, and fully utilizes multi-scale spatial-spectral information, enabling precise capture of map element shapes and positions for improved vectorization accuracy. Simultaneously, ESSA module reduces computational complexity from quadratic to linear, significantly alleviating computational burdens and meeting real-time requirements for processing large-scale, high-resolution map data. Experiments on the nuScenes dataset demonstrate that the proposed architecture outperforms state-of-the-art methods in metrics such as average precision, achieving superior mapping performance. This framework holds significant application potential, offering more accurate and real-time map information for autonomous driving development.

title: BERT-TFPAM: An Improved Attention Mechanism-Based Approach to Telecommunication Fraud Event Argument Recognition
authors: Wen Zhou, Qian Wang, Fumin Wang, Anying Chai
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In telecommunication fraud prevention, information technology has helped reduce crime rates and eased the burden on police. However, the unstructured and colloquial nature of case texts still requires significant manual effort. Existing event extraction models often fail to capture fine-grained semantics, leading to incomplete extractions. To address this, we propose BERT-TFPAM, a model optimized for telecommunication fraud detection. It enhances role-trigger interaction with expanded BERT embeddings, introduces a multi-granularity attention layer to aggregate semantics across levels, and uses a BiLSTM module for improved contextual understanding. A CRF layer is applied for optimal label sequence generation. Experiments on a custom TFEE dataset show that BERT-TFPAM achieves an F1 score of 73.02, outperforming the Doc2EDAG model by 3.08 percentage points, demonstrating its effectiveness in extracting key elements from fraud case texts.

title: An Individual Depression Detection Method based on Bi-LSTM and Attention Mechanism in Sina Weibo
authors: Haichao Jin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Research to date indicates that information on social networking websites can be used to predict the early stages of depression. There is still considerable scope for further advancement and development of associated research methodologies. This is evidenced by the fact that studies using machine learning and other methods for identifying depression still lack satisfactory classification performance. The Weibo User Depression Detection Dataset, which comprises over 10,000 depressed users and 20,000 healthy users, was examined in this study. A deep neural network classification model, designated BBA, was constructed using the pre-trained model Bert to extract features from textual data. The aforementioned representations were then passed through a bidirectional long short-term memory (Bi-LSTM) layer, which was employed to capture the sequence information present within the input. Subsequently, an attention mechanism was applied to the output generated by the Bi-LSTM. The experimental results demonstrate that the performance of the model can be significantly improved by introducing a Bi-LSTM and an attention mechanism, as well as pre-training on a large-scale sentiment classification dataset, resulting in an F1 value of 97.83% and a recall value of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 7. 8 2 \%}$</tex>.

title: A Next-Generation AI based Predictive Model for Indian Rainfall using Real-Time API Data
authors: G. Chamundeswari, V.Santha Kumar, Prasad Babu Bairisetty, Chiranjeevi Aggala, Nagendra Kumar Yakkala, Ch.Venkatesh Ch.Venkatesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Accurate rainfall prediction is more useful for government sectors to prevent huge losses from heavy rainfall. Rainfall prediction affects various fields, including agriculture, water utilization, disaster management, and environmental condition prediction in India. In this paper, a Next-Generation Artificial Intelligence (NG-AI)–based predictive model is presented that accurately detects and predicts rainfall by combining real-time meteorological metrics from Weather APIs to improve dynamic rainfall prediction. The proposed NG-AI processes live climate predictors, such as ClimateStreamNet (CS-Net), enabling regular, up-to-date predictions. The proposed NG-AI refines the final output by integrating ensemble feature maps with enhanced classification layers, which are applied across multiple datasets. The NG-AI uses the Python API to find the accurate climatic conditions. The results show that the proposed approach achieved MAE (mm) of 5.32, MSE (mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) of 111.56, RMSE (mm) of 11.67, and MAPE (%) of 5.23. These results represent superior performance.

title: Synthetic IM-Based Fraud Conversations for Efficient LLM Fine-Tuning and Evaluation
authors: Xinkuan Su, Mingyu Wang, Yujiao Han, Lu Wang, Jizhi Wang, Yong Fu, Guang Yang, Xiaoming Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: With the significant shift of telecom fraud vectors from traditional phone calls to instant messaging (IM) software, fraud detection technologies tailored for IM scenarios face severe challenges. Current research primarily focuses on phone fraud detection, lacking effective and scalable solutions for IM fraud dialogue detection. This gap is further exacerbated by the scarcity of real-world fraud dialogue data due to privacy concerns. To address this, this paper proposes IMAFD(IM AntiFraud Detector), a modular framework for IM fraud dialogue detection. The framework comprises three core modules: (1) Fraud Dialogue Dataset Generation: Innovatively leverages large language models (LLMs) combined with real-world fraud cases to generate high-quality, diverse simulated fraud dialogue corpora, effectively alleviating data scarcity; (2) Fraud Dialogue Dataset Processing: Processes generated dialogues through fusion, segmentation, and rigorous data anonymization to construct structured, model-ready datasets in JSONL format; (3) Model Fine-Tuning: We employ the advanced Qwen3 series as the primary base model and utilize efficient LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) parameter fine-tuning techniques for model training. To demonstrate that the effectiveness of data fine-tuning is not unique to the Qwen3 series’ inherent data processing mechanisms, training was also conducted on numerous smaller models, such as the MiniCPM4-0.5B. Furthermore, we propose LightF1, an evaluation metric designed to balance the F1 score with model lightweighting specifically for the task of fraudulent dialogue detection. Experimental results indicate that the fine-tuned models achieve a significant improvement in F1 score compared to the baseline models, thereby validating the effectiveness of the proposed framework. This study offers a novel technical pathway for fraud defense in IM environments. The relevant code has been open-sourced at: https://github.com/suxinkuann-arch/IMFraud

title: Convolutional Neural Network-based Bioacoustic Tarsier Detection for Wildlife Monitoring
authors: Jhonnel Carlo F. Capule, Gabriel Adrian C. Villamor, Jennifer C. Dela Cruz
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Philippine Tarsiers are known to be sensitive especially to loud noises, physical contact, and bright lights; and with the decreasing population of the Philippine tarsiers, conservation methods and wildlife monitoring are necessary to alleviate this problem. One way to monitor them is by using their bioacoustic characteristics. Together with a machine learning model called Convolutional Neural Networks or CNN, a noninvasive method for monitoring them is possible. For this to happen, the training of the ML model is necessary. To do this, the researchers gathered an audio dataset of the Philippine Tarsiers, then the researchers converted the gathered data into mel spectrograms then annotated and pre-process the dataset in order for the CNN model to tell apart three types of sounds, namely: background noise, a single tarsier calls, and multiple tarsier call. There were a total of 3 runs made; and with each run, a different and updated fine-tuning method was used in order to match the needs of the model. The latest accuracy of the model reached up to 96.50 percent correctness. A Raspberry Pi 5 with 8 GB RAM was used together with the TensorFlow software in order to train the model. A shotgun microphone was also connected to the Raspberry Pi 5 together with a 20x4 I2C LCD screen for the feedback. With this, it is possible to design and run a non-invasive wildlife monitoring device for the tarsiers and can be used for future research in monitoring animal census and studying their behaviour.

title: A Cybersecurity Event Reconstruction Framework Considering Source Accuracy and Conflict Validation
authors: Shuaichen Ye, Zhe Bu, Yuetao Ge
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: With the continuously increasing of the cybersecurity event frequency and intensity, requirements for the timeliness response and loss mitigation are growing. Generally, event extracting and reconstructing is regarded as the basis of the response process, its accuracy and completeness determines the efficiency of the response. Current literature major focuses on using neural network-based algorithms to improve the performance of event content extraction. Research on multisource information fusion and event reconstruction remains insufficient. To address this issue, this paper proposes a cybersecurity event reconstruction framework to aggregate the most credible information for generating an integrated event. Four reconstruction scenarios are selected and conducted on a dataset with 1352 identified events to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. Comparison results indicate the accuracy and completeness enhancing of the reconstruction framework.

title: Research on Spiral Trajectory Modeling and Collision Control Based on Runge-Kutta Method and Genetic Algorithm
authors: Mingzhi Liu, Siyao Lu, Xiushan Ding
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In this paper, a spiral motion model and related optimization methods are proposed, focusing on the motion laws of the head, body and tail of the spiral, the collision detection mechanism and the pitch optimization problem. Firstly, the polar coordinate equations of the spiral are established and converted into the right-angle coordinate parametric equations, and the position change of the spiral head under variable angular velocity is calculated by the integral method combined with the Runge-Kutta numerical solution method, so as to clarify the recursive position updating method of the spiral body and the tail segments. Secondly, the collision model is constructed, and the relevant parts are abstracted as quadrilaterals, and the collision between non-adjacent parts is detected by geometric boundary analysis, and the earliest collision time and the corresponding state parameters are determined by gradual iteration. Finally, a genetic algorithm is used to optimize the pitch parameter, taking the minimum pitch as the target, combining collision, spatial boundary and velocity constraints, and solving the optimal solution through selection, crossover and mutation operations. The model can accurately describe the spiral trajectory motion, and realize the motion simulation, collision detection and parameter optimization through numerical methods and intelligent algorithms, which are both accurate and efficient, and can effectively guide the design and optimization of spiral-like motion systems.

title: Evaluating Artificial Intelligence Based Predictive Analytics for Improving Performance Management and Employee Satisfaction in Organizations
authors: T. Deepika, Srinivas. D
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This article analyses the contribution of Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based predictive analytics to both the performance management systems and employee satisfaction in the organization. Subjective performance assessment and regular reviews have been ineffective in the traditional performance management as they could not give timely and accurate performances of employees and their satisfaction. The use of AI models, based on the machine learning algorithms, is a potential game changer by providing real-time, datagenerating efforts that match organizational objectives with individual performance. This paper is a predictive analytics model derived with data of the organization, including past performance history, the training attendance history, and the employee engagement measurement. The findings prove that predictive analytics are much more effective in terms of accuracy of performance forecasting and even enables providing feedback to employees in a personalized manner. In addition, the integration of these predictive models in performance management processes can be found to have a positive impact on employee satisfaction and offer them more customized growth opportunities and less dissatisfaction. These findings stress the opportunities available to AI to enhance the operation of performance management systems and create an increasingly engaged and more satisfied workforce. Moral aspects of data privacy and fairness of algorithms are another issue. The next generation of research could be focused on examining the long-term effects of AI-based interventions on the performance of the organization, such as retention and productivity of employees.

title: Circular Patch Antenna with Corrugated Slot for Millimeter Wave Wireless Links
authors: Anslin Star R, Anitha G
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: A circular patch antenna operating at 60 GHz has been designed and simulated using Rogers RT/duroid 5870 as the substrate material. The initial model based on a conventional circular patch exhibited only moderate performance. To improve the performance two corrugated slots were incorporated into the patch structure, which enhanced energy coupling and reduced signal reflection. The optimized antenna achieved a return loss of −77.26 dB at a resonant frequency of 57.45 GHz and a peak gain of 7.53 dB. These results confirm that slot integration is an effective technique for boosting antenna efficiency, making the proposed design well suited for advanced wireless applications such as 5 G networks and short-range high-speed communication technologies.

title: Predictive Analysis and Review of Cardiovascular Diseases in Women Using Artificial Intelligence with Clinical and Ethical Implementation Issues
authors: Pallavi Upadhyaya, Pradeep Tomar
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Cardiovascular Diseases (CVDs) are the leading global cause of death and affect women disproportionately due to sex-specific risk factors, atypical symptoms, and frequent underdiagnosis. Despite advances in AI and ML that improve prediction and diagnosis, most models are male-biased, lowering accuracy for women. This paper presents the review of AI/ML Techniques for CVD Detection Techniques including the Deep learning methods, including CNN–LSTM and transformer architectures enhance ECG analysis and plaque detection but face issues with data imbalance, heterogeneity, and explainability. Bridging these gaps requires sex-balanced datasets, fairness-aware algorithms, and inclusion of female biomarkers. Future research should focus on sex-stratified validation, transparent reporting frameworks like TRIPOD-AI and CONSORT-AI, and prospective clinical evaluation in women with clinical and ethical implementation issues.

title: Optimization of Operations in Industry 4.0 Settings with Real-Time Predictive Maintenance Working with Machine Learning
authors: S.B. Prakalya, V. Seethalakshmi, Murthoty Satheesh Babu, Zakaria Azzam, Ginni Nijhawan, M. Dinesh
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Since Industry 4.0 introduced cutting-edge technologies like IoT, Big Data, and AI into operational processes, the industrial and manufacturing sectors have changed drastically. Predictive maintenance with these technologies is promising. It reduces maintenance costs and downtime by predicting equipment failure using real-time data. In Industry 4.0, this project studies real-time predictive maintenance utilizing ML techniques to optimize industrial operations. IoT sensor data can be used to build predictive models to detect wear and malfunction in machines and tools. This makes procedures short and effective, allowing rapid action. Deep learning models, decision trees, regression models, and machine learning methods are used to analyze massive amounts of time-series data produced by industrial machinery. By finding new sensor data patterns and correlations, these systems may be able to predict equipment failure. Another essential part of the project is integrating predictive models with IoT systems. It tracks equipment health and operation in real time. Technology’s continual machine data analysis improves maintenance plans and reduces unscheduled downtime. It also sends out real-time alerts to users. Data quality, interpretability of models, and scalability are three more issues that this study attempts to resolve for predictive maintenance systems operating in complicated industrial settings. The findings show that in an Industry 4.0 environment, predictive maintenance driven by machine learning may greatly increase operational efficiency while decreasing costs and boosting total production.

title: Semantic Alignment of Multilingual Knowledge Graphs via Contextualized Vector Projections
authors: Abhishek Kumar
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: The paper presents our work on cross-lingual ontology alignment system which uses embedding based cosine similarity matching. The ontology entities are made contextually richer by creating descriptions using novel techniques. We use a fine-tuned transformer based multilingual model for generating better embeddings. We use cosine similarity to find positive ontology entities pairs and then apply threshold filtering to retain only highly similar entities. We have evaluated our work on OAEI-2022 multifarm track. We achieve 71% F1 score (78% recall and 65% precision) on the evaluation dataset, 16% increase from best baseline score. This suggests that our proposed alignment pipeline is able to capture the subtle cross-lingual similarities.

title: Mitigation of low-frequency oscillations at light loads in inverter-fed induction motors under constant V/f control
authors: Mohammad Khalilzadeh, Pooya Ghani, Hamidreza Hafezi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Induction motors in the mid- and high-power range are widely deployed across industrial sectors due to their robustness, efficiency, and cost-effectiveness. To enable variable-speed operation, these motors are typically paired with a drive system. However, a well-known challenge arises when operating inverter-fed induction motors under the constant volts-per-hertz control method in light-load and low-speed conditions: the system may exhibit sustained torque oscillations, which in severe cases can induce speed fluctuations and escalate into unstable behavior. This paper investigates the phenomenon mathematically and proposes a novel damping strategy based on a non-ideal proportional resonant controller. A simulation study conducted in MATLAB Simulink is used to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method. The results show a significant improvement in mitigating sustained low-frequency torque oscillations compared to conventional constant volts-per-hertz control.

title: A Novel Non-Isolated DC–DC Converter with Hybrid Inductor–Capacitor Stages for High-Gain EV Applications
authors: Seyed Moein Mortezaie, Mohammad Sarvi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: The rapid growth of electric transportation has intensified the demand for compact and efficient power conversion systems capable of delivering large voltage gains from low-voltage supplies. In this work, a new non-isolated converter topology is introduced, employing a hybrid combination of inductive and capacitive components arranged to maximize boosting capability. The architecture utilizes two independent duty ratios, allowing wide control flexibility and significant voltage enhancement without resorting to extreme switching operation. Unlike conventional transformer-based solutions, the proposed design minimizes voltage stress on the semiconductor devices and reduces the overall size of passive elements. Analytical derivations confirm the controllable gain characteristics of the system, which makes it well-suited for vehicle applications where both high efficiency and reliability are crucial. Simulation results and theoretical analysis verify the feasibility of the converter and demonstrate its potential for integration into advanced electric vehicle powertrain systems.

title: Contextual Understanding in Retrieval Augmented Generation: A Comparative Evaluation of BLOOM-560M and Pythia-410M on Bhagavad Gita Interpretation
authors: Divyansh Tuli, Hardik Yadav, Aryan Rawat
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Large Language Models have advanced a lot in natural language understanding and generation. But still their implementation in specialized fields needs further contextual understanding. Retrieval Augmented Generation has proven to be an important model for integrating external knowledge sources and generative models for generating more accurate and specialized responses. In this study, we evaluate the behavior of medium scale LLMs through a RAG pipeline for contextual question answering from the Bhagavad Gita. We curate a dataset from the Bhagavad Gita translations, interpretations and commentaries and build knowledge retrieval using FAISS vector databases and embeddings. We also test the models under different prompting techniques. Our study offers a benchmark for domain specific reasoning for RAG systems, studying trade offs between model selection and contextual accuracy. It also lays a foundation for further work using LLMs for spiritual and philosophical writing.

title: Surrogate-Assisted Reliability Analysis of Traction Induction Machine Used for Urban Bus with Reduced Design Parameters
authors: Amir Hossein Hajizamani, Amir Rashidi, Seyed Morteza Saghaian Nejad
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This study presents a reliability analysis framework for induction traction machines (ITMs), with a focus on fault probabilities arising from manufacturing tolerances. Sensitivity analysis is employed to identify the most influential input parameters, enabling dimensionality reduction and streamlining the computational process without compromising accuracy. Reliability is estimated using the Adaptive Response Surface Method (ARSM) combined with directional sampling, while the Moving Least Squares (MLS) surrogate model effectively captures the machine’s nonlinear behavior across multiple physical domains. The framework is evaluated over the full operating range of a 200 kW ITM equipped with spiral water jacket cooling, representative of systems used in heavy- duty electric urban buses. Results demonstrate that the proposed approach accurately quantifies fault probabilities under realistic production conditions, confirming its applicability for industrial-scale reliability assessments.

title: End-to-End Advanced Visual Speech Recognition Using 3D-CNN and BiLSTM with Beam Search Decoding
authors: Medha Mummigatti, Pankaj Saini, Burungale Swapnil Birudev
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This paper presents an advanced visual speech recognition, focusing on Beam Search decoding for sentence-level performance improving the accuracy. The proposed architecture combines 3D-CNN and BiLSTM layers along which is optimised for CTC loss function that employs an end-to-end learning on the GRID dataset. Experimental results indicate that Beam Search decoding significantly reduces the number of transcription errors, achieving a CER of 2.4 % and a WER of 9.0 %, outperforming traditional greedy decoding with accuracy of 96 %. Improved contextual understanding and robustness allow for more accurate lip reading in silent and noise-prone environments. Furthermore, Beam Search also enables the model to assess several possible decoding paths, which helps it maintain consistent sentencelevel predictions and recover from unclear visual cues. This is particularly helpful for phonemes that are visually similar, like m, b, and p, where greedy decoding frequently fails. Additionally, the system adapts to differences in lip motion and articulation across speakers in the GRID dataset. Overall, the integration of Beam Search strengthens contextual understanding and improves robustness without modifying the core architecture, making the approach suitable for silent communication, assistive technologies, and noise-resilient speech interfaces.

title: An Improved Force-Directed Layout for Interactive Visualization of Medical Knowledge Graphs
authors: Junqiang Zhao, Meng Chen, Zezhou Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Medical knowledge graphs contain heterogeneous entities and complex relationships, making visualization crucial for learning in medical education. Traditional force-directed algorithms struggle with large-scale graphs, leading to congestion, edge crossings, and slow convergence. This paper proposes an improved force-directed layout with three enhancements: (1) semantic weighting to cluster domain-specific entities, (2) damping and dynamic step-size control for faster, stable convergence, and (3) parallel computation with Web Workers to improve efficiency. Experiments on graphs of up to 10,000 nodes show significant improvements over Fruchterman-Reingold and ForceAtlas2 in frame rate, convergence time, and layout quality. A user study further confirms clearer structures and smoother interaction. The approach demonstrates both technical advantages and practical value for building effective medical education visualization systems.

title: XG-MSTA Machine Tool Anomaly Detection Method Based Multi-Modal Signals
authors: Xiyang Zhang, Feiyang Li, Yu Dong, Yi Hu, Yanqing Zhao, Yongze Ma
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Computer Numerical Control (CNC) machine tools are pivotal in modern manufacturing, yet their machining performance, precision, and service life are frequently compromised by anomalies in axis motion arising from assembly quality issues or wear due to prolonged use. To address the challenge of accurate anomaly detection in CNC machine tools, this study proposes a novel Cross-Modal Gated Multi-Scale Transformer (XG-MST) model, leveraging multi-axis vibration and current signals for supervised learning. Evaluated on a self-collected dataset derived from real-world CNC machine tool operations, the model achieves a detection accuracy of over 95%. Further validation through small-batch multi-axis simulation experiments on the open-source Paderborn Bearing Dataset yields an accuracy of over 90%. These results underscore the XG-MST algorithm's superior performance and significant practical value for enhancing machine tool quality inspection and predictive maintenance in manufacturing.

title: Optimization of Concrete Crack Feature Extraction in YOLO for High-Precision Crack Detection
authors: Yingbo Weng
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Crack identification in concrete structures plays a vital role in maintaining infrastructure safety. Nevertheless, current detection approaches struggle to accurately identify fine cracks in intricate backgrounds. To overcome this challenge, we introduce an enhanced YOLOv8-CRC series algorithm aimed at improving both detection accuracy and efficiency for concrete cracks. Integrating the CBAM attention mechanism strengthens the model's focus on crack-damaged areas, enhancing feature extraction in challenging environments. The backbone network incorporates ResNet50, utilizing depthwise separable convolutions and residual connections to strengthen the detection of fine cracks and mitigate information loss. Moreover, the CSPPC module is incorporated into the neck network to enhance feature fusion while minimizing redundant computations and memory usage. The experimental findings indicate that the YOLOv8-CRC algorithm exhibits outstanding performance in detecting concrete cracks. Compared to the standard YOLOv8, our model achieves enhancements of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12.6 \%, 5.6 \%, 6.1 \%$</tex> and 6.3% in precision, recall, mAP50, and mAP50-95, respectively. These findings confirm that the YOLOv8-CRC algorithm provides a reliable solution for automated concrete crack detection and lays a solid foundation for structural health monitoring systems.

title: Deepseek-R1 Enhanced Adaptive RAG Framework for Intelligent Optimization of Titanium Alloy Melting-Casting Processes
authors: Hongliang Wang, Binglong Ji, Haoran Wang, Jing Xu, Pengfei Xiu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This paper proposes an adaptive Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework integrated with domain-tuned DeepSeek-R1 (14B) for optimizing titanium alloy melting-casting parameters. Addressing critical industrial challenges including nonlinear thermal-stress coupling and realtime process control, our system implements a closed-loop “retrieve-generate-verify” workflow. Key innovations include: 1) Physics-constrained generation embedding metallurgical principles as differentiable loss functions; 2) Multi-scale knowledge retrieval combining ChromaDB-based case matching and knowledge graph reasoning; 3) Ray-accelerated finite element verification achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12.7 \times$</tex> simulation speedup. Validated on TC4/TC11 titanium alloys, the framework reduces optimization cycles by 37.6% while maintaining 92.4% parameter accuracy against physical constraints. Industrial trials demonstrate porosity reduction from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 2 \%}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 1 \%}$</tex> and dimensional accuracy improvement to IT6 tolerance class.

title: Trajectory-Tracking for Elbow Joint Robot Based on Riccati Equation and Iterative Learning Control
authors: Jinmiao Zhao, Hao Ding, Lu Song, Xin Gong, Xiaoxia Luo, Yiye Li, Erzhe Cheng, Da Xu, Yihan Ma, Wanying Gan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Trajectory tracking is one of the hot topics of robotic arms and is of great significance. It mainly refers to the coincidence of the trajectory of the robot arm with the given trajectory within a certain period of time. It is necessary to ensure that no collision occurs during the movement of the robotic arm and that it consumes less time. Trajectory tracking modeling is difficult and the algorithm is slow. In response to the above situation, this paper designs a disturbance Riccati iterative learning control (ILC) algorithm for trajectory tracking under the disturbance. When the elbow joint robot arm encounters external interference, it can also quickly adjust its trajectory through system stability.

title: Accelerating SPGD-AO Compensation: A High-Performance FPGA-Based Approach for Optical Communication
authors: Jiaming Liang, Yue Pan, Huan Chang, Ran Gao, Fei Wang, Qi Xu, Ting He, Dong Guo
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: The Stochastic Parallel Gradient Descent (SPGD) algorithm combines the advantages of stochastic gradient descent and parallel computing, and has been widely applied in adaptive optics. However, traditional SPGD algorithms suffer from long operation cycles and numerous iterations, making it difficult to meet the real-time requirements of adaptive optical systems in certain application scenarios. To address this issue, this paper proposes an FPGA-based adaptive optics scheme optimized with the Adam algorithm. By integrating the Adam optimizer to enhance the convergence efficiency of SPGD while leveraging the high-speed parallel computing and low-power characteristics of FPGAs, the proposed design significantly improves the system's real-time processing capability while effectively correcting beam distortions caused by atmospheric turbulence. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared with the MATLAB-based adaptive optics approach, the proposed system reduces the runtime per iteration from 501 ms to 5.7 ms with an error of approximately 0.1%, thereby verifying its accuracy and efficiency. This work provides an effective solution for developing high-performance and low-latency adaptive optics systems.

title: Smart Classroom Assistant Based on Raspberry PI
authors: Huijie Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In today's increasingly digital education background, traditional classroom management is faced with many challenges, including teachers' difficulty in effective management, students' low learning efficiency and low utilization of classroom resources. The advancement of science and technology and the improvement of the performance of various intelligent devices have provided great support for the realization of smart classroom solutions. In this project, we integrate Android Studio platform, SQLite database, TCP/IP communication technology and other advanced technologies to create intelligent classroom assistants for teachers and students. This paper aims to design a set of intelligent solutions, with Raspberry PI as the core, a variety of sensors and mobile application software, including real-time environmental parameter monitoring of temperature and humidity sensors, real-time monitoring of classroom seat use by infrared sensor components and user seat selection services. In the future, we will further expand and optimize the system functions, explore intelligent application scenarios to ensure cross-platform compatibility of the system, strengthen system security and privacy protection, ensure better support for teachers and students' daily learning, and achieve intelligence and efficiency. Contribute to better classroom utilization, more effective classroom management and more targeted instructional activity design.

title: Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)

title: Predictive Analytics for Sales Forecasting at Ono Cafe Using Linear Regression and Business Intelligence Dashboard
authors: Danial Shah Iman Bin Mohamad Noor Khairul Azhillah, Arifah Fasha Rosmani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: This study addresses the lack of systematic, datadriven sales forecasting practices among small food and beverage (F&B) enterprises, using Ono Café as the case context. Many SMEs continue to rely on manual judgment and intuition, resulting in inconsistent inventory planning and limited operational foresight. To mitigate these challenges, this study develops a predictive analytics framework using linear regression to forecast monthly sales based on historical data from 2023–2024. The research fills a gap in existing literature by exploring underexplored advanced forecasting tools in small food and beverage (F&B) businesses, despite their proven benefits in larger retail environments.
