Using RapidMiner, multiple regression models were tested across three data split configurations (70/30, 80/20, 90/10), with the 80/20 split demonstrating the strongest predictive performance (RMSE = 172.679, MAE = 125.279, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.991). A Power BI dashboard was subsequently deployed to provide managers with actionable insights through interactive visualizations. The findings demonstrate the practical value of integrating predictive modeling and business intelligence to enhance decision-making, operational efficiency, and strategic planning in F&B SMEs.

title: CogRec: Neural Cognitive-Style Modeling for Adaptive Learning Path Recommendation in Communication-Centric Environments
authors: Huiying Wan, Yanan Gong
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: In online learning platforms, intelligent recommendation algorithms are now an essential tool for boosting individualized learning experiences and increasing learning effectiveness. However, current approaches frequently ignore the cognitive distinctions across learners, which leads in recommendation outcomes that are not sufficiently flexible. This study suggests the CogRec (Cognitive Style-Aware Neural Recommendation) model as a solution to this problem. This model uses adaptive path optimization and multimodal cognitive style modeling to dynamically suggest learning paths. Eye movement signals, electroencephalogram (EEG) patterns, microexpression features, and learning behavior records are all used by CogRec to create a cognitive style vector. Additionally, it uses a self-attention technique to combine multimodal elements. At the modeling level, the model combines an extended restricted Boltzmann machine (RBM) with an attention-based heterogeneous graph neural network (A-HGNN). This approach captures the complex relationship between users and learning resources. Then, a cognitive style adaptation function is introduced to dynamically adjust the recommendation list based on task difficulty and individual processing preferences. This allows for the generation of personalized learning paths that are highly tailored to the learner's characteristics. The experimental part is verified based on two open education datasets (EdNet, MOOCCube) and a self-built multimodal dataset (CogLearn). At the same time, it is compared with four baseline methods: BPRMF, GRU4Rec, LightGCN and DGSR. Multi-dimensional indicators such as Precision@K, Recall@K, NDCG@K, ItemCoverage and Average-Popularity are used for comprehensive evaluation. The results show that CogRec outperforms the baseline model in accuracy and diversity indicators on all datasets. On the CogLearn dataset, CogRec's NDCG@10 is improved by about 36.5 % compared with DGSR, and it achieves the best performance in diversity and novelty indicators. The results of the ablation experiment further show that removing the cognitive style encoding module will result in a 21.7 % reduction in NDCG@10.

title: Forecasting UFO Sightings via Deep Learning and Natural Language Generation
authors: Lucas Costa, Komalpreet Kaur
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Reports of Unidentified Flying Objects (UFOs) are historically anecdotal and unstructured, lacking predictive frameworks capable of forecasting when, where, and how sightings may occur. This gap limits both scientific investigation and potential applications in aerospace monitoring and anomaly detection. This study introduces a deep learning system that predicts future UFO sightings based on historical data and generates related witness comments. The model uses Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to learn patterns over time and space, enabling accurate forecasts of where and when sightings may occur. In addition, a Natural Language Processing (NLP) pipeline creates text descriptions based on predicted features. To manage both numeric and textual outputs, a dual-branch architecture is implemented. The best-performing model achieved a mean absolute error of 0.23 for prediction tasks, which in one case study corresponded to a spatial deviation of approximately 240 km. Generated narratives were qualitatively coherent and semantically aligned with predicted attributes, demonstrating the feasibility of combining quantitative forecasting with descriptive synthesis. The system also includes a user-friendly interface built with Gradio and Folium, showing results on an interactive map. To our knowledge, this is the first deep learning system to combine temporal forecasting with predicted narrative generation for UFO sighting forecasting. This study demonstrates how machine learning can bring structure and prediction to a field previously based on anecdotal reports, with broader applications in anomaly detection and aerospace monitoring.

title: An Enhanced Lightweight Model for Real-time Pavement Condition Index Prediction
authors: Anthony Dontoh, Logan Sirbaugh, Andrews Danyo, Stephanie Ivey, Blessing Agyei Kyem, Armstrong Aboah, Jude Dontoh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: This study proposes a lightweight, attention-enhanced deep learning framework for real-time Pavement Condition Index (PCI) prediction from pavement imagery. The model integrates a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) into an EfficientNet-B0 backbone to enhance feature representation without significantly increasing computational cost. Extensive experiments conducted on a publicly available urban pavement dataset demonstrate that the proposed EfficientNet-B0 + CBAM architecture outperforms seven standard CNN baselines, achieving a test MAE of 9.102 and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.738. Statistical analysis confirms that these improvements are significant. The model maintains a compact size and inference efficiency, making it suitable for deployment on edge devices and UAVs. Future work will assess the model's reliability at PCI class boundaries and reframe PCI prediction as a classification task to better align with transportation agency decision-making practices.

title: Token-Invariant Contrastive Pretraining for Robust Domain Adaptation in Text Classification
authors: Aadhish C, Aravinthkumar P, Naagadiyanesh. N, Santhosh R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Domain adaptation is still an important issue in text classification, since models often break when faced with new words and semantic drifting. Current methods often only align high-level sentence or document embeddings while overlooking the evolution of individual token-level behavior, which forms the basic units of representation. This paper presents a lightweight, domain-aware Token Sorting Contrastive Pretraining (TSCP) framework to filter and prioritize domain-invariant tokens to facilitate representation learning to address distributional shifts. TSCP calculates the variance of token frequency between the source and target domains, emphasizing transferable and stable vocabulary via contrastive learning with a de-emphasis on domain-specific or noisy words. There is no modification to the architecture, nor the addition of parameters, making the approach feasible and efficient. We adopt two benchmarking datasets to evaluate TSCP: the Amazon Review Polarity dataset <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(3.6 \mathrm{m}$</tex> labeled sentences collected over 18 years) and the AG's News Topic Classification dataset (having more than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 m}$</tex> news passages). TSCP improves cross-domain sentiment classification accuracy on Amazon from 85.0% to 87.2% and on AG's News from 88.0% to 89.3%, compared with baselines including fine-tuned BERT and SimCSE. We conducted all experiments on an average consumer laptop (Ryzen 55500 U, 16 GB RAM) for accessibility purposes. TSCP outperforms the state-of-the-art models and emerging applications in sentiment analysis, news filtering, and content moderation. In the future, we plan to study dynamic token weighting, multilingual adaptability, and apply it to generative pretraining frameworks.

title: Efficient SOMP Algorithm Implement on FPGA for DOA Estimation
authors: Xiang Li, Mengjiang Sun, Peng Chen
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Due to the parallel characteristics and high-speed computing features, field programmable gate array (FPGA) is widely used in prototype development, with direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation being an important application area. However, existing high-precision DOA estimation algorithms suffer from high computational complexity, and thus direct implementation on FPGA often faces challenges such as insufficient resources and failure to meet real-time requirements. In this paper, an improved DOA estimation algorithm is proposed, which significantly reduces computational complexity compared to traditional algorithms without compromising estimation accuracy. Especially in the residual update part, the computational complexity of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex>-th iteration is reduced from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{O}\left(n^{3}+n^{2}+n\right)$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{O}(n)$</tex>. Then the parallel processing characteristics of FPGA are fully utilized to reduce the overall algorithm computation time, while the reusability of data in the algorithm is thoroughly explored to minimize resource usage and computational time. Finally, the data flow in FPGA is shown and test results are given to demonstrate the proposed algorithm can operate effectively after implemented on FPGA, and the usage of look-up tables (LUTs), digital signal processing blocks (DSPs), flip-flops (FFs) and block random access memory (BRAMs) are reduced <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$34.64 \%, 32.27 {\%}$</tex>, 30.50% and 38.71% respectively.

title: Transforming LLM Two-Stage Dialogue Summarization Techniques
authors: Vijay Kumar Joshi, Manish Motghare, Basudeb Dey, Nilesh Shelke, Nilesh Ashtankar, Syamprasad Guda
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Dialogue summarization is essential for distilling key insights from conversations in domains like customer service, education, and healthcare. This paper introduces the Dual Refinement Paradigm, a two-stage framework that enhances large language model performance through scene-aware context extraction, iterative text revision, and multi-task learning. Evaluated on a benchmark dataset, our approach achieves a ROUGE-L score of 48.2 and a novel composite M score of 0.84, outperforming established baselines. Key contributions include a scalable low-resource adaptation strategy, a new evaluation metric blending content fidelity and coherence, and an open-source implementation to foster further research in conversational AI.

title: Gamified Cognitive Interaction Design for Zakat Education: Usability and Learning Evaluation of a Digital Board Game
authors: Alias bin Deraman, Mohd Shukor Bin Abd Rahman, Nor Intan Shafini Nasaruddin, Mohd Ali bin Mohd Isa, Syahbudin Bin Senin, Nur Alya Batrisyia Ahmad Suhairi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: This study investigates the potential of gamified cognitive interaction design to enhance zakat education. A prototype board game was developed following the ADDIE instructional design framework and used as a research instrument to test learning outcomes and usability. Thirty students participated in experimental (gamified) and control (lecture-based) groups, with data collected through pre/posttests, the System Usability Scale (SUS), and classroom observations. The results showed a significant improvement in learning outcomes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{t}(\mathbf{1 4})=\mathbf{8. 1 2}, \mathbf{p}<\mathbf{0. 0 0 1})$</tex>, high usability (mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SUS}=88.2, \text{SD}=3.45$</tex>), and strong reliability (Cronbach's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha=0.89$</tex>). These findings suggest that gamified interaction design can effectively facilitate complex religious financial concepts and support sustainable, technologyenhanced learning.

title: A Systematic Mapping Study on Accessibility Design Guidelines of Assistive Technology for Students with Autism
authors: Muhammad Mu'izzuddin Mohd Yusri, Nor Shahida Mohamad Yusop
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Designing for accessibility is an important aspect in the development of assistive technology (AT) for individuals with autism spectrum disorder (ASD). Although technology continues to experience accessibility problems that are generally accepted without in society, especially in educational intitutions, these problems need more attention and systematic intervention. This research presents a systematic mapping study (SMS) that summarizes design recommendations aimed at improving the accessibility of assistive technology (AT) for autistic students. The research is important for the field of inclusive interaction design because it finds important accessibility factors that might make the user experience better and reduce cognitive load for students with autism to learn. This study seeks to enhance inclusive educational practices and facilitate the creation of functional, accessible technology designed to accommodate varied learning requirements by synthesising existing evidence. The results present six main categories of accessibility design guidelines and seven types of AT devices. The results are useful for teachers, therapists, and developers who work on creating, testing, and putting into use accessible assistive technology in education center.

title: Traffic Flow Monitoring and Congestion Control Using VANETs
authors: Sheenam Middha, Bobbinpreet Kaur, Narinder Kaur
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: VANETs, or vehicular ad-hoc networks, are a new way to think about Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS). They make it possible for automobiles and infrastructure to talk to each other quickly, which makes the roads safer, speeds up traffic, and makes people more aware of what's going on around them. This paper lays out a complete plan for employing VANETs to find and lessen traffic jams in a wide range of settings, such as cities, rural areas, and highways. We use SUMO and NS2 to assess how well vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V), vehicle-to-infrastructure (V2I), and infrastructure-to-infrastructure (I2I) communication models work in real-world traffic situations. Greedy +BFS uses AODV routing technology because it is flexible and performs well in networks that change all the time. The findings of the simulation show that VANETs can minimize traffic jams by sending more packets and lowering end-to-end latency in all scenarios. The research looks into a new way to employ VANET technologies to make it easier for boats to talk to each other, which will make fishing safer. The suggested paradigm lays the groundwork for smart, connected transportation systems on land and sea.

title: Predictive Analytics for Vehicle Utilization and Fleet Decision Support in Higher Education
authors: Sharifah Masyitah Binti Syed Mohamad, Norulhidayah Binti Isa, Syahrul Hezrin Mahmud
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Efficient vehicle utilization is vital for higher education institutions (HEIs) that rely on transportation for academic, administrative, and operational needs. Traditional manual fleet management methods often poor resource allocation and limited visibility. This study analyzes vehicle utilization and travel patterns in a HEI to support datadriven fleet management. Predictive analytics techniques were applied to historical vehicle logbook data (2023–2024) using machine learning models, including Random Forest, Decision Tree, and Support Vector Machine. Two experiments were conducted; classification of vehicles based on utilization trends and prediction of daily trip counts. Results demonstrated that Random Forest achieved the best performance, with an accuracy of 77.86% in the classification task and a root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.773 in the regression task. An interactive dashboard was developed using Power BI to visualize vehicle mileage, driver activity, and underutilized resources. The findings highlight the potential of integrating predictive analytics and dashboard visualization to improve fleet planning, optimize resource allocation, and enhance operational efficiency in higher education environments. Future work may include real-time tracking, automated data collection, and predictive fuel monitoring.

title: Analysis on the 3D Electromagnetic Characteristics of a Permanent Magnet Governor with a Double-Layer Permanent Magnet Rotor
authors: Ke Mei, Mengmeng Tian, Wenliang Zhao, Hailiang Cai, Pu Zhao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: Based on a permanent magnet governor (PMG) with a double-layer permanent magnet rotor, the three-dimensional finite element method (3D FEM) is adopted to investigate its electromagnetic characteristics in this paper. And comparing the results with those of the 2D finite element simulation. Firstly, the topology and operating principle of the studied PMG are described comprehensively. Then, a 3D model is established, and 3D FEM is adopted to analyze its electromagnetic characteristics of the two-layer PMRs at different relative positions, including magnetic flux density characteristics and mechanical characteristics. And comparing the results with those of the 2D finite element simulation, the results show that a certain deviation is observed due to the influence of end magnetic leakage.

title: Cross-platform digital economy data security sharing and modeling method integrating federated learning and privacy computing
authors: Yu Zhu
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: In the digital economy, cross-platform data sharing is the key to release data value. However, due to the difficulties of privacy protection regulations and heterogeneous data collaboration, the traditional centralized model faces compliance and efficiency challenges. Therefore, this paper proposes a cross-platform data security sharing and modeling method that integrates federated learning (FL) and privacy computing. This method constructs a hierarchical FL architecture, and solves the problem of heterogeneous data collaboration in financial, e-commerce, logistics and other platforms through the three-layer structure of local feature coding, cross-domain integration of graph attention network (GAT) and global prediction model, avoiding the limitation of forcing data schema unification and high-precision ID alignment. A dynamic differential privacy mechanism based on sensitivity evaluation is introduced, and the privacy budget is dynamically adjusted according to data types and training rounds, so as to balance the protection of highly sensitive data and the utility of low sensitive data. The lightweight mechanism of "knowledge distillation+sparse update" is designed. By screening the important parameters of Top-k and the knowledge transfer of teacher-student model, the communication overhead is reduced and the response speed of dynamic environment is improved. The experimental results show that the AUC of this method is 0.832 in the cross-platform user purchase prediction and credit risk assessment task, which is 23.1% higher than that of independent training. The single-round parameter transmission capacity is reduced to 14.2MB, the update time is shortened to 31s, and the convergence speed is increased by 171% in the scenario of sudden change of user preferences in the promotion season, which effectively supports the demand for safe data circulation and efficient modeling in the digital economy scenario.

title: Research on the Sentiment Analysis Algorithm of Works based on Deep Learning
authors: Muyang Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In the context of information explosion, sentiment analysis of works is crucial to content processing and value mining. This paper proposes a sentiment analysis algorithm based on multimodal fusion and dynamic attention. It extracts text semantic features by improving the Transformer architecture, obtains image visual features by using lightweight convolutional neural networks, extracts audio acoustic features by combining Mel-spectrograms and recurrent neural networks, and introduces a dynamic attention mechanism to adaptively focus on key sentiment information. Experiments were conducted on a public multimodal sentiment analysis dataset. Compared with six mainstream algorithms such as LSTM, BERT, and Text CNN, the algorithm in this paper achieved an accuracy of 93.2% in sentiment classification, which is 4.7 percentage points higher than the suboptimal algorithm, the F1 value is 92.1% and the recall rate is 91.8%, which are significantly better than the comparison algorithms. Ablation experiments show that multimodal fusion and dynamic attention mechanism increase 3.5% accuracy and 2.8% respectively, which proves the effectiveness of this module. Experimental results show that this algorithm is effective in integrating multimodal information and capturing sentiment features accurately.

title: Refining Data Fusion and Production Process Optimization Based on Multimodal Large Models
authors: Tao Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: This article focuses on the fusion of refining data and intelligent optimization of production processes based on multimodal large models. The refining production process is highly complex and strongly coupled, and abnormal working conditions are often sudden and diffuse. If not identified and intervened in a timely manner, it can easily trigger a chain reaction and cause serious losses. Therefore, this article proposes a deep learning (DL) anomaly monitoring framework that integrates multimodal data. In response to the bottleneck of short prediction cycle and low precision in traditional methods for abnormal data recovery, this paper constructs a time-series recovery model based on optimized long short-term memory network (LSTM). Firstly, principal component analysis (PCA) is used to reduce the dimensionality of high-dimensional data. Then, an LSTM network is constructed to capture temporal dependencies and integrate elastic network regularization to globally optimize hyperparameters, significantly improving the model's generalization ability and prediction stability. At the same time, in order to effectively process multi-source heterogeneous instrument image data on site, a lightweight YOLOv5s (You Only Look Once) network is introduced to achieve real-time visual monitoring and anomaly localization. The results indicate that the model can achieve high-precision and low latency recognition of abnormal states.

title: Organizing Committee
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)

title: Applications and Study of DenseNet-121 in Multi-class Chest X-ray Diagnosis
authors: Ling-Feng Chiang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: This study presents a systematic comparison of deep learning models for multi-class chest X-ray diagnosis, followed by a finding-level analysis. We adopt DenseNet-121 as a strong baseline and select the most comparable alternative— Swin+DenseNet+CrossAttention—as the primary comparator. Under an identical data split, we establish a train-validation-test pipeline with class-imbalance mitigation via weighted sampling. Training proceeds by freezing the backbone to learn the classification head, followed by full-model fine-tuning. We apply temperature scaling for probability calibration and report both overall and finding-level metrics, including Accuracy, Precision, Sensitivity, Specificity, F1, and AUC. To assess uncertainty and practical robustness, we define the T95 confidence threshold and Acc@95% coverage, and evaluate performance under brightness/contrast shifts, small rotations, Gaussian noise, and mild central cropping. Results show that DenseNet-121 achieves high accuracy and AUC overall and across most findings. Cross- attention fusion reaches comparable aggregate performance while exhibiting modest advantages in calibration and selective accuracy (T95 ≈ 0.59–0.63; Acc@95 ≈ 0.92–0.94), and it maintains slightly better median performance under several corruption settings. Finding-level analysis reveals different error profiles on harder classes, offering clinically meaningful guidance for model selection. Overall, DenseNet-121 balances effectiveness and efficiency; where clinical workflows demand tighter confidence thresholding and stronger shift robustness, cross-attention fusion provides added practical value. The proposed evaluation pipeline is fully reproducible and is intended to advance reliability and transparency in automated chest X-ray interpretation.

title: Research on Data Mining and Analysis Algorithm of Personnel Files in Big Data Environment
authors: Hong Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In the context of big data, the mining and analysis of personnel file data have emerged as crucial factors in enhancing the efficiency of human resource management. This treatise unveils an algorithm leveraging the Stacking-based ensemble learning approach to augment the prognostic precision of personnel file data analysis. This method combines XGBoost, random forest and logistic regression as the base classifier, and uses convolutional neural network as the meta-classifier to reduce the overfitting risk of a single model by learning the prediction pattern of the base classifier. To bolster the model's capacity for generalization, cross-validation is employed during the training phase, thereby ensuring the independence of the base classifier's training and validation processes.

title: CBBA-Based Task Planning for Heterogeneous Unmanned Surface Vehicles in Area Search Missions
authors: Yue Yang, Binyi Xia, Nanxuan Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: This paper presents an analysis and mathematical modelling of the cooperative area search problem employing heterogeneous unmanned surface vessels (USVs). By incorporating practical constraints such as communication range and communication quality during task allocation, the study establishes clear principles for task assignment and defines specific objectives for search trajectory planning. Subsequently, an enhanced Consensus-Based Bundle Algorithm (CBBA) with dynamic communication reallocation is proposed. This improved algorithm explicitly accounts for limited inter-platform communication ranges and incorporates a redefined objective function and an enhanced conflict resolution mechanism tailored to operational requirements. Finally, simulation results are presented to validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach under representative mission scenarios.

title: Pediatric T1D Copilot: LSTM-Driven Glucose Forecasting and Conversational Decision Support from Real-World CGM, Insulin, and Meal Streams
authors: Krishna Karan Ghantasala
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This paper presents a machine learning pipeline for pediatric type 1 diabetes that fuses continuous glucose monitoring, insulin delivery, and meal inputs to forecast short-term glycemia and deliver actionable recommendations through a safety-aware conversational interface. A stacked LSTM model trained on the AZT1D real-world dataset learns 12-step temporal contexts over 62 engineered features, with regularization, early stopping, and patient-wise validation; clinical evaluation reports MAE, RMSE, MARD, time-in-range, and error-grid analyses to align with bedside utility. The system operationalizes predictions into individualized guidance for dosing, nutrition timing, and activity via a pediatric-oriented chat UI, illustrating how sequence models can translate multi-modal diabetes telemetry into interpretable, point-of-care decision support in ML-enabled healthcare.

title: Design of Intelligent Report Automatic Generation System and Optimization of Generative Algorithm in Power Business Scenarios
authors: Li Chen, Hao Yang, Juntai Shi, Yixuan Chen, Xunquan Lu
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: The digital transformation of power industry challenges the value mining of massive data, and the traditional report generation mode is inefficient and difficult to meet the professional and real-time requirements. In this paper, an intelligent automatic report generation system driven by business rules and optimized by generative algorithm is proposed to solve the problems of low efficiency, poor accuracy and poor readability of report generation in power scene. The system architecture is divided into a data fusion layer, a business rule layer, an intelligent generation layer, and an application interaction layer. A dynamic template engine is constructed through a knowledge graph, and combined with a rule engine and a domain fine tuned Large Language Model (LLM) to achieve automatic generation, verification, and optimization of reports. The experimental results show that the system can significantly improve the efficiency of report generation, reduce error rates, and support human-machine collaboration, providing an effective tool for the digital transformation of the power industry.

title: Instrument Development for Measuring the Effectiveness of Government Campaigns in Scam and Fraud Prevention: An Extension of Protection Motivation Theory
authors: Akhyari Nasir, Mohd Shah Shafie Idris, Muriati Muda
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: The growing frequency of online scams and fraud has caused governments all over the world to undertake public awareness programs to encourage internet users to be more cautious. However, there is a major gap in assessing the impact of these programs. This study fills that gap by creating a theorybased and empirically informed tool for assessing the impact of government-led scam and fraud prevention programs on individuals' intentions to participate in protective behavior. Based on Protection Motivation Theory, the measure includes two fundamental cognitive appraisal constructs: perceived severity and perceived vulnerability. These are supported by three communication-related variables: campaign exposure, message understanding, and message credibility. A systematic approach was followed to operationalize each construct, using established definitions and adapting items to suit the context of scam and fraud prevention. The final measurement model consists of six constructs and eighteen items, reflecting the complex relationship between threat appraisal and communication in shaping protective behavior. The proposed instrument positions campaign exposure, credibility, and message clarity as key antecedents to threat appraisal, offering a structured basis for evaluating the effectiveness of scam prevention messages within a PMT-based research model. This study provides a robust tool for researchers and policymakers to assess the impact of public campaigns and offers practical insights into the design of effective communication strategies for cyber fraud prevention.

title: Evaluating Lightweight Vision Transformers for Chest Disease Detection in Low-Resource Clinical Settings
authors: Mridul Banik, Ismail Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive evaluation of lightweight Vision Transformers (ViTs) for chest disease detection in low-resource clinical settings using the Indiana Chest X-ray Reports dataset. The research addresses the critical need for computationally efficient diagnostic models that can operate effectively in resource-constrained healthcare environments. Through systematic preprocessing and exploratory data analysis, 349 validated normal chest X-ray cases were analyzed across ten distinct radiological finding categories. The dataset demonstrated excellent characteristics for training lightweight ViTs, with balanced distribution (CV = 0.47) and strong clinical indication-finding correlations (r ¿ 0.6, p ¡ 0.05). Performance evaluation revealed that lightweight Vision Transformers achieved promising diagnostic accuracy with precision scores reaching 1.0, while maintaining computational efficiency suitable for deployment in low-resource settings. The keyword extraction algorithm successfully identified medical conditions with 100% data quality assurance following preprocessing. Statistical analysis confirmed dataset suitability for machine learning applications, with comprehensive terminological diversity (847 unique medical terms) and clinically meaningful correlations between indications and findings. The findings demonstrate that lightweight Vision Transformers represent a viable solution for addressing diagnostic challenges in resource-limited healthcare environments, offering an optimal balance between diagnostic accuracy and computational efficiency. This research contributes to the broader goal of democratizing AI-powered healthcare solutions and reducing diagnostic disparities in underserved populations worldwide.

title: Research on Intelligent Storage and Structure Reconstruction of Multi-source Heterogeneous Data in Power Grid Based on Graph Neural Network
authors: Bin Liu, Yong Feng, Yang Yu, Gang Luo, Chunyang Zhang, Jianbo Lu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: An intelligent storage and structure reconstruction method of multi-source heterogeneous data in power grid based on graph neural network is proposed. Firstly, a three-mode unified graph model of topology-measurement-image is constructed, the time series and image features are extracted by 1D-CNN and ResNet, and cross-modal semantic fusion is realized through the attention mechanism of multi-head graph, which improves data utilization and fault location accuracy. Secondly, a graph compression storage architecture based on Laplacian spectral clustering is designed, which compresses the 12 000-node power grid to 3.1 TB, which is only 19.6% of the total graph storage. With the incremental Delta update and loading according to the dynamic subgraph of electrical distance, the delay of querying P99 is reduced to 32.5 ms, which meets the demand of millisecond real-time decision. Finally, markov decision processes (MDP) is used to describe the reconfiguration task, and a hybrid strategy network of GRAPHAGE-Reinforcement Learning (RL) is adopted to pre-train offline and fine-tune online security constraints, so as to achieve 3.8 s rapid recovery, 96.5% load recovery rate and 0.1 times/scene security violation in fault scenarios. The experiment is based on 12 TB SCADA data, 120,000 infrared images and 3,485 fault records. The results show that the fault location accuracy of this method is 93.7%, F1 is 0.929, and the reasoning delay is 32.6 ms, which is significantly improved compared with the benchmarks such as CNN+LSTM and traditional GCN, and provides a feasible technical path for the transformation of power grid from "data-driven" to "knowledge-driven".

title: Improved YOLOv8-Based Surface Defect Detection for Aluminum Alloy Profiles
authors: Xiaoyu Li, Xiaojun Xia, Ying Gao, Hejian Sun
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Aluminum alloy profiles are critical in industries such as aerospace and automotive manufacturing due to their lightweight, high strength, and corrosion resistance. However, detecting small-sized and low-contrast surface defects remains challenging, with traditional methods suffering from high false detection rates 15%–20% and limited adaptability to multi-scale defects. This paper proposes an enhanced YOLOv8-based algorithm for aluminum alloy profile defect detection, addressing issues like small defect detection, class imbalance, and discrimination of similar defects. Three key improvements are introduced: (1) a C2f-SE module integrating Squeeze-and-Excitation attention to enhance fine defect feature extraction, (2) an Asymptotic Feature Pyramid Network (AFPN) for improved multi-scale feature fusion, and (3) a multi-component joint loss function incorporating category-adaptive focal loss, scale-aware IoU loss, and contrastive classification loss to optimize training. Experiments on the Tianchi Aluminum Profile Surface Defect Dataset demonstrate significant improvements, with the proposed algorithm achieving an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> of 72.45% (up from 56.74%), a 43.9% increase in small defect recall, and a 24.13% AP for challenging defects like bruises. These results highlight the algorithm's potential for high-precision, real-time industrial defect detection.

title: Blockchain-Based Voting System with OTP Authentication and Off-Chain Privacy Preservation
authors: Rahul Aravindh M, Prasannavelan R M, Pradeep N, K. Malathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: A secure and transparent blockchain-based voting system is proposed, designed to preserve voter anonymity, prevent tampering, and ensure one-vote-per-user compliance in decentralized digital elections. The system introduces a lightweight voter authentication layer using one-time password (OTP) verification, with off-chain hashed identity storage to prevent exposure of personal data. Unlike traditional models that rely solely on smart contract logic, this approach strengthens the end-to-end security boundary by validating user eligibility before on-chain interaction. Votes are cast through smart contracts deployed on a public blockchain, ensuring immutability and auditability, while maintaining voter anonymity by detaching authentication logic from vote recording. To address performance bottlenecks and storage limitations, non-critical identity data is excluded from the blockchain, with hashed authentication tokens acting as cryptographic proofs of voter legitimacy. The proposed method was validated through simulation of small-scale voting rounds, demonstrating secure vote casting with a rejection rate of 100% for duplicate or invalid attempts. Average authentication time remained under 200 milliseconds per session. The modular design facilitates integration with government or institutional ID systems and supports anonymous and verified voting modes, making it adaptable for educational, corporate, or civic deployment. Future iterations will explore zero-knowledge proofs to further enhance privacy guarantees while preserving voter eligibility validation.

title: Corrections to “Artificial Intelligence in Quantum Communications: A Comprehensive Survey”
authors: Istiak Mahmud, Ahmed Abdelhadi
abstract: Presents corrections to the paper, (Artificial Intelligence in Quantum Communications: A Comprehensive Survey).

title: Resource Allocation for IRS-Assisted Secure NOMA-ISAC Network
authors: Zhengqiang Wang, Miao Chen, Yongjun Xu, Haibo Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: In the design of dual-function waveforms for an integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system, confidential information transmitted by the base station (BS) to legitimate users is designed to be embedded in the radar sensing signal. This generates a critical security concern, as the information becomes vulnerable to eavesdropping by the sensing target, threatening the physical layer security of the system. To address this problem, this paper introduces a non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) protocol to mitigate the interference problem in the multiple-input single-output ISAC network. Additionally, the sensing component within the dual-function waveforms is employed not only for target detection but also as artificial noise to interfere with the potential eavesdropper. Furthermore, deploying an intelligent reconfigurable surface (IRS) with optimized phase design in the ISAC network can further enhance the communication security by reshaping the wireless environment. For the IRS-assisted multi-user NOMA-ISAC network, we jointly optimize the resource allocation problem for the precoding matrix of the BS and the phase shift matrix of the IRS, considering constraints including decoding order, quality of service for legitimate users, security, sensing performance, and upper bound on the transmit power, to maximize system throughput. Given the non-convex nature of the optimization problem, an efficient joint optimization algorithm based on semidefinite relaxation and alternating optimization algorithm is proposed to find the solution for the problem. Simulation results validate the convergence of the proposed algorithm and demonstrate its superiority over other benchmark algorithms.

title: Design and Optimization of the Simultaneous Wireless Power and Data Transfer System Based on Power Channel Harmonic Modulation
authors: Zhijun Wu, Linlin Tan, Heqi Xu, Xueliang Huang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: To enhance the reliability of simultaneous wireless power and data transfer (SWPDT) systems and meet the structural compactness required for miniaturized applications, this paper proposes an SWPDT system based on power channel harmonic modulation. The proposed system adopts a symmetrical dual-sided LCL compensation topology and utilizes its resonant characteristics and coupling parameter tuning to enable data transmission without introducing an additional information carrier source. The method allows simultaneous power and data transfer without interfering with the power channel, while significantly simplifying the information channel and improving system integrability and engineering feasibility. Frequency-domain modeling and analysis of both the power and data channels are conducted to identify and optimize key parameters affecting system performance. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that the prototype system achieves 277 W power transfer with a DC–DC efficiency exceeding 88%, and supports half-duplex information transmission with a forward rate of 100 kbps and a reverse rate of 50 kbps, thereby validating the feasibility and practicality of the proposed approach.

title: Turn Your Data into Dashboards
authors: Miranda Carney-Morris, Karl Owens, Scott Trimmer
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 ACM SIGUCCS Annual Conference
abstract: None

title: APEX-DE: Adaptive Parameter Control and Selection Strategy for Differential Evolution With Exponential Crossover_supp1-3673122.pdf
authors: Zhenyu Meng
abstract: Since the proposal of the differential evolution (DE) algorithm, most improvement efforts have concentrated on binomial crossover. However, we find that DE variants using exponential crossover can also outperform those using binomial crossover in optimization performance, provided that an appropriate parameter control scheme is employed. To develop a high-performance DE algorithm with exponential crossover, this article introduces an adaptive parameter control and selection strategy for DE with exponential crossover (APEX-DE). The main contributions of APEX-DE are summarized as follows: First, a novel adaptive parameter control (APC) technique is proposed, incorporating an automatically generated crossover rate <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$CR$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, a dual-stage scale factor <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$F$</tex-math> </inline-formula> generation mechanism, and a new adaptive strategy for the scale parameter <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sigma _{F}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. Second, a novel selection mechanism is introduced to replace the classical DE selection, thereby enhancing the ability to escape from local optima. Third, a redirection strategy is developed to regenerate individuals and adjust their scale factor <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$F$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, enhancing evolutionary potential. APEX-DE is evaluated on a large test suite comprising 88 benchmark functions, as well as on a challenging uncrewed aerial vehicle (UAV) path-planning task in multithreat environments. Experimental evaluation confirms that APEX-DE delivers better performance than a broad set of state-of-the-art algorithms.

authors: Imlijungla Longchar, Hemangee K. Kapoor
