title: Dynamic Trust-Driven Blockchain Federated Learning System for Medical Insurance Fraud Detection
authors: Jeevarathinam M, Kumaravelan G
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Medical insurance fraud is on the increase due to the increased digitization of the transactions and the exposure of sensitive medical data. The paper will introduce a Dynamic Trust-Aware Blockchain Federated Learning (DT-BFL) framework that is capable of detecting fraud in insurance claims, not to mention data privacy. The framework is a federation of learning to train decentralized models using blockchain to allow transparent and tamper-proof record keeping. The contribution of each participant is modulated by a dynamic trust-based weighting system according to its reliability and minimized according to malicious or poisoned updates. A synthetic insurance dataset test demonstrates that DT-BFL has an accuracy of 96.4 % and F1-score of 94.8 %, which is also superior over the conventional FedAvg and Biscotti algorithms. The results verify the usefulness of the framework in both cases of data-poisoning attacks and its ability to offer high privacy protection and accountable model governances.

title: GFT-CRN: A Group Frequency-Temporal Convolutional Recurrent Network for Lightweight Speech Enhancement
authors: Chuanbo Liu, Yunxia Yu, Lin Li, Shuyun Jiang, Quanbin Li
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Edge computing scenarios impose strict resource constraints on single-channel speech enhancement. While existing lightweight models like GTCRN achieve low complexity, their "heavy temporal, light frequency" architecture fails to capture long-range frequency dependencies, causing spectral damage in non-stationary noise. To address this, we propose the Group Frequency-Temporal Convolutional Recurrent Network (GFT-CRN). By introducing a parameter-efficient Lightweight Frequency Domain Attention (LFDA) module, GFT-CRN achieves global frequency modeling via time-dimension compression, complementing temporal attention. Additionally, a hybrid loss function integrating perceptual and multi-scale STFT losses is designed to enhance auditory quality. Experiments show that GFT-CRN outperforms baselines in PESQ and SISNR with negligible computational increase (only 0.1 MMACs), establishing a new benchmark for ultra-lightweight speech enhancement.

title: Research on YOLOv8 Fire Detection Algorithm based on Multi-Module Improvement
authors: Jiamiao Lu, Faxiang He
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Frequent fires and delayed initial detection can lead to the escalation of fire incidents, making the development of precise and efficient fire detection technologies of great significance. To enhance the performance of the YOLOv8 algorithm in fire detection, this study proposes optimizing the input image quality through LAB-CLAHE contrast enhancement, replacing traditional convolutions with GSConv, incorporating the ECA attention mechanism to improve the backbone network, fusing multi-scale features to optimize the neck network, adding a small-sized detection layer and anchor boxes to improve the head network, and thereby enhancing the YOLOv8 fire detection algorithm. Ablation experiments show that the improved model outperforms baseline YOLOv8 in terms of accuracy, recall rate, mAP50, and mAP50-95 by 0.3%, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.1 \%, 1.2 \%$</tex>, and 1%, respectively. The confidence level of targets in complex environments is significantly improved, providing technical support for three-dimensional fire prevention and early warning systems, thereby enhancing early warning efficiency and reducing fire-related losses.

title: Performance Evaluation Ensembling of CNN Models for KL-based Knee Osteoarthritis Grading
authors: V. Vijaya Kishore, M.Indu Priya, P.Venkata Rohith, M.Siva Diwakar Reddy, K.Tharik Baba Tajuddin
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Knee osteoarthritis (KOA) is a progressive degenerative joint disorder that affects millions worldwide and leads to substantial pain and mobility limitations. Timely and accurate grading is essential for improving clinical decision-making and slowing disease progression. The Kellgren–Lawrence (KL) grading system is widely used to assess KOA severity from X-ray images, but manual grading suffers from observer variability and inconsistency. This study investigates an ensemble-based deep learning framework for automatic KL grade prediction using transfer learning on eight convolutional neural network architectures: NASNetLarge, ResNet101V2, InceptionV3, ResNet152V2, DenseNet169, NASNetMobile, ResNet50V2, and InceptionResNetV2. The pretrained backbone layers are frozen, while custom classification heads with three activation function schemes (AFS-1, AFS-2, and AFS-3) employing Swish, ReLU, and ELU are explored to enhance representational capacity. A soft-voting ensemble combines the individual models, and the final sigmoid layer outputs KL grades 0–4. This work addresses the lack of clear criteria for activation-function selection by systematically rotating activation functions within an ensemble framework to improve robustness and KOA severity classification performance.

title: Research and Application of the Dynamic Adjustment Mechanism of Substation Project Cost Index under Market-Oriented Reform
authors: Chao Ge, Yishu Fu, Jin Pan, Qi Xue, Weimao Xu, Junru Lin, Xiaodong Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: To address the challenges of price fluctuations brought by the liberalization of the electricity market, this paper proposes and verifies a substation project payment adjustment mechanism based on a dynamic cost index. Addressing the shortcomings of traditional static management methods, the study innovatively integrates Gradient Boosting Decision Tree to construct a dynamic weighted cost index, overcoming the rigidity of traditional fixed-weight indices and basic time-series models. This endows the index with short-term forecasting capability, achieving a fundamental shift from passive, lagging compensation to active, forward-looking prediction. On this basis, a contract payment adjustment formula linked to the index and a standardized implementation process were designed. Case studies show that this mechanism can more sensitively capture cost changes, effectively improve investment control accuracy, and provide an innovative solution for market-oriented cost management in power grid projects.

title: Data-driven core loss modeling of magnetic components
authors: Manman Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Magnetic components are critical for energy transmission, storage, and filtering, with core loss directly impacting power converter efficiency and volume. To address the low accuracy of traditional models under complex working conditions, this paper proposes a data-driven modeling and optimization method. First, an ensemble learning-based excitation waveform classification model is built using SVM, random forest (RF), and KNN as base classifiers, combined with Voting strategy to enhance generalization via feature fusion. Second, a Stackingbased core loss prediction framework is designed, with XGBoost and RF as base learners and XGBoost as the meta-model, outperforming traditional methods. Finally, NSGA-II algorithm realizes multi-objective optimization of core loss and transmitted magnetic energy, obtaining the Pareto optimal solution set. Experiments validate the model’s superior classification accuracy, prediction performance, and optimization effect, providing a reliable tool for efficient magnetic component design.

title: Memory-Driven Agent Planning for Long-Horizon Tasks via Hierarchical Encoding and Dynamic Retrieval
authors: Yutong Wang, Ruobing Yan, Yujie Xiao, Jinming Li, Zizhao Zhang, Feiyang Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: This study addresses the challenge of long-term dependency modeling in agent behavior planning for long-horizon tasks and proposes a memory-driven agent planning framework. The method introduces hierarchical memory encoding and dynamic memory retrieval structures, enabling the agent to selectively retain and effectively utilize historical information across multiple time scales, thereby maintaining policy stability and goal consistency in complex dynamic environments. The core idea is to construct an interaction mechanism between short-term and long-term memory, where attention-guided retrieval integrates historical experience with current perception to support continuous planning and decision optimization in long-term tasks. The proposed framework consists of four key modules: perception input, memory encoding, state updating, and behavior generation, forming an end-to-end task-driven learning process. Experimental evaluations based on success rate, average planning steps, memory consistency score, and policy stability demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves superior performance in long-term task scenarios, effectively reducing planning redundancy and improving strategy coherence and task efficiency. The results confirm that the memory-driven mechanism provides a novel theoretical foundation and algorithmic framework for developing long-term task agents, establishing a solid basis for adaptive decision-making and continuous planning in complex environments.

title: PBEC-Net: An Emotion-Aware Personalised Prediction Model under Multimodal Attention Mechanisms
authors: Tiantian Zhang, JiaQi Zhao, Chengrui Li, Duo Xu, Xinyue Wang, Yao Xiao
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: The effective integration of dynamic behavioural data with static individual characteristics represents a key challenge in the field of multimodal feature learning. Its core difficulty lies in precisely correlating temporal dynamic information with static prior knowledge to enhance performance in downstream predictive tasks. To address this issue, this study proposes the Personality-Behaviour-Emotion Coupling Network (PBEC-Net). Its architecture, built upon multimodal fusion and attention mechanisms, achieves deep coupling between dynamic behavioural sequences and static features. Utilising static features as prior knowledge, the model dynamically assigns weights to behavioural features across different temporal phases via attention mechanisms, thereby strengthening representations of behavioural patterns highly correlated with static features. It generates context vectors fusing bimodal information through feature encoding and coupling mechanisms, combining these with a multi-layer perceptron for prediction outputs. Experimental validation demonstrates that PBEC-Net efficiently achieves collaborative modelling of dynamic and static features, significantly improving prediction task accuracy. This provides an effective technical solution for the integrated modelling of multimodal temporal data and static features.

title: MicroEthics: Harnessing Small Data Intelligence to Recode Big Data Ethics for a Responsible Digital Future
authors: Rahul Jalinder Jadhav, Kondragunta Rama Krishnaiah, Suhalekhya Immadisetty, Jayendra Gopal Thatipudi, Lalitha Palthiya, Shrikant Upadhyay
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The exponential growth of big data has revolutionized AI, yet it has simultaneously triggered ethical concerns involving over-collection, privacy erosion, opaque decision-making, and algorithmic bias. Here, the paper proposes changing the paradigm of data maximalism to small data intelligence (SDI)-based MicroEthics, which is more ethical-minimalist, transparent, and stakeholder-trustful. The proposed system does not use massive datasets but rather uses ethically curated low volume data with context, processed on interpretable AI models embedded on federated learning environments. There is a custom lightweight decision engine, Symbolic Decision Matrix Engine (SDME), that makes decisions without compromising privacy or explanations. In several fields such as healthcare, finance, and digital governance, the MicroEthics was compared to the conventional big data pipelines. The findings demonstrate a federated model accuracy of 93.2 with privacy compliance score of 0.97 that is better than centralized big data systems with privacy compliance score of 0.71. MicroEthics Ethical Risk Scores were 0.91, which is significantly more than 0.65 of the traditional models. Stakeholder ratings ensured increased transparency (0.93) and user trust (0.91) using MicroEthics than the big data methods. This paper confirms that MicroEthics is a practical ethical framework that unites the utility of AI with human values. Not only does it show similar performance, but it goes higher in terms of ethical compliance and reliability than those that are already in place. The solution is scalable, modular and easily applicable in areas where moral accountability and data sensitivity are paramount. The results promote the concept of a responsible transition to ethical AI frameworks that do not rely on the quantity of data, but on data virtue.

title: Machine Learning Approaches for Early Warning of Stock Market Crashes
authors: Wafae Saifi, Said Nouh, Said Bahassine, Imrane Chemseddine Idrissi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Crashes in the stock market are uncommon but powerful events that provide considerable challenge for quantitative finance. This work investigates the performance of seven machine learning models for early detection. Data were collected from 32 worldwide stock indices covering the timeframe from January 1, 2015, through January 1, 2025. Our method addresses the problem of imbalanced data by the application of the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique, facilitating effective learning from rare instances. Empirical evidence for both one- and five-day intervals indicates that tree-based ensemble models consistently outperform alternative methods. The Extra Trees Classifier had strong performance for one-day predictions, with an F1-score of 0.97. Despite a slight decrease, this model maintained a high F1-score of 0.96 over the five-day period, a commendable outcome given the increased complexity. The slight difference in performance between the two periods suggests that the importance of predictive characteristics diminishes over time, highlighting the inherent challenges of long-term forecasting. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of machine learning in creating reliable warning systems for risk management.

title: An Efficient Methodology to Develop Data Encryption Algorithms for IoT Networks
authors: Swapna Rani Thandra, M Rajendra Prasad, Maznu Shaik, K Rajasekhar, Pavan Kumar Tatavarathi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Enhancement of data security in IoT networks by implementing cryptographic algorithms and functions to encrypt data transmitted by IoT systems over Wi-Fi networks is proposed. Since there is not much attention given to data security while using IoT, there are chances of data theft as hackers target the vulnerable devices and valuable data can be stolen. If plain text or strings are transmitted over IoT networks, it is easily identified by hackers. The goal is to address these challenges by implementing data encryption mechanisms for IoT networks and to protect sensitive data from unauthorized access by ensuring that intercepted data cannot be understood by potential attackers. The proposed methodology is to create a comprehensive encryption mechanism for safeguarding data stored in ESP8266 and transmitted over networks. The proposed approach has Caesar’s Cipher, and custom encryption (XOR) methods to ensure maximum data protection. Additionally, the mechanism will include encryption for string data received from a dedicated webpage, simulating real-life scenarios where users utilize a custom webpage to communicate with the ESP micro-controller. The primary purpose of this research is to develop a robust encryption solution that renders data incomprehensible to unauthorized users.

title: Deep Neural Network–based Retail Business Analysis using AI for Smart Environments and IoT
authors: B Eswaran, K.Vengatesan, Sayyad Samee, Kabir G. Kharade, M. Rajesh, Rupam Bhagawati
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Integration of data preprocessing, dimensionality reduction, clustering, association rule mining, and predictive modelling are all part of this study's deep neural network–based machine learning method for retail business analysis. Customer purchasing behavior is used to efficiently segment them using Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and K-Means clustering, which enhances personalized marketing efforts. Using data-driven inventory management and targeted advertising, the Apriori algorithm discovers common item sets. To provide accurate demand and sales forecasting, a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) model is used for predictive analytics. The model shows great performance with a low Mean Absolute Error (0.0898) and test loss (0.0293). A potent tool for retail decision-making, the optimized parameters of the structured deep learning model allow it to generalize to unseen data with ease. In the end, this method boosts inventory optimization, pricing tactics, and consumer insights, which leads to an increase in income. To enhance the accuracy and flexibility of predictions in retail settings, future developments might involve hyper parameter tweaking and the inclusion of real-time information.

title: Adaptive AI-Driven Threat Intelligence Framework for Proactive Cyber Defense in Dynamic Network Environments
authors: Mohit Garg, Phani Rohitha Kaza, Akash Kilaru, Syed Sadique Basha, Chaitanya Gunupudi, Kalyana Krishna Kondapalli
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The rapid evolution of dynamic network infrastructures has intensified exposure to sophisticated cyber threats, including zero-day attacks and advanced persistent threats, which traditional signature-based security mechanisms fail to address effectively. This paper proposes an Adaptive AI-Driven Threat Intelligence Framework (AID-TIF) that integrates machine learning, deep learning, reinforcement learning, and real-time threat intelligence to enable proactive cyber defense. The framework continuously monitors network traffic and system behavior, detects both known and unknown threats in real time, and dynamically adapts defense strategies based on evolving attack patterns. AID-TIF was implemented and evaluated in realistic network environments against multiple attack scenarios, including DDoS attacks, malware propagation, and insider threats. Experimental results demonstrate a detection accuracy of 95% for known attacks and 85% for previously unseen threats, with a low false positive rate of 3%. Additionally, the framework achieves rapid threat detection within 1.5 seconds and automated mitigation within 5 seconds, significantly reducing potential damage and downtime. The key contributions of this work include a hybrid AI-based adaptive architecture, seamless integration of external threat intelligence, and automated real-time mitigation. Overall, AID-TIF offers a scalable, resilient, and forward-looking cybersecurity solution for modern network environments.

title: Design and Verification of Intelligent Scheduling Algorithm for Aeronautical Precision Hydraulic Parts Processing for Digital Workshop
authors: Ziyang Liu, Zhihua Wang, Shaohua Ma
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: This article proposes an intelligent scheduling algorithm (MOALQR) for digital workshops. This algorithm is based on digital twin technology to obtain real-time production data, and integrates improved MOEA/D algorithm (Multiobjective Evolutionary Algorithm Based on Decomposition), adaptive large neighborhood search (ALNS), and Q-learning agents to achieve multi-objective collaborative optimization of production efficiency, equipment utilization, and quality risk. By constructing a scheduling model including quality risk transfer mechanism and green energy consumption constraints, the algorithm significantly improves the dynamic response ability. The experimental verification shows that the average delivery punctuality rate of MOALQR in the actual data of aviation enterprises is 98.4%, the equipment utilization rate is 89.7%, the quality risk is reduced by 47.2%~57.0%, and the response time to equipment failure and other emergencies is shortened by more than 77% compared with the traditional methods. The industrial field application further proves that the algorithm can shorten the delivery cycle by 22.3%, reduce the scrap rate of parts to 1.3%, and reduce the unit energy consumption cost by 18.3%, which provides an efficient and stable scheduling solution for the intelligent transformation of aviation manufacturing industry.

title: ViMoNet: A Multimodal Vision-Language Framework for Human Behavior Understanding from Motion and Video
authors: Rajan Das Gupta, Lei Wei, Md Yeasin Rahat, Nafiz Fahad, Abir Ahmed, Tze Hui Liew
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: This study investigates the use of large language models (LLMs) for human behavior understanding by jointly leveraging motion and video data. We argue that integrating these complementary modalities is essential for capturing both fine-grained motion dynamics and contextual semantics of human actions, addressing the limitations of prior motion-only or video- only approaches. To this end, we propose ViMoNet, a multimodal vision-language framework trained through a two-stage alignment and instruction-tuning strategy that combines precise motion-text supervision with large-scale video-text data. We further introduce VIMOS, a multimodal dataset comprising human motion sequences, videos, and instruction-level annotations, along with ViMoNet-Bench, a standardized benchmark for evaluating behavior-centric reasoning. Experimental results demonstrate that ViMoNet consistently outperforms existing methods across caption generation, motion understanding, and human behavior interpretation tasks. The proposed framework shows significant potential in assistive healthcare applications, such as elderly monitoring, fall detection, and early identification of health risks in aging populations. This work contributes to United Nations Sustainable Development Goal 3 (SDG 3: Good Health and Well-being) by enabling accessible AI-driven tools that promote universal health coverage, reduce preventable health issues, and enhance overall well-being.

title: Distributed Channel Estimation for Grant-Free Cell-Free Massive MIMO Systems
authors: Zilu Zhao, Dirk Slock
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: We investigate semi-blind channel estimation in Grant-Free Cell-Free Massive Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (GF-CF-MaMIMO) systems. In cell-free architectures, pilot contamination makes pilot-based channel estimation insufficient. As the Internet of Things (IoT) continues to expand, the Grant-Free (GF) access scheme has gained attention for its ability to support massive device connectivity. However, integrating GF access with CF-MaMIMO introduces new challenges, particularly in designing distributed algorithms for user activity detection and pilot contamination mitigation. Variable-level expectation propagation (VL-EP) is a promising bilinear estimation method based on expectation-maximization, but its high computational complexity, primarily due to matrix inversion, limits its practical applicability. Meanwhile, previously proposed iterative maximum likelihood (ML) methods for activity detection require careful initialization to perform well. In this paper, we focus on channel estimation in GF-CF-MaMIMO systems by treating both user activity and transmitted data as latent variables. We propose an extended version of VL-EP, called VL-EPx, which avoids matrix inversions and is amenable to parallel implementation. VL-EPx can handle both continuous and finite-alphabet inputs. Furthermore, since it models user activity as a latent variable, it can be effectively combined with iterative ML-based activity detection, enhancing precision.

title: Physics-Embedded ML Framework for Reconfigurable Bandstop Surface for Beyond 5G/6G Applications
authors: Alisha Najma Rashid, Hajra Khan, Syed Muzahir Abbas, Yash Shrivastava
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)
abstract: A physics-embedded machine learning framework is developed for predicting and classifying the stopband of a reconfigurable D-band metasurface for EMI shielding. Three lightweight models were evaluated, a state-conditioned mixture-of-experts neural network (MoE NN), a monotone-constrained gradient-boosted tree (XGBoost), and monotone neural additive model (mNAM). On the in-distribution test set, all models achieved strong classification performance with accuracies of 0.79-0.81 and macro-F1 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.79-0.81$</tex>, while regression errors remained low (MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx \mathbf{1. 3 6 - 1. 4 3 ~ G H z}$</tex>, RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\approx} \mathbf{1. 7}-\mathbf{1. 8 ~ G H z}, \mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{>} \mathbf{0. 9 2}$</tex>). Under shifted out-of-distribution conditions, accuracy declined modestly to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim \mathbf{0. 7 7 - 0. 7 8}$</tex>, confirming robustness but highlighting the limits of extrapolation. These results demonstrate that embedding monotonicity and LC-style priors enables reliable GHz-stopband modelling while reducing reliance on expensive fullwave solvers.

title: Retinal Fundus Disease Detection and Classification using ResNet-50
authors: Himal Acharya, Susan Adhikari, Sujal Bikram Thapa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: Retinal diseases, including diabetic retinopathy, glaucoma, and age-related macular degeneration, are causes of permanent vision loss globally. This paper presents a solution utilizing a machine learning system for the automated detection and classification of retinal diseases from fundus images. The research uses a Kaggle dataset with 3483 images, each accompanied by labels for six distinct disease categories. The system is built on a ResNet50 deep learning algorithm with transfer learning and is designed for multi-label classification. The classification model is validated on $20 \%$ dataset. The experimental results show that the suggested system achieves state-of-the-art performance.

title: Development of an EV Multispeed Transmission Testing Test Rig for Early Stage Validation and System Level Integration
authors: Hiralkumar Patel, Rakesh Kushwaha
abstract: One of the emerging trends in electric vehicle (EV) development is the integration of multispeed transmissions, aimed at enhancing overall drivetrain efficiency and vehicle performance. As EV technology matures, the role of multispeed transmissions is becoming increasingly prominent in addressing the limitations of single-speed architectures, especially in high-performance and commercial applications. The growing complexity of EV powertrains introduces a significant demand for early-stage validation of transmission systems. Validating such systems in the initial development phase necessitates advanced test bench setups and procedures capable of assessing full system-level functionality, even when hardware and software maturity are still in preliminary stages. With the global push for platform modularity and shared architectures, multispeed EV transmission components are often standardized across multiple OEMs. However, the proprietary nature of the control strategies and actuator systems necessitates a strong focus on system-level integration and testing by each OEM. This paper discusses the development of an EV multispeed transmission test rig, achieved by modifying an existing system-level durability rig originally designed for gearshift endurance testing. The reconfigured test bench enables early validation of mechanical and control integration, identification of failure modes, and execution of base-level calibrations. Furthermore, the paper highlights the implementation of a communication protocol (e.g., CAN) for real-time interaction with the transmission control unit (TCU), allowing the simulation of critical vehicle-level parameters in a hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) environment. This integrated testing approach supports both the development and production phases by capturing key feedback data required for performance optimization and quality control.

title: Hybrid AI Framework for Personalized Learning and Student Progress Analysis
authors: M. Babu, Subashri E, Vincy J
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The traditional models of course delivery are unable to fulfill the individual needs of the student and lead to disengagement and knowledge gaps. To eliminate the problem, the current project proposes hybrid recommendation engine using AI that customizes course material based on preference and performance of every student. It is the engine that synthesizes Collaborative Filtering that finds content that students with similar learning patterns have watched and Reinforcement Learning that refines recommendations based upon real-time feedback such as content ratings and activity completed. The point is to optimize the learning rate by delivering the material at a speed and modality preferred by the student in form of videos, pdf, and exercises. The performance analysis module relying on Random Forest and cosine similarity is also available on the platform to measure the strengths and weaknesses, as well as the lack of learning. The use of individualized report cards helps usher students into basis or remediation content. The engine of such an engine is the hybrid AI-based recommendation strategies coupled with real-time adaptive learning and data-based analyzing the performance with the possibility of providing the really personalized learning paths.

title: AI-Driven Intelligent Baggage Screening for Modern Airport Security: Review of Methods and Challenges
authors: Laxmi, Mukesh Kumar Tripathi, Amolkumar N. Jadhav, Abhijeet Sanjay Tadasare, Ajit R. Patil, Narendradatta S. Kadam
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Airports need to provide a secure environment and handle the highly dynamic passenger demand, where operator fatigue, cluttered X-ray/CT images and increased throughput requirements are challenging the manual baggage screening. Here we introduce an AI-based bag screening framework that integrates image pre-processing, deep learning-based threat detection/classification and explainability to help ensure a fast and confident decision-making at contemporary lanes. The proposed architecture takes X-ray/CT scans as input, and normalizes and augments the data for introducing robustness to CNN models before it is used for localizing suspicious objects and assigning threat labels such as weapon, explosive or contraband. An interpretation module extracts visual evidence to assist screeners in verifying the alarms and trust enhancement. We also address close-to-production deployment considerations, such as class imbalance caused by the low occurrence rate of threat samples, target domain mismatches between scanners and airports, runtime latency constraints, and the requirement to manage false alarm rates to prevent operational bottlenecks. The paper brings together methods and challenges towards a deployment-centric perspective, as well as provides motivations to address cross-site generalization, disruption-aware evaluation, or human-in-the-loop learning to achieve stable performance under realistic operating conditions.

title: Intelligent Deployment Techniques for Cloud Applications with Downward Load Balancing in Multi-Cloud Environments
authors: Zhe Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Cloud application deployment is the process of deploying applications to run in the cloud, which are deployed and run through scalable resources of the cloud service provider, thus reducing the dependence on hardware devices and infrastructure. In the cloud computing scenario, the number of tasks as well as applications is very high due to the large number of users, for the traditional distributed computing tends to cause extreme imbalance in resource demand and extremely low resource utilization. In this paper, for the current problem of uneven resource allocation in cloud computing, we propose an optimized task scheduling algorithm based on particle swarm, which optimizes the cloud application deployment from two perspectives: user demand and cloud service supply, and verifies the effectiveness of the algorithm through simulation experiments.

title: HA-DAGP: A Hierarchical Attention-Driven Adaptive Graph Partitioning Model
authors: Caidie Li, Xiong Zhang, Jing Sun, Fang Hu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Predicting drug-drug interactions (DDIs) is crucial for ensuring drug safety and guiding rational drug combination strategies. However, due to the inherent complexity and multilevel nature of molecular structures, accurately predicting interactions between drugs remains a challenging task. Existing computational methods often fail to capture the hierarchical relationships between the atomic, motif, and molecular levels, which limits their prediction performance across different types of interactions. To address these limitations, we propose HA-DAGP, a hierarchical attention-driven adaptive graph partitioning model for DDI prediction. HA-DAGP incorporates an adaptive graph partitioning module that dynamically partitions molecular graphs into functional motifs, constructing a unified heterogeneous drug graph that integrates representations at the atomic, motif, and molecular levels. A heterogeneous message-passing mechanism is designed to propagate hierarchical information, while a co-attention mechanism is employed to fuse multi-scale embeddings and capture fine-grained interaction patterns between drug pairs. Extensive experiments on a benchmark data set demonstrate that HA-DAGP achieves state-of-the-art performance, consistently outperforming existing baselines in terms of prediction accuracy. These results validate the effectiveness of hierarchical attention and adaptive graph partitioning in enhancing molecular representation learning for improved DDI prediction.

title: Design and Implementation of a NARMA-L2 Controller for Dynamic Wireless Power Transfer in Electric Vehicles
authors: Javad Shariatzadeh, Seyed Ehsan Abdollahi, Sayyed Asghar Gholamian, Jafar Adabi, Seyed Reza Abdollahi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Wireless power transfer (WPT) systems provide a promising solution for efficient charging of electric vehicles (EVs); however, their performance is often challenged by nonlinear dynamics arising from variations in mutual coupling and load fluctuations. This paper introduces a novel control framework based on the Nonlinear Auto Regressive Moving Average model with exogenous inputs (NARMA-L2) to achieve robust output voltage regulation in Series-Series(SS) compensated WPT systems tailored for EV applications. The proposed methodology involves identifying the NARMA-L2 model using a multilayer perceptron neural network trained via the Levenberg–Marquardt optimization algorithm, followed by the design of an inverse feedforward controller derived from the identified model. To validate the proposed control approach, extensive simulations in MATLAB were conducted and benchmarked against the conventional proportional–integral (PI) controller. The results demonstrate that the NARMA-L2 controller achieves superior performance, with negligible overshoot (<3%) and shorter settling times in both reference tracking and load disturbance scenarios. These improvements are particularly advantageous for EV charging, where fast dynamic response and minimal overshoot are essential to ensure stable power flow and prevent potential battery degradation.

title: A Smart MedCare Scheduling with Real-Time Hospital Acceptance and Ambulance Dispatch
authors: Kayalvizhi P, Prethiga S, Ragavi S, Sumaya S
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The Emergency Medical Services (EMS) are time sensitive and any delays in dispatching an ambulance and in the decision-making process in the hospital will minimize the likelihood of the survival of a patient. These delays are normally brought by traffic jams, absence of real-time coordination, insufficient hospital capacity and inadequate road visibility of emergency resources. This paper introduces MedRoute, which is a real-time emergency medical routing system to enhance the communication of ambulances and hospitals. The system also encourages priority according to severity, real-time tracking of ambulance, checking of the availability of hospitals, and route selection with real-time traffic information. Preparedness can be achieved by hospitals and calculating the availability of ICUs and emergency preparedness prior to admitting patients. There are notification systems that facilitate the effective communication between the ambulance crews and the hospital as well as patients. MedRoute also keeps a patient visit journal and system performance information to help analyze and plan resources in the future. Experimental results show a reduction in response time, improved hospital utilization, and more accurate arrival time estimation. These results indicate that MedRoute can support faster and more coordinated emergency response while improving the overall efficiency of emergency healthcare services.

title: Digital Predistortion Method with Two-Dimensional Complex-Valued Spline-Interpolated Lookup Table for Joint Impairment Compensation
authors: Tianfu Cai, Jianzhuang Li, Mingyu Li, Shuman Kong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: In this paper, a digital predistortion (DPD) scheme based on two-dimensional complex-valued spline interpolation for memory polynomial (2D-CSPMP) lookup table (LUT) is proposed, which is designed to compensate for joint impairments in zero intermediate frequency (zero-IF) broadband transmitters. A generalized complex-split nonlinear structure is constructed using two-dimensional (2-D) spline interpolation functions to generate comprehensive in-phase/quadrature (I/Q) cross-terms, effectively compensating for both I/Q imbalance and power amplifier (PA) nonlinearities. Furthermore, each memory branch employs independent parallel processing through 2-D spline functions, enabling comprehensive modeling of wideband dynamic memory effects. Extensive measurements demonstrate that the proposed 2D-CSPMP DPD scheme achieves 1.3 dB superior linearization performance compared to conventional conjugate polynomial models when addressing cascaded impairments from I/Q imbalance and Doherty PA nonlinearity, while reducing computational complexity by 39%.

title: CondenseGraph: Communication-Efficient Distributed GNN Training via On-the-Fly Graph Condensation
authors: Zizhao Zhang, Yihan Xue, Haotian Zhu, Sijia Li, Zhijun Wang, Yujie Xiao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Distributed Graph Neural Network (GNN) training suffers from substantial communication overhead due to the inherent neighborhood dependency in graph-structured data. This neighbor explosion problem requires workers to frequently exchange boundary node features across partitions, creating a communication bottleneck that severely limits training scalability. Existing approaches rely on static graph partitioning strategies that cannot adapt to dynamic network conditions. In this paper, we propose CondenseGraph, a novel communication-efficient framework for distributed GNN training. Our key innovation is an on-the-fly graph condensation mechanism that dynamically compresses boundary node features into compact super nodes before transmission. To compensate for the information loss introduced by compression, we develop a gradient-based error feedback mechanism that maintains convergence guarantees while reducing communication volume by 40-60%. Extensive experiments on four benchmark dataset s demonstrate that CondenseGraph achieves comparable accuracy to full-precision baselines while significantly reducing communication costs and training time.

title: Intelligent Layered Injection and Production Technology and System Control Strategy for Fault-Block Reservoirs
authors: Yanfen Yu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: This article focuses on the difficult problem of fault-block reservoir exploitation, and studies the intelligent layered injection-production technology and system control strategy of well group. By setting up a physical simulation experimental platform, a typical fault-block reservoir is taken as an object to simulate its geological characteristics, and the traditional uniform injection-production scheme and the injection-production scheme based on algorithm model optimization and real-time adjustment combined with system control strategy are set up for comparative experiments. GA (Genetic algorithm) is used to construct the injection-production optimization algorithm model, and the injection-production parameters are adjusted according to the reservoir dynamic data. The experimental results show that the injection-production scheme optimized based on the algorithm model reduces the pressure fluctuation of the reservoir, and the average relative error of the predicted injection-production volume of each layer is 5.4%, and the recovery rate is 42.8%. The injection-production scheme with real-time adjustment combined with system control strategy has better effect, and the recovery ratio is increased to 51.3%, which is significantly better than the traditional uniform injection-production scheme of 32.5%. The research proves that the proposed algorithm model has high prediction accuracy, and the system control strategy can effectively improve the recovery ratio and mining stability of fault-block reservoirs, which provides feasible technologies and strategies for efficient development of fault-block reservoirs.

title: Optimized Multimodal Diffusion Transformer Architecture for Real-Time Semantic Control of Digital Human Micro-Expressions in Embedded AI Systems
authors: Hanwen Wang, Gaojing Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Generative AI is experiencing high-paced iterations and is steadily transforming the operational paradigms of embedded systems on the computational level. This transformation depends on reconfigured neural structure customised as per the requirements of real-time data processing. The algorithmic enhancements of the Stable Video Diffusion (SVD) model combined with the Audio2Face tool have been thoroughly studied by researchers to obtain a perfect control of the facial semantics of virtual agents, but to pay attention to the performance of computational efficiency and the actual utilisation efficiency of hardware devices. Stable Video Diffusion (SVD) has the capability to produce highly realistic dynamic sequences using given text specifications; Audio2Face has the capability to perform an exact match of synchronisation of acoustic animations with contextually relevant semantics. The hybrid architecture of the research team consisting of various modules incorporates spatiotemporal transformers and ControlNet adapters to provide micro-expression control of virtual agents, thus reaching high fidelity of images and low data processing latency of data processing in various embedded devices. The model training phase relies on the FER2013 data, combined with MEAD, primarily aiming at the quality of the emotion recognition and the accuracy of the mapping of the AU intensity, with variational autoencoders and perceptual loss functions making specific modifications to the latent space in order to minimise the likelihood of the occurrence of distortion phenomena, including temporal discrepancies. According to various test results, the model is accurate to the level of 0.85, which surpasses the majority of similar benchmark models and costs the overall processing workflow a reduction of 20-30 percent due to a reasonable distribution of GPUs. This structure reduces bottlenecks in the process, maximises the reality of content presentation during virtual content previsualization, rendering, and post-processing phases, and is scaled to different applications of embedded AI. Problems like leakage of identity information, content defects can also be resolved in the solution attaining scalable models supporting real-time functionality, and capable of adjusting to various application conditions, including mobile communication and edge computing. Ablation experiments confirm the soundness of the model that emphasises the profound fusion implementation of embedded technologies and AI innovation in the digital realm.

title: A Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Service Function Chain Placement Strategy for Latency-Sensitive Tasks in Power Information Networks
authors: Shengyong Ye, Chuan Long, Xinting Yang, Ting Li, Xuna Liu, Liyang Liu, Yuqi Han, Da Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: While the performance of power information networks is critical for real-time smart grid applications, existing task placement strategies suffer from high latency and poor scalability by treating complex computations as monolithic blocks and using static allocation heuristics. To address this, we propose a novel framework that uniquely models complex tasks as decomposable Service Function Chains (SFCs) and utilizes a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) agent to learn an adaptive placement policy across the cloud-edge continuum. We evaluate our approach through extensive simulations in a custom-built, discrete-event network environment, comparing it against random, greedy, and cloud-first baseline algorithms under diverse workloads and network scales. The results demonstrate exceptional scalability, showing that as the network scales from 5 to 25 edge nodes, our proposed method improves average end-to-end latency by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2. 4 \%}$</tex> and increases system throughput by over 297 %, in stark contrast to baseline methods whose performance degrades significantly. This work provides a robust and highly scalable solution for dynamic service placement and offers a new paradigm for designing intelligent, autonomous resource management systems for latency-critical applications in power systems and other industrial IoT domains.

title: Risk State Estimation and Assessment Method for New Energy Integration into Power Grids
authors: Xing Zhang, Mingyun Luo, Zhanhua Huang, Shizhang Li, Zhe Li, Nan Jiang, Hushan Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: The integration of distributed renewable energy sources such as wind and photovoltaic power introduces significant randomness, volatility, and intermittency into the power system. As these sources are increasingly connected to the grid at large scales, the system's operational dynamics become highly nonlinear and uncertain, leading to reduced observability and challenges in accurately estimating real-time system states. To address this issue, this paper proposes a risk-aware state estimation method for power systems based on a temporal generative adversarial network. The proposed approach incorporates gated recurrent units into the generative adversarial framework to effectively capture the temporal evolution patterns of renewable energy output. By utilizing update and reset gate mechanisms, the model enhances the generator ’ s learning capability under fluctuating and uncertain input conditions, thereby improving the accuracy of state estimation under renewable energy risks. The proposed method is tested on the IEEE 14-bus and IEEE 57-bus systems. Simulation results, evaluated through root mean square error, mean absolute error, and coefficient of determination, demonstrate that the method achieves high estimation accuracy even in the presence of renewable energy fluctuations, thus providing a reliable tool for enhancing the secure and stable operation of power systems.

title: Halbach-Enhanced Reluctance Magnetic Gear Assisted by Superconductive Flux Channels
authors: Alireza Sohrabzadeh, Aghil Ghaheri
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Magnetic Gears (MGs) can be considered as the game-changer technologies when it comes to power transferring. They can not only transfer mechanical power touchlessly but also bring merits such as lower noise and vibration with respect to typical gears. However, utilizing permanent magnets (PMs) puts them under scrutiny in terms of cogging torque, which leads to the ripple on the torque profile. The use of reluctance cores instead of PM-assisted sides in one category of design introduces a cost-effective assembly and levels up rigidity and robustness, which is especially considerable in high-speed applications. However, the essence of saliency operation during the flux modulation causes less average torque and more torque ripple, too. This paper seeks a novel thought about increasing torque density, which includes structural modification by utilizing Halbach arrays as well as applying high-temperature superconductors (HTS) in flux channels. Due to the lack of numerical design equations, finite element analysis (FEA) alongside scenario analysis was used to show the effectiveness of the suggested assembly.

title: Research on Trusted Exchange and Data Traceability Mechanism of Health Information System Integrating Blockchain and Internet of Things
authors: Yanjun Meng
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: The existing health information system has the contradiction between the surge in demand for cross-institutional data sharing and the rising risk of privacy leakage caused by centralized storage, and faces challenges such as the conflict between data sharing and privacy protection, the difficulty of static storage in adapting to dynamic medical processes, and the contradiction between cross-domain trust establishment and system scalability. In this study, blockchain and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies are integrated to build a three-tier architecture of "IoT-Edge Computing-Blockchain" to solve the problem of dynamic data winding. The IoT layer ensures the authenticity and integrity of the data source through the unique identification, time stamp, numerical value and digital signature of the device; The edge computing layer uses adaptive differential compression algorithm to filter redundant data, reducing the amount of uplink data by 60%-80%, and filtering compliance; The blockchain layer realizes data trusted anchoring in the form of hash digest, and introduces a dynamic weight voting mechanism based on the historical credibility of nodes and the number of mortgage passes to optimize the traditional PBFT (Practical Byzantine Fault Tolerant) consensus mechanism. At the same time, innovative dynamic traceability intelligent contract model, through the version hash chain to achieve the full life cycle traceability of medical data; Design privacy- weighted data sharing protocol, and combine multi-level access control matrix and weighted authorization model to ensure data sharing security; The system scalability and processing efficiency are improved by using segmented chain storage architecture. The experimental verification shows that the scheme is superior to the traditional system and pure blockchain scheme in data uplink efficiency, throughput, version history query efficiency, tamper detection rate and privacy protection, and can effectively support large-scale real-time medical data scenarios, providing a technical path for solving the problem of medical data sharing and helping the modernization of health governance.

title: Improving Rock Unit Classification in Hyperspectral Images Using 3D Discrete Wavelet Transform Texture Features
authors: Senmiao Guo, Qigang Jiang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Hyperspectral data contain multiple similar bands, making texture feature extraction while avoiding data redundancy a challenge for rock classification. Three-dimensional Discrete Wavelet Transform (3D-DWT) can both extract spatial and spectral features from hyperspectral data, enhancing rock image textures and providing more usable information for rock classification. This paper utilizes Gaofen-5 Advanced HyperSpectral Imager (AHSI) data from the Eastern Tianshan region of China to extract multi-scale, 3D wavelet features for different rock units. Jeffries-Matusita distance is applied for feature ranking, with a Bayesian-optimized random forest classifier selected for classification. The traditional texture extraction method—the grayscale co-occurrence matrix (GLCM) —is used for comparison. For lithological mapping, the classification results using 3D-DWT statistical textures achieved an overall accuracy of 79.71%, outperforming both original statistical texture features (42.72%) and original pixel values (52.08%). For rock unit classification, neither reflectance images nor wavelet-transformed images were suitable for texture extraction by GLCM. When texture features can assist hyperspectral data in rock classification, remote sensing rock classification accuracy in complex terrain will be further enhanced.

title: Distributed Deep Learning Training on PARAM Seva: Empirical Analysis of Data Parallelism with PyTorch DDP
authors: Adithya M S, Brindha G
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Distributed deep learning training is essential for scaling model training on high-performance computing (HPC) clusters. This work presents an empirical study of distributed data parallel training on PARAM Seva, an Indian supercomputer with 10 GPU-accelerated nodes using NVIDIA Tesla V100 GPUs. We implement and benchmark four convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures: SimpleCNN, ResNet-18, ResNet-50, and VGG-16. We use PyTorch Distributed Data Parallel (DDP) across 1, 2, 4, and 8 GPUs on the CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 datasets. Our results show a maximum speedup of 3.49× on 8 GPUs for ResNet-50 on CIFAR-10, with the best efficiency at 90.6% when using 2 GPUs. Importantly, the study reveals a significant accuracy drop at 8 GPUs for ResNet-50 (from 88.64% to 70.05%, an 18.59% decrease) and for VGG-16. This indicates communication issues beyond 4 GPUs. ResNet-18 maintains stable accuracy across GPU counts, with a maximum degradation of 2.77%. We provide a detailed analysis of training dynamics, communication overhead, and comparisons of CNN architectures, along with practical recommendations for HPC administrators. The implementation uses SLURM job scheduling and can be fully reproduced on similar HPC clusters.

title: Extraction and Feature Recognition of Gravity and Magnetic Anomaly Information in Gold Mining Belts Based on Multi-scale Wavelet Analysis
authors: Qun Liang, Peng Yang, Dong Li, Tao Zhang, Chengyuan Liu, Meiling Xu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: This article explores the extraction and feature recognition methods of gravity and magnetic (G&M) anomaly information in gold ore belts based on multi-scale wavelet analysis. Gravity and magnetic exploration play an important role in the exploration of polymetallic deposits due to their high sensitivity response to density and magnetic anomalies, as well as their advantages of low cost and high efficiency. The boundary recognition technology using G&M data can effectively extract the fault structure information contained in anomalies. However, existing boundary recognition methods have their own advantages and limitations. Wavelet analysis, as a mature multi-scale analysis tool in recent years, has strong noise resistance and fine scale decomposition characteristics, which can identify and extract more detailed geological boundary information. Therefore, this article proposes a comprehensive method that integrates multi-scale wavelet analysis and deep learning (DL) for enhancing G&M anomaly information, noise suppression, and spatial structure feature extraction. The results indicate that this method can more accurately identify the G&M anomaly features related to gold ore belts and has good potential for application.

title: Edge AI-Enabled Multi-Purpose Camera System: ACost-Effective Solution for Autonomous Computer Vision Applications
authors: ManiMaran R, Maneesh V, Meena Kumari Bugatha N
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The growth of intelligent surveillance and computer vision technologies is leading to rapid growth in the use of edge AI (Artificial Intelligence) technology, and it has been estimated that the global edge AI market will increase from USD 8.7 billion in 2024 to USD 56.8 billion in 2030. The design and testing of a multi-functional camera system with Edge AI capabilities for real-time computer vision tasks without relying on any Cloud infrastructure is presented in this paper. The camera system consists of a high resolution camera sensor, a Raspberry Pi 5 host computer, and a Coral Edge TPU which allows for using on-device inference for a variety of applications: (i.e., object detection, counting people, monitoring accidents, detecting anomalies, and analyzing large groups). The use of Edge AI to do inference means that the camera system performs these tasks with significantly less latency than traditional systems and uses far less bandwidth and has no ongoing computation costs in the cloud. The camera system also provides increased data privacy and increased reliability while performing this work. Model optimization methods such as quantization, pruning, and knowledge distillation are used to effectively utilize the deep neural network models on resource-limited hardware platforms. The system was tested and validated and shown to provide real-time performance with a high degree of accuracy and an estimated cost savings of up to 68% long term over traditional cloud surveillance systems, making it an excellent candidate for potentially scalable safety-critical monitoring systems.

title: A Study of Adaptive Optics Based on Altitude Profile of Atmospheric Turbulence for Uplink Pre-compensation in Laser Satellite Communication
authors: Yoshimi Rokugawa, Kenta Sakabe, Yoshihiko Saito, Hideki Takami
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: Laser satellite communications have been promoted to improve communication environments achieving a high capacity and low delay, and it is necessary to couple the beam into single-mode fibers. NICT has been conducting research and development regarding laser satellite communications, both downlink and uplink at greater than 10 Gbps. However, each downlink and uplink must overcome the effects of atmospheric turbulence that prevent stable optical communication even though it is under favorable weather conditions. This is because when the communication beam passes through atmospheric turbulence, its optical wavefront is distorted, and it would be challenging to couple to the single-mode fiber. These days, adaptive optics, a technology that was significantly advanced in astronomy, is recognized as the solution to compensate for the effects of atmospheric turbulence in laser space communication. For the downlink, the condition is similar to that in astronomy, and an adaptive optics system can be easily applied. On the other hand, it is not directly applicable to the uplink because it requires pre-compensation and takes into account the point-ahead angle. In addition, the communication beam path would be different from that of the downlink. To address these issues regarding uplink adaptive optics, it is necessary to estimate and reconstruct the atmospheric turbulence along the uplink path using information obtained during downlink compensation. This study examines the estimation methods based on such data for uplink pre-compensation and presents the findings

title: Dynamic Prediction and Application of Carbon Emissions of Power Grid Enterprises Based on Time Series Deep Learning
authors: Yuan Zhang, Shu Xia, Peng Chen, Li Lan, Yanyuan Qian
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: The carbon emission of power grid is affected by multiple dynamic factors, so it is difficult for traditional statistical models to capture the characteristics of nonlinear and non-stationary time series, and the existing methods have problems such as single data source and insufficient generalization ability of models. Therefore, this study proposes a LSTM-Transformer hybrid model (Da-LT model) based on dynamic attention enhancement. In this model, local time series trends are captured by LSTM, and global periodic patterns are extracted by Transformer to realize multi-scale feature fusion. At the same time, the external factors such as policy changes and new energy output are coded into learnable dynamic weights to enhance the adaptability to unexpected scenarios, and a business-oriented loss function integrating direction accuracy indicators is designed to improve the ability to judge the changing trend of carbon emissions. The experiment adopts the monthly data of a regional power grid enterprise in China from 2017 to 2023, and divides it into training set (2017-2021), verification set (2022) and test set (2023). The results showed that the root mean square error (RMSE) of the Da LT model on the test set was 103.7 tons, the mean absolute error (MAE) was 78.5 tons, the mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) was 5.6%, and the directional accuracy (DA) reached 91.7%, significantly better than ARIMA, SVR (Support Vector Regression), LSTM, Transformer, and the ablation version LT without introducing dynamic attention. The ablation experiment further validated the dynamic attention mechanism, directional loss function, and the necessity of combining LSTM (Long Short Term Memory Network) with Transformer. The model proposed in this study can effectively support practical business such as the formulation of carbon trading strategies for power grid enterprises, providing a new and effective method for dynamic prediction of carbon emissions in power grid enterprises.

title: Research on Characterization Models of Peak Shaving Capacity for Thermal Power Units Based on Thermal Network Heat Storage
authors: Lingkai Zhu, Kai Liang, Zhiqiang Gong, Ziwei Zhong, Junshan Guo, Wei Zheng, Yuheng Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: The characterization of peak capacity for thermal power units based on thermal network thermal storage requires consideration of the time-delay characteristics of the thermal network, thermodynamic models of thermal power units, and the implementation of corresponding constraints and adjustment strategies to enhance peak capacity. This paper establishes models for thermal network characteristics across three levels: primary thermal network, secondary heat exchange stations, and thermal users.

title: Hierarchical LEO-Based Positioning with GNSS-Aided Clock States Estimation
authors: Hammam Salem, Malek Karaim, Qamar Bader, Hesham Elsawy, Aboelmagd Noureldin
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite constellations are a promising alternative to traditional Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) for Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) services. Although LEO satellites offer advantages like reduced signal propagation delay, their lack of precise onboard clocks and dependence on ground-based infrastructure limit their standalone use. To address this, this paper introduces a two-layered hierarchical framework for autonomous LEO-based positioning. The first layer has LEO satellites estimate their navigation data (position, velocity, and clock information) by using GNSS measurements. In the second layer, the LEO satellites broadcast this navigation data to ground users. The user's device then combines this data with local Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) data using a Loosely Coupled Extended Kalman Filter (LCEKF) to improve positioning accuracy. The proposed framework is validated through simulations utilizing the PULSAR LEO constellation. Results demonstrate sub-2-meter 3D positioning errors in open-sky conditions. This underscores the potential for infrastructure-light LEO-based navigation as a resilient solution in diverse scenarios.

title: OpenEP: Open-Ended Future Event Prediction
authors: Yong Guan, Hao Peng, Xiaozhi Wang, Lei Hou, Juanzi Li
abstract: None

title: Embedded Solar Energy Monitoring System Using Arduino for Stand-Alone Photovoltaic Applications
authors: Sugandan N, Jayaprakash D, Kannan R
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: With the embedded energy monitoring system, recent developments on solar applications are aimed at making systems adequate in managing and analysing both the generation and consumption of power in stand-alone systems. The system combines photovoltaic energy to a DC-DC converter to provide regulated power flow, which in turn allows to charge and discharge a battery attached to the system. There are actual loads of various types like a fan and a DC motor, which are used to show real-time use of the stored solar energy. Current sensors and voltage sensors are used to measure input and output parameters so that the performance of the system can be properly tracked. An Arduino microcontroller receives the data sensor and manages the flow of power and displays the results on an LCD screen, where the user can interact with the results. This is a cost-effective, trusted, and scalable method of using solar energy in rural and urban settings that can allow maximum use of renewable energy and facilitate sustainable development.

title: Ghostden: Hybrid Framework for ECG Image Based Heart Disease Classification
authors: S. Hemalatha, D. Sivabalaselvamani, T. Kavitha, K. Kaliswary, V. Dhanush, J. Jaisapthagiri
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Heart disease is one of the major causes of death around the world today. Detecting heart disease early through electrocardiogram (ECG) analysis can significantly improve chances for timely medical treatment. This paper introduces GhostDen, a deep-learning-based framework that classifies ECG images into four main classes: Normal, Abnormal, History of Myocardial Infarction (MI) and Myocardial Infarction. The presented system employs GhostNet as a lightweight and efficient base for feature extraction. To cause the model to focus on critical patterns shown in ECG images, two attention mechanisms, Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) and Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM), are integrated into the architecture. Subsequently, dense layers and softmax activation are used to accomplish the classification. Experiment-based results show the SE model achieves a classification accuracy of 97.00 %, with a recall of 96.64 % and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of 96.39 %. By comparison, the CBAM model has a reported accuracy of 95.34 %. These results indicate the successful integration of attention modules with GhostNet to support accuracy in diagnostic prediction; SE performs best with ECG image-based heart disease classification and prediction.

title: Research on a Large Model for Children's Multimodal Emotion Recognition Based on Multi-Feature Cross-Fusion
authors: Rui Zhao, Zhiyan Liu, Shuai Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: To accurately identify children's emotional tendencies in multimodal data from daily and educational scenarios (supporting psychological needs understanding, educational strategy optimization, and mental health interventions), this study proposes a multi-feature cross-fusion-based children's multimodal emotion recognition model (BCVAFC) to solve existing issues of fragmented feature extraction and insufficient inter-modal fusion. The model enhances features by introducing CLIP alongside BERT (text) and ViT (image), mapping text (e.g., speech-to-text) and image (e.g., facial frames) to a shared semantic space via contrastive learning; it then captures inter-modal dependencies through a text-image dual-guided cross-attention mechanism fused with self-attention and Fourier convolution, and achieves deep fusion of low-level and high-level features via global attention and residual connections to boost recognition accuracy. Experiments on children's emotion datasets show BCVAFC outperforms traditional multimodal models in recognizing emotions like joy, anxiety, and grievance, with accuracy converging stably to 92.3% and good robustness; adapted to children's data characteristics, it can be expanded with more modalities (e.g., body movements, heart rate) for wider applications.

title: AIST 2025 Cover Page

title: Integrating Mosaic Loss of Y Chromosome, Clinical and Lifestyle Factors, and Heart Rate Zones for Early Cardiovascular Risk Prediction in Men
authors: M. Ayyadurai, Krithika R
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: CVD (cardiovascular disease) continues to be one of the primary causes of death among men worldwide; thus, there is an urgent need for an established system for determining which men will develop CVD at an early age. Current traditional clinical scoring methods fail to capture the multifaceted ways in which genetic, physiological and lifestyle factors interact and/or influence cardiovascular health in men. An example of a newly identified genetic marker that is associated with CVD is the loss of the Y chromosome (mLOY), which occurs during the aging process of males; to date, the mLOY has not yet been incorporated into any predictive CVD models. The present study presents a combination of machine learning methodologies for estimating CVD risk in males by utilizing mLOY as a proxy for determining CVD risk along with lifestyle patterns and heart-rate dynamics (HRZ). The multisource database (10,175 participants) has been processed/merged/engineered to create clinically pertinent predictors based upon the most reliable CVD risk factors available (MHR, % MHR, Hypertension stage, BMI category).

title: Incentive Mechanism-Based Coordinated Jamming Channel and Power Joint Decision-Making Method
authors: Xing He, Zhibin Feng, Yuping Gong, Kexin Liang, Hao Han, Wenqi Li
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In wireless communication confrontation under complex electromagnetic environments, users tend to be clustered, frequency pattern being unpredictable and the jamming energy of multi-jammer is limited. These makes it difficult for traditional jamming methods to achieve ideal results. Therefore, this paper proposed an Incentive Mechanism-based Coordinated Jamming Channel and Power Joint DecisionMaking Method for energy-constrained multi-jammer. Firstly, the method introduced an incentive mechanism to encourage jammers to jam as many users as possible while maintaining the jamming effectiveness. Secondly, jammers employed the Markov Decision Process (MDP) framework and Q-learning algorithm to select optimal jamming strategies based on Boltzmann probability. Finally, the simulation results showed that the proposed algorithm can effectively resolve the joint decisionmaking problem of multi-jammer under energy constraints, and compared with the jamming algorithm without incentive mechanism, it also improves the success rate of jamming.

title: Deep Learning-Based Automated Identification of Date Fruit Varieties Using CNNs
authors: Raghavendra Sridhar, Jhulan Kumar, Rashi Nimesh Kumar Dhenia, Ishva Jitendrakumar Kanani, Premlata Chopra, Gaurav Sharma, Deepak Banerjee
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: The classification of dates is a way of approaching these three facets of agriculture: Research, Quality Control, and Market Distribution. This paper presents a possible Convolution Neural Networks (CNN) application to classify 9 different types of date fruits (Ajwah, Galaxy, Medjool, Menafi, Nabtat Ali, Rutab, Shaishah, Sokari, and Sugaey). Deep learning provides the ability to utilize CNNs accurately for this type of work. The accuracy achieved by this model illustrates the future of sustainable agriculture, food security, and responsible production and consumption with deep learning technologies. CNN performance is evaluated via Accuracy, Recall, F1 score, and Confusion Matrix; results show excellent methods of classification with low misclassifications and high classification capability. Training and validation loss analyses reflect adequate learning and balanced performance, while the confusion matrix reveals areas for improvement. Another purpose of this research is to illustrate the role of CNN models in improving resource use efficiency, developing climate-smart agricultural practices, and boosting economies by providing enhanced quality control of agriculture and market analysis for sustainable development.

title: ICEMD 2025 Breaker Page

title: Research on Small Lesion Detection in Medical Imaging Based on Improved YOLO
authors: Xingyi Zhan
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Aiming at the challenges of low accuracy, poor robustness and high hardware dependence in the detection of small lesions in medical images, this study proposes an attention-enhanced detection system based on the improved YOLOv8. This system embeds the ECAttention module into the backbone network based on the YOLOv8 benchmark framework, enhancing the global context feature aggregation ability through efficient one-dimensional convolution. Meanwhile, a multi-scale enhanced attention module (MultiSEAM) is designed and integrated into the detection head. By using a parallel multi-branch structure to fuse the features of different receptive fields and dynamically calibrate the channel response, the perception and positioning accuracy of the model for small lesions with variable scales and blurred boundaries is effectively improved. Experimental verification was conducted on a dataset containing 3064 brain MRI images. The results show that the comprehensive performance of this system is excellent, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> reaching 92.9%. Among them, the detection accuracy for meningioma and pituitary tumor is as high as 99.1% and 97.2% respectively, which is significantly better than the benchmark model.

title: Alt-Eye: A Smart Vision Assistance Device for the Visually Impaired Using Deep Learning
authors: K. Kartheeban, Thummala Nithin Reddy, Pamulapati Pooja Sri, Enjirapu Yuva Sekhar Nikhil Eswara Achari
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This paper introduces Alt-Eye, a wearable smart vision assistance system that enables visually impaired individuals to recognize objects in real time, perform optical character recognition, detect currency, and understand contextual scenes. The system comprises a Raspberry Pi Zero 2W interfaced with a Pi Camera V1 (OV5647) mounted on custom 3D-printed spectacle frames, connected to Bluetooth earphones for audio feedback. Alt-Eye adopts an edge-cloud hybrid architecture: while images are captured at the edge device, inference is executed on the cloud infrastructure provided by Microsoft Azure running a deep learning multimodal fusion pipeline that merges the output of a visionlanguage model with large language model reasoning. The system has an end-to-end latency of 2.5-3 seconds and is able to identify objects, extract text from product labels including MRP and expiry dates, recognize Indian currency denominations, and generate human-understandable descriptions using speech synthesis. Power is supplied via a custom battery management system using a 3.7 V 1000 mAh lithium polymer battery integrated with a charging and 5 V boost circuit that enables portable operation for 8-10 hours. Experimental validation is performed by achieving reliable detection accuracy across common indoor scenarios and product recognition tasks and represents a significant advancement in assistive technology by delivering context-aware, multimodal intelligence in a lightweight, ergonomic wearable form factor.

title: Advancement in Supply Chains using Blockchain in Tracking Goods and Transactions
authors: Saurav Singh, Rajbala Simon, Kamini Sharma
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Supply chains means to transfer the goods from one place to another place and the transaction done from one end to another.
