This process needs to be more secure and transparent owner and client side. The proposed system component-based architecture comprises the following modules: Product Registration, Order Management, Shipment Tracking, and Payment Processing. All of them are based on smart contracts that streamline validation, authorization, and transaction compliance processes. Chain code that serves as smart contracts is programmed in Go, implemented and deployed on a permissioned blockchain network to facilitate data integrity and process automation across all parties. The framework is prototyped in a simulated environment through Dockers containers and its performance in terms of latency and data immutability across multiple nodes is measured. Experimental results show that the traceability is enhanced, the process latency is reduced and the resistance against tampering is increased. This study provides an efficient and scalable architecture for blockchain-based supply chain management, Upgrades will add AI-based predictive analytics and dynamic

title: Dual-Attention Enhanced Diffusion-Based Medical Image Diagnosis
authors: Hang Zhou, Tao Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Medical image classification plays a crucial role in clinical diagnostic support, yet this task remains fraught with challenges. These include domain shifts arising from variations in imaging equipment, feature instability caused by noise and artifact interference, as well as issues such as variable lesion sizes, blurred boundaries, and uneven distribution. Recent novel approaches based on diffusion models have encountered certain issues, such as the lack of interconnectivity between extracted lesion regions. This paper proposes a novel framework named GLADiff based on diffusion models, aiming to address general medical image classification problems by eliminating unexpected noise and perturbations in medical images while robustly capturing semantic representations. We designed a novel Global-Local Attention Guidance (GLAG) strategy to finely regulate each diffusion step from two perspectives, thereby enhancing progressive regional attention. The global stream incorporates a channel attention mechanism, while the local stream innovatively introduces a Bi-Level Routing Attention (BRA) and Generalized Pooling Operator (GPO). This enables precise targeting of lesion regions while suppressing background noise. We evaluated the effectiveness of GLADiff on two medical classification tasks involving different image modalities: skin lesion classification using dermatoscopic images, and diabetic retinopathy grading using fundus images. Our experimental results demonstrate that GLADiff achieves highly competitive results across all tasks through its unique guidance mechanism, showcasing the proposed model's efficacy.

title: Object Recognition Method in Silicone-Encapsulated Structures Based on Tactile PSS-NET
authors: Xiaolong Guo, Yongkang Kong, Fubang Zhao, Tian Tang, Dapeng Wei
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: As progress is made in materials science and engineering, the significance of recognizing embedded objects within materials is on the increase. In various sectors, including manufacturing, medical diagnostics, and safety inspections, the precise detection and identification of embedded objects inside materials are crucial for ensuring product quality, enhancing diagnostic precision, and safeguard safety. Prevailing recognition techniques predominantly focus on classifying one-dimensional time-series signals, often neglecting the spatial features of these signals and the orientational attributes of embedded objects. This research introduces a novel approach to embedded model identification, which is based on the processing of time-series pressure data. Furthermore, a Convolutional Neural Networks named Pressure Signal Shape Net(PSS-Net), with the capacity to recognize spatial features and perform feature fusion, is devised to capture the rotational changes of the embedded model. The method attained a classification accuracy as high as 97.11% when applied to a time - series current signal dataset that was generated by pressing a silicone cube with nine embedded Arabic numerals.

title: Integration of Multimodal Perception and Path Planning in Autonomous Driving
authors: Tianzuo Yuan, Zhanwei Wang, Xiaolong Jia, Chenhao Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: To achieve safe, comfortable, and efficient driving of autonomous vehicles in complex dynamic environments, this paper proposes a model predictive control trajectory planning method that integrates multimodal perception information. Firstly, based on information such as environmental obstacles, road boundaries, and dynamic traffic participants acquired by the multimodal perception system, a “three-circle” inflated safety zone model is constructed. Environmental modeling driven by perception data simplifies the planning complexity and effectively avoids collision risks caused by deviations between the idealized vehicle model and the actual perceived environment. Secondly, a planning architecture with horizontal and vertical spatial decoupling is adopted. At the horizontal level, combined with the perceived road topology, model predictive control is used to generate a set of candidate trajectories that meet the constraints of continuous curvature and obstacle avoidance. At the vertical level, based on dynamic information such as the preceding vehicle's speed and traffic flow status obtained through perception, dynamic programming is applied to quickly solve the optimal speed profile, significantly improving planning efficiency and real-time performance. Finally, a multi-objective optimal trajectory evaluation function is constructed by synthesizing environmental constraints fed back by multimodal perception, vehicle dynamics limitations, and ride comfort requirements, realizing the coordinated optimization of path and speed planning. A closed-loop verification environment for multimodal perception-planning is built using the Matlab/Simulink, Prescan, and Carsim co-simulation platform. Experimental results show that the longitudinal acceleration change rate (comfort index), steering wheel angle smoothness, and trajectory tracking accuracy of the vehicle under the proposed method are all superior to traditional planning methods. The average deviation between the planned curve and the actual tracking curve is less than 0.15 m, which fully verifies the effectiveness and superiority of the fusion architecture of multimodal perception and path planning.

title: The Hybrid Deep Learning Framework to Identify Real World Papaya Leaf to Detect Disease by Approximating the Severity
authors: Joan Yazhini J, Nikhita Sherlin Christopher, Sowmiya L
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Despite the fact that papaya is a staple of tropical farming, the growth of the crop is frequently crippled by viral infections which affect the foliage and loss of output is a norm. The need to detect and accurately quantify the severity of infection in time comes to the fore, so as to reduce the cost of loss, and to take corrective actions on time. A second problem of the real-world detection is clutter field scene, changeable illumination patterns and partial foliage coverage. To overcome this challenge, the disease classification and severity levels are combined to present the Hybrid Deep Learning Framework of intelligent papaya leaf disease management. It consists of a transfer learning-explained joint of both Xception and DenseNet201 backbones to learn representations of non-uniform vein patterns and surface textures. The mask-aware severity estimator applies the infection proportionally to the surfaces of the leaves after classification to provide actionable and transparent disease information. To enable the capacity to make decisions at a contextual level, a context sensitive feature such as climatic variations and geo-spatial tags are incorporated thus allowing a series of agro-ecological zones to adapt. This methodology opposes to exhaustive augmentation methods and generative approaches, which enables data augmentation of such challenging and uncontrolled real-field situations which require increasing ground truth data that impiles generalization directions, even though the data insufficiency problem exists. The framework should also offer AI-based treatment recommendations in the local languages, using an edge-computing-based mobile platform which can be implemented in rural settings with limited connectivity. The research paper builds a farmer friendly, interpretable solution, which not only helps in preserving sustainable farming, but also helps in precision agriculture, by implementing the deep learning models in practical contexts..

title: Uncertainty-Aware Edge Data Caching with Robust Optimization and Approximate Linear Models
authors: P Srinivasarao, Vamsi Kumar Arava, V. Margasahachary, J. Syam Babu, Ch Mandakini, Swapna Raghunath
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Edge computing has become essential for supporting low-latency applications by storing popular data closer to users. However, conventional edge data caching techniques often assume predictable demand patterns and overlook real-world uncertainties such as user mobility, abrupt shifts in content popularity, and edge server failures. These assumptions limit their practical effectiveness, especially in dynamic environments where user behavior and system conditions fluctuate unpredictably. In this work, we present an uncertainty-aware edge data caching framework that explicitly accounts for both data popularity variations and potential failures in edge servers. The proposed methodology formulates the caching problem as a two-stage robust optimization task, ensuring resilient performance even under volatile network conditions. To address computational challenges in large-scale scenarios, we further introduce an approximate solution, uEDC-L, based on linear decision rules, significantly reducing overhead while maintaining strong performance. Extensive experimental evaluations demonstrate that our methods outperform established caching strategies, including MagNet and ARO. Notably, the optimal algorithm uEDC-B reduces data retrieval latency by up to 68.4% and lowers storage costs by over 50%, compared to baseline approaches. The approximate method uEDC-L achieves comparable performance with much faster computation, making it a practical choice for real-world deployments. The results confirm that incorporating uncertainty handling into edge caching design can significantly improve both efficiency and system robustness. These contributions advance the reliability of mobile edge computing, especially for applications demanding high data availability under dynamic conditions.

title: Efficient Sign Language Classification Utilizing Neural Networks
authors: Basel A. Dabwan, Majda O. Elbasheer, Arwa M. Eldhai, Nabila A. Saeed, Soad M. Almula, Yahya A. Ali
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The Translation System (TS) is a crucial innovation aimed at supporting people with disabilities by enhancing their ability to communicate effectively within society. As computers become increasingly embedded in everyday activities, progress in human-computer interaction has brought significant advantages to individuals who are mute or deaf. Gesture language serves as a fundamental medium for closing communication gaps faced by those with hearing impairments. Our proposed system presents an intelligent translation model capable of converting sign language gestures into written text, thereby enabling smooth interaction between individuals with speech or hearing difficulties and the wider community. The system is built on a RegNet-inspired neural architecture and trained using a comprehensive Sign Language Dataset A total of 27,455 instances are used for training, while 7,172 are allocated for testing. With an outstanding accuracy rate of 99.86%, as illustrated in the accuracy, confusion matrix, and class distribution figures, the model proves to be highly efficient and dependable in translating sign language gestures into text form.

title: Robust H ∞ control under stochastic requirements: minimizing conditional value-at-risk instead of worst-case performance
authors: Ervan Kassarian, Francesco Sanfedino, Daniel Alazard, Andrea Marrazza
abstract: Conventional robust H2/H∞ control minimizes the worst-case performance, often leading to a conservative design driven by very rare parametric configurations. To reduce this conservatism while taking advantage of the stochastic properties of Monte Carlo sampling and its compatibility with parallel computing, we introduce an alternative paradigm that optimizes the controller with respect to a stochastic criterion, namely the conditional value at risk. We present the problem formulation and discuss several open challenges toward a general synthesis framework. The potential of this approach is illustrated on a mechanical system, where it significantly improves overall performance by tolerating some degradation in very rare worst-case scenarios.

title: Longitudinal, non-invasive magnetocardiography of avian embryos with optically pumped magnetometers_supp1-3672704.pdf
authors: Xiang He
abstract: Optically pumped magnetometers (OPMs) provide high-sensitivity, contact-free detection of biomagnetic signals, yet their use in avian developmental monitoring has not been validated across full incubation or in conservation-relevant species. We present an OPM-based magnetocardiography (MCG) workflow that records embryonic heart activity throughout the 21-day incubation of White Leghorn (Gallus gallus domesticus) eggs. A rotating platform enabled four orientations per egg in under 2 minutes, yielding comprehensive MCG datasets with minimal handling. Quantitative analysis revealed a progressive increase in R-wave amplitude (from 0.70 pT at day 13 to 0.95 pT at day 20) and a systematic upward shift in dominant spectral frequency from 2–4 Hz to 3–5 Hz over the course of incubation, providing a detailed electrophysiological maturation profile. Extending beyond a model species, We demonstrate, for the first time, successful MCG recordings in a conservation-relevant, endangered species Crested Ibis (Nipponia nippon) embryo. Together, these results establish OPM-MCG as a rapid, longitudinal, and non-invasive approach for assessing avian cardiac development, with implications for developmental biology, hatchery monitoring, and species conservation.

title: Thermal Modeling of Battery Cells for Optimal Tab and Surface Cooling Control
authors: Godwin K. Peprah, Yicun Huang, Torsten Wik, Faisal Altaf, Changfu Zou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: Optimal cooling that minimizes thermal gradients and the average temperature is essential for enhanced battery safety and health. This work presents a new modeling approach for battery cells of different shapes by integrating the Chebyshev spectral-Galerkin (CSG) method and model component decomposition. As a result, a library of reduced-order, computationally efficient battery thermal models is obtained, characterized by different numbers of states. These models are validated against a high-fidelity finite-element model and are compared with a thermal equivalent circuit (TEC) model under real-world vehicle driving and battery cooling scenarios. Illustrative results demonstrate that the proposed model with four states can faithfully capture the 2-D thermal dynamics, while the model with only one state significantly outperforms the widely used two-state TEC model in both accuracy and computational efficiency, reducing computation time by 28.7%. Furthermore, our developed models allow for independent control of tab and surface cooling (SC) channels, enabling effective thermal performance optimization. Additionally, the proposed model’s versatility and effectiveness are demonstrated through various applications, including the evaluation of different cooling scenarios, closed-loop temperature control, and the thermal assessment of cell aspect ratio.

title: Efficient Joint Estimation of Optical Flow and Stereo Disparity with Event Cameras
authors: Muhammad Ahmed Humais, Sajid Javed, Yahya Zweiri
abstract: Optical flow and stereo disparity, both are fundamental in the perception pipeline of robotic systems, enabling 3D understanding and motion estimation of the robot itself and the dynamic objects around. In this context, event cameras offer great potential to reduce latency and improve the efficiency of the perception pipeline. However, existing event-based approaches often deal with flow and disparity estimation tasks separately, potentially leading to redundant computations and reduced efficiency. In this work, we developed a joint flow and depth estimation network, featuring shared feature encoders for high computational efficiency. Moreover, we introduce a novel Bidirectional Mamba module to enhance feature expressiveness by increasing the spatial receptive field to capture global context with significantly lower computational overhead than vision transformers. We further improve efficiency by incorporating informed priors to reduce the number of refinement iterations in the sequential estimation task. Additionally, we extend our framework to combine the estimated flow and disparity to predict 3D scene flow, an important task in many robotics applications.

title: Real-time Bidirectional 235.2-Tb/s S+C+L Band Transmission over 10.9-km AR-HCF with 1200/800 Gb/s/λ
authors: Shuchao Mi, Honglin Ji, Xu Zhang, Xingfeng Li, Zhaopeng Xu, Yao Lu, Siyue Jin, Jingbin Feng, Sen Ma, Rongjun Xu, Lei Wang, Qibing Wang, Hui Chen, Feng Gao, Linye He, Houling Ji, Xueyang Li, Qijie Xie, Lulu Liu, Chao Li, Ming Luo, Quan Cao, Peng Li, Jie Luo, Zhixue He
abstract: the extremely low Rayleigh backscattering and ultralow loss characteristics of the hollow core fiber, we experimentally demonstrate a real-time same wavelength bidirectional transmission system over a 10.9-km nested anti-resonant nodeless fiber (NANF) link, which achieves a bidirectional net capacity of 235.2 Tbit/s using commercial real-time coherent optical transceivers. The system used a fully loaded S+C+L band WDM configuration over 19.65-THz spectrum, with 131 channels on a 150 GHz grid (51 in S-band, 40 in C-band, and 40 in L-band), resulting in 262 simultaneously transmitted channels bidirectionally. We deployed commercial optical modules across the operating bands, using 800 G modules in the S and L bands and a hybrid of 1.2 T and 800 G modules for the conventional and extended C band, respectively. The successful real-time operation of all channels within the BER requirement validates hollow-core fiber as a viable platform for wideband, same-wavelength bidirectional transmission, underscoring its potential for next-generation ultra-high-capacity optical interconnects.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A

title: A Unified Security Framework for Smart City AI-Systems: Cross-Domain Vulnerability Analysis and Mitigation Strategies
authors: Rachid Sakassa, Oussama Aziz, Khawla Tadist, El Bachir Tazi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of AI-driven systems across smart city infrastructure has created a complex landscape of interconnected security vulnerabilities that transcend traditional domain boundaries. While existing research predominantly focuses on single domain security analysis, this paper presents the first comprehensive cross-domain security framework for smart city AI-implementations. We synthesize insights from cyber security research across traffic management, energy grids, healthcare, and public safety to identify five critical vulnerability categories: data centric attacks (poisoning and integrity violations), model centric attacks (adversarial examples and evasion), infrastructure compromises (supply chain and edge computing), operational integrity threats (bias and concept drift), and privacy violations (inference and reconstruction attacks). In this paper, we do two main things. First, we build a unified vulnerability taxonomy that organizes threats by how the attack happens, which part of the city it affects, and how the effects can spread. This shows security gaps that don’t appear when each system is analyzed on its own. Second, we propose a mitigation road map that ranks countermeasures by how feasible they are, what they cost in terms of resources, and how much disruption they could cause in the city. Our risk matrix highlights data poisoning and algorithmic bias as the top priorities (risk scores 4.0 and 3.7). We also show that, if the prioritized mitigations are applied, overall vulnerability exposure can be reduced by roughly $\mathbf{5 0 - 7 5 \%}$ over a 12-18 month period.

title: Coordinated Operation of Diode Rectifier and MMC Hybrid HVDC System
authors: Yang Gao, Yuqin Gao, Jin Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To meet the demand for large-scale, cost-effective DC transmission of new energy sources such as deep-sea wind power, this paper conducts research on a hybrid DC system based on diode rectifier units and modular multilevel converters. Firstly, the system compared and analyzed the advantages and disadvantages of series and parallel typologies of DRU and MMC on the DC side, clarifying the characteristics of the series structure in device light-weighting and parallel structure in control flexibility. On this basis, this article proposes an active power collaborative allocation strategy based on the inner loop current control of the MMC. This strategy precisely regulates the AC bus voltage through MMC. It utilizes the linear relationship between DRU transmission power and bus voltage to achieve dynamic and efficient power distribution between the two, effectively solving the active coupling problem in hybrid systems. Through PSCAD/EMTDC electromagnetic transient simulation verification, the proposed system and control strategy demonstrate excellent performance: the DC side power can quickly stabilize to the set value within 0.5 seconds, and the total harmonic distortion of the AC side current is suppressed at a low level of 2.326%. The results indicate that the DRU & MMC hybrid system combines efficiency and flexibility, providing a feasible technical solution for the next generation of new energy DC transmission systems.

title: Safe and Agile Transportation of Cable-Suspended Payload via Multiple Aerial Robots
authors: Yongchao Wang, Junjie Wang, Xiaobin Zhou, Tiankai Yang, Xin Zhou, Chao Xu, Fei Gao
abstract: Transporting a heavy payload using multiple aerial robots (MARs) is an efficient manner to extend the load capacity of a single aerial robot. However, existing planning schemes for the multiple aerial robots transportation system (MARTS) still lack the capability to generate a collision-free and dynamically feasible trajectory in real-time. Therefore, they are limited to low-agility transportation in simple environments. To bridge the gap, we propose a complete planning scheme for the MARTS, achieving safe and agile aerial transportation (SAAT) of a cable-suspended payload in complex environments. Flatness map for the motor's revolutions per minute (RPM) of the aerial robot, considering the complete kinematic constraint and the dynamical coupling between each aerial robot and payload, is derived. To improve the responsiveness for the generation of the safe, dynamically feasible, and agile trajectory in complex environments, a real-time spatio-temporal trajectory planning scheme is proposed for the MARTS. Besides, we break away from the reliance on the state measurement for both the payload and cable, as well as the closed-loop control for the payload, and integrate a fully distributed control scheme to track the agile trajectory that is robust against imprecise payload mass, non-point mass payload, wind disturbances, and communication delays. The proposed schemes are extensively validated through benchmark comparisons, ablation studies, and simulations. Finally, extensive real-world experiments are conducted on practical MARTSs containing different numbers of aerial robots with onboard computers and sensors. The result validates the efficiency and robustness of our proposed schemes for the SAAT in complex environments.

title: ANN-Based Smart Controller for Optimized Power Management in DC Nano-Grid for EV Applications
authors: Suman Kumar, Priyank Srivastava, Rahul Singh, Lalit Jain
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The intensive development of electric vehicles (EVs) insists on the effective and smart power management system to guarantee stable and reliable energy usage and its optimal use. In this paper, an Artificial Neural Network (ANN)-based smart controller will be introduced to handle power in a DC nano-grid that will be integrated with renewable energy sources to serve EVs. The suggested system will form a dynamic balance of sharing power between PV modules, battery storage units, and EV charging stations based on anticipating changes in loads and energy availability. The ANN controller is also trained to reduce energy losses, increase the stability of the voltage, and charge efficiency amid the change in loads and generation states. A comparative study of the conventional proportional-integral (PI) and fuzzy logic controllers establishes that ANN-modeled system responds quicker, with lesser steady-state error and greater overall efficiency of the system. The effectiveness of the proposed method is proven by the simulations conducted in MATLAB/Simulink, which demonstrate that it leads to considerable improvements in the field of energy consumption and grid stability. Intelligent control framework allows making real time choices and responsive power distribution, and as such, it would be very applicable to the next generation EV charging systems and smart cities energy networks. The work in future will involve using the proposed controller in a hardware prototype to determine its effectiveness given the real time operating environment.

title: Hybrid Knowledge Graph–Deep Learning Framework for Automated Exception Handling and Investigation in Complex Insurance Claims
authors: P.R. Sudha Rani, Keerthi Amistapuram, Vamsee Pamisetty, Sneha Singireddy, Dwaraka Nath Kummari, Goutham Kumar Sheelam
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The processing of insurance claims encounters difficulties in managing complex exceptions and fraudulent activities, frequently demanding manual investigation, which is labour-intensive and susceptible to errors. Current AI methodologies primarily concentrate on tabular data or text analysis, resulting in the absence of semantic context and interpretability. This study presents a hybrid system combining knowledge graphs and deep learning, which merges structured tabular data, textual adjuster notes, and knowledge graph embeddings to autonomously discover, classify, and analyse exception claims. The system develops a domain-specific knowledge graph, extracts multi-modal characteristics, and utilises transformer-based text encoders and graph neural networks to forecast exception types, risk severity, and root causes. A module for explainable reasoning produces actionable investigation reports based on subgraphs. Experimental evaluation on synthetic and real-world Kaggle datasets reveals exceptional performance: accuracy 0.94, recall 0.92, F1-score 0.93, ROC-AUC 0.96, RMSE 0.08, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> 0.91, and explanation fidelity 0.92. Comparisons with baseline models, such as XGBoost, BERT+MLP, and GraphSAGE, demonstrate substantial enhancements across all measures. The hybrid approach delivers superior accuracy, interpretability, and efficiency, surpassing current methodologies by combining multi-modal inputs with semantic reasoning for automated exception management in insurance workflows.

title: Transforming Guest Experiences in the Hotel Industry: Harnessing Generative AI for Personalized and Seamless Hospitality Solutions
authors: Soumit Roy, Rajarshi Roy, Mayank Agrawal, Jaya H. Dewan, Pallavi S. Kulkarni
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Generative AI is making hospitality frictionless by changing how hotels tailor and optimize guests' experiences. This article will address a new approach to bettering service customization through methods under the umbrella of Generative AI, which in turn strengthens operational efficiency and customer happiness. Each guest's preferences can be dynamically set, service requests can be automated, and real-time needs can be anticipated by employing machine learning algorithms and natural language processing. The ability to process large amounts of consumer data and render useful insights is the premise on which mathematical models such as customization frameworks and recommendation algorithms are developed and justified. This demonstrates how a number of use cases, from chatbots to tailored room configurations, can enhance guest experiences by examining them. After AI-driven solutions were implemented, operational KPIs and customer satisfaction both increased statistically significantly. This research illustrates the way Generative AI can transform the hotel sector by opening the door for more effective and adaptable service delivery.

title: An Adaptive Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring System with Human-in-the-Loop Machine Learning for Enhanced Appliance Disaggregation
authors: Gurusigaamani Ayyanar Muthulingam, A C Deena, J Hariram Prasath, M Sabareesan, S Hari Murugan
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The paper involves design and implementation of a new Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring (NILM) system used to disaggregate residential energy in detail. The increased energy prices and environmental issues are calling out the need to have the solutions of advanced monitoring which will offer the appliance level of consumption without the need to install perunit sensors. The solution to this requirement that is provided by our system is an advanced combination of Internet of Things (IoT) hardware, machine learning algorithms, and a real-time web dashboard. The suggested architecture involves the use of ESP32 microcontroller and one PZEM-004T sensor to acquire aggregate electrical values (voltage, current, power, power factor) at the main supply of a home. This information is sent to a Python based backend server that is developed by Flask framework, which uses transition detention and multi-parameter matching algorithm to recognize individual appliances by comparing transitions to a database containing known signatures. An important innovation is an adaptive learning system in which feedback on ambiguous events provided by the user is used to keep on improving the accuracy of identification. The dashboard allows real-time user feedback, which is provided through a Socket.IO-based data visualization dashboard. Findings prove that the system is effective in terms of disaggregating loads, it is able to learn when the user provides feedback, and it can also be practical to encourage energy savings and sustainable consumption patterns. The applied NILM solution is an energy-efficient and scalable solution to the residential energy management.

title: Mixture-of-Experts under Finite-Rate Gating: Communication–Generalization Trade-offs
authors: Ali Khalesi, Mohammad Reza Deylam Salehi
abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures decompose prediction tasks into specialized expert sub-networks selected by a gating mechanism. This letter adopts a communication-theoretic view of MoE gating, modeling the gate as a stochastic channel operating under a finite information rate. Within an information-theoretic learning framework, we specialize a mutual-information generalization bound and develop a rate-distortion characterization D(Rg) of finite-rate gating, where Rg := I(X; T), yielding (under a standard empirical rate-distortion optimality condition) E[R(W)] ≤ D(Rg) + δm + √(2/m) I(S;W). The analysis yields capacity-aware limits for communication-constrained MoE systems, and numerical simulations on synthetic multi-expert models empirically confirm the predicted trade-offs between gating rate, expressivity, and generalization.

title: Mechanical–Optical Co-Design for Structural Integrity Optimization in Heterogeneous VCSEL Array Packaging
authors: Kung-An Lin, Thu Huong Bui, Te-Hua Liu, Chao-Hsin Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: Vertical-cavity surface-emitting laser (VCSEL) arrays are a key light source for high-speed data transmission in Co-Packaged Optics (CPO). In VCSEL array packaging, Au–Au bump bonding is commonly used to connect the VCSEL array to the substrate. However, the force applied to flatten Au bumps during the flattening process, along with the layout of the cold-work die steel bonding head in the Au-Au bonding process, affects both the mechanical and optical performance of the VCSEL array. In this work, the effects of Au bump flattening force and bonding head design on VCSEL array packaging are systematically investigated through a combination of experiments and Finite Element Analysis (FEA). Flattening forces of 500 gF, 1000 gF, and 2000 gF were evaluated to study Au bump mechanical before bonding the Au-Au bump. Results show that increasing flattening force improves bump flatness but significantly increases equivalent stress and induces corner cracking. A flattening force of 500 gF provides an optimal balance between flatness and mechanical integrity. Furthermore, two bonding head layouts were compared during Au–Au bonding process at 310°C. The type 1 bonding head applies uniform force over the entire VCSEL surface, resulting in lower equivalent stress but causing surface damage and reduced luminance due to direct contact with the emitting area. In contrast, the type 2 bonding head avoids direct contact with the emission region, achieving higher overall luminance. However, it introduces a non-uniform force distribution and higher Au bump equivalent stress. These results provide critical insights for optimizing Au bump processing and bonding head geometry, enabling more reliable and high-performance VCSEL array packaging for next-generation CPO applications.

title: Bayesian Belief Networks for Predicting Ecosystem Collapse Using Climate and Satellite Data
authors: Hrithvi D Salian
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Ecosystem collapse is an increasingly imminent global threat driven by accelerated climate change and environmental degradation. This study presents a data-driven framework using Bayesian Belief etworks (BB s) to predict ecological collapse by modeling the probabilistic relationships among critical environmental stressors. Real-world datasets from ASA's MODIS satellites—including DVI and land surface temperature (LST)—alongside IPCC CMIP6 climate model outputs are utilized to assess changes in vegetation cover, rainfall variability, and temperature anomalies. The BB structure integrates historical ecological data to infer collapse probabilities under various climate scenarios, particularly in vulnerable biomes such as the Amazon. Results reveal strong correlations between multi-year DVI degradation and extreme temperature and precipitation shifts, aligning with documented collapse events. The probabilistic nature of BB s allows for the quantification of uncertainty and enhances interpretability, making them a robust tool for risk assessment. This approach supports proactive decision-making for conservation planning, ecosystem resilience analysis, and policy design. By integrating climate models with remote sensing indicators, the framework facilitates timely identification of high-risk areas and provides a scalable methodology for global ecological monitoring. (Abstract)

title: A Comparative Study of YOLO and Deep Neural Network Models for Vehicle Number Plate Detection
authors: Ebai Geslen Muwa, Vaneet Kumar, Ruchika Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Due to the increasing number of commercial vehicles, fluctuating weather conditions, and long-existing cars with extremely noisy back-grounds, Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) is crucial in improving traffic management, optimizing transportation networks, and enhancing security zone access control. The accuracy of different license plate detection models trained on diverse datasets was analysed, compared, and evaluated to propose an improved system for the automatic license plate detection and recognition of characters. The objective was to achieve state-of-the-art license plate detection and character recognition accuracy, particularly in challenging scenarios. Several deep learning models were explicitly trained on manually collected and annotated datasets under various environmental conditions. It was shown that YOLOv5 (You Only Look Once model) outperformed other models, achieving a consistent average of 99.69% for detection and 98.37% for recognition across multiple conditions, including rainy, cloudy, and nighttime scenarios.

title: Fast Optical Phased Array Calibration Using a Hybrid GS-SPGD Algorithm
authors: Yanlin Chen, Hao Lou, Xiaodong Xu, Xiaowei Qin
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Optical phased arrays (OPAs) offer a solid-state beam steering solution for Light detection and ranging (LiDAR) and free-space optical (FSO) communication systems. To achieve high-precision beam control, phase calibration of each element is essential. The stochastic parallel gradient descent (SPGD) algorithm is a mainstream model-free calibration method that enables simultaneous phase calibration for all array elements. However, it typically suffers from slow convergence, particularly in large-scale arrays. To address this problem, we first propose the adaptive-weight GS-SPGD (aGS-SPGD) algorithm. This method concurrently executes both the Gerchberg-Saxton (GS) and SPGD approaches, adaptively computing weighting coefficients based on the similarity of phase updates to achieve optimized phase calibration. To further reduce computational complexity, we propose the compact GS-SPGD algorithm (cGSSPGD) that alternatively executes GS and SPGD operations, with switching moments determined by monitoring the evolution of fitness function. Simulation results show that under different array scales and phase noise conditions, the proposed algorithms can efficiently calibrate the OPA output beams. Specifically, in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times 8$</tex> arrays, the aGS-SPGD algorithm exhibits superior convergence speed, completing calibration <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 3 \%}$</tex> faster than SPGD. The cGS-SPGD algorithm maintains comparable convergence performance while delivering a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 2 \%}$</tex> improvement in computational efficiency.

title: CardioFusion: An Ensemble Machine Learning Approach to Cardiovascular Anomaly Detection
authors: Shambhavi Singh, Hari Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Electrocardiograms (ECGs) are essential for identifying cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) and abnormalities related to the circulatory system, but manual interpretation of ECG records is labor-intensive and error-prone. The existing research often relies on high-dimensional data which requires significant computational resources. The proposed framework, CardioFusion, provides a revolutionary approach to solve these challenges by combining medical imaging, artificial intelligence, and signal analysis for signal digitization. The core of the paper involves exploring and implementing data pre-processing and signal extraction techniques to convert ECG images into 1D signals, allowing for the rapid and accurate automatic diagnosis of cardiovascular abnormalities. CardioFusion aims to reduce medical workload, improve patient outcomes, and minimize diagnostic delays by automating ECG interpretation.

title: FPGA-Based Visual Reminder System for Dementia Care
authors: Divya Vani Gaddam, Nikhil Varma, Sai Anirudh V, Shafiya Tahreen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: In today’s world the Dementia cases are increasing primarily due to the growing global population of elderly people. This highlights the importance of interactive reminder systems, by which older people or anyone with dementia can manage their day-to-day tasks independent of others. Existing systems like mobile notifications or software based are lacking visually engaging features which leaves a major gap in user-friendly and hardware related notifications. This paper presents Visual Reminder System which is implemented on FPGA for time-to-time hardware-based reminders. The objective of our work is to develop a reliable time-controlled logic for reminder schedules, by using a 3*3*3 LED cube as visual alert and some on board LED’s for specifying the tasks. This system is developed using Verilog HDL in VIVADO XILINX and implemented on Nexys 4 DDR FPGA. Results demonstrate accurate time progression, reliable reminder activation at scheduled times, and effective user acknowledgment through a stop button.

title: A Large Language Model for Hybrid Table-a nd-Text Question Answering Based on a Dynamic Chain Reasoning Framework
authors: Yongzeng Zhang, Bo Sun, Yilong Wen, Kang Han, Lei Zhang, Dianwen Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: To address the challenges of question-answering with mixed table and text data in smart transportation scenarios, this paper proposes a large language model solution based on a dynamic chain reasoning framework. By constructing a multistage reasoning mechanism that includes situation analysis, information filtering, logical construction, intelligent validation, and engine execution, the solution effectively addresses the collaborative understanding issues between structured tables and unstructured text. Using the DeepSeek R1 model for instruction fine-tuning and combining it with the professional dataset RTTQA(Rail Transit Table Question Answering Dataset) in the rail transit domain, the solution achieves deep integration of domain-specific knowledge. Experiments on the HybridQA, TATQA, and RTTQA datasets demonstrate that the proposed 32B/72B models achieve significant improvements over generalpurpose large language models in Exact Match (EM) and F1 score metrics. Notably, the EM score on the RTTQA dataset in the rail transit domain reached 79.91, validating the method's superiority in complex business scenarios. This study provides an innovative solution for intelligent question-answering systems in the smart transportation domain, offering significant engineering application value.

title: Employ the Machine Learning Models for immediate Predictive Diagnostics: Anomaly detection of Industrial IoT
authors: Jubert R. Pagunuran, Khaled Alqawasmi, V. Samuthira Pandi, Navyatha Ravi, M. Dinesh, A.Rajalingam
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) has transformed plants and factories, facilitating real-time monitoring, automation, and informed decision-making. Nevertheless, in such complex data-rich environments, identification of the fault in a timely manner, detection of the anomaly, has become one of the most important challenges. Unresolved anomalies in IIoT systems can cause unscheduled maintenance, slippage in quality, or even catastrophic failure. The conventional rule-based models are not capable of accommodating the complicated dynamics and the non-linear dependencies that widely exist in industrial systems. In this study, the mixture machine learning (ML) models are applied for real-time predictive diagnostics and anomaly detection in the IIoT networks. System Overview The proposed system combines both supervised and unsupervised ML models getting the best of each of them such as Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Autoencoders, Isolation Forest, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. These models are learned and tested over massive time-series sensor data derived from IIoT ready industrial machines (e.g., pumps, motors, turbines, and conveyors). Method of feature extraction and the detection of temporal patterns are applied to find the abnormal behavior in the operational parameters: temperature, vibration, pressure and energy consumption. One of the key contributions in our study is that models could be deployed in real time and this is made possible by edge computation. By moving ML inference to the edge, the system provides quick response times even under bandwidth-constrained conditions. Alongside, long-term analytics trend forecasting and cross-facility model improvement is made possible via cloud-based aggregation. The combined alerting and strategic system along with hybrid structure allows operators to avoid catastrophic failures before they occur. Experiments on benchmark IIoT datasets show that the proposed models achieved excellent detection performance, e.g., more than 95% precision and recall for the temporal based model-LSTM. The ML-based approach is shown to vastly outperform conventional threshold-based detections in the presence of noisy, multivariate and asynchronous data. In addition, machine behavior evolves, and with it the ability to learn adapts diagnostic accuracy. This work underlines the disruptive nature of the ML for predictive maintenance and real-time industry diagnosis. Through a transition from reactive to proactive approaches, the developed framework minimizes downtime, lowers maintenance costs, and extends asset life. Incorporating smart diagnostics into the IIoT networks not only promotes operational efficiency, but also provides an infrastructural building block for the next-generation autonomous industrial systems that are data, intelligence, and connectivity enabled.

title: Deep Hybrid Networks for Early Detection of Breast Cancer from Multi-Modal Imaging Data
authors: Neha Nahariya
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Breast cancer remains a major global health challenge, which emphasizes the need for early and accurate diagnostic approaches. This study proposes a deep hybrid network that integrates convolutional and transformer architectures for early breast cancer detection using the MultiModel Breast Cancer MSI Dataset. The data set includes ultrasound, histopathology, and chest radiographs, which collectively capture diverse diagnostic perspectives. The proposed model employs modality-specific feature extraction followed by a late-fusion mechanism to combine spatial and contextual information from multiple modalities. The evaluation results demonstrate that the hybrid multimodal network achieves an overall precision of 94.5% and an ROC–AUC of 0.97, significantly outperforming conventional CNN and ensemble-based baselines. Furthermore, interpretability is improved using Grad-CAM visualization, which highlights clinically relevant regions corresponding to malignant features. The framework provides an interpretable, robust and scalable solution for computer-aided breast cancer diagnosis, demonstrating strong potential for integration into real-world healthcare systems to assist radiologists in early detection and decision support.

title: Discovering Top 10 Risk Factors for Type 2 Diabetes through HNB and AODE with Methodological Insights
authors: Chavali Raja Sricharan, Jadi Meghana, Rishika Thakur, Yogash Joshi
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: T2DM is one of the most urgent health conditions on the planet that should be diagnosed earlier and analyzed to take the necessary measures. Based on an enormous publicly available health (Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System; n 2,53,680) dataset, we identify the top-10 risk factors of T2DM and determine the approximate explainable performance of the probabilities-based classifiers. Specifically, we compare and minimise variance respectively Hidden naivete bayes (HNB) the two variants of the classical Naivete Bayes model to learn conditional dependencies and minimise variance respectively. The SHAP values are ranked by the importance of the feature and the top ten risk factors of HighBP and GenHlth, HighChol, BMI, Age, DiffWalk, HeartDiseaseorAttack, PhysHlth, stroke and MentHlth. It provides experimental findings on stratified samples of the data, that AODE and ROC-AUC are 85% and 0.88 accurate relative to HNB (79% and 0.83). That positive case recall on default thresholds is not as good as positive case recall on non-default thresholds, and is subject to both threshold adjustments and class-weight adjustments. The evidence confirms the study hypothesis that AODE is a good interpretable model and risk factors modeled by AODE are consistent with the clinical data. Along with the methodological requirements according to which dependency-based probabilistic classifier must be applied in clustering diabetes, we also obtain in the paper the tradeoff between classification and explainability.

title: RAM Calendar
authors: N/A

title: Hybrid Transformer–Federated Learning Model for Secure Release Engineering in Global Payment Networks
authors: K. Reddy Madhavi, Vijaya Rama Raju Gottimukkala, Lahari Pandiri, Harish Kumar Sriram, Murali Malempati, Balaji Adusupalli
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Global payment networks encounter significant difficulties in administering safe software releases owing to diverse systems, sensitive client information, and escalating deployment difficulty. Conventional centralised learning methods pose privacy risks and do not effectively identify cross-silo patterns. This study introduces a Hybrid Transformer– Federated Learning model for secure release engineering, utilising Transformer encoders to extract multi-modal features from CI/CD logs, release notes, and transaction monitoring, while ensuring data privacy through federated optimisation, secure collection, and differential privacy. Solid methods, such as Krum and reduced mean aggregation, counteract hostile client attacks and abnormal updates. Experimental assessment utilising datasets from Kaggle reveals exceptional performance with an Accuracy of 0.95, Accuracy of 0.93, Recall of 0.95, F1-score of 0.94, and AUC of 0.96, surpassing baseline models such as Random Forest, LSTM, and FedAvg Transformer. The proposed architecture facilitates cross-silo knowledge sharing, secure risk prediction for releases, and anomaly detection, all while preserving client data security. The model surpasses existing methods in predicting accuracy, robustness, and privacy protection, providing practical utility in the deployment pipelines of global payment networks. This study establishes a basis for privacy-conscious, scalable, and dependable release engineering systems within distributed financial contexts.

title: Iterative Floorplan Refinement for Efficient Area Utilization and Reduced Congestion in VLSI Physical Design
authors: P.Kishore, B.Haindavi, V.Priyanka
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The rapid evolution of Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) technology has enabled the creation of complex chips and System-on-Chip (SoCs) by integrating billions of transistors within a single integrated circuit. The design of such advanced chips is a lengthy process that involves multiple stages, particularly in the domain of physical design. Physical design converts the Register Transfer Level (RTL) netlist into a manufacturable layout, commonly referred to as a GDS. It involves Floorplan, Powerplan, Placement, Clock tree synthesis, and Routing stages. Among them, floorplanning is a crucial phase, as it influences design performance and manufacturability by addressing congestion and area utilization. This paper focuses on a block-level implementation of an ORCATOP block using 32nm technology. It employs Synopsys’ IC Compiler II tool, using nine metal layers for routing and operating at a maximum frequency of 200 MHz. This study focuses on optimizing the design from the initial floorplan to various placement stages. Initially, the design exhibits severe congestion issues. Upon modifying the floorplan, significant improvements were achieved, particularly in congestion reduction. The results indicate that the iterative approach and a well-optimized floorplan highlight the significant reduction in congestion while achieving better utilization of the area by improving the overall design efficiency.

title: Editorial—March 2026
authors: Pak Chung Wong

title: Assessment of Contrast-Enhanced Ultrasound Detection Limit for Abdominal Hemorrhage_supp2-3677094.mp4
authors: Brian Telfer
abstract: Hemorrhage is the leading cause of preventable death from trauma, with most fatalities occurring in pre-hospital settings. The current standard for point-of-care hemorrhage detection is the focused assessment with sonography in trauma (FAST) exam, or computed tomography (CT) angiography after hospital arrival. Contrast-enhanced ultrasound (CEUS) has been proposed as a next-generation FAST exam, due to potential for (1) differentiating active vs. inactive bleeding, (2) localizing bleeding sources, and (3) guiding percutaneous and/or endovascular treatments to temporize bleeding for prolonged transport. While these advantages have been reported in human trauma case studies, characterization of the minimum hemorrhage rate detectable by CEUS remains a translational research need. Prior work using conventional B-mode imaging and microvascular CEUS algorithms showed detection of 5 mL/min flow in a generic hemorrhage phantom. Here, we apply nonlinear imaging waveforms (pulse inversion, PI, and amplitude modulation, AM) with an iterative spatiotemporal filter that further enhances contrast signals (fast iterative-shrinkage thresholding algorithm, FISTA) to detect significantly lower flow rates in an anatomically realistic abdominal hemorrhage phantom. We demonstrate detection of bleeding at a minimum flow rate of 0.5 mL/min, comparable to multidetector CT. We achieve this detection limit even with 10% of the standard contrast dose (area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.90 and 0.84 for PI+FISTA and AM+FISTA, respectively, compared to 0.38 for conventional B-mode), suggesting that CEUS examination is feasible throughout contrast wash-in / wash-out. Finally, to bridge towards in vivo characterization, we present a novel porcine liver hemorrhage model tailored to evaluating CEUS capabilities.

title: Taking the Pulse
authors: Gary Singh
abstract: A contemporary bio-art pioneer working at the nexus of digital, physical, and biological systems, Karle grew up in Endicott, New York, the home of IBM, where the future of new computing technology influenced her at a young age. Parallel to this, she underwent experimental surgical procedures to fix a birth defect that left her with missing skin and bone on her head. All of which led to an expansive career. Now, she regularly investigates the impact of emergent and exponential technologies on the body and humanity.

title: Research on Thermal Management Algorithm for Chips with Few-Shot Deep Reinforcement Learning Integrating Graph Neural Networks
authors: Jing Liu, Xihua Qiu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address the shortcomings of traditional thermal management algorithms, such as insufficient accuracy under dynamic loads, poor generalization of DRL algorithms with few shots, and superficiality in the fusion of GNN and DRL, this paper proposes a graph attention-enhanced meta-learning deep reinforcement learning algorithm (GAM-SSDRL). The algorithm constructs a three-level closed-loop architecture of “thermal field graph modeling - few-shot decision-making dynamic collaborative optimization.” It accurately captures spatial correlations of the thermal field through a graph attention network, achieves efficient few-shot training based on an improved MAML framework, and adaptively matches feature extraction and decision execution through a dynamic collaborative strategy. Experiments based on a 16 -core CPU chip simulation model show that GAM-SSDRL performs optimally under high, medium, and low load scenarios, with an average temperature deviation as low as 1.6° C, representing reductions of 50.0 %, 23.8 %, and 15. 8 % compared to traditional PID, conventional DRL, and GNN+DRL, respectively. The thermal runaway response time is 44.3 ms, a reduction of over 50.6 %; the average chip power consumption is 78.9 W, a reduction of 18.3 %; and only 100 training samples are needed to achieve the target accuracy, improving sample efficiency by over 54.5 %. This algorithm fills the technical gap in precise control of dynamic thermal fields with small sample sizes, providing a new solution for thermal management of high-power-density chips, and has significant theoretical and engineering value.

title: Practice of Blockchain Technology in Trusted Notarization for Emerging Contaminants Monitoring Data
authors: Shuang Shen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Emerging contaminants monitoring data face challenges such as susceptibility to tampering, difficulty in traceability, and insufficient mutual trust among multiple parties during collection, transmission, and storage. To address these issues, this study proposes and implements a blockchain-based trusted data notarization solution. A distributed notarization framework is designed and built using Hyperledger Fabric. A collaborative notarization model is then constructed to optimize on-chain storage load. Building on the conventional Kafka consensus, a voting verification mechanism weighted by monitoring task types and node reputation is introduced. Finally, core smart contracts are developed. Experimental results demonstrate that the system achieves 100% tamper detection accuracy in 1,350 simulated attacks, 95% of cross-institutional queries (5,000 tests) are responded to within 3.17 seconds, under 400 concurrent loads, the dynamic consensus mechanism achieves 205 TPS with an average latency below 2.5 seconds. This study confirms the solution's effectiveness in resolving trusted notarization challenges for emerging contaminants monitoring data, providing innovative technical support for transparent and reliable environmental data management systems.

title: Title Page i
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)

title: Mitigating Congestion with Physics-Informed Neural Networks and Adaptive Traffic Signal Control
authors: Ntemi Masanja, Abdul Ngereza, Tumaini Sakaza, Philip Balyagati, Emmanuel Kidando
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Traffic congestion and delays at signalized intersections remain a challenge in urban mobility. To minimize congestion, adaptive traffic signal control systems have been adopted across the United States. However, for these systems to function at their best, systems require an accurate estimation of traffic states, such as traffic density and queue length. This paper implements a relatively novel method, Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs), for real-time traffic density estimation to enhance adaptive intersection signal control. The methodology leverages PINNs, which integrate both data-driven learning and physical constraints imposed by the Lighthill-Whitham-Richards (LWR) macroscopic traffic flow model. A simulation of a four-way intersection was used to generate realistic spatio-temporal traffic data to evaluate the performance of the model. Separate PINNs models, one trained for each incoming approach using simulation-based spatio-temporal density data, provide segment-level density predictions. These real-time density estimates are then employed within an adaptive traffic signal control algorithm designed to adjust green light phasing based on detected demand. The proposed PINNs control strategy was evaluated through simulation, comparing its performance against the Webster traffic timing method using key metrics such as average vehicle delay at the intersection and average vehicle travel time. The findings show an approximate 18% decrease in average vehicle delay and more than a 6% reduction in average travel time when utilizing adaptive traffic control based on the PINNs method for determining density values. These findings highlight the potential of leveraging PINNs for accurate, real-time traffic state estimation, which can effectively inform and improve the performance of adaptive traffic signal control systems, leading to reduced congestion and enhanced mobility at intersections.

title: Wearable HRV Stress Detection and Intervention for Sensory Overload in Children with Autism in the Philippines
authors: Berenice Medina, Miguel Villanueva, Norshuhani Zamin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) are often highly susceptible to sensory overload, triggered by factors such as excessive noise, unfamiliar environments, or challenges in emotional regulation. Sensory overload can result in intense reactions, including tantrums and meltdowns, which may escalate into heightened anxiety, withdrawal, self-stimulatory behaviors, or even impaired communication. These responses make it difficult for caregivers to manage and comfort the child effectively and can hinder their learning, social interactions, and emotional well-being. Existing assistive technologies, such as wearable devices, have been developed to detect sensory overload and provide real-time information to parents. This study proposes an innovative assistive technology system, designed to enhance the management of sensory overload in children with ASD. The system integrates a wearable device equipped with a DFRobot Gravity photoplethysmography (PPG) heart rate sensor, using Arduino with an ESP32 microcontroller. The wearable continuously measures heart rate and derives heart rate variability (HRV) metrics, which serve as physiological indicators of stress associated with sensory overload. Validation experiments were conducted with five high-functioning children with ASD, where heart rate and HRV stress scores were measured before and after controlled physical activity, using wrist and finger sensor placements. Comparative analysis with a commercial wearable band showed a strong correlation (r = 0.97) and a minimal mean difference in BPM of 2.00, highlighting the feasibility of our HRV-based stress wearable in real-world use. Our system aims to improve stress management and strengthen communication between children with ASD and their caregivers in educational and everyday settings.

title: Machine Learning-Based Load Forecasting for Vehicle-to-Grid Scheduling: A Case Study on the Italian Power System
authors: Zahid Ullah, Michele De Santis, Luigi Rubino
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) are emerging not only as a sustainable mode of transportation but also as a promising source of flexibility in modern power systems using vehicle-to-grid (V2G) technology. This study investigated the integration of short-term load forecasting with V2G scheduling using open-access hourly data from the Italian grid. Multiple forecasting models, including linear regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, and a hybrid ARIMA + XGBoost model, were evaluated for their accuracy using standard metrics. The hybrid model demonstrated superior performance, with an RMSE of 376.91 MW and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.9969. This model was then used to simulate two V2G discharging strategies, forecast-driven discharging during the top-5 peak hours and fixed-window strategy. The fixed-window strategy resulted in 2320 MWh of energy discharge over the month and contributed to improved load curve flattening. These results demonstrate the potential of coupling accurate forecasting with well-timed V2G operations to support peak-load management and enhance grid flexibility. This study provides a standardized process for integrating forecasting into V2G planning, supporting smart grid development and policy design.

title: Research on Carbon Emission Allocation Methods in the Production Stage of Electrical Smart Meters
authors: Jun Yi, Bo Miao, Jie Wen, Haotian Zhang, Wanshui Yu, Ying Xing
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Carbon footprint of products (CFP) accounting needs reasonable energy allocation, but existing studies lack methods for smart electricity meter production. This study proposed four co-produced product carbon allocation methods for power equipment, then applied three (output, mass, output value) to calculate production-stage carbon emissions of a DDZY242-Z Class A smart meter. Results showed its emissions were 0.43,0.26,0.25 kgCO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> e via the three methods, with mass and output value results closer to the industry average (0.28 kgCO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> e). Conclusion: Mass allocation is preferred, providing a reference for power equipment carbon accounting; future research will expand case studies.

title: Mind the Gap: The Laggards in a G2B Digital Service
authors: Apriasih
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: HM Land Registry for England and Wales introduced the Digital Registration Service (DRS) in 2021 to replace the Electronic Document Registration Service (EDRS). Despite its aim to accelerate property registration, many clients had not adopted it as of four months before the 2022 deadline. This study examined the cognitive barriers to adopting DRS using a qualitative approach. Five participants, three clients and two HM Land Registry staff, were interviewed, and the data were thematically analyzed using the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM). Although clients perceived DRS as easy to use, perceived incompleteness arising from temporal misalignment reduced its usefulness, leading obligated users to adopt laggard behavior driven by intertemporal cost considerations.

title: Llm-Fuzz: Adaptive, Structure-Aware Fuzzing of Javascript Engines with Large Language Models
authors: Kongyang Liu, Chengxiang Tan
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Fuzzing modern JavaScript (JS) engines remains challenging due to the semantic complexity required to explore deep program states and advanced components such as Just-InTime (JIT) compilers. Traditional mutation-based fuzzers often generate invalid inputs, while existing structure-aware techniques still face a semantic wall, producing syntactically valid but semantically flawed programs. This paper presents LLM-Fuzz, a coverage-guided fuzzing framework that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to perform AST-based, semantics-aware mutations. Unlike prior fuzzers with fixed operators, LLM-Fuzz introduces an adaptive feedback loop that fine-tunes the mutation strategy during fuzzing via Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). Experiments on V8 and ChakraCore show that LLM-Fuzz reduces runtime error rates and achieves higher edge coverage compared to state-of-the-art fuzzers. These results demonstrate that incampaign, LLM-driven adaptation can improve the effectiveness of fuzzing structured inputs.

title: CAGNet - Comprehensive Node-Labeled Graph Analysis of Mobile Malware Using Force Atlas 2 and Deep Learning
authors: Nur Khairani Kamarudin, Ahmad Firdaus, Mohd Faris Mohd Fuzi, Nurul Hidayah Ahmad Zukri, Mohammad Hafiz bin Ismail, Muhamad Arif Hashim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Mobile malware continues to pose a significant threat as the use of Android-based devices expands across personal, financial, and professional domains. The constant evolution of malware techniques makes early and accurate detection a critical requirement to safeguard users. Traditional detection approaches, which rely mainly on static statistical features, often fail to capture deeper semantic patterns of malicious behavior. In this study, we propose CAGNet, a graphbased detection framework that utilizes fully labeled call graphs to preserve detailed semantic and structural information of applications. By applying the Force Atlas 2 layout to model graph relationships and employing a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) as the detection engine, CAGNet effectively leverages node-level information for robust analysis. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that this approach achieves a highest detection accuracy of 95.89 %, surpassing conventional static analysis methods and highlighting the advantage of full node labeling in mobile malware detection.

title: Smart Lung Health Analyzer: A Sensor-Based Device for Early Detection of Respiratory Diseases
authors: Kiruthika B, Manikandan L, Birundha Devi P, Jaisiva S, Prema S
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Early detection and monitoring of respiratory and physiological illness are instrumental in enhancing the outcomes of patients and alleviating healthcare loads. This study presents an IoT-based health monitoring system that incorporates several biomedical and environmental sensors such as ESP8266, Flow Sensor, DHT11, MAX30100, PPG Sensor, pH Sensor, and MQ135. The system gathers real-time information on lung airflow, body temperature, heart rate, oxygen in blood, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex> level, and parameters on air quality. The sensor values are sent through the ESP8266 to a computing system, where complex machine learning algorithms analyze and process the data. The main purpose is to detect and classify health conditions like pneumonia, tuberculosis, asthma, and Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD), as well as detecting normal health conditions. Using sensor fusion and smart data processing, the presented system presents an inexpensive, non-invasive, and scalable method for disease detection and continuous health monitoring. This methodology not only increases diagnostic precision but also lays the groundwork for distant care applications, facilitating timely intervention and better patient care.

title: Ethernet-Based Inter-FPGA Data Transfer Using Softcore Processors for Kintex Ultrascale
authors: Harshvardhan Singh, Veer Mukadam, Hem Raj Shau, Durga Digdarsini, Mahesh Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: In this paper, high-speed data transfer is carried out between the two different FPGA boards using two different softcore processor configurations. First configuration involves, the MicroBlaze softcore processor with the lwIP stack to implement TCP/IP over Ethernet, enabling reliable, protocol-compliant data transfer while the second configuration comprise the LEON3 softcore processor with the GRETH MAC core to implement raw Ethernet communication, allowing low-latency, high-speed data exchange without any transport-layer protocol. The designs are implemented using Xilinx Kintex-7 and Xilinx Kintex Ultrascale FPGA based boards, both equipped with on-board Ethernet PHYs and support for embedded data processing.

title: Dynamic Time-Stamped Network for Optimizing Project Approval Workflows
authors: Fan Shuxian, Wang Hao, Du Bin, Yuan Xiaoyong, Zhang Sisi
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In the context of digital transformation driven by information technology, traditional project approval processes often fail to meet the efficiency demands of modern enterprises due to complex procedures, poor information flow, and excessive manual intervention. Existing studies show limitations in identifying critical nodes and coordinating resource and time optimization, and they lack effective mechanisms for dynamic optimization under uncertainty. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a comprehensive optimization method for project approval processes based on critical node identification and resource optimization. The approval process is modeled as a directed acyclic graph (DAG) through the construction of a timelabeled network data structure. Forward and backward time calculations combined with float time analysis are applied to identify critical paths and nodes. A multi-dimensional evaluation model is developed to assess node importance for accurate critical node detection. Furthermore, a multi-level resource management strategy is designed to balance workloads by adjusting non-critical activities and optimizing resource utilization. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively identifies critical paths and nodes, achieving a 10.1% reduction in total project duration and a 15.6% improvement in resource utilization, showing strong applicability in practical engineering project management.

title: ChunkGraph: Relationship-Driven Retrieval Through Progressive Complete Graphs
authors: Jiale Zhang, Kui Zhao, Hao Zhang, Fuzhe Zhang, Xu Liu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Traditional Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems focus on similarity-based retrieval, often failing to capture the relationships necessary for complex reasoning. We introduce ChunkGraph, a relationship-driven retrieval method that constructs progressive complete graphs from document chunks. Our approach progressively refines retrieval through three stages: (1) complete graph initialization where every chunk pair has potential relationships, (2) adaptive relationship discovery using BiModal distribution analysis, and (3) progressive pruning via Personalized PageRank to focus on the most relevant relationship paths. Unlike entity-based knowledge graphs, ChunkGraph treats chunks as nodes in a complete graph structure, enabling comprehensive relationship modeling without expensive entity extraction. Evaluation on HotpotQA and Quality datasets demonstrates that relationship-driven retrieval achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1=0.71$</tex>, matching state-of-the-art methods while providing superior computational efficiency compared to entity-based graph methods <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(60 \%)$</tex> faster indexing time). ChunkGraph proves that progressive complete graphs can effectively bridge the gap between similarity and relevance in retrieval tasks.

title: Quantum Annealing Algorithm Based Power Optimization for Electric Vehicle Charging Using a Buck Converter
authors: B Sharmiladevi, T Bhuvanasundar, NP Bose, J Dharunkumar, M Gokul
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: DC-charging Electric Vehicle (EV) systems commonly use DC-DC buck converters to regulate voltage and manage power. Conventional control techniques, such as Proportional-Integral (PI) controllers or Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), often struggle with dynamic load and input variations, resulting in higher switching losses, voltage ripples, and reduced efficiency. This paper proposes a novel Quantum Annealing Algorithm (QAA)-based approach for real-time power optimization in EV charging using a buck converter. By leveraging quantum tunneling principles, the proposed method dynamically adjusts the converter duty cycle to minimize switching losses, reduce harmonic distortion, and stabilize voltage under varying load and input conditions. Simulation results demonstrate that the QAA-based controller converges faster, improves energy efficiency, and enhances robustness compared to conventional methods. This framework offers scalable and flexible solutions for modern EV charging infrastructure, combining quantum optimization with classical converter technology to achieve energy-efficient and highperformance operation.

title: GCWCN 2025 Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)

title: Neuro-Symbolic Dynamic Pricing for Intent-Driven Sustainable UAV Swarm Task Allocation
authors: Jianrui Fan, Lei Lei, Shengsuo Cai, Pan Cao
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: We present a hierarchical Neuro-Symbolic Intent-Driven Networking (NS-IDN) framework for sustainable cooperative task allocation in UAV swarms. Our approach innovatively bridges the intent-to-action gap via a three-layered architecture: an LLM-based translator (L1) converts natural-language commands into structured tasks; a reinforcement learning (RL) commander (L2) optimizes the meta-parameters of a hybrid dynamic pricing formula, broadcasting strategic economic incentives rather than low-level actions; and a lightweight Gossip-Intent-Response (GIR) protocol (L3) drives distributed execution and incorporates a decentralized self-preservation logic for autonomous energy management. The L2 commander's policy is learned using a shared Transformer backbone to process variable-length state inputs, enabling sophisticated, contextaware decision-making. Simulation experiments reveal NS-IDN's fundamental advantages over state-of-the-art baselines.
