While market-based methods like CBBA suffer a performance collapse in large swarms due to quadratic communication explosion, and end-to-end MARL agents like MAPPO incur prohibitive bandwidth costs due to continuous vector exchange, NS-IDN maintains a linear overhead and sustains a near-perfect Task Completion Rate (TCR) of 0.99. Furthermore, unlike MAPPO which fails to generalize to novel intents, NS-IDN demonstrates instantaneous adaptability, re-tasking agents in under a second in response to novel, mid-mission intents. These advances, coupled with its modular design, establish NS-IDN as a robust and practical framework for scalable intent-driven swarm intelligence.

title: Real-Time Polyp Detection and Classification in Colonoscopy Videos Using Lightweight Transformer Networks
authors: Chanakya Kumar Jha, Deepak Gupta, Pallavi Jha, Gulnora Khudayorova, Rakhimjon Rajapboyevich Rakhimov, Sardor Sabirov
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Colorectal cancer remains one of the leading causes of cancer-related deaths worldwide, with early detection through colonoscopy being critical for prevention. This paper presents a novel lightweight transformer-based architecture for real-time polyp detection and classification in colonoscopy videos. Our proposed method, termed LightPolyp-Former, combines the efficiency of depthwise separable convolutions with the global attention mechanisms of transformers to achieve superior performance while maintaining computational efficiency. We introduce a multi-scale feature aggregation module and a temporal consistency constraint to handle the challenges of varying polyp sizes and video frame continuity. Extensive experiments on five benchmark datasets (Kvasir-SEG, CVC-ClinicDB, ETIS-LaribPolypDB, CVC-ColonDB, and EndoScene) demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art detection accuracy (mAP of 94.7%) and classification performance (F1-score of 93.2%) while operating at 67 FPS on a single GPU, making it suitable for real-time clinical deployment. The model size is reduced by 73% compared to existing transformer-based methods while maintaining comparable accuracy.

title: Fault-Tolerant Sliding Mode Leader-Follower Strategy for Multiple Charging of Electric Vehicles
authors: Desh Deepak Sharma, Jeremy Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Rapid adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) necessitates a reliable and efficient charging infrastructure. However, charging systems are susceptible to faults caused by power fluctuations, grid instability, cyber threats, and equipment failures. This paper explores fault-tolerant control (FTC) strategies for EV charging, focusing on adaptive fault detection, resilient leader-follower control, and decentralized architectures. This paper proposes a Fault-Tolerant Sliding Mode Leader-Follower strategy for multiple EV Charging. For obtaining the robustness in multiple charging of electric vehicles, the sliding mode control laws for leader and follower EVs incorporate sliding mode gain for the leader, reaching law gain, and the signum function. The reduction in charging power and overcharging faults are considered in multiple charging of EVs. The efficacy of the proposed strategy is tested with simulation work carried out using python software.

title: A Novel Constraint-Aware Fixed Time Stabilization Technique for Micro Grid Integrated Multi-Area Network
authors: Anamika Talukdar, Tapan Kumar Maiti
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: This paper emphasizes on the mathematical analysis of load frequency control (LFC) of two interconnected areas where one is thermal area and another one is a micro grid. The modeling of the whole system is done with the help of state-space form with consideration of non-linearity such as governor dead-band, saturation and generation rate constraint. The proposed idea is novel in case of designing the sliding surface considering area control error (ACE) and governor command. The design of the new controller- a fixed-time integral sliding mode control (FTI-SMC) and the Lyapunov stability analysis, both make sure to converge the ACE to zero within a bounded time that is independent of initial conditions. The approach of this paper is to fill the gap present in the existing simulated versions by explaining the theoretical and mathematical solutions in case of novel designing of controllers as well as their stability analysis.

title: Task-Aware Remote Sensing Image Semantic Segmentation via Multi-Layer Semantic Fields and Adaptive Decoding
authors: Tengfei Sun, Shixing Zhang, Peng Bei
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Remote sensing image segmentation can be vital for a variety of tasks including rescue, supply delivery, and military operations. Yet existing methods remain task-agnostic, focusing mainly on improving pixel-level accuracy while ignoring further functional and risk semantics, thus failing to produce missionsensitive outputs. We propose a task-adaptive framework that extends RemoteSAM with a lightweight Semantic Field Head (SF-Head) and a Task-Adaptive Decoder (TAD). The SF-Head introduces geometry, functionality, and risk layers, while a CLIPbased text encoder embeds mission-level descriptions into task vectors. TAD fuses task semantics with multi-layer features, enabling “same image, different tasks” segmentation. Experiments show that our method maintains competitive accuracy while uniquely generating task-aware results.

title: Bridging AI and Practical Implementation: A Low-Cost, High-Accuracy Motion Capture System for Dynamic Environments
authors: Inder Pal Singh, Mahesh Chandrashekar, Jubert R. Pagunuran, D.Shanmugam, A.Rajalingam, C.Murugamani
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: This research introduces a cost-effective, high-precision human motion capture system designed for real-time performance in dynamic and unconstrained environments. Leveraging lightweight artificial intelligence techniques, the proposed framework integrates the Continuously Adaptive Mean Shift (Camshift) algorithm with Kalman filtering to deliver robust tracking under conditions of variable lighting, rapid motion, and partial occlusion all while requiring only standard video cameras and minimal computational resources. A novel algorithm for motion data extraction from video sequences further enhances the system’s responsiveness, achieving an average processing time of just 54 milliseconds per instance significantly outperforming conventional approaches. The system’s modular, scalable architecture enables seamless deployment across a range of practical applications, including sports analytics, physical rehabilitation, interactive animation, and intelligent human-computer interfaces. This work bridges the gap between AI-driven theoretical innovation and real-world applicability, resulting in a scalable and versatile motion capture framework optimized for the complexities of modern intelligent systems.

title: Comparative Analysis of Vision Transformer, DenseNet201, and InceptionResNetV2 for Accurate Skin Lesion Recognition
authors: Nandini MS, J.R. Arunkumar, Prakash Dhanavelu, Durairaj. M
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: It is only possible to find melanoma and other skin diseases early by carefully dividing and classifying skin lesions. This project aims to compare three advanced deep learning architectures for autonomous skin lesion detection based on dermoscopy: Vision Transformer (ViT), DenseNet201, and InceptionResNetV2. We used softmax entropy to see how uncertain the model was. ViT has the least amount of entropy (0.5530 nats), which means that its predictions are more accurate than those of DenseNet201 (0.6781 nats) and InceptionResNetV2 (0.6902 nats). We used standard measures like Intersection over Union (IoU), Pixel Accuracy (PA), Sensitivity, Specificity, and Precision to see how well the segmentation worked. The segmentation was rather well, with an IoU of 0.5084 and a pixel accuracy of 0.6355. In medical diagnostics, it is very important to be able to find lesions accurately, with a sensitivity of 0.7643, to avoid false negatives. The specificity of 0.5102 means that there are a lot of false positives and that the data is likely to be over-segmented. The precision of 0.6029 shows that the estimated lesion locations are somewhat reliable. The framework consistently identifies lesions; however, the findings suggest that enhancements in segmentation accuracy and background differentiation are necessary to elevate diagnostic precision.

title: Sky-ground Collaborative Perception and Image Recognition Algorithm for Transmission Lines Facing Complex Terrain
authors: Jinhu Liu, Shuntao Huang, Xiao Zeng, Songhao Shi, Dianhong Xu, Yadi Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of low efficiency, high risk and data fragmentation of traditional manual and single unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) inspection in complex terrain such as mountains and forests, this paper proposes a three-layer cooperative sensing framework of "satellite -UAV- ground robot", and designs a cross-modal feature alignment network (CMFA-Net) and a lightweight YOLOv8 detection model. The system uses satellite remote sensing to generate global patrol heat map, and guides multi-UAVs to dynamically partition based on improved Voronoi diagram and energy consumption model, achieving 98% coverage in 3.9 h and shortening the range by 44%. CMFA-Net aligns the features of satellite, UAV and ground heterogeneous images with cross-correlation attention mechanism, and the accuracy of cross-view defect association is still 91.8% when the vegetation is blocked by 60%, and the mAP@0.5 is improved to 93.6%. At the same time, the dynamic safe distance model and improved A* path planning are proposed to reduce the collision risk of UAV to 2.3%. Through mixed quantization compression, the parameter quantity of YOLOv8 is reduced to 1.2 M, and real-time reasoning at 25 FPS is realized on Jetson AGX Orin, and the loss of mAP is only 0.3%. The experimental results of 83 km transmission line in Hengduan Mountains show that this method has the advantages of high efficiency, safety and accuracy under the conditions of complex terrain and strong wind and rain fog, and provides a feasible scheme for intelligent inspection of transmission lines.

title: Research on the Construction of Data Standard Management System
authors: Wei Wu, Liang Ge, Yaping Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: Effective management of data standards is crucial for ensuring data quality and interoperability in cloud-native and distributed data environments. This paper proposes a data standard management system that enables integrated governance of both foundational and application-level standards through a standardized pedigree model and clause-level differential analysis. The system incorporates a unified database schema supporting version control, AI-enhanced feature extraction, and efficient retrieval mechanisms. Developed within a B/S architecture using Spring Boot and Vue.js, it provides granular permission management, real-time visualization of modifications, and external API integration. Results demonstrate that the framework significantly improves standard consistency, traceability, and operational adaptability, offering a scalable solution for complex data-driven organizations.

title: A Zero-Knowledge-Based Approach to Resist Poisoning Attacks in Federated Learning
authors: Juhao Wang, Xiaosong Guan, Changxin Gao, Shijuan Yang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: In the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) network, numerous vehicle terminals are required to continuously upload local data to maintain the latest service models, which supports intelligent transportation and personalized services. However, the privacy risks posed by this continuous data uploading cannot be ignored. Federated learning, as a distributed ma-chine learning paradigm, enables global model training without sharing original data. The introduction of blockchain further supports decentralization and immutability. However, federated learning also faces the risk of poisoning attacks, where malicious clients may upload abnormal or tampered model updates, severely impacting global model performance. To address this, this paper proposes a security framework that combines zero-knowledge proofs, federated learning, and blockchain. Clients use zero-knowledge proofs to ensure the legitimacy of uploaded updates, while the blockchain is responsible for verification and storage. Ultimately, a robust global model is obtained through federated aggregation. Experimental results demonstrate that this scheme effectively resists poisoning attacks, significantly improving system security and reliability while protecting user privacy.

title: A Significance Measure for Node Splits in C4.5
authors: Nicholas Bidler, Shawn X. Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Decision trees remain one of the most interpretable and computationally efficient classification methods. The ID3 (Iterative Dichotomiser 3) algorithm is a classic algorithm in machine learning used to construct decision trees from a dataset. C4.5 extends ID3 to handle numeric attributes using thresholding and information gain. The C4.5 algorithm recursively partitions data based on maximum information gain, producing trees that progress from the most informative attributes at the root to the least informative at the leaves. While inner nodes are chosen by optimizing entropy-based criteria, leaf nodes do not admit further entropy comparisons, leaving the importance of the last split ambiguous. We propose a simple, dataset-agnostic scoring scheme that quantifies the significance of a parent node’s final subdivision into its leaves, enabling a direct measure of the utility of the final split. After training a full tree, we record predictions and ground truth at each leaf on a held-out test set, compute per-leaf scores as accuracy × correct, and then "merge" sibling leaves back into their parent by concatenating their predicted/actual lists; the same score is computed for the merged node. The final-split significance is measured as the difference between the sum of leaf scores and the merged-parent score; zero indicates no benefit from splitting. Across several public datasets, the measure highlights when small but accurate leaves or imbalanced families meaningfully improve performance, and when splits add little value. The measure is simple to compute, compatible with standard pruning workflows, and can act as a practical heuristic for late-split evaluation. Our experiments on multiple real-world datasets show that this approach can identify and prune non-beneficial splits, improving model interpretability without compromising accuracy. This method offers a statistically aware pruning criterion that complements existing entropy-based splitting strategies.

title: An Integrated Learning Framework for Hypertension Risk Prediction Using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Jiasuo Xu, Siwei Liao, Shuxing Luo, Xipei Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In this study, a high-precision classification prediction framework fusing Random Forest algorithm and XGBoost algorithm is constructed. First, four single models, including logistic regression, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Decision Tree and Random Forest, were constructed and evaluated, and the evaluation results showed that logistic regression, SVM and Random Forest performed better in terms of interpretability and accuracy, while the Decision Tree was relatively poor; second, in order to break through the bottleneck of the performance of a single model, Random Forest was fused with XGBoost to construct a stacked integrated model, which adopts a two-layer architecture. Secondly, in order to break the performance bottleneck of a single model, Random Forest and XGBoost are fused together to construct a stacked integrated model, which adopts a two-layer architecture: the first layer trains the two base models independently and records the prediction probabilities, and the second layer splices these prediction probabilities with the original features to train the meta-model, which gives full play to the complementary advantages of the two features - Random Forest is based on the idea of Bagging, which can This architecture gives full play to the complementary advantages of the two features - Random Forest is based on the idea of Bagging, which can enhance the generalisation ability of the model and reduce the variance, and XGBoost is based on the idea of Boosting, which can effectively reduce the bias and deal with complex relationships. Through this stacking integration strategy, the models are deeply integrated to form a high-precision classification prediction system. Through multi-level model construction and integration optimisation, the framework significantly improves the accuracy, stability and generalisation ability of classification prediction, and shows excellent performance in classification prediction tasks, providing innovative prediction methods and technical paths for related fields.

title: Smart Community Health Monitoring and Early Warning System for Water-Borne Diseases in Rural
authors: Preethi P, Sri Vishruthi C, Revathi R
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Water-borne diseases continue to be a major concern in rural regions, primarily due to inadequate monitoring of water quality and delayed identification of outbreaks. Recognizing the recurring nature of such diseases emphasizes the importance of proactive health surveillance in these communities. This study introduces an Intelligent Community Health Monitoring and Early Warning Framework that integrates Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Networks (ST-GNN) with advanced Boosting algorithms, including XGBoost and LightGBM. By analyzing both patient health records and environmental factors, the proposed model aims to predict potential outbreaks, thereby connecting data- driven research with practical public health applications. By integrating rural health surveillance, anomaly detection, and patient data prediction, the proposed framework can generate early warnings, allowing healthcare professionals to intervene before diseases become widespread. This proactive approach addresses a critical gap in low-resource settings. Ultimately, the aim of this system is to improve community health monitoring, reduce response times, and support health authorities in making data-driven decisions in resource-limited regions. From an academic perspective, it demonstrates how data-science techniques can be effectively applied to enhance the quality of life in rural communities. In addition to conventional monitoring approaches, the proposed framework integrates a dynamic AI-driven pipeline capable of adapting to evolving disease patterns over time. The Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network (ST-GNN) component captures intricate spatial relationships among rural wards, while ensemble models such as XGBoost and LightGBM strengthen the temporal analysis, enabling accurate outbreak predictions. Incorporating real-time patient and environmental data enhances the system's responsiveness and interpretability. Experimentally, the model achieved an F1-score of 0.71 to 0.79 and a sensitivity of 0.69, demonstrating its effectiveness for real-world health surveillance in rural settings. This framework exemplifies how AI-based hybrid systems can transition from experimental research to practical early-warning applications in resource-limited regions

title: An Efficient Deployment of Deep Learning LSTM Models using Docker and Kubernetes (Minikube)
authors: Kunal Rathor, Hari Singh, Prateek Thakral
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: The substantial growth in the intricacy of deep learning applications has heightened the necessity for scalable, efficient, and portable deployment environments. Conventional standalone systems frequently do not satisfy these criteria, especially for resource management, scalability, and reproducibility. This study illustrates the implementation of LSTM-based rainfall prediction models with Docker containers managed by a local Kubernetes cluster via Minikube. The experimental configuration facilitated a comparative assessment of standalone and containerized executions, emphasizing training performance, resource utilization, and deployment flexibility. The results underscore the efficacy of container orchestration in maintaining consistency across environments and facilitating model deployment and management. The paper highlights the significance of these designs in enhancing reproducible and maintainable machine learning workflows, with possible applications in cloud-native Kubernetes systems such as Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) and Amazon EKS for managing large-scale real-world applications.

title: A Privacy-Compliant Hybrid Framework for Real-Time Offensive Text Moderation in Dual-Mode Chat Systems
authors: Raghu Yashwanth T M, Parivel R, Priyan S, Santhosh R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: An automatic text moderation system that provides secure, synchronized, real-time communication in chat-style venues for anonymous and non-anonymous modes is described. It is constructed to prevent inappropriate content by targeting and blocking messages at the edge, forming a protective layer that ensures inappropriate content is intercepted before delivery. Any flagged message is blocked and never transmitted to the recipient and never be transported to the end user. A hybrid filtering approach was developed to combine the rule-based approach with AIinduced detection to handle phonetically changed and structurally modified word forms, which are often employed to bypass the regular filters. This manually compiled dataset was used to mimic those linguistic variants and train moderation models that are tailored towards them. Furthermore, generative language models were integrated to increase the dataset and generalize to unseen inputs. The test results indicate that the detection ratio was 96.4% with a false positive ratio of 2.1%, and the average processing time was 117 milliseconds, regardless of the message size. The modular design allows for a lightweight deployment on various digital platforms, such as educational, corporate, and public-based systems. Continual adjustment is supported through the use of dynamically updated wordlists and scalable moderation logic. The system emphasizes privacy-preserving incognito modes alongside policy-enforced permanent chat, enabling flexible deployment while ensuring regulatory compliance and communication safety. Further work will add deeper semantic models informed by a context-aware language model and improve the quality of the moderation by refining the user feedback loop.

title: M4Rec: Multi-Modal Knowledge Graph Modeling of Multi-Dimensional User Preferences for Next-POI Recommendation
authors: Jinpeng Chen, Fan Zhang, Huan Li, Hua Lu, Kaimin Wei, Senzhang Wang, Christian S. Jensen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Next Point-of-interest (POI) recommendation has been widely used in real scenarios to predict the next possible location based on user behavior patterns. However, existing methods predominantly rely on spatio-temporal associations and check-in sequence relationships between users and POIs, which fall short for users with limited interactions with POIs. Moreover, user preferences are inherently multi-dimensional, rendering user selections often influenced by multiple factors such as location categories and multi-modal information. To mitigate these issues, we introduce a Multi-Modal Knowledge Graph Modeling of Multi-Dimensional User Preferences for Next-POI Recommendation (M4Rec for short). First, we define a multi-modal knowledge graph to organize the relationships among users, locations, categories, and multi-modal information. Subsequently, we use the multi-modal knowledge graph-based relation-aware network to derive comprehensive entity representations from the constructed knowledge graph. Next, employing the temporal knowledge prediction method, we predict the user's next-POI category and next-POI. Finally, the final recommendation results are obtained by enhancing the corresponding location prediction scores through category semantics. Extensive experimentation conducted on real-world datasets validates the superiority of our proposed method over state-of-the-art competitors.

title: Research on 5G Traffic Prediction Method Based on VMD-XGBoost and Bootstrap
authors: Xiang Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: In order to improve the accuracy and reliability of 5G network traffic prediction, this study proposes a 5G traffic prediction framework based on Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) and the XGBoost model. First, VMD is used to decompose 5G traffic data into intrinsic modes, extracting multi-scale features from the data. This process reduces noise influence and enhances the model's predictive capability. Next, the prediction performance of three machine learning models-XGBoost, Random Forest (RF), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)-is compared. The results show that XGBoost exhibits the highest predictive accuracy on the test set under all experimental conditions. Additionally, this study applies the Bootstrap method for interval prediction using the XGBoost model, assessing the prediction uncertainty under different confidence intervals. Experimental results demonstrate that combining XGBoost with the Bootstrap method effectively provides prediction intervals, achieving high prediction coverage and narrow interval width at various confidence levels. This study provides a new perspective for 5G traffic prediction and offers a research foundation for further improvements in network resource management and optimization.

title: Z-Transform Model of a Coherent Receiver for Satellite-to-Ground Laser Links Under High Doppler Rates
authors: Sophonie Pech, Fabien Destic, Arnaud Dion, Angélique Rissons
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: Following the transition of terrestrial networks to optical fibers in the 1980s, optical laser links are increasingly being explored as an alternative to Radio Frequency (RF) communications in space, especially to address congestion in the microwave bands. For downlinks, free-space propagation remains a major challenge, mainly because of atmospheric turbulence. Coherent detection is becoming the preferred approach due to its superior overall performance. In Low Earth Orbit (LEO)-to-ground downlinks, an additional issue for coherent demodulation arises from the Doppler frequency drift caused by the orbital motion of the satellite.In this study, a Z-transform modeling of a coherent receiver based on a hybrid Optical-Digital Phase-Locked Loop (ODPLL) for Doppler compensation and frequency synchronization is proposed. In the envisioned architecture, the optical signals are processed in the analog domain by optoelectrical components, with no speed limitations other than propagation delays; in turn, the control for frequency tracking is performed in the digital domain, thus easing the design. The system latency is taken into account, in view of implementing the algorithm on a Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) and to later integrate the photonic elements to build the hardware receiver. The simulated behavioral model achieves successful Quadrature Phase-Shift Keying (QPSK) demodulation even when submitted to strong Doppler rates, and under realistic received power levels.

title: Message From the General Chair
authors: N/A

abstract: On behalf of the Organizing Committee, it is my great pleasure to welcome you to IEEE MECOM 2025, the IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking. We are delighted to bring together researchers, practitioners, industry leaders, and students from around the world for a vibrant forum to exchange ideas, present cutting-edge research, and foster collaboration in the rapidly evolving field of communications. title: Shape oscillation and 3-D microstreaming profile of a phospholipid-coated microbubble attached to a wall_supp5-3676695.avi
authors: Klazina Kooiman
abstract: Ultrasound-activated microbubbles display diverse dynamics, including shape oscillations and microstreaming, both of which are important for applications in particle transport, drug delivery, and bacterial interventions. Despite advancements, a knowledge gap persists in our understanding of the correlation between shape oscillations, corresponding microstreaming and resulting mechanical impact to neighboring boundaries for microbubbles of clinically relevant sizes. This study investigates this correlation for biotinylated phospholipid-coated microbubbles with radii of 3.0-7.3 μm (N=79) when attached to a streptavidin-coated wall, imaged at 5 million and 10 thousand frames per second to visualize both shape oscillations and microstreaming. Driven at 1.25 MHz frequency, 25,000 cycles and varying peak negative acoustic pressures (170-420 kPa), the larger sized microbubbles showed higher shape modes (up to mode 6) than the smaller ones. A shape stability model predicted the dominant shape mode with an 89% agreement with experimental findings. Four distinct 3-D microstreaming patterns were observed by employing astigmatic particle tracking velocimetry: patternless, radial, dipole and quadrupole. Modal decomposition of shape oscillations revealed that the quadrupole pattern occurred from self-interaction of the dominant shape mode, while the dipole pattern resulted from two strongly interacting shape modes. The quantified microstreaming showed maximum velocities ranging from 2-10 mm/s, which decayed with increasing distance from the microbubble center. Notably, lower shape modes and the quadrupole pattern were associated with higher wall shear stress (up to 13 Pa). These unique 3-D microstreaming profiles have the potential to enhance the efficiency of therapeutic microbubble-mediated ultrasound applications through controlled shape oscillation and mechanical impact.

title: Electricity Certificate Mechanisms and Supply Chain Decisions under Renewable Energy Consumption Policies
authors: Yao Duan, Chong Gao, Zhiheng Xu, Ran Cheng, Songyan Ren, Donghong Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: The separation and bundling of electricity certificates based on consumption responsibility is an effective strategy for promoting the use of renewable energy sources. In this context, this paper focuses on power generation enterprises, power grids and power sales enterprises as research subjects. It employs the Stackelberg game method to construct four modes and compares the optimal decision-making of renewable energy supply chains under the weight of consumption responsibility of each subject in the supply chain. Furthermore, it analyses the impacts of key parameters on their decision-making. The findings of the study indicate that when the cost coefficient for green power production is low, the retail price of green power increases in tandem with the price of excess consumption certificates. Conversely, when the cost coefficient for green power production is high, the retail price of green power decreases in conjunction with the price of excess consumption certificates. Additionally, the level of green power production effort, the total amount of electricity, and the profit of each subject in the supply chain decline as the responsibility weight for renewable energy consumption increases. Overall, the supply chain profit reaches its maximum in the HF mode(separate pricing model for green power and green certificates with cooperation between power producers and grid companies) and its minimum in the BK mode(bundled pricing model for green power and green certificates without cooperation between power producers and grid companies).

title: Digital Processing Method for Building Structure Crack Segmentation Based on U-Net with Dynamic Snake Convolution
authors: Chunlin Liu, Shengfang Qiao, Hang Chen, Dianpeng Li, Zewei Wu, Peng Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: The forms of cracks in building structures are complex and have various causes. Digital and precise identification of cracks in building structures is a key challenge in the field of structural health monitoring. Existing methods have discontinuous segmentation for slender and winding cracks and weak resistance to background interference. Therefore, the article proposes a digital processing method for building structure crack segmentation based on the U-Net model and introduced dynamic snake convolution. This method enhances the network’s ability to perceive the irregular geometric shapes of cracks through a dynamic serpentine convolution module. This enables it to adaptively adjust the feature extraction path along the crack direction, and optimize the training process in combination with a composite loss function. Experiments on public crack datasets demonstrate that this method can achieve more continuous and precise segmentation results compared to baseline models, effectively enhancing the digitization and intelligence of crack detection.

title: Light-Neck: Memory-Aware Feature Fusion for Real-Time Object Detection on Edge Devices
authors: Changyan Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Running object detection in real time on edge devices means trading off speed, accuracy, and limited memory. YOLOv8 performs well, but its default feature fusion stage often depends on channel concatenation, which can be costly in memory. In practice, this "memory wall" is a real bottleneck on Windows laptops, where VRAM is shared dynamically with the system and background processes through WDDM.We therefore present Light-Neck, a memory-aware feature fusion design for edge deployment. We introduce Concat-Free Fusion (CF-Fusion), which removes concatenation entirely. Instead, features are first aligned using parallel 1 × 1 convolutions and then combined by element-wise addition, so large intermediate tensors do not need to be created. On an NVIDIA RTX 4060 Laptop GPU (FP32, batch size = 1), this design cuts peak allocated runtime memory by 50.7% (from 60.0MiB to 29.6MiB) when deployed with optimized ONNX Runtime. The speed impact is small (∼ 0.2ms), and accuracy drops only slightly, making the approach practical for memory-constrained edge devices.

title: Design and Implementation of a Heterogeneous Network Communication Gateway for Industrial Control Systems Based on APL
authors: Bingjun Yan, Mingxu Gang, Bo Hu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Aiming at the interconnection bottleneck between APL (advanced physical layer) communication network and traditional can/RS485 heterogeneous devices in industrial scene, a heterogeneous network communication gateway for industrial control system based on APL is designed and implemented. Based on the FreeRTOS embedded real-time operating system, the gateway realizes the protocol conversion and real-time transmission of multi-source heterogeneous device data by optimizing the APL data frame structure adapted to IEEE 802.3 protocol and adopting the slot multiplexing scheduling strategy. Finally, the simulation test platform is built to test and verify. The test results show that the transmission packet loss rate of the communication gateway is less than $0.01 \%$, and the transmission delay is less than 150 us, which meets the requirements of industrial real-time and reliability, and effectively solves the problem of heterogeneous network data interaction.

title: Wearable IMU-based System for Proactive Fall Detection via Gait Pattern Recognition
authors: Mangai S, Maheswari S, Pavalakodi S, Sridharshini T, Swetha G, Tamil S
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: For senior citizens, falls pose a serious health risk since they frequently cause serious harm or even death. Current market solutions are predominantly reactive, alerting carers only after a fall has occurred. This paper describes a new proactive prevention and detection system that makes use of a Wireless Body Area Network (WBAN) with three nodes. The system obtains a full kinematic profile of body dynamics by carefully positioning Inertial Measurement Units (IMUs) at the thoracic (T12), lumbar (L3), and foot positions. Trained on SisFall, UpFall, and manually gathered datasets, a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) neural network analyses sensor inputs in real-time to accomplish dual functionality: (1) fall detection with 98.2% accuracy, and (2) proactive identification of pre-fall risk states through analysis of abnormal gait patterns and centre of gravity (COG) deviations. The system alerts users before falls occur, shifting the paradigm from post-incident response to preventive intervention. Experimental results demonstrate 85% success in early risk detection with mean response time of 400ms prior to loss of balance.

title: Dynamic Resource Scheduling for UAV-Assisted Vehicular Edge Computing: A Multi-Agent RL Approach
authors: Zhuorong Li, Wanli Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Resource scheduling for Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV)-assisted Internet of Vehicles (IoV) is challenging in highly dynamic environments. Existing Multi-Agent Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (MADDPG) algorithms often struggle to effectively cope with complex long-term temporal dependencies and environmental non-stationarity. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes an enhanced MADDPG framework integrating sequence modeling and an attention mechanism. This approach effectively captures historical environmental characteristics and models the cooperative interactions among multi-agents. The proposed method aims to minimize the long-term system cost subject to Quality of Service (QoS) constraints. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves approximately 15% improvement in convergence speed, as well as significant gains in task success rate and resource utilization efficiency compared to existing algorithms.

title: Analysis of the Impact of Initial Rotor Position Determination Error on the Speed, Current, and Startup Torque of Synchronous Reluctance Motors (SynRM) Considering Magnetic Saturation
authors: Farzad Bodaghi, Hamidreza Pairo
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: The lack of an accurate determination of the initial rotor position of the SynRM can lead to a reduction in starting torque and a delay in the rotor speed response during startup. Several approaches have been developed to determine the initial rotor position of the SynRM. These methods can be classified according to their complexity, ease of implementation, and accuracy in rotor position determination. Although some of these methods are highly complex to implement, they considerably reduce errors in determining the initial rotor position of the SynRM. On the other hand, certain methods offer low computational complexity and are easy to implement, but tend to produce higher errors in determining the initial rotor position, sometimes reaching up to 30 degrees. This paper investigates the impact of errors in determining the initial rotor position on the performance of the SynRM from multiple perspectives. To analyze this effect on key motor parameters, initial position errors of 0, 15, and 25 degrees were applied, and their influence on electromagnetic starting torque and the time required for the rotor to reach the reference mechanical speed was evaluated. The results of the simulations conducted in the MATLAB/Simulink environment are presented.

title: Software Design and Implementation of Multi-Module Collaborative Interaction System Based on STM32
authors: Zhang Luopu, Yang Pengju
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: In response to the common problems of high module coupling, poor real-time performance, and stability in multi module collaborative interaction in embedded communication systems, this paper designs a software solution for Multi-module collaboration based on STM32 controller, achieving efficient collaboration of GPRS communication, touch screen, speech recognition, and broadcasting modules. Firstly, in terms of serial communication, configure DMA channels and circular data buffers for each serial port to avoid real-time loss caused by polling waiting; In terms of interrupt handling mechanism, a multi interrupt nested architecture is implemented through a layered design that minimizes interrupt content and priority, avoiding data confusion caused by conflicts between different levels of interrupts; In terms of human-computer interaction mechanism, diversified and efficient matching of user instructions and system states is achieved through the design of multi-modal input of screen and voice, and the eventization of user input. The experimental results show that the software scheme has achieved stable communication among various modules, reducing communication delay by 80%, proving the reliability of the scheme and meeting the multi-scenario requirements of embedded systems. This study advances the technical solution for multi-module interaction and high-stability communication coordination optimization in demanding embedded scenarios, providing a reusable software architecture framework for multi-module system design in fields such as intelligent terminals and industrial control.

title: Opinion Manipulator Identification Based on Semantic Mutation and Abnormal Propagation Analysis
authors: Ziang Gao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: To identify online opinion manipulators, we propose a framework integrating semantic mutation detection with abnormal propagation analysis. By leveraging LLM-based interpretation and novel manipulation indices (IMI/PDI), our method accurately locates narrative shifts and exposes malicious users, as demonstrated on real-world Weibo data.

title: Application of Multi-Objective Optimization Algorithm for Intelligent Distribution Network in Power Distribution
authors: Shijian Wang, Xiaopeng Cai, Zijie Wang, Li Ding, Chenyuan Xu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: With the massive access of new energy and the increasing demand for electricity, smart distribution network is facing multiple challenges, such as reducing network loss, improving voltage stability and increasing the utilization rate of renewable energy. Therefore, this paper constructs an optimization model with three objectives: economy, safety and reliability, and uses NSGA-II algorithm with elite strategy to solve it. Through the simulation experiment of the improved IEEE 33-node distribution network model, two scenarios with different new energy permeability are set up and compared with MOPSO algorithm and traditional single-objective optimization method. The results show that NSGA-II algorithm performs well in economy, security and reliability, especially in the scenario of high-tech energy penetration, which can effectively balance economy and security and avoid power supply risks caused by new energy fluctuations. Its elite reservation strategy and non-dominated sorting mechanism successfully solve the multi-objective conflict problem and provide an efficient and balanced solution for power distribution in smart distribution network.

title: Non-Invasive Computational Diagnostics for Laryngeal Pathology: Voice-Based and Multimodal Learning Perspectives
authors: Kanza Boujraine, Karima Lakhdadi, Hassan Satori
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Timely and precise identification of laryngeal pathologies plays a critical role in preventing cancer progression and optimizing treatment effectiveness. Contemporary developments in machine learning and computational diagnostics have facilitated the creation of both imaging-dependent invasive techniques and voice-analysis-based non-invasive solutions for detecting laryngeal and throat malignancies. This work offers a comparative evaluation of these computational methodologies, examining their diagnostic capabilities, interpretive transparency, and practical clinical utility. Evidence from contemporary research demonstrates that deep learning architectures utilizing invasive imaging modalities, especially convolutional neural networks trained on endoscopic or tissue biopsy data, deliver exceptional diagnostic precision, frequently surpassing 94% accuracy. In contrast, non-invasive acoustic analysis methods that employ voice features including MFCC, PLP, jitter, and shimmer represent accessible and economical alternatives, yielding accuracy rates ranging from $82 \%$ to $\mathbf{9 0 \%}$. Although their precision is somewhat reduced, these approaches offer considerable advantages in simplicity and patient acceptance, rendering them particularly valuable for preliminary screening programs and remote medical consultation services. This research emphasizes the expanding significance of integrated multimodal computational systems that synthesize acoustic signals, medical imaging, and clinical parameters to strengthen diagnostic reliability and robustness. The study addresses continuing obstacles, such as unbalanced training datasets, model performance across varied recording environments, and the explainability of sophisticated learning models. The findings establish that voice-based computational diagnostics constitute a significant advancement toward interpretable, non-invasive, and patient-focused technologies in laryngeal healthcare.

title: Ensemble CNN Approach with Activation Functions Combination for Knee Osteoarthritis Grading
authors: V. Vijaya Kishore, G. Maha Lakshmi, P. Tejaswini, P. Pavithra, K. Uday Kiran, M. Sanjjeva Rayudu
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Knee Osteoarthritis (KOA) is a degenerative joint disorder that significantly affects mobility and quality of life. Severity assessment using the Kellgren–Lawrence (KL) grading system from knee X-ray images is commonly performed manually, which may lead to observer variability. To address this limitation, this study proposes an ensemble-based convolutional neural network (CNN) framework for automated KOA severity classification. Multiple pretrained CNN architectures are employed using transfer learning, where the base layers are frozen and additional dense layers are introduced. To improve feature learning and robustness, an activation function rotation strategy using Swish, ReLU, and ELU is applied through three activation function schemes. The models are combined using a soft-voting ensemble approach, and KOA severity is classified into KL grades (0–4) using a SoftMax layer. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed ensemble model improves classification performance and reduces activation function selection bias, making it suitable for reliable automated KOA assessment.

title: Dark-BPP: A Nighttime Single Object Tracking Method Based on Bidirectional Prompt Propagation
authors: Yanyan Chen, Ruigang Fu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Nighttime environments induce significant visual deterioration, which complicates feature extraction and often leads to trajectory drift in UAV-based object tracking. Current solutions exhibit notable shortcomings: the "enhance-then-track" paradigm suffers from inefficiency, while domain adaptation techniques are hampered by demanding training procedures and data prerequisites. Current prompt-based learning techniques employ one-way information flow, which inadequately leverages high-level semantic information and restricts the model’s ability to distinguish targets effectively. We propose Dark-BPP, a bidirectional prompt propagation framework that constructs feedback paths from deep to shallow layers for direct semantic guidance. A channel-level gated fusion module adaptively integrates prompts with backbone features. Experiments demonstrate state-of-the-art performance on nighttime datasets.

title: Decentralized Peer to Peer Ride Sharing System using Block-Chain
authors: Vallamkonda Jyothi, Pokala Jyothi, Sikhakolli Raj Kumar, Pendyala Srihari Chowdary, P.N.V.B. Ravi Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Most ride-sharing applications we use today, such as Uber or Lyft, operate using centralized platforms, meaning a single company Central authority controls all the user data, trip details, and payment- related information; this often results in problems such as high service fees and limited transparency, lesser user privacy, and even the potential failure of the entire system in case of the fall of a central server. This project proposes a better alternative-a blockchain-based decentralized ride-sharing system. Making it possible to directly peer-to-Peer communication between the riders and the drivers, the system removes middlemen, increases trust, secures privacy, and provides for a fairer and more transparent ride-sharing. Platform embodies "private blockchain" and therefore "Proof of Authority". Consensus mechanism for fast and secure transaction processing while putting less load on computational resources. The system allows "login-free ride booking, decentralized identity verification, and an escrow-based payment mechanism intertwined with tamperproof; the reputation system assures reliability and accountability in a trust-less environment by applying the power of blockchain transparency with optimized P2P networking, the platform enforces integrity, fairness, and lower operational costs.

title: Multi-Objective Optimization-Based Multiple Sequence Alignment Algorithm
authors: Huazheng Zhu, Jun Zhang, Fukang Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Multiple sequence alignment (MSA) plays a fundamental role in bioinformatics analysis, serving as the basis for comparative genomics, protein structure prediction, and evolutionary studies. However, most existing MSA approaches rely on single-objective scoring schemes, in which alignment quality is highly sensitive to parameter settings such as gap penalties and residue weights. In practice, fixed parameter configurations often fail to provide robust performance across heterogeneous datasets. To explicitly address this limitation, this paper formulates MSA as a bi-objective optimization problem that simultaneously maximizes residue matching quality and minimizes gap penalties. Based on this formulation, a novel algorithm named MONUMSA is proposed by integrating a new utility function into the MOEA/D-DRA framework. The proposed method dynamically balances conflicting alignment objectives and produces a diverse set of Pareto-optimal solutions, offering flexible trade-offs for different alignment scenarios. Extensive experiments conducted on BAliBASE 2.0 and 3.0 benchmark datasets demonstrate that MONUMSA achieves competitive or superior alignment accuracy compared with several state-of-the-art multi-objective and classical MSA algorithms. The results indicate that the proposed multi-objective framework improves robustness while maintaining alignment quality, making it suitable for complex and large-scale sequence alignment tasks.

title: Coherent Combination of Deep-Space Optical Signals Under Weak Atmospheric Turbulence
authors: Antoni Mikos-Nuszkiewicz, Karol Łukanowski, Marcin Jarzyna, Konrad Banaszek
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: We propose a multi-stage coherent beam combination architecture for the reception of photon-starved deep-space optical communication signals. In the presented scheme, multiple individual signal beams collected by an array of telescopes are added together coherently, mimicking the functionality of a single large receive aperture with adaptive optics. The setup consists of a cascade of interferometers controlled by a Bayesian feedback algorithm utilizing single photon detection events in dark output ports. The operation of the system is numerically simulated for a realistic deep-space optical link scenario including atmospheric turbulence effects that result in both phase and intensity fluctuations for collected signal beams.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-Driven Adaptive Control and Optimal Dispatch for New-Type Power Systems
authors: Yiran Chen, Yang Miao, Xiaojun Xu, Tingfang Tan, Ling Ji, Haining Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: High renewable penetration and heterogeneous flexible loads reshape power-system operation. They also expose the limits of model-heavy dispatch and rule-based control in fast-changing conditions. This paper proposes an adaptive control and optimal dispatch method for new-generation power systems based on an attention-enhanced deep deterministic policy gradient (DDPG) agent. Panoramic sensing and forecasting streams (PMU/SCADA, renewable and load forecasts, and asset telemetry) are mapped to a compact state through normalization and feature fusion. The actor outputs continuous actions that coordinate generation, storage, demand response, and OLTC/tap devices, while a constraint-aware safety layer enforces operational limits.A multi-objective reward explicitly balances reliability, renewable utilization, and network-loss cost. In a benchmark grid with 30% renewable penetration, the proposed method achieves a 0.8 s closed-loop response, improves renewable utilization to 91.3%, and reduces the loss rate to 2.1%. It also increases peak–valley load smoothing by 28% relative to conventional dispatch. Results indicate that the proposed controller can deliver fast decisions with strong robustness under uncertainty, supporting secure and economical operation under "dual-carbon" goals.In practice, operators also face telemetry gaps and data latency. A control strategy must remain stable when a subset of measurements is delayed or missing. This is one reason we emphasize feature normalization and a state builder that can fall back to robust aggregates (e.g., area frequency, regional voltage envelopes) when fine-grained signals become unreliable.

title: Optimized Design of Worm Gear Shaft Based on Strength Calibration Theory Calculation and Finite Element Analysis
authors: Liangyu Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: In this paper, we conducted three-dimensional modeling and static simulation analysis of a worm gear shaft from a worm gear reducer using the SolidWorks software platform. The working stress of the shaft, subjected to combined bending and torsional loads, was determined through theoretical calculations. Finite element software, Simulation, was employed to perform mesh division and set boundary conditions, enabling the simulation of stress, strain, and displacement distributions under the combined effects of radial force and torque. The simulation results indicate that the original design exhibits a stress concentration at the shaft shoulder, with a maximum stress of 423.4 MPa. To mitigate this issue, two optimization strategies were proposed: increasing the shoulder fillet angle to R2.5 mm and enlarging the shaft diameter to ϕ40 mm. Post-optimization simulation results revealed that the maximum stresses decreased to 404.9 MPa and 279.4 MPa, respectively, effectively alleviating stress concentration and enhancing the load-carrying capacity and safety coefficient of the shaft. This study validates the feasibility and effectiveness of integrating theoretical strength analysis with finite element simulation for the structural optimization of shaft components.

title: High-capacity implicit embedding and lossless traceable image methods for quality information in intelligent manufacturing
authors: Hesong Hu, Hang Chen, Chunlin Liu, Shengfang Qiao, Xiaoting Chen, Chun Shan, Mianjie Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Aiming at critical bottlenecks in the whole process of intelligent concrete production, such as the low security of synchronous transmission between quality traceability data and image carriers, the frequent failure of physical tags, and the high risk of information loss in harsh environments, this paper proposes an advanced high-capacity implicit embedding and lossless traceable image method based on an Improved Prediction Error Expansion (IPEE) framework. The proposed method performs meticulous structural processing on multi-dimensional quality parameters—including mix proportions and environmental sensor data—and utilizes Logistic chaotic mapping for bit-level scrambling to enhance data security significantly. To leverage the inherent high-frequency texture characteristics typical of concrete surfaces, a local-variance-driven adaptive prediction model is developed. This model optimizes the prediction error histogram (PEH) modification by intelligently steering pixel modifications toward high-correlation regions, thereby enabling high-capacity embedding while maintaining superior perceptual transparency and structural fidelity. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that at a standard payload of 0.5 bpp, the PSNR consistently exceeds 48 dB. Remarkably, even when the capacity scales to a high payload of 1.2 bpp, the PSNR remains as high as 41.5 dB, representing a 3.5 dB improvement over traditional PEE by effectively leveraging the high statistical redundancy in fine-grained concrete textures. This method ensures that critical parameters are bound in real-time, achieving pixel-level lossless restoration at the traceability end. Consequently, it provides a complete technical closed-loop for reliable quality evidence storage, supporting high-precision post-analysis and automated recognition tasks on digital construction sites.

title: Real-Time Localization of Short-Circuit Faults in Power Systems Based on the Triangulation Principle for Protection Relays
authors: Mbakob Ndagoua Polster Kader, Fouad Slaoui Hasnaoui, Semaan Georges
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: In this paper, we propose an innovative method for locating short-circuit faults in electrical power networks, based on the principle of triangulation. The approach relies on angular measurements of three-phase voltages recorded at the ends of transmission lines to estimate fault locations in real time. The model is tested on the IEEE 9-bus network modeled in MATLAB/Simulink, simulating various types of faults (single-phase, two-phase, three-phase, with or without ground). Simulation results show a localization accuracy above 90% in all cases, with an overall average exceeding 95%, confirming the effectiveness of the method in diverse scenarios. This method stands out for its simplicity of implementation, fast processing, and robustness against different fault scenarios, making it particularly suitable for real-time protection systems in modern meshed networks.

title: From Book to AR: Enhancing Interactive Learning Through Augmented Reality
authors: S. Rajeswari, Kirthana Gangadharan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Augmented Reality is about visualizing things or objects in a 3D view were we can rotate and view in any dimension which gives immersive experience and clear knowledge of understanding. This article is about an interactive learning using augmented reality. Speaking of which, $A R$ has several applications to view or explore small or large objects in our day to day life like navigating the destinations through maps, learning sessions or subjects like in the medical to view parts of the body in 3D format to detect the presence or absence of tumor or some other disease which is very helpful for new surgeons to learn, and in social studies, to view galaxies, solar system which makes easy to understand and learn students about the structure, color, distance among planets, and the enjoying part is about developing games just for fun or to make the users interactive learning more attention by conducting games like puzzle or quiz. Commonly used tools are ARCore (android), ARKit (ios), Unity with AR Foundation and Vuforia for mobile applications. Languages used c, c++, c# and java. Potential of $A R$ is to enhance educational experiences, providing a framework for integrating technology in classroom to make interactive learning.

title: End-to-End WiFi CSI Sensing Framework for Accurate and Privacy-Preserving Activity Recognition
authors: Hicham Boudlal
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Recognizing human activities in indoor environments is a key component in smart home and surveillance systems. In this study, we introduce an innovative method for activity recognition using Channel State Information (CSI) of WiFi signals. Compared with traditional approaches that rely on wearable devices or cameras, our method utilizes existing WiFi infrastructure, ensuring privacy and minimal user intrusion. By analyzing the changes in CSI induced by human motions, we develop a robust model capable of accurately identifying a range of activities. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in various indoor settings, highlighting its promise for broad implementation in practical scenarios.

title: Intelligent SOC Estimation Method for CC-CV Wireless Electric Vehicle Charger
authors: M. El Ancary, H. El Fadil, A. Lassioui, M. Chiheb, Y. El Asri, A. Hasni, H. Abbade
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This paper presents an innovative wireless charging method for lithium-ion batteries based on a single-phase seriesseries compensation topology and a constant current-constant voltage (CC-CV) charging profile. The originality of this work lies in the use of an artificial neural network (ANN) for real-time estimation of the state of charge (SOC), thereby enabling automatic and accurate triggering of the transition between CC and CV modes. A complete model of the WPT system is developed, followed by the design of the phase-shift modulation control strategy. Simulation results show that the proposed method ensures stable charging current, smooth transition and high energy efficiency, even in the presence of variations in charging parameters. This approach is a promising solution for wireless charging systems for electric vehicles (EVs), combining simplicity of implementation and robustness in dynamic conditions.

title: Campus Safety Entity Recognition Model Based on Relative Position Attention and LEBERT
authors: Chong Chen, Yuntao Shi, Shuqin Li, Jie Li
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Attempting to solve the problem of low accuracy in entity recognition for campus safety incidents, this paper proposes a campus safety named entity recognition model based on the relative position attention mechanism and LEBERT. The model was experimented with on the constructed campus safety EVENT dataset. The results show that the F1 scores of the proposed model in the recognition of campus safety entities (EVENT, OBJECT, CONSEQUENCE) have increased by 2.3%, 1.1% and 2.1% respectively compared to the LEBERT-BiLSTM-CRF model. It has effectively improved the accuracy of extracting campus safety entities.

title: Detecting AI-Generated Content: A Multi-Layered Approach for Web and Document Analysis
authors: Tanvi Chiman, Karan Mehta, Mohamed Husein Panjwani, Kajol Bhandari, Shahzia Sayyad
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The rapid adoption of large language models has significantly increased the presence of AI-generated text across academic, professional, and online platforms, raising concerns regarding content authenticity, misinformation, and academic integrity. This research work presents a multi-layered AI-generated text detection framework capable of analyzing web pages, PDFs, and user-selected text segments through a browser extension and web-based interface. The proposed system integrates transformer-based classifiers with optimized scoring, caching, and probabilistic aggregation mechanisms to accurately differentiate between human-written and AI-generated content. Unlike single-model detectors, the framework supports modular model integration, adversarial robustness evaluation, and scalable deployment. A Bayesian updating strategy is employed to combine real-time detection scores with historical priors, improving decision reliability. Experimental evaluation demonstrates improved detection performance over baseline methods, achieving higher F1-score and lower inference latency while maintaining deployment efficiency. The system supports educational institutions, publishers, and content moderation platforms by enabling transparent, explainable, and efficient AI content verification, thereby contributing toward responsible AI adoption and academic integrity.

title: Multi-scale Frequency-domain Reconstruction and Multi-dimensional Collaborative Attention Network for Forest Fire Detection in Remote Sensing Imagery
authors: Changhui Ding, Haiyan Li, Yajie Liu, Bingbing He, Xun Lang, Guanbo Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Remote sensing-based forest fire detection is vital for early warning, yet existing methods struggle with cross-scale interaction and frequency-domain reconstruction. This paper proposes the Multi-scale Frequency-domain Reconstruction and Multi-dimensional Collaborative Attention Network (MFRMC-Net). Specifically, a Deep-Aware Channel Enhancement and Multi-dimensional Interaction Attention (DACDI) module is designed to model parallel cross-dependencies for improved multi-scale representation. Additionally, a Spectral Reconstruction and Multi-semantic Collaborative Attention (SRSMA) module integrates semantic guidance for high-resolution restoration of critical fire regions. Evaluated on multisource datasets, MFRMC-Net demonstrates superior performance in detection accuracy and small object recognition while maintaining low computational cost.

title: Construction of Energy Efficiency Scheduling Model of Smart Campus Wireless Network Based on Multi-objective Optimization
authors: Xiaojun Chai
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: The traditional wireless network scheduling model has some problems, such as low energy efficiency, high delay and idle resources, which is difficult to meet the demand. In this paper, an energy efficiency scheduling model of smart campus wireless network based on multi-objective optimization is constructed, which adopts a three-layer architecture of spatio-temporal feature perception layer, multi-objective optimization layer and dynamic scheduling execution layer. By defining the multi-objective optimization problem, comprehensively considering the goals of energy consumption, delay and packet loss rate, and setting the constraints of coverage, quality of service (QoS) and spectrum resources, the optimization of network performance and energy efficiency is ensured. A hybrid optimization algorithm based on MOEA/D and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) is proposed, which combines offline optimization and online adjustment to improve scheduling efficiency and adaptability. The experiment was carried out in the main campus of a 985 university. The results show that the model can significantly improve the energy efficiency while ensuring QoS. The energy consumption is reduced by 27.5% to 31.2%, the time delay is reduced by 24% to 55%, the packet loss rate is reduced by 49.5% to 63.4%, and the resource utilization rate is significantly improved. The average utilization rate of AP (access point) is increased by 49%, and the number of channel conflicts is reduced by 32.4%. In addition, the spatio-temporal dynamic adaptation mechanism effectively improves the traffic prediction accuracy and QoS stability, and verifies the effectiveness and practicability of the model in smart campus scenarios.

title: Compact Super Wideband Slot Antenna for Wireless Applications
authors: Budhadeb Maity
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)
abstract: In this article, a compact super-wideband (SWB) annular-ring shaped rectangular-slot (ARSRS) antenna is proposed for high-speed wireless communication systems. The proposed antenna employs a modified annular-ring patch with a defective ground structure to achieve an enhanced impedance bandwidth (IBW). The ARSRS antenna is designed on an FR-4 substrate with overall dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$33.4 \times 26.8 \times 0.8 ~\text{mm}^{3}$</tex>. The proposed design covers the frequency range from 2.352-33.415 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{GHz}(173.73 \%)$</tex>. It achieves enhanced IBW, better impedance matching with a return loss below -10 dB across the operating band and exhibits nearly stable radiation characteristics. The simulated results indicate a peak gain of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5. 9 4 ~ d B i}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6. 3 2 ~ G H z}$</tex> and radiation efficiency nearly 80.5%, confirming its suitability for wireless applications.

title: GPU-Accelerated Multilayer Turbulence Simulation for Modeling of Space-to-Ground Optical Links
authors: Hamed Akhlaghi, Michael Taylor, Antony Orth, Steve Hranilovic
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: Satellite-to-ground free-space optical links are limited by atmospheric turbulence that distorts the received wave-front and induces scintillation. We present a GPU-accelerated multilayer turbulence simulator that models the dynamic, altitude-stratified propagation for space-to-ground link geometries. The simulator is based on standard Kolmogorov phase screen and split-step Fourier propagation, but implemented in TensorFlow for significant parallelization and accelerated computation time. The simulation stores propagation operators and covariance terms as GPU tensors to avoid transfer overhead and enable high throughput at significantly reduced computation time compared with freely available turbulence propagation tools. Using a single mid-range gaming GPU the simulator was able to achieve up to 310 times speed up over existing tools, with better scaling to larger grid sizes. This acceleration enables larger-aperture, long time series, and high resolution simulations of downlink geometries for machine-learning-driven adaptive optics and signal-processing mitigation in next-generation FSO systems.

title: Research on an Efficient Data Statistics Upgrade Algorithm Based on the Spark Framework
authors: Zhenlei Bao
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: With the wide application of various data collection systems, the traditional storage and computing architecture based on relational databases is facing severe challenges in dealing with the tens of gigabytes of daily incremental data generated by tens of millions of terminal devices. The simple SQL queries of this architecture are difficult to meet the complex statistical analysis requirements and are inefficient. Therefore, this study aims to explore a data query and statistics solution that is compatible with the existing SQL ecosystem and has high scalability and high performance. This paper proposes an efficient data statistics upgrade algorithm based on the Spark framework (ESUA). ESUA aims to systematically solve the three key bottlenecks of traditional Spark in large-scale statistical tasks: load imbalance caused by fixed task partition granularity, phase blocking and redundant computing caused by static DAG scheduling strategy, and memory waste and access delay caused by non-adaptive cache mechanism. The core of the algorithm builds a multi-level model integrating task partition optimization, dynamic DAG scheduling, cache gain evaluation and cost minimization decision, with the fundamental goal of minimizing the Spark computing cost function J through theoretical modeling. ESUA adopts a four-layer architecture of “collection-scheduling-computation-aggregation”, deeply integrated into the Spark SQL framework. Theoretically, it innovatively establishes a task partition and load balancing model, solving the optimal load distribution scheme through the Lagrange multiplier method; designs a dynamic DAG scheduling optimization mechanism, adjusting the task execution path based on real-time dependency relationships to minimize the global scheduling cost; and proposes an adaptive cache and incremental computing model, optimizing the cache strategy through the “access frequency-dependency depth” priority function and significantly reducing the repetitive computing cost by using differential updates. The experimental results show that in an 8node Spark cluster environment, ESUA performs better than the traditional Spark SQL solution when processing data. In most cases, it has superior performance, especially when dealing with large data volumes, providing a feasible technical solution for building a large-scale real-time analysis platform.

title: Research on Optimization of Deep Learning Algorithms in the Effectiveness Analysis of Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Inspection Data
authors: Chengming Jin, Jinyang Zhao, Shuliang Zhang, Jin Tao, Tianxiao Li, Haodong Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: This study focuses on the optimization problem of deep learning algorithms in the performance analysis of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) inspection data, and proposes a novel object detection algorithm that integrates self-supervised learning and efficient network structure design. The core innovation of this algorithm lies in the design of a two-stage optimization framework: Firstly, in the pre-training stage, an improved contrastive learning strategy is adopted to conduct self-supervised pre-training by taking advantage of the unlabeled nature of the inspection data itself, enabling the model to learn general visual representations with strong robustness to rotation, scale changes, etc., effectively alleviating the reliance on a large number of manual annotations. Secondly, in terms of the detection network structure, a lightweight backbone network was constructed and integrated with channel and spatial attention mechanisms, enabling the model to adaptively focus on the key areas in the image. Meanwhile, a novel attention-guided bidirectional feature pyramid fusion module was designed. This module utilizes the attention map generated by high-level semantic information to dynamically weight and enhance the detailed information in shallow features, thereby significantly improving the feature representation ability for minor defects. Through systematic experiments on the real unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) power inspection dataset, the results show that this algorithm demonstrates significant advantages in overall detection accuracy, especially in the recognition of small targets and partially occluded targets. Meanwhile, the model maintains low complexity and computational overhead, and achieves efficient inference speed on embedded hardware. It provides a high-precision and high-efficiency solution for the intelligent analysis of unmanned aerial vehicle inspection.

title: Simulation of Industrial System Fault Detection Based on Weak Signal Injection and Wavelet Time-Frequency Analysis
authors: Sijie Tao, Xiaoping Tian, Yunlong He, Xianwu Tang, Yuyang Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Early faults in industrial systems often manifest as faint signals drowned out background noise. Traditional detection methods struggle to achieve effective early warning due to low signal-to-noise ratios. This paper proposes a novel fault detection framework integrating active weak signal injection with multi-level signal processing. First, we inject specific-frequency weak diagnostic signals into the system, transforming the complex "fault pattern recognition" problem into the more manageable "known signal detection" problem. Subsequently, a multi-level signal processing workflow—including notch filtering, wavelet denoising, and time-frequency analysis—is designed to handle complex environments featuring both strong power-frequency interference and broadband noise. Cascaded notch filters precisely suppress strong power-frequency interference, while wavelet threshold denoising suppresses broadband Gaussian noise. Finally, a time-frequency distribution map is generated via wavelet transform time-frequency analysis, enabling intuitive identification and feature extraction of the weak injected signal from the input signal. MATLAB simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method can reliably extract weak signals from complex environments with signal-to-noise ratios as low as -80 dB, achieving signal-to-noise ratio improvements exceeding 35 dB. The presence of these signals is visually and accurately identified through the time-frequency map.
