The analysis of feature importance indicated that temperature was the most important predictor (importance=0.424), then humidity (0.341) and noise level (0.208). This was proved by continuous 24-hour monitoring, where the system could have constant environmental surveillance with a noise level reading of 48 to 78 dB.

title: Adaptive Lighting Control in Visible Light Systems: An Integrated Sensing, Communication, and Illumination Framework
authors: Xinyan Xie, Xuesong Wang, Xin Lai, Yongheng Wen, Fengrui Yang, Haoyang He, Lai Zhang, Dong Zhao
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Indoor visible light communication (VLC) is a promising sixth-generation (6G) technology, as its directional and sensitive optical signals are naturally suited for integrated sensing and communication (ISAC). However, current research mainly focuses on maximizing data rates and sensing accuracy, creating a conflict between high performance, high energy consumption, and user visual comfort. This paper proposes an adaptive integrated sensing, communication, and illumination (ISCI) framework that resolves this conflict by treating energy savings as a primary objective. The framework's mechanism first partitions the receiving plane using a geometric methodology, defining an activity area and a surrounding non-activity area to match distinct user requirements. User location, determined using non-line-ofsight (NLOS) sensing, then acts as a dynamic switch for the system's optimization objective. The system adaptively shifts between minimizing total transmit power while guaranteeing communication and illumination performance in the activity area and maximizing signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) uniformity in the non-activity area. Numerical results confirm that this adaptive ISCI approach achieves 53.59% energy savings over a nonadaptive system and improves SNR uniformity by 57.79%, while satisfying all illumination constraints and maintaining a mean localization error of 0.071 m.

title: Hybrid Plant Disease Detection and Treatment Prediction Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Bestley Joe S, Vanitha V, Aarav Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Agriculture is the backbone of every nation worldwide. It is the main prerequisite for the overall economic betterment and stability of any country as it provides raw material, food for people and employment to those who are connected to this sector. Traditional methods of disease identification are impractical, requires special knowledge and are time consuming. This research work aims to address these limitations by providing a system that leverages cloud-based tools like OpenAI vision and gemini vision wherein an image upload of a diseased agricultural specimen utilizes the pre-trained models to diagnose the issues. A simple client-server architecture using flask is developed that communicates with front end which are capable of managing posts like farmer community forum, chatbot that is offered with multi-language option. Six samples of agricultural specimens including one healthy sample was tested and the detection cum diagnosis results are obtained. Future opportunities for further enhancement of the system are also suggested.

title: Ease and Equity of Point of Interest Accessibility via Public Transit in the U.S.
authors: Alexander Li, Aurimas Racas, Junaid Syed, Mengyang Liu, Przemek Zientala, Tejas Santanam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Equitable access to essential Points of Interest (POIs) such as healthcare facilities and grocery stores via public transit is a critical urban planning challenge. This paper introduces an interactive tool that analyzes the ease and equity of such access in major U.S. cities. Using a 2-step floating catchment area (2SFCA) approach, we calculate granular accessibility indices and present them on an interactive, web-based platform. A key contribution of this work is a scenario analysis feature that allows users to simulate the impact of adding or removing POIs and visualize the resulting changes in accessibility equity. Our analysis reveals significant disparities across demographic groups, and we further utilize machine learning models to demonstrate a strong, quantifiable link between accessibility scores and neighborhood racial composition, highlighting systemic inequities. The tool provides a practical framework for policymakers to make more informed and equitable infrastructure decisions.

title: QSE-QA: A Question Space Expansion Framework for Creative and Diverse Answer Generation
authors: Zhikai Mi, Bo Yu, Jianing Tong, Lijun Fu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In generative question answering (QA) systems, a key challenge in the field of natural language generation lies in enhancing the creativity and diversity of answers while maintaining semantic relevance. Traditional QA systems typically adopt a single question-single answer mapping mechanism, which results in limited answer diversity and restricts their ability to accommodate multi-dimensional information needs and user expressions in open-domain contexts. This paper proposes a novel QA generation framework called QSE-QA (Question Space Expansion for Question Answering), designed to improve semantic diversity and creative expressiveness of generated answers through expansion of the question space. The core idea is to generate semantically relevant question variants via an intermediate modeling mechanism, which guides large language models to activate different knowledge pathways and thereby produce more creative and multi-perspective responses. The proposed method consists of two main stages: Based on prompt engineering techniques, guiding the model to generate a diversified question set Q2 from the original QA pair (Q, A); Using a fine-tuned intermediate middleware model, the system automatically generates a set of diverse questions when encountering new queries, which serve as generative prompts to drive the answer generation process. Experiments on a medical QA dataset demonstrate that QSE-QA significantly outperforms traditional methods on evaluation metrics such as ROUGE, and shows enhanced multi-view and creative expressiveness in human evaluations. This research introduces a “human-like question construction” capability into generative QA systems, expanding the pathways for creative modeling in QA generation tasks. The proposed framework exhibits strong generalizability and potential for future extensions.

title: Real-Time Data Modeling and Anomaly Identification Method of Terminal Battery Digital Monitoring Device for Smart Grid Application
authors: Huixuan Zeng, Xuzhao Lin, Fang Zou, Xiaoqing Su, Xiaotong Zhang, Jiawei Ren
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In order to solve the problems of data island, response lag and insufficient model adaptability in smart grid terminal battery monitoring, this paper proposes an integrated technical scheme integrating real-time data modeling and anomaly identification. Firstly, a layered monitoring architecture of "edge feature extraction+cloud depth analysis" is designed. Four-dimensional parameters of battery voltage, current, temperature and internal resistance are collected at high frequency at the edge layer, and the data compression is realized by using the improved piecewise aggregation approximation (PAA) combined with wavelet transform algorithm (the reconstruction error is less than 1.5% when the compression ratio is 15:1), which reduces the transmission bandwidth pressure; The cloud supports large-scale model training and anomaly traceability. In the aspect of real-time data modeling, an improved incremental extreme learning machine (I-ELM) is adopted. By introducing gradient descent term and adaptive regularization, the concept drift is alleviated, and the continuous data stream is dynamically updated, taking into account the computational efficiency and model robustness. Three-level linkage mechanism is constructed in the anomaly identification link: based on sliding window Mahalanobis distance (SW-MD), millisecond response of sudden anomalies such as voltage sag is realized; The LSTM-Attention model is used to give an early warning of gradual anomalies such as capacity attenuation more than 72 hours in advance; Combined with Battery Causality Graph (BCG), the fault tracing is completed. The experimental results show that the total delay of edge processing is reduced to 53ms, the F1-score of sudden anomaly detection is 0.962, and the accuracy of progressive fault early warning is 88% 72 hours ago, which effectively supports the safe operation of smart grid and battery life cycle management, and provides technical support for energy transformation under the goal of "double carbon".

title: ZoneFit: Enabling Zoned Flash Benefits via Granularity-Matched SLC Management
authors: Dingcui Yu, Zonghuan Yan, Yunpeng Song, Yanyun Wang, Xinghui Duan, Congming Gao, Yina Lv, Liang Shi
publish: 2025 IEEE 14th Non-Volatile Memory Systems and Applications Symposium (NVMSA)
abstract: Quad-Level Cell (QLC) flash technology is increasingly adopted in consumer-grade storage systems due to its high density and cost efficiency. However, its inherent two-step programming process and lack of power-loss protection necessitate routing writes through a Single-Level Cell (SLC) cache to ensure data integrity. Under the Zoned UFS protocol, the SLC cache suffers from mismatched management granularity, leading to an enlarged mapping table, inefficient space reclamation, and migration conflicts. To address these issues, we propose ZoneFit, a zoned SLC–QLC co-management framework that incorporates zone-level SLC capacity allocation and zone-level SLC migration. Evaluations on a consumer-grade zoned storage emulator demonstrate that ZoneFit significantly enhances read performance and prolongs the lifespan of flash storage.

title:
Structured Grid-Based G
                    <sup>1</sup>
                    Cubic Trigonometric Triangular Spline Surface with Quantitative Error Evaluation
authors: Muhammad Syawal Abd Halim, Normi Abdul Hadi, Rusliza Ahmad, Syaiful Anam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Digitization of an object from visual to data point can help researchers manipulate the data for further use - particularly in reconstructing a surface. The use of trigonometric function in generating a triangular surface offers a smooth surface with flexibility as ensued from its derivation and cyclability advantages. This study used a structured 3-dimensional data of a human head as control points to reconstruct a surface using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$G^{1}$</tex> cubic trigonometric triangular spline with rational basis functions. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$G^{1}$</tex> continuity was used to generate a smooth surface with rational basis functions that were closer to the control points, as the root mean square error (RMSE) and maximum error were smaller. The generated human head surface was illustrated in this study.

title: AI-Driven CV Analysis and Job Matching in HRM Using a Hybrid Fuzzy Logic-Deep Neural Network Approach for Higher Education
authors: Naveen Nandal, Kartik Tyagi, Love Kumar, Anil Lokesh Gadi, Rita, Sathiyanathan S
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Companies can't function without efficient human resource management, especially when it comes to matching open positions with qualified applicants. Nevertheless, in the modern HRM environment, recruiters have obstacles like managing a high number of resumes, catering to candidates' varied interests, and satisfying employers' growing need for specialised skills. Intelligent CV analysis and job matching solutions are necessary since these time-sensitive judgements frequently degrade selection quality. The research methodology used in this study is a hybrid approach that combines machine learning, fuzzy logic, and NLP. Tokenisation, phrase splitting, named entity recognition, and part-of-speech tagging are preprocessing stages that are carried out before BERT is used for training, engineering, and feature extraction. Combining ANNs with fuzzy regression models improves predicted accuracy by taking advantage of both the adaptability of ANNs and the partial data handling capabilities of fuzzy algorithms. The results demonstrate that the suggested Fuzzy ANN model outperforms the competition, reaching a 98.38% analytical accuracy rate. Based on these results, it's clear that the model can make HRM more efficient, less taxing on recruiters, and better at connecting candidates with jobs.

title: An Intelligent Cross-Platform System for Indian Sign Language Translation
authors: K. Sakthivel, Pavithra K, Sonika N
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Effective communication through sign language is essential for people with hearing or speech challenges, yet its limited use among the broader population often creates obstacles to interaction. This project introduces a deep learningpowered system designed to identify static hand gestures from Indian Sign Language (ISL) using images. We built and trained three convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures InceptionV3, ResNet50, and DenseNet121 on a curated dataset of labeled ISL gestures. The incorporation of a user-friendly Streamlit web application allows users to upload images, select a model, and view the predicted gesture with confidence scores. The application also offers a comparative visualization of confidence scores across the models, showcasing DenseNet121's exceptional accuracy for ISL gesture recognition. This solution provides a reliable and adaptable framework for sign language recognition, fostering more inclusive communication and supporting the development of assistive technologies.

title: AI-Driven Waste Segregation Using Convolutional and Deep Neural Networks
authors: Chandragandhi S, Dharanidharan S, Koteeswarar MM, Shahanas M, Ajay Christo P
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Managing waste efficiently has become a serious challenge due to the growing amount and variety of garbage produced every day. Manual waste segregation is slow, costly, and often inaccurate. To address this problem, this work explores an AI-based approach for automatic waste segregation using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Deep Neural Networks (DNN). Waste images are classified into multiple categories such as glass, plastic, metal, organic, and hazardous using popular benchmark datasets like TrashNet, TrashBox, and a Kaggle waste dataset. The system applies image preprocessing techniques (resizing, normalization, and augmentation) and uses transfer learning with advanced architectures including ResNet, DenseNet, and ResNeXt, implemented in TensorFlow and PyTorch on GPU platforms. Our experiments show that CNN models deliver high classification accuracy. For example, a fine-tuned ResNet50 achieved 87% accuracy on TrashNet, DenseNet121 reached 95%, and ResNeXt-101 obtained 89.6% on TrashBox. A hybrid CNN model used in a smart-bin prototype achieved up to 98.9 % accuracy. These results highlight strong performance not only in accuracy but also in precision, recall, and F1-score. The proposed AI system can be applied in real-world environments such as smart waste bins, automated sorting conveyors in recycling plants, and IoT-enabled municipal systems. This demonstrates that CNN/DNN-based waste segregation can significantly improve waste handling, making recycling faster, more reliable, and environmentally sustainable.

title: Hybrid Learning-based Demographic Estimation and Nutrition Recommendation using Dental Panoramic X-Ray Images
authors: J. Ponnudurai, M. Kaliappan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Dental radiography is essential to the diagnosis and treatment planning process in the modern oral healthcare setting. Using dental panoramic X-ray images, this study suggests a hybrid computational architecture that might not only estimate age and gender but also offer personalized food recommendations. Although the Convolutional Neural Networks are used to extract precise radiographic information from the dento-maxillofacial region that classical machinelearning classifiers are used to improve prediction accuracy and generalization across a range of populations. A rule-based nutrition recommendation system recommends appropriate dietary practices to improve oral health and lower the risk of dental conditions including caries and enamel erosion based on the anticipated demographic profile. By lowering the amount of manual interpretation work and facilitating effective decision assistance in both clinic-based and remote dental care settings, the technology is intended to help dental professionals. During the expert review, the experimental assessment shows high acceptability of dietary recommendations and great performance in the demographic prediction. For better clinical applicability, future improvements will involve extending the dataset, adding multimodal imaging and implementing the model in real-time scenarios.

title: Improvised HPR Multiplier Using Fast Bipartitioned Hybrid Adder by Approximate Computing
authors: K. S. Neelu Kumari, P. Agniraj, I. Azarudeen, P. Chandraprakash
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The triple modular redundancy (TMR) scheme is generally used to build a fault-tolerant arithmetic unit in critical applications that require higher reliability. While TMR is effective, it also incurs a significant area and power/energy overhead. The primary voter in most TMR designs can also be highly susceptible to soft errors and may result in an error for only a small difference between the modules' outputs. There is also a wide spectrum of applications deployed in critical systems that are inherently error-resilient. In particular, they can tolerate some degree of inexactness at their output while still meeting some reliability level. In this paper, propose a high-precision redundancy multiplier (HPR-Mul) architecture, implementing principles of approximate computing, augmented with a Fast Bipartitioned Hybrid Adder (FBHA) to achieve higher performance while still meeting the prescribed reliability level. The HPR-Multiplier consists of one full-precision (FP) and two reduced-precision (RP) multipliers, together with a simple voter to arrive at an output. Unlike most current <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R P}$</tex> redundancy multipliers (RPR-Multiplier) that require a complicated voter, the voter of the present HPR-Mul architecture relies on mathematical formulas resulting in a much simpler structure. In addition, the intermediate signals of the FP multiplier are employed as inputs to the RP multipliers thus greatly improving HPR-Mul accuracy. The main contribution of this paper is to replace the HPR-Mul's traditional summation block that suffers from delay, with a Fast Binary Hierarchical Adder (FBHA), a high-speed adder and architecture that utilizes parallel carry-lookahead and carryselect logic. This change is to target the multiplier's critical path to reduce delay and improve performance overall.

title: VGGMobiNet: A Unified VGG16-MobileNetV2 Model for Brinjal Leaf Disease Analysis and Management
authors: Gogineni Manju Sri, Akavarapu Kavya Sri, Palaparthi Samarpan, Vangalla Veera Babu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This study describes a complete, automated, deep learning-based approach for diagnosing and recommending treatments for brinjal (eggplant) leaf diseases. A carefully designed hybrid architecture using VGG16 for feature extraction and MobileNetV2 for classification is employed as the backbone of the present method. The model has been trained using a dataset containing brinjal leaf images of both healthy and diseased categories, and it can detect with precision six diseases along with the healthy state: Insect Pest, Small Leaf, Leaf Spot, Mosaic Virus, White Mold, and Wilt Leaf. The system exhibits remarkable classification accuracy by making use of transfer learning and data augmentation techniques and at the same time reducing the requirement of manual feature engineering and computation overhead to a great extent, the model achieves a classification accuracy of 97 %. The small size of the hybrid model provides the advantage of fast inference and easy deployment in terms of resources, thereby making it suitable for real-time field applications and mobile-based agricultural systems. The entire agricultural sector, in general, and the smart agriculture sub-sector, in particular, will benefit from the proposed system through supporting early disease diagnosis, sustainable crop management, and increased agricultural productivity.

title: Unified Predictive Maintenance with Generative Anomaly Injection, RL Scheduling in Data-Driven Digital Twins
authors: Raj Kumar Myakala, Sanjeev Kumar Pedhapally, Vinithya Reddy Podduturi, Akhil Reddy Jagirapu, Praveen Kumar Nagata, Sampath Lavudya
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: In modern industrial systems, unplanned downtime leads to significant operational and financial losses. To address this, we propose a unified predictive maintenance pipeline that integrates generative anomaly injection, explainability, and reinforcement learning (RL) within a digital twin framework. Our system uses sensor data from real-world datasets (XJTU-SY, AI4I, and C-MAPSS) for Remaining Useful Life (RUL) prediction, fault simulation, and proactive maintenance policy generation. First, a GAN-based fault simulation module injects rare but realistic failure patterns using low discriminator confidence. Next, SHAP-based explainability is applied for feature attribution and drift monitoring. LSTM and Prophet models are employed to forecast RUL trends, which are fed into a Q-learning-based RL scheduler to optimize prescriptive actions like maintenance or load reduction. These actions are sent to a virtual command layer and closed-loop feedback is achieved via MQTT/OPC-UA to simulate interaction with the physical twin. We quantitatively evaluate the synthetic fault realism using t-SNE, PCA, and classifier generalization. The overall pipeline improves prediction accuracy by 12.6% and reduces failure downtime by 19.4% on average. SHAP analysis reveals interpretable patterns across synthetic and real failures. Our modular architecture demonstrates a self-optimizing, explainable, and simulation-augmented predictive maintenance system for Industry 4.0 environments. This work highlights the practical synergy of generative models, RL, and digital twins for scalable, real-time industrial reliability solutions.

title: Ensemble Machine Learning Approaches for Crop Yield Prediction Using Agronomic and Meteorological Features
authors: Diksha, Simrandeep Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: The right prediction of crop yield is essential in planning agriculture sustainably and optimizing resources. The proposed research is about forecasting crop yield through agronomic and meteorological features like temperature, humidity, soil moisture, area, season, crop type, and location by using machine learning(ML) and deep learning(DL) ensemble models. The used dataset consists of the crop yield records of 7 states, 112 districts, and 80 crops over many years. The dataset got judged by the seven models mentioned, which are Random Forest, Gradient Boosting Regressor, AdaBoost, Random Subspace, XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost along with a Feed Forward Neural Network (FFNN). The model was evaluated based on R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), and Mean Absolute Error (MAE) metrics. Out of all the methods used, CatBoost gave the highest prediction accuracy, thus surpassing both traditional ensemble methods and the neural network model. The results emphasize the power of tree based boosting methods, particularly CatBoost, in forecasting crop yield, thus offering a digital agricultural future ready for data driven decision making.

title: Instance-dependent uniform tail bounds for empirical processes
authors: Sohail Bahmani
abstract: We formulate a uniform tail bound for empirical processes indexed by a class of functions, in terms of the individual deviations of the functions rather than the worst-case deviation in the considered class. The tail bound is established by introducing an initial “deflation” step to the standard generic chaining argument. The resulting tail bound is the sum of the complexity of the “deflated function class” in terms of a generalization of Talagrand’s γ functional, and the deviation of the function instance, both of which are formulated based on the natural seminorm induced by the corresponding Cramér functions. Leveraging another less demanding natural seminorm, we also show similar bounds, though with implicit dependence on the sample size, in the more general case where finite exponential moments cannot be assumed. We also provide approximations of the tail bounds in terms of the more prevalent Orlicz norms or their “incomplete” versions under suitable moment conditions.

title: Heterogeneous-Sensing Bionic Skin for 3D Force-Vector Measurement and Slip Characterization
authors: Fangfang Liu, Chennan Yang, Wenhao Yang, Shizhao Li, Han Zheng, Huining Zhao, Hongli Li, Haojie Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: With the rapid development of flexible electronics and smart sensing technology, electronic skin has attracted increasing attention from academia and industry as a bionic sensing system. This paper presents a biomimetic skin system that integrates heterogeneous fiber Bragg grating (FBG) distribution with a bionic fingerprint structure to enable high-precision detection and identification of three-dimensional force vectors and slip states. The system eliminates the need for additional temperature-compensation components, while still effectively reducing the impact of environmental temperature variations. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system exhibits good linear response characteristics for both normal and shear force measurements. The normal force measurement range is 0-5 N, with a resolution of 0.042 N and a sensitivity of 1.54 V/N. The resolution of the X-direction shear force is 0.023 N with a sensitivity of 3.79 V/N, while the Y-direction shear force achieves a resolution of 0.012 N and a sensitivity of 7.78 V/N. Furthermore, a Random Forest (RF) algorithm is applied to train and classify slip signals, which enables prediction accuracies exceeding 97.2% for slip direction and velocity. These results provide new insights and methods for improving the performance and expanding the functionality of electronic skin technology, supporting its broader application in intelligent sensing.

title: Predictive Analysis of Olympic Medal Distribution Based on Deep Learning and Statistical Models: A Multi-Method Approach for the 2028 Summer Olympics
authors: Jiayi Yang, Jiaxiang Lin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Edge Computing (ICDSEC)
abstract: Understanding and predicting Olympic medal distribution has become increasingly important for national Olympic committees and sports policymakers. This study presents a comprehensive data-driven framework to forecast the 2028 Summer Olympics medal standings and identify key determinants of Olympic success. We employ multiple analytical approaches, including Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for temporal pattern recognition, Back Propagation Neural Networks (BPNN) for identifying emerging medal-winning nations, and statistical methods such as Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) regression and Cumulative Sum (CUSUM) control charts to quantify coaching impacts. Our analysis of historical Olympic data reveals that sustained investment in sports infrastructure, strategic coaching programs, and systematic athlete development significantly influence medal outcomes. The LSTM model achieves strong predictive performance with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{\mathrm{2}}$</tex> values of 0.98531 on training data and 0.96068 on validation data, forecasting that USA, China, and Great Britain will dominate the 2028 medal standings. Additionally, we identify countries with high probability of winning their first medals and quantify the substantial impact of elite coaching on national performance. These findings provide actionable insights for optimizing resource allocation and strategic planning in Olympic preparation.

title: Research on Optimization of AIGC Technology for Enhanced Short Video Creation Processes
authors: Zhiyong Wang, Kewen Cai
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This research present an AIGC-accelerated pipeline that reduces 60-s video production time from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8\text{-}12~\mathrm{h}$</tex> to 45–90 min (85 %) and cost from $150–300 to $15–40 (90%). A modular API couples GAN-based frame synthesis, ViT temporal modeling and diffusion post-processing; reinforcement-learning policy gradients optimize engagement proxies while actor-critic training stabilizes convergence. Progressive resolution and knowledge distillation cut GPU memory by 40 %. Cloud rendering with VMAF-guided QA delivers 87/100 quality at 94 % human-level retention and 94 % interaction rate. A/B tests across 10 k TikTok clips show <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15 \times$</tex> more variants (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0}-\text{5 0}$</tex> vs <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1}-\text{3}$</tex>) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{6 0} \times$</tex> language coverage (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$120+$</tex> vs 1-2). The system embeds into Premiere/DaVinci via plugins and supports federated fine-tuning for cultural or brand customization within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 - 1 0} \%$</tex> of original training cycles. Ethical safeguards include blockchain watermarking and EU AI Act compliance.

title: A Hierarchical Graph-Language Model for Detection of Malicious Rumors in Social Media
authors: Jianbin Wang, Yujie Lin, Qitao Tan, Changxing Chen, Yue Su
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Social Network Systems (AISNS)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of social media, online rumors spread at an alarming rate, posing a serious threat to public perception and social stability. To address this, this paper proposes a Hierarchical Graph Language Model (HGLM) based on Graph Attention Networks (GAT) and Large Language Models (LLM) for early detection of online rumors. HGLM captures multi-layered semantic features of posts and their reply chains through hierarchical text modeling. It dynamically adjusts the influence between nodes in the propagation graph via an adaptive edge weight mechanism and employs multi-model expert fusion to achieve collaborative discrimination of text semantics and propagation structure. Experimental results demonstrate that on the Twitter15, Twitter16, and PHEME datasets, HGLM significantly outperforms traditional machine learning models and deep graph neural network models across metrics including Accuracy, F1 true, and F1 false. It exhibits outstanding stability, versatility, and cross-dataset transferability. This approach provides an efficient, scalable, and robust solution for early rumor identification within complex social network environments. Experimental results on Twitter15, Twitter16, and PHEME datasets demonstrate the model’s superiority. HGLM achieves accuracy rates of 89.2%, 89.1%, and 86.8% respectively, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines by approximately 6–9%. These results validate HGLM as a robust solution for early rumor identification, significantly advancing the capability to maintain social stability in complex network environments.

title: Cnn-Bilstm Based Cross-Condition Fault Diagnosis for Crane Gearboxes with Attention and Residual Enhancement
authors: Feiqing Zhang, Shihua Chen, Zhenyu Yin, Qingfang Zhang, Leile Ma
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Gearbox fault diagnosis is vital for crane safety and reliability. However, existing intelligent models often show weak generalization and poor noise robustness. This paper proposes a CNN-BiLSTM-based approach enhanced with Efficient Channel Attention and residual structures. CNN captures spatial features, BiLSTM models bidirectional temporal dependencies, Efficient Channel Attention highlights fault-sensitive channels, and residual connections improve gradient flow. Experiments on the Southeast University gearbox dataset verify superior accuracy and robustness compared with CNN-LSTM and CNN-GRU, demonstrating strong adaptability under varying loads and noisy conditions.

title: A Multi-Object Tracking Framework Integrating Gait Features
authors: You Zhai, Minghua Li, Jianhua Xie, Kaicheng Yu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Traditional MOT algorithms rely solely on appearance or motion features, and their robustness is insufficient due to constraints such as target occlusion and appearance changes. To address this issue, a multi-target tracking framework that integrates gait features to enhance tracking stability in complex scenarios is proposed. The framework constructs a multifeature fusion model of “appearance + motion + gait”. In the framework, YOLOv11 provides high-precision target detection boxes, DeepSORT realizes cross-frame target association through Kalman filtering and Hungarian algorithm, and GaitSet extracts gait features with strong scene adaptability, making up for the defects of traditional features in scenarios such as occlusion and clothing changes. The ReID appearance features and gait features are fused through dynamic weight adjustment. Short-term tracking mainly relies on appearance features, and long-term tracking mainly relies on gait features, forming a spatiotemporal complementary recognition system. Experimental results show that after fusing GaitSet, MOTA is further improved to 3.1%, and the number of identity switches (IDs) is reduced by 9.2%, verifying that gait information can enhance MOT performance in complex scenarios.

title: Steel Surface Defect Detection Algorithm Based on Improved RT-DETR
authors: Xiyang Zhang, Youlin Chai, Yi Hu, Yuxuan Shang, Zeng Tao, Yongze Ma
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Given that Steel surface defects generally exhibit characteristics such as small size, edge blur, irregular shape, complex background page, and variable light, Traditional detection methods and existing Deep learning models still have deficiencies in terms of Detection accuracy, Robustness, and Real-time performance. To address this, this paper proposes an optimization-oriented Lightweight surface defect detection model for Industrial grayscale images based on the RT-DETR framework. By introducing three Improved modules: SNI-FCA, CGFM, and GSConvE-GACW, the model's Perception ability for Subtle defects is enhanced through three key aspects: Feature reconstruction, Multi-scale fusion, and Input representation. The experiment results on the NEU-DET dataset show that the proposed method achieves a Mean Average Precision of 77.43 % without relying on a Bigger Backbone network, which is significantly superior to Mainstream models such as YOLOv5l, YOLOv8I, and YOLOv11I, achieving significant improvement in recall rate compared to other methods. Meanwhile, the Model parameters are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 9. 7 2 M}$</tex>, and the Computational complexity is 56.4 Giga Floating - Point Operations Per Second, meeting the industrial Real-time performance requirement. Ablation experiment verifies the effectiveness and Synergistic gain of each module. This method achieves a good Balance among accuracy, velocity, and Generalization ability, providing an efficient and robust solution for Automated surface quality inspection in Industrial realms such as steel.

title: Design and Application of Sliding Mode Controller for Roller Microporous System Based on Disturbance Observer
authors: Liu Yingming, Sun Chang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: In order to improve the nonlinear control performance of roll gap structure, the differential geometric feedback technology was introduced to complete the linear decoupling process. According to the sliding mode control function of disturbance observer, the sliding mode controller design of roll micro-hole system based on disturbance observer was established and its application verification was carried out. The control system can achieve ideal tracking performance and effectively compensate the errors caused by the gap, which indicates that the control algorithm designed has a strong feasibility. The sliding mode control simulation test of the disturbance observer is carried out, and the effectiveness of the synovial controller is judged according to the actual test results, which can realize the function of tracking trajectory accurately. The application verification shows that the rolling error of the modified rolling mill can be reduced, and the sliding mode control method is helpful to achieve higher qualified rate of rolling blades. It is a great significance to improve the rolling quality of sheet metal and has great practical application and popularization value.

title: Multi-Level Frame Preemption Mechanism for TSN Based Avionics Systems
authors: Nan Liu, Da Chai, Xin Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Avionics systems, the core of modern aviation, demand stringent real time performance, determinism, and reliability. TSN with deterministic capabilities like precise synchronization has become critical for next generation avionics networks. However, IEEE 802.1Qbu's single-level preemption, which divides frames into “Express” and “Preemptable”, struggles with complex avionics traffic. The same level frames cannot preempt each other, causing critical Preemptable traffic to suffer excessive delays due to lower priority frames. This paper proposes an Aviation Enhanced Multi-Level Frame Preemption mechanism. Based on IEEE 802.1Qbu, it dynamically subdivides preemptable frames by avionics standards with priority mapping to preemption categories. It extends the MAC Merge sublayer's SMD field to identify subclasses and adds logical interfaces with adjusted state machines for fine-grained scheduling. To further coordinate transmission among these subclasses, a credit-based mechanism is introduced to quantify blocking time, with credit accumulating in proportion to the duration of delay. This accumulated credit, combined with the PCP, forms a composite scheduling metric that enables higher-priority preemptable subclasses to preempt lower ones fairly and deterministically. Simulations in OMNeT++ show that the mechanism significantly reduces end-to-end delay for high-priority Preemptable traffic, ensures timely response to time-sensitive events, and maintains network determinism.

title: A Method for False Alarm Identification in Industrial Gas Detection Systems through the Integration of Meteorological and Multi-Sensor Data
authors: Hong Hu, Jingcheng Liu, Bao Zhang, Weijie Zhang, Jian Wang, Guorong Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address the issue of false alarms in industrial gas alarm systems under complex meteorological conditions, this paper proposes a dynamic identification method that integrates multi-source monitoring data with meteorological information. By establishing a five-dimensional meteorological characteristic system that includes temperature, humidity, wind speed, atmospheric pressure, and wind direction, this study conducts correlation analysis and trend modelling with historical alarm records. Experimental results indicate that wind speed is the key driver amplifying false alarms, and high temperature and humidity backgrounds exhibit a synergistic enhancement effect. Under conditions of high wind speed, both alarm intensity and frequency significantly increase, with false alarms occurring concentratedly. Quantile regression further reveals that meteorological disturbances significantly enhance the marginal response at high-risk thresholds, demonstrating the value of dynamic threshold modeling. Based on this, a false alarm identification logic combining wind directionality and wind speed thresholds was established, effectively identifying “high wind-external source type” false alarms, resulting in a significant reduction in the system's overall false alarm rate while maintaining stable true alarm rates. The study provides empirical support and modeling methods for intelligent early warning and false alarm suppression in industrial gas monitoring systems.

title: Comparative Analysis of Few-Shot Learning and Transfer Learning for Medical Imaging Tumour Classification
authors: Sheenam Middha, Abhay Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Prostate cancer is the second most common disease in men in the United States and touches the lives of millions of men every year, thereby becoming an important global health issue. The glandular prostate (PG), which plays a pivotal role in seminal fluid production, is the main organ that gets targeted by this disease. Since magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is not invasive and gives accurate soft-tissue contrast, it has become an essential diagnostic tool for prostate cancer. To enhance the accuracy and efficacy of prostate cancer detection from MRI images, we present in this paper an improved deep learning approach that employs the InceptionResNetV2 model and exploits transfer learning. Compared with traditional diagnosis approaches, our model is effective in extracting intricate features and enhancing sensitivity. We also explore the viability of few-shot learning in medical imaging using prototypical networks with a minor portion of the dataset and reveal the potential of few-shot learning strategies in cases with limited annotated medical images and lay the groundwork for future studies. Large-scale validation shows strong and stable results, endorsing the applicability of our method in clinical environments for early detection and tailored treatment planning, eventually leading to enhanced patient outcomes.

title: Brain Tumor Detection in MRI images Using Hybrid Machine Learning Model
authors: Pradeep Kumar Gupta, Neha Gupta, Utkarsh Pandey, Parulpreet Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Medical imaging is very important for diagnosing and treating patients in healthcare. Oncologists examine images to identify the distinct features of malignant diseases, formulate treatment plans, and evaluate disease progression [25]-[27]. This study aims to develop an approach for the identification of brain tumors. Appropriate segmentation techniques can be used to find brain malignancies in Magnetic Resonance (MR) pictures. Recent improvements in brain radiographic imaging show that deep learning techniques can be used to find certain features of brain tumors, which can help doctors make better diagnoses. Consequently, the majority of data scientists and AI researchers focus on Machine Learning techniques to develop automated screening systems. An automated method would yield quicker segmentation results, delivering reliable insights into data source discrepancies, mostly attributable to varying data recording and storage techniques, hence facilitating more consistent brain tumor identification. This study proposes and assesses innovative structures to enhance the effectiveness of the segmentation method. We suggest using deep neural networks to find brain cancers by training them using MRI scans of patients' brains. The proposed architectures employ convolutional neural networks and inception modules for brain tumor segmentation.

title: A Gesture-Based Virtual Typing Interface
authors: Gannoji Durga Prasad, Amireddy SreeLasya, Sowmith Sharan Vallaji, Ch. Sreenivasa Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Human-computer interaction (HCI) systems that are both inventive and intuitive are in high demand in today’s rapidly changing digital world. Even though physical keyboards and touchscreens are efficient, they frequently have drawbacks in situations that call for contactless or hands-free interaction. This project presents the idea and execution of a virtual keyboard using hand gestures, a system intended to replicate keyboard input using real-time recognition and interpretation of human hand movements. The system uses deep learning models such as Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and computer vision and machine learning algorithms, including Mediapipe, to track and detect the location and movement of hand joints. Users can input data in a smooth manner by making hand movements in front of a camera by mapping these gestures to particular characters or commands. The system’s main components are gesture classification, hand landmark detection, and a virtual interface that looks like a keyboard. Particularly useful applications for this virtual keyboard include augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), assistive technology for people with limited mobility, and public terminals where touch-based input presents hygienic concerns. To ensure practical usability in a variety of environments, the project places a strong emphasis on high accuracy, low latency, and user adaptability. To evaluate the robustness and efficacy of the created system, it was tested in various lighting scenarios and hand orientations. With precise gesture recognition and seamless user interaction, the results indicate encouraging possibilities for real-world deployment. Multilingual support, gesture customisation, and integration with current operating systems are possible future enhancements.

title: CLAP: Cross-Layer Adaptive Pipelining Inference Scheduling for Resource-Efficient Edge-Cloud Vision Systems
authors: Zheming Yang, Wen Ji, Qi Guo, Jian Zhao, Chang Zhao, Xingzhou Zhang, Yangyu Zhang, Zhicheng Li, Yang You
publish: ACM Transactions on Architecture and Code Optimization
abstract: None

title: Design of Hardware Devices for Network Asset Discovery
authors: Bin Zhang, Yuguang Zhang, Dong Chen, Yan Chen, Nanyang Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: Network asset discovery is a core method used in the management of network asset exposure surfaces. Most existing network asset discovery products are implemented in software form, which often suffer from limitations such as single detection approaches and constrained performance. This paper analyzes the principles of network asset discovery and its typical application scenarios, and proposes a hardware-based design method for network asset discovery devices. The method systematically details the selection of hardware platforms, the design of software architecture, and the construction of core modules such as the detection engine. Based on this, a system prototype integrating both active and passive detection mechanisms is designed. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the proposed hardware device significantly outperforms software tools in terms of detection efficiency. The proposed approach provides a feasible pathway and technical foundation for building high-performance network asset discovery systems.

title: Message from Program Chair
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)

title: Spectral Graph Filtering for Long-Term Multivariate Time Series Forecasting
authors: Zongyao Feng, Konstantin Markov
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Long-term multivariate time series forecasting requires modeling complex spatiotemporal dependencies, yet existing approaches tend to focus on addressing the problem from the temporal perspective using channel-independent modeling, with few works exploring effective spatial modeling for long-term horizons. While recent methods use attention mechanisms to capture spatial interactions, they suffer from producing diffuse patterns when the number of variables increases. We propose a spectral graph filtering approach that transforms learned attention weights into graph Laplacians, enabling frequency-aware spatial modeling through Chebyshev polynomial approximations. This design treats the multivariate time series as signals over a graph, allowing the model to adaptively learn spatial patterns most critical for long-term forecasting through learnable spectral filters. Our method first projects temporal sequences into a latent space, computes attention-based adjacency matrices, then applies multi-scale spectral filtering to extract predictive spatial patterns. Experiments on three benchmarks, Electricity, Solar and Weather, demonstrate consistent improvements over the representative baselines, validate that frequency-domain spatial modeling offers a principled and scalable alternative to conventional attention mechanisms for long-term multivariate forecasting.

title: Lightweight CP-ABE Scheme with Puncturable Revocation for Smart Civil Aviation
authors: Lizhe Zhang, Yiao Ma, Zhijun Wu, Jin Lei, Lihan Yang, Ruiqi Li
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: With the rapid development of smart civil aviation, the digital transformation in the civil aviation field has raised higher requirements for the security and efficiency of data sharing. However, the current access control mechanisms in civil aviation data sharing are still inadequate. Ciphertext-Policy AttributeBased Encryption (CP-ABE), as a flexible and efficient access control technique, provides effective security guarantees for data sharing. Nevertheless, traditional CP-ABE schemes based on bilinear pairing operations involve high computational complexity, making them unsuitable for resource-constrained terminals used in smart civil aviation. To address this problem, we proposed a lightweight CP-ABE scheme with puncturable revocation. This scheme uses the scalar multiplication operation on the elliptic curve to replace the complex bilinear pairing calculation, and the decryption operation is performed by the edge node, thereby reducing the computational overhead. At the same time, we use puncturable revocation to achieve fine-grained aviation data access control without requiring ciphertext re-encryption, thus reducing the revocation overhead. Through performance evaluation and security analysis, we verified that our scheme can meet the security and lightweight requirements of smart civil aviation.

title: Collaborative Decision-Making System for Drone Swarms in Dynamic Cross-Regional Pollution Source Inspections Based on Federated Reinforcement Learning (Federated RL)
authors: Zeli Cui, Shanlei Xin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: To address the challenges of dynamic cross-regional pollution source inspections, such as regional data silos, high privacy and security requirements, dynamic source migration, and low drone collaboration efficiency, this paper proposes a collaborative decision-making system for drone swarms based on federated reinforcement learning. The system first constructs a hierarchical federated architecture, then designs a federated experience replay sharing pool, and finally employs multi-agent proximal policy optimization to achieve distributed task allocation and conflict resolution. Experimental results show that the proposed method improves inspection coverage by 24.6% compared to independent learning strategies, reduces the attack inference success rate to 51.7%, and reduces communication overhead by 85.7%. The results validate the proposed framework's comprehensive advantages in coverage, privacy protection, communication efficiency, and real-time performance, providing an effective technical path for building a secure, efficient, and adaptive wide-area dynamic environmental monitoring system.

title: RPKT: Learning What You Don’t Know – Recursive Prerequisite Knowledge Tracing in Conversational AI Tutors for Personalized Learning
authors: Jinwen Tang, Qiming Guo, Zhicheng Tang, Yi Shang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Educational systems often assume learners can identify their knowledge gaps, yet research consistently shows that students struggle to recognize what they don’t know they need to learn—the "unknown unknowns" problem. This paper presents a novel Recursive Prerequisite Knowledge Tracing (RPKT) system that addresses this challenge through dynamic prerequisite discovery using large language models. Unlike existing adaptive learning systems that rely on pre-defined knowledge graphs, our approach recursively traces prerequisite concepts in real-time until reaching a learner’s actual knowledge boundary. The system employs LLMs for intelligent prerequisite extraction, implements binary assessment interfaces for cognitive load reduction, and provides personalized learning paths based on identified knowledge gaps. Demonstration across computer science domains shows the system can discover multiple nested levels of prerequisite dependencies, identify cross-domain mathematical foundations, and generate hierarchical learning sequences without requiring pre-built curricula. Our approach shows great potential for advancing personalized education technology by enabling truly adaptive learning across any academic domain.

title: An Integrated Transformer Learning Based Framework for Multi-Stage Feature Optimization in Naval Intention Detection
authors: Md Azizul Hakim, Monica Nicolescu, Mircea Nicolescu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: To enhance naval security systems, accurate detection of intentions in naval domain plays a vital role. A set of important predictive features can strengthen the strategy of decision making and reduce the likelihood of possible incidents. In this paper, we present a transformer learning based multi-stage feature optimization framework that enables multi-class classification of seven different behaviors in the naval environment to minimize potential threats. Our framework involves several key steps: first, important features are identified based on a machine learning technique; second we introduce a multi-headed transformer learning based deep neural network to extract the optimal subset of features; and finally the intentions are predicted by using a fully connected multi-class feedforward network. The experimental results demonstrate that our proposed model can effectively classify the seven different naval intentions with an overall accuracy of 96%, which outperforms baseline deep learning models such as fully connected deep neural network and LSTM.

title: Graph Attention on Hasse-Based Simplicial Complexes
authors: Hong Wu, Sheng Wu, Hui Feng, Bo Hu
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In real-world systems, multi-way interactions beyond pairwise relations are pervasive and play a crucial role in uncovering the underlying structural and functional patterns. While Simplicial Neural Networks (SNNs) extend Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to model multi-way interactions via simplicial complexes, existing SNN methods struggle to capture explicit cross-order dependencies due to their reliance on the local propagation of the Hodge Laplacian. In this work, we propose HGSNN (Hasse-based Graph Attention Simplicial Neural Network), a novel framework that represents simplices of all orders as nodes in a unified Hasse diagram. Built upon this representation, our model employs graph attention mechanisms to adaptively weight the influence of neighboring simplices across different orders, enabling flexible and data-driven cross-order message passing. Moreover, HGSNN supports unified input and end-toend training of multi-order signals, providing a scalable and expressive framework for higher-order representation learning. Extensive experiments on a real-world dataset demonstrate that HGSNN consistently outperforms existing models.

title: A Health Status Detection Device for Sub-Modules of Flexible DC Converter Valves
authors: Yue Chen, Baolong Liu, Shi Jiao, Lifeng Zu, Linyang Teng
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Aiming at the problems existing in the current maintenance of flexible DC converter valve sub-modules, such as low testing efficiency, complex operation, and high susceptibility to additional hidden risks, this paper develops a health status detection device for flexible DC converter valve sub-modules. This device is composed of a sub-module charging and discharging device, a valve control auxiliary testing device, and testing background software, focusing on the special detection of the sub-module's power supply function, communication function, and bypass switch function. Through comparative tests with traditional testing schemes and engineering verification, this device enables convenient testing without disconnecting the main circuit copper bars or plugging/unplugging the valve control optical fibers. The testing time for a single group is reduced to 10 minutes, and 9 sub-modules can be tested at one time, with the detection efficiency improved by 18 times compared with the traditional method. The test results show that all functions of the device meet the design requirements, which can provide reliable guarantee for the safe and stable operation of the flexible DC transmission system.

title: DEFF: A Distributed Edge Fusion Framework for Cybersecurity Incident Prediction
authors: Victor Chang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Edge Computing (ICDSEC)
abstract: Enterprise security operations centers evaluate millions of events daily, yet <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$85-95 \%$</tex> are false positives or innocuous activities, causing analyst overload and delayed threat identification and response. Endpoint Detection identifies device vulnerabilities, while User Behavior Analytics detects anomalies in user behavior. They operate independently with limited detection capabilities. The Distributed Edge Fusion Framework (DEFF) enables network-edge agents to correlate various signals without centralizing raw data. Edge processors standardize vulnerability counts, patch age, and operating system risk and behavioral indicators (logon velocity, geographic disparities, and temporal entropy) before sending aggregated scores to a central system using weighted sigmoid activation functions. Central ensemble classifiers, utilizing Random Forest, XGBoost, and logistic regression, outperform community benchmarks <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.84-0.87)$</tex> by 6 % to 10 %. DEFF reduces false positives by 72 %, data transmission volume by 87 %, and detection delay by 92 %. GDPR and CCPA-compliant architecture protects privacy and enables precise detection. 13.6 million event reports demonstrate business system capability using real class distributions. DEFF shows that decentralized edge-level integration of diverse security signals meets operational, latency, and legal constraints better than centralized ensemble learning.

title: A Small Object Detection Algorithm for UAV Aerial Photography with Improved YOLOv11n
authors: Junjie Sun, Shiyu Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of inconsistent changes in the scale of small target features, fuzzy details, poor convergence performance of localization, as well as the susceptibility to misdetection and omission of detection in UAV aerial photography missions, an improved YOLOv11n algorithm for detecting small targets in UAV aerial photography is proposed. First, the C3k2 module is improved by Iterative Attentional Feature Fusion (iAFF) with nested attention mechanism and feature-weighted fusion, which realizes the efficient fusion of semantically and spatially inconsistent features and improves the processing capability of multi-scale information; second, the neck Secondly, the Transformer Feature Encoder (TFE) and Scale Sequential Feature Fusion (SSFF) modules are introduced into the neck feature fusion network, which improve the recognition of image details and the expression ability of detailed features by fusing feature maps at different scales. Finally, Focal Weighted Intersection over Union (Focal-WIoU) is used to replace the CIoU loss function in the original model, which enhances the localization performance of the model while accelerating the convergence speed, and accurately identifies the targets that are missed or misdetected by YOLOv11n. The experimental results show that the P, R, mAP50, and mAP50-95 are improved by 1.1%, 2.4%, 2.7%, and 1.5%, respectively, over the baseline model on the public dataset VisDrone2019. In addition, the generalization experiments on the RSOD and SIMD datasets also show that the improved model improves P, mAP50, and mAP50-95 by 1.3%, 0.5%, and 0.8%, respectively, on the RSOD dataset, and by 5%, 2.8%, and 1.6%, respectively, on the SIMD dataset, which sufficiently validates the validity and applicability of the model.

title: Federated Deep Learning for Intrusion Detection in Vehicle-to-Vehicle Networks: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Ranjima P, Devi Kannan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) communication functions as a core technology for Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) because it allows vehicles to exchange safety data in real-time for enhanced traffic control and improved road safety. The open nature of V2V networks together with their ability to move freely creates significant security risks because they can be easily targeted by cyber-attacks which include spoofing and denial-of-service and new adversarial machine learning threats. The operation of traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) becomes difficult because they experience scalability issues and high latency and limited adaptability in dynamic environments. Deep learning (DL) shows excellent ability to detect intricate and changing intrusion patterns. The decentralized structure of federated learning (FL) maintains user privacy protection through its distributed operation which matches the characteristics of vehicular networks. The survey provides an extensive evaluation of DL- and FL-based IDS systems which operate in V2V networks. The paper introduces a systematic classification system which organizes security threats and IDS architectures and federated aggregation techniques and performs an evaluation of available datasets and tools and robustness approaches. The research paper outlines future study paths to create explainable IDS systems which will scale and remain resilient for future vehicular networks.

title: Advancing AI-Driven Disease Diagnosis: A Hybrid MDCN and Transfer Learning Framework for Brain Tumor Diagnosis
authors: Neetu Settia, Pawan Kumar Singh, Monica Bhutani
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Healthcare sector is evolving in all fields with the introduction of Artificial Intelligence (AI). AI methods aid in precise health diagnosis, enables detection at early stages of disease and makes personalised treatment procedures hence resulting in overall better patient care. With the advancement in AI, their further incorporation in healthcare sector will speed up the accuracy of diagnosis and detection of diseases. This research has dual purposes: first, to demonstrate dual model AI architecture for diagnosis of brain tumor using MDCN and Transfer Learning and second, to address global challenges associated with integration of AI models with existing healthcare sector. It demonstrates that Transfer learning model achieved a higher accuracy of 97% as compared to MDCN for brain tumor detection. This dual model approach would prove beneficial to clinical staff due to enhanced accuracy and reliability in automated brain tumor detection in clinical field.

title: Bridging Temporal Reasoning and Language Planning for Vision-and-Language Navigation with Large-Scale Pretraining and LLMs
authors: Xingjian Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: We propose a two-stage framework for Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN) that integrates temporal-aware pretraining with large language model (LLM) reasoning. In the first stage, we introduce RHTAVLN, a pretraining pipeline based on multiple proxy tasks–including trajectory order modeling and instruction matching–to improve instruction–trajectory alignment and temporal understanding. In the second stage, we design NavLLM, an interpretable planning agent that transforms visual observations into language prompts and uses LLMs to generate chain-of-thought reasoning for action selection. Experiments on the Room-to-Room dataset demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in both seen and unseen environments. Ablation studies further validate the contributions of each component. Notably, NavLLM supports zero-shot deployment with strong generalization and transparent decision-making. Our results suggest that combining multimodal pretraining with LLM-based symbolic reasoning can lead to more robust, adaptive, and interpretable embodied agents.

title: Impact of Poisson Surface Reconstruction on Quality Assessment of Compressed Point Clouds
authors: Zhang Chen, Luqian Bai, Mengting Yu, Shuai Wan
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Surface reconstruction is generally required for displaying point clouds in practice. To explore the influence of point cloud surface reconstruction on its perceptual quality regarding compression distortion, we apply Poisson surface reconstruction for point clouds with compression distortion and evaluate the subjective quality of the point clouds before and after surface reconstruction. The reconstruction process improves the overall subjective quality, but this improvement is not uniform for point clouds with different quantization parameters and compressed distortion. When there is significant geometric and attribute compression distortion, surface reconstruction enhances subjective quality. However, as the geometric and attribute quantization parameters decrease, the improvement from surface reconstruction gradually diminishes. Surface reconstruction can reduce the subjective quality when there is slight geometric and attribute compression distortion due to over-smoothing or incorrect surface fitting. The findings in this work are helpful for the design of point cloud compression technologies. A further contribution of this work is a mesh subjective dataset with 36 different compressed distortions. This dataset enriches the data for point cloud quality evaluation and provides a valuable reference for compression. Our subjective database is available at the following link: https://github.com/ZhangChen2022/ICCC2025.

title: Simulation of Autonomous Navigation and Obstacle Avoidance Method for Smart Car based on SLAM Algorithm
authors: Yupeng Yin, Zhi Yang, Yunchang Xiao, Zixuan Long, Aoxiang Wang, Tengda Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: Aiming at the autonomous navigation challenges of smart cars in narrow static environment and dynamic complex environment, this paper proposes an autonomous navigation and obstacle avoidance method based on SLAM algorithm. The improved A* algorithm is adopted, the dynamic heuristic function is introduced, and the B-spline curve post-processing is combined to significantly optimize the smoothness of the path; the improved IRRT* method integrates the target offset sampling and the surrounding sampling of obstacles, and embeds the DWA dynamic window mechanism to improve the dynamic obstacle avoidance ability. The experimental results show that the improved A* algorithm reduces the inflection point by 45%; the convergence speed of the improved IRRT* algorithm is increased by 30%, and the success rate of dynamic obstacle avoidance is 92.3%. The system is significantly superior to traditional methods in path quality and real-time performance, and provides reliable technical support for intelligent storage, emergency rescue and other scenarios.

title: GA-LSTM Based Method for PV Power, Wind Power and Electricity Load Forecasting
authors: Feng Zhou, Kunpeng Li, Weijie Tang, Yu Chen, Shanmao Gu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid model based on a Genetic Algorithm (GA) optimized Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network, aiming to enhance the forecasting accuracy of photovoltaic (PV) power, wind power, and electricity load. This method innovatively utilizes GA to automatically fine-tune the crucial hyperparameters of the LSTM network, effectively overcoming the challenges of manual parameter selection inherent in traditional LSTM models. Case studies conducted on actual electricity load data from Australia, wind power data from a wind turbine, and generation data from a PV power plant validate the significant superiority of the proposed GA-LSTM model. Results indicate that, compared to standard LSTM models, the GA-LSTM model substantially improved the R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value to 0.928 for electricity load forecasting, and to 0.889 and 0.979 for wind power and PV power forecasting, respectively, while also significantly reducing MAE and RMSE error metrics. These improvements demonstrate the GA-LSTM model's robust capability in handling highly volatile time series data, providing more reliable forecasting support for stable power system operation and efficient renewable energy utilization.

title: Topology Optimization of Motor Bracket and its 3D Printing
authors: Zhifeng Nie, Kai Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: The traditional design of motor brackets is often constrained by empirical construction patterns, leading to some problems such as structural redundancy and material waste. In contrast, topology optimization technology systematically reconstructs the spatial distribution of materials through intelligent algorithms, promoting the evolution of electromechanical equipment towards greater efficiency and energy savings while meeting core indicators such as stiffness, strength, and safety factors. This paper employs topology optimization algorithms to design a lightweight solution for the motor bracket, geometric reconstruction is performed based on the preliminary optimization scheme, and the final optimized scheme for the motor bracket is determined through comparison and strength analysis. In the final, a physical model of motor bracket is obtained using 3D printing.

title: SPPINN: Spiral Phase Plate Integrated Neural Network for High-Fidelity OAM Demultiplexing in Satellite-to-Ground Optical Links
authors: Harshit Tiwari, Miguel Ángel Vázquez, Satyendra Kumar Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: This paper investigates the use of orbital angular momentum (OAM) light beams for satellite-to-ground (S2G) free-space optical (FSO) communication to enable high-capacity and robust data transmission. While OAM beams provide high-dimensional multiplexing, their performance is severely affected by turbulence-induced mode degradation and inter-mode crosstalk, leading to increased bit-error rates when multiple channels are employed.To address these challenges, we propose an all-optical neural network framework, the Spiral Phase Plate Integrated Neural Network (SPPINN), which operates in conjunction with combined spiral-phase-plate and grating structure for simultaneous demultiplexing and beam steering, to correct intensity and phase distortions in OAM-carrying Laguerre–Gaussian beams across six multiplexed OAM channels (ℓ = −3,…,+3). Evaluated under three atmospheric turbulence strengths (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">n</inf><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−15</sup>, 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−16</sup>, 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−17</sup> m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−2/3</sup>), SPPINN reduces the mean loss across the six channels by roughly 4.0 dB on average compared to the baseline SPP demultiplexer. Per-mode improvements reach up to ≈13.1 dB (mode ℓ = −3 at C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">n</inf><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> =10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−17</sup>), with consistent 7–12 dB gains observed for several high |ℓ| channels. For the low order |ℓ| modes (notably ℓ = ±1) the distribution of the power among the other modes in specific conditions (up to ≈7.7 dB), highlighting the impact of cross-talk among the modes, and channel performance.

title: Factors Influencing Employee Adoption of AI-Driven Valuation Tools in the Real-Estate Appraisal Industry
authors: Tang Chao, Wang Shan
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This study aims to explore the factors that influence the willingness of employees in the real estate appraisal industry in the Greater Bay Area to adopt AI driven valuation tools. By using a qualitative method with 15 employees from leading companies in the Greater Bay Area, the results indicate that successful integration depends on resolving the fundamental contradiction between operational efficiency and human judgment validation. The results of this study contribute to the literature on technology acceptance by contextualizing adoption barriers in high-risk valuation environments, and provide the following clues: (1) combining artificial intelligence deployment with specialized evaluation workflows, (2) establishing accountability governance protocols, and (3) redesigning training for human AI collaboration. These practical contributions may enable the implementation of strategic artificial intelligence to enhance industry competitiveness in a downward trend.

title: Using Machine Learning Algorithm to Predict Climate Change Pattern in Natural Geographical Environment
authors: Yu Lu, Yanxia Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Traditional climate prediction methods are limited by the processing capacity of nonlinear and complex climate systems, while machine learning algorithms show significant advantages in capturing complex patterns. In this paper, a hybrid model is adopted, which combines the Gradient Boosting Decision Tree (GBDT) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM). By comprehensively applying the advantages of these two algorithms, the accuracy and stability of climate change prediction are improved. The research data comes from weather stations and satellite observations, covering key climate variables such as temperature, humidity and wind speed. In the data preprocessing stage, the abnormal values are removed by IQR rule, and the missing values are supplemented by time series interpolation method, and feature selection and standardization are carried out. The experimental results show that the hybrid model (GBDT+LSTM) significantly improves the prediction accuracy compared with the single GBDT and LSTM models in the prediction of climate variables such as temperature, humidity and wind speed. Specifically, the accuracy of temperature prediction reached 92.6%, and the accuracy of humidity prediction and wind speed prediction increased to 88.5% and 85.7% respectively. In addition, the predicted values of the hybrid model in different time periods are in good agreement with the actual observed values, which shows the stability and effectiveness of the model in dealing with complex climate data. The research in this paper not only verifies the effectiveness and potential of machine learning algorithm in climate change prediction, but also further improves the prediction performance through reasonable selection and combination of features and optimization of model structure.
