authors: Manuel T. Tabada
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: This study examines the effectiveness and reliability of a smart security system. It focuses on how the If-and-Only-If (Iff) Algorithm and digital logic work together with multiple layers of sensors. The system uses sensors at doors, windows, and hallways to detect intrusions. The key is the Iff Algorithm, which ensures a precise match between sensor data and system responses. The results show that the system accurately manages data from these sensors, garnering a majority 0% error. By converting electrical signals into binary values, the system reliably created a set of sequenced binary digits, in which each digit corresponds to the installed sensor. This precise method improves the system's ability to detect intrusions and reduces the chance of false alarms, making it a reliable security solution.

title: GAN-Fusion-Based Face De-Identification with Multi-Scale Semantic Suppression and Security Performance Analysis
authors: Guangyu Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To meet the growing need for privacy-preserving sharing of face data in medical imaging, smart surveillance and edge AI, this paper proposes a GAN-fusion face de-identification model. The network couples a multi-scale semantic suppression (MS-SS) subnetwork with an attribute-aware injection (AAI) module so that identity cues on skin texture, periocular regions and global shape are attenuated, while non-identity attributes such as pose, expression and illumination are kept stable. A dual-discriminator design-an identity-aware discriminator and an image-quality discriminator-plus a VGG19 perceptual feature loss is adopted to keep the synthesized face close to the source in structure but far from it in embedding space. On the CelebA-HQ dataset, the proposed model achieves PSNR 27.38 dB and SSIM 0.942 on anonymized images. Evaluation against a strong ArcFace recognizer shows an identity shielding rate of 97.4%, which is about 42% relative improvement over Gaussian blur (68.6%). When the FGSM adversarial perturbation with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon =0.03$</tex> is added, the attack success rate falls to 2.8%, indicating that the de-identified manifold is already hard to exploit. However, under a diffusion-based de-anonymization model similar to recent reversible/detector-guided diffusion methods, a 16.2% identity matching rate remains, suggesting that latentlevel identity hiding still needs reinforcement. The method complies with national-level requirements that demand <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 9 0 {\%}}$</tex> anonymization detection rate for released face data.

title: Adaptive Ensemble Learning for Malicious Website Detection Under Concept Drift
authors: Gaddam Srikanth Reddy, R Jothikumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: The rapid advancement of malicious websites poses significant cybersecurity risks. Traditional detection models fail to maintain accuracy under changing attack patterns due to concept drift. This paper proposes an adaptive ensemble learning framework for malicious website detection that dynamically updates classifier weights in response to detected concept drift. The framework combines multiple classifiers and selectively retrains those with performance below threshold, ensuring resilience to shifting data distributions. Evaluation on benchmark datasets shows superior detection accuracy and stability compared to static and baseline adaptive models. Results show the proposed method effectively captures adversarial evolution, maintaining robust performance with reduced retraining overhead. This approach provides a scalable, adaptive solution for real-time cybersecurity defense.

title: Active Damping Implementation for Controller Performance Optimization in DSSC System
authors: He Liu, Yonghao Fang, Huiyuan Zhang, Jiayun Zhu, Zhiyuan Li, Pengfei Jia
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: As Distributed Static Series Compensators (DSSCs) become widely researched in renewable-integrated grid for power flow control and fault management, their harmonic susceptibility remains a challenge. Conventional damping methods relying on parasitic or additional resistors inherently suffer from significant resistive losses, limiting their performance. This paper presents an optimized active damping control strategy for DSSC to mitigate inherent LC filter oscillations in impedance regulation application. The model for single-phase LC-type DSSC inverter and dual-loop control is established. By transforming passive resistor-capacitor network into an active damping solution, equivalent stability is achieved without extra physical resistor. Parameter design rules are established along with primary performance analysis. Simulation results verify effective resonance suppression, stable mode transitions, and improved harmonic performance compared to conventional passive methods.

title: A Controlled Regenerative Braking Approach for Induction Motor-Based Electrified Vehicles
authors: Nikhil Suresh Chavan, Srikanthan Sridharan
abstract: During certain operating conditions in electrified vehicles, energy recovery through regenerative braking is either intentionally restricted or rendered infeasible due to practical considerations. As an alternative during such situations, this work proposes to investigate the dissipative braking capabilities of an induction motor, used for traction in a vehicle's electric drive system. Existing literature lacks comprehensive analysis of slip magnitude's impact on transient torque characteristics during braking, compared to steady-state motoring and generating operations. This paper analyses and determines the appropriate operating slip values in the regenerative braking region, such that the regenerated power is dissipated within the motor partially or completely, ensuring a controlled energy recovery. The proposed method is based on implementing a simple scalar control strategy involving dynamic variation of the motor's terminal voltage and frequency as a function of the motor speed, with a novel dynamic slip- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V / f$</tex> coordination framework to control energy recovery. Simulation results indicate that using an appropriate slip value during braking effectively enhances the dissipative braking capability. However, the initial braking torque achievable is observed to be reduced at higher slip magnitudes, highlighting critical transient torque trade-offs. A detailed quantitative comparison of braking performance among various operating points corresponding to low- and high-slip values is also provided to understand the trade-offs between net energy recovered and vehicle deceleration characteristics.

title: Defect Detection Algorithm for Steel Plate Surfaces Based on Improved YOLOv8n
authors: Zhou Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: The present study proposes a model, designated as ARS-YOLOv8n, to address the issue of insufficient recognition accuracy in steel plate surface defect detection. First, the ADown module is introduced to enhance the features of steel plate surface defect targets while suppressing irrelevant area features. This enables the model to capture more defect feature information. This approach effectively addresses the challenge of low pixel proportions in small defects in steel plate surface datasets. Secondly, the C2f-iRMB module is introduced to significantly reduce the number of parameters and computational complexity, while maintaining high feature extraction capability. The integration of the slim-neck lightweight structure design, the GSConv module, and the cross-stage partial network module (VoV-GSCSP) culminates in a model that exhibits several notable advantages. Chief among these is a reduction in time complexity, a decrease in the number of parameters, and an enhancement in performance. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed enhanced algorithm achieves a 3.1% increase in accuracy, a 4.5% increase in recall, and a 4.4% increase in mean average precision (mAP), thereby attaining an accuracy rate of 88.1%. The improved network achieves a significant enhancement in the performance of detecting surface defects on steel plates.

title: Assessment of the Strength and Fatigue Behavior of Generator Rotor Fan Blades under Static and Cyclic Loading
authors: Alireza Shamaei-Kashani, Meysam Davoudabadi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: In this study, a novel analytical model is developed to predict the service life of generator rotor fan blades under both static and fatigue loading conditions. The static model assesses failure using a failure assessment diagram (FAD) based on fracture mechanics by calculating the fracture and load ratios. In the fatigue model, forging defects are initially represented as cracks with defined geometries based on available empirical relationships. Critical regions are then identified based on the stress distribution in the blade under cthe generator’s cyclic loading condition. The blade’s service life is subsequently estimated using Paris’ law, fracture toughness, and crack growth constants, taking into account the critical crack size and the number of load cycles. The results reveal that the lowest safety factor for the blade occurs under static loading conditions, with a value of 2.02.

title: Research and Application of Multi-Agent Cooperative Decision Algorithm Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Hongyi Hao
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In complex dynamic environment, there are some problems in multi-agent collaborative decision-making, such as non-stationarity, credit distribution deviation and imperfect topological relationship modeling. In this article, the deep reinforcement learning (DRL) algorithm Att-GNN-MARL, which combines attention mechanism and GNN, is proposed to improve the cooperative efficiency and strategic stability of multi-agent system (MAS). Based on the centralized training decentralized execution (CTDE) framework, this study uses graph neural network (GNN) to model the spatial connection of agents, introduces attention mechanism to enhance the perception of key neighbors, and designs a counterfactual reward correction mechanism to optimize local credit allocation. In the collaborative control scenario of urban traffic signals, the experiments based on real road network on SUMO simulation platform show that the proposed algorithm reduces the average waiting time of vehicles to 54.3 seconds during rush hours, which is 22.3% less than that of QMIX. The total traffic volume reached 24,108 vehicles, which was significantly better than the comparison method. The number of rounds of strategy convergence is 640, and the learning efficiency is good. The research shows that the cooperative learning mechanism combining structural perception and dynamic attention can improve the overall performance of MAS in highly coupled tasks to some extent.

title: 3-D Thermal Modeling and Simulation of Wide Band Gap Semiconductor Devices Based CSI-fed PMSM Integrated Motor Drive System
authors: Hossein Azizi Moghaddam, Reza Mirzahosseini
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: In this paper, three-dimensional (3-D) thermal analysis and design of the cooling system of a wide bandgap (WBG) based CSI-fed PMSM integrated motor -drive system is presented. In this structure, the motor and the drive are integrated in a common enclosure, and their mutual thermal effects are considered. The sources of losses in different components of the motor and drive are calculated and updated using the coupling between the magnetic finite element simulations of the motor and the dynamic simulation of the drive system in MATLAB and PLECS software. The thermal and magnetic simulations are performed in a coupled manner to accurately evaluate the temperature distribution under real operating conditions. The results show that heat transfer in the integrated structure occurs axially and radially, and 3-D thermal modeling is essential for accurate prediction of the thermal behavior of the system.

title: GEM-ARC: AI Impelled Gamified Engagement System with Smart Reward Redemption Card for Universities and Students
authors: Ranjit Kaur, Aman Kumar, Jyotiranjan Mishra, Aakash Deep, Anuj Kumar Jha, Anuja Kumari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: University events are a fundamental part of student life and educational experiences, but the digital platforms or systems currently used for managing them are still fragmented and non-customizable. This research presents an AI-powered ecosystem which is created especially for managing university events that resolve current constraints in a coordinated manner. Our system comprises: (1) a multi-layered personalized recommendation system which integrate reinforcement learning, content-based filtering, and collaborative filtering; (2) dynamic notification management based on their skills and interests; (3) an intelligent gamification mechanisms in which students will rewarded with coins that can redeemable using cards or digitally; (4) a chatbot processed by natural language processing (NLP) for event idea generation and query resolution; and (5) sentiment analysis for feedback evaluation. We show how these interrelated elements work together to produce a self-improving system that raises student engagement metrics, decreases administrative load, and increases event participation. Compared to traditional systems, preliminary assessments indicate a 37% decrease in administrative workload and a 42% increase in event participation. In contrast with simply integrating fragmented technologies, the architecture offered offers a novel framework which applies AI techniques to the domain of university event management, resulting in a enriched ecosystem.

title: Automatic Classification of Agricultural Crops based on Satellite Data Time Series and Neural Networks
authors: Karimov Sardor Ilkhom Ugli, Xaydarova Inoyatxon Patidin Qizi, Abdurasulova Nasibaxon Abduvohid Qizi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: This paper addresses the development and investigation of a hybrid neural network architecture for automatic classification of agricultural crops based on satellite data time series. The proposed model combines convolutional neural networks (CNN) with long short-term memory (LSTM) networks to effectively capture both spatial features and temporal patterns in multi-temporal satellite imagery. The objective is to improve the accuracy and robustness of agricultural land classification through the integration of remote sensing data obtained from Sentinel-2 and Landsat-8 satellites across multiple growing seasons. The work analyzes spectral characteristics of vegetation and their seasonal variations using NDVI, EVI, and SAVI indices derived from time-series data. Experimental results demonstrate that the CNN-LSTM hybrid architecture achieves superior performance compared to conventional CNN approaches, with an accuracy of $94.3 \%$ on test data from agricultural areas in Central Asia.

title: A Modular Framework for Transcription, Diarization, and Language-Adaptive Summarization of Multi-Party Conversations
authors: Hasna El Maizi, Rachid Fateh, Mathieu Pouliquen, Kaoutar Ahed, Yasmine Lamari, Miloud Frikel, Said Safi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This paper introduces a modular, language-adaptive framework for the automated analysis of multi-party conversational data. The proposed system orchestrates a sequential pipeline comprising three core modules: (1) automatic speech recognition (ASR) with integrated language identification, (2) speaker diarization, and (3) abstractive summarization. A key contribution is the dynamic adaptation of the summarization module, which leverages a large language model (LLM) guided by language-specific structured prompts selected based on the output of the ASR stage. The framework is implemented using a scalable microservices architecture that decouples the AI processing pipeline from the core application logic. We demonstrate the system’s efficacy through empirical evaluation, establishing performance benchmarks and validating the quality of its multilingual analysis capabilities. The result is a generalizable, open-source-based architecture for transforming unstructured, multilingual conversational data into structured, actionable knowledge.

title: Evaluation of the Energy–Capacity Split for System Balance and the Competitiveness of Renewable Energy in Peru after Law 32249
authors: Raul Enrique Perez Loayza, Dennis Raul Garay Aquino, Liliana Fidencia Valero Salazar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: This study evaluated how the energy and capacity split introduced after Law 32249 can support system balance and improve the competitiveness of renewable energy in Peru by remunerating electrical energy and firm capacity as separate products. The adequacy driver was defined using the 2025 to 2034 Efficient Generation Requirement under dry season conditions, and 2027 was selected as the focal milestone because the cumulative requirement reaches 600 megawatts beyond the committed projects window. A 2027 renewable led portfolio was designed to deliver 600 megawatts of firm capacity using hydropower support, wind with a conservative effective load carrying capability credit, solar made peak deliverable through a four hour battery energy storage system, and efficient combined cycle natural gas backup, yielding 3,662 gigawatt hours per year of net energy. Under a decoupled Regulated Market and Free Market contracting structure, the portfolio generated 154.3 million United States dollars per year from energy sales and 46.7 million United States dollars per year from capacity payments under a Basic Capacity Price cap.

title: A Point Cloud Processing Method Based on Weighted Filtering
authors: Yixuan Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of noise and jump in 2D laser point clouds, this paper proposes a point cloud processing method based on weighted filtering. The method handles outliers through a local distance adjustment mechanism and achieves adaptive smoothing based on a local linear model and Gaussian weighting. Comparative experiments in typical scenarios show that compared with traditional filtering methods, the proposed method performs better in key indicators such as absolute error and range. It can effectively suppress noise while preserving contour features, providing a more robust solution for 2D point cloud processing.

title: CoShMDM: Contact and Shape-Aware Latent Motion Diffusion Model for Human Interaction Generation_supp2-3675725.mp4
authors: Ali Asghar Manjotho
abstract: Generating realistic two-person interaction motions from text holds immense potential in computer vision and animations. While existing latent motion diffusion models offer compact and efficient representations, they often fail to produce physically plausible contacts and are typically constrained to a single canonical body shape. As a result, the generated motion sequences exhibit substantial mesh penetrations and lack interaction realism. To address these limitations, we propose a contact and shape-aware latent motion representation and diffusion model (CoShMDM) for generating realistic two-person interactions from text. Our framework begins by constructing contact-compatible motion using SMPL-based meshes and a normal alignment-based mesh contact matrix to capture fine-grained mesh-level contacts. To account for shape diversity, we incorporate SMPL shape parameters and iteratively learn contact dynamics across different body shapes. Additionally, a reinforcement learning-based mesh penetration avoidance policy network, guided by signed distance fields, is introduced to minimize mesh penetrations while preserving contact fidelity and shape-aware motion. We further employ a dual-encoder VQ-VAE to learn disentangled latent representations for motion and contacts, which are then utilized in a text- and body-shape-conditioned diffusion model. To ensure spatial, temporal, and semantic coherence, we integrate a novel contact and motion consistency module into the diffusion transformer. Extensive evaluations on the InterHuman and InterX datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches achieving lowest FID scores (4.801 and 0.013), with 19% and 17.3% reductions in mesh penetrations, and 17.8% and 33.2% gains in contact similarity, respectively.

title: C2|Q>: A Robust Framework for Bridging Classical and Quantum Software Development
authors: Boshuai Ye, Arif Ali Khan, Teemu Pihkakoski, Peng Liang, Muhammad Azeem Akbar, Matti Silveri, Lauri Malmi

title: Legal and Ethical Considerations for Translating Federated Learning into Cross-Border Healthcare Innovation
authors: Luana F. Alvarenga, Nickerson F. Ferreira, André S. C. Peres, Renan C. Moioli, César Rennó-Costa
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) offers a privacy-enhancing architecture for training artificial intelligence on decentralized healthcare data, yet the prevailing mantra to “move the model, not the data” obscures significant privacy risks, ethical dilemmas, and regulatory conflicts. This article challenges the assumption that FL inherently solves cross-border compliance by analyzing a hypothetical consortium involving the United States, the UK, EU, China, and Brazil. We identify a specific compliance deadlock arising from the friction between Western rights-based frameworks (HIPAA, GDPR, LGPD) and state-centric security models (China's PIPL/DSL), particularly regarding model inversion attacks and data localization. Moving beyond validatory analysis, we propose a multi-layered Federated Governance Framework to operationalize data diplomacy. This contribution introduces novel legal and structural mechanisms: the Federated Data Sharing & Use Agreement (F-DSA) to codify binding mandates like privacy-enhancing technologies and isolated training; Governance as a Service (GaaS) to neutralize conflicts of interest through third-party administration; and relational mechanisms, including blockchain-based dynamic consent to solve the cascade of consent problem. This framework provides a blueprint for converting theoretical FL potential into a legally compliant, ethically sound, and functioning international Learning Health System (LHS).

title: Pre-Matching Enhanced Hierarchical Double Auction for Collaborative Task Offloading in Vehicle-Edge-Cloud Systems
authors: Kangli Zhao, Xincao Xu, Penglin Dai, Huanlai Xing, Xiao Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Edge computing holds great potential to enhance the real-time capabilities of intelligent vehicles by deploying resources at the network edge. However, due to the dynamic and heterogeneous nature of vehicular networks, several challenges remain. These include workload imbalances caused by the interplay between mobility patterns and varying resource availability, the fluctuating decision spaces resulting from the continuous entry and exit of vehicles, and the absence of effective incentive mechanisms for resource preservation. To address these challenges, this paper formulates the task offloading and resource allocation (TSRM) problem within the vehicle-edge cloud cooperation framework by characterizing transmission and computation times accounting for the characteristics of heterogeneous resources and vehicle mobility. We prove that this problem is NP-hard, with the objective of optimizing the cumulative task service ratio to enhance overall system efficiency. We propose a Pre-Matching enhanced Hierarchical Double Auction (PMHDA) algorithm based on hierarchical auction and matching theory, which consists of two main auctions: the vehicle-edge auction (VEA) and the edge-cloud auction (ECA). In the VEA, client vehicles must decide whether to process tasks locally or delegate them to server vehicles or edge nodes. A pre-matching algorithm is designed to identify the optimal offloading candidates, taking into account vehicle mobility and dynamic network conditions. In the ECA, edge nodes determine whether to process tasks locally or forward them to other edge nodes or the cloud. Extensive simulations on real-world datasets demonstrate that PMHDA provides substantial service-rate gains over baseline methods, achieving an average improvement of 75.47% under diverse scenarios.

title: Robust Beamforming for Practical RIS-Aided RSMA Systems with Imperfect SIC under Transceiver Hardware Impairments
authors: Xuejun Cheng, Qian Zhang, Yunnuo Xu, Zheng Dong, Ju Liu, Bruno Clerckx
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-aided rate splitting multiple access (RSMA) systems have demonstrated remarkable potential in enhancing spectral efficiency. However, most existing works rely on ideal hardware, which is unrealistic. In practical deployments, RIS elements suffer from amplitude–phase coupling, where transceivers are subject to hardware impairments (HWI), and successive interference cancellation (SIC) in RSMA networks cannot achieve perfect interference elimination for decoded signals. To address these limitations, we investigate a robust beamforming design for RIS-aided RSMA systems under practical hardware imperfections. We first characterize the asymptotic signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of practical RIS systems when the beamformer is designed based on ideal RIS model, thereby theoretically quantifying the resulting performance degradation. We then derive a closed form expression for the distortion noise power induced by transceiver HWI, while also accounting for residual interference due to imperfect SIC. Building on these insights, we establish a comprehensive system model that jointly incorporates all hardware-induced impairments and formulate a multiuser sum rate maximization problem. To solve the resulting non-convex optimization problem, we develop an efficient block variable relaxation algorithm. Simulation results verify that the proposed scheme significantly outperforms conventional non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) approaches, and achieves superior robustness compared with benchmark schemes neglecting HWI, imperfect SIC, or amplitude–phase coupling.

title: MIRAGE-DoH: Metamorphic Intelligence and Resilient AI Grid for Autonomous Governance of Encrypted DNS
authors: Basharat Ali, Guihai Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Existing DNS over HTTPS defenses have demonstrated limited resilience against polymorphic traffic shaping, staged tunneling, and adaptive mimicry, largely because they rely on static learning pipelines and rigid cryptographic configurations. MIRAGE-DoH was designed to examine whether adaptive inference, persistent structural encoding, and calibrated cryptographic agility could be integrated into a deployable and measurable encrypted DNS control architecture. The framework combined flow-level Cognitive MetaAgents capable of internal reconfiguration, Topological Memory Networks that preserved stable geometric irregularities across temporal windows, and Metamorphic Cryptographic Shards that adjusted key encapsulation policies according to empirically calibrated threat severity. A Causal Counterfactual Environment modeled constrained attacker decision pathways, while Spectral Game Intelligence analyzed flow interaction graphs to anticipate structural attack transitions.Evaluation on extended CIC-DoH2023 and Gen-C-DDD-2022 datasets was conducted under fixed flow-level decision intervals with explicit accounting for synchronization overhead, spectral graph construction cost, and cryptographic rotation latency. Cross-dataset experiments yielded a mean detection accuracy of 97.8% with a 0.41% false positive rate, sustaining median inference latency of 62μs and cryptographic morph latency of 3.7 ms under load. Quantum-assisted inference was assessed through bounded simulations, indicating constrained information gain within the adopted lattice-based configuration, without asserting unconditional post-quantum immunity. These results demonstrated that adaptive encrypted DNS governance can be empirically grounded, operationally bounded, and stress-evaluated without reliance on unqualified claims of perfect security.

title: A Robustness-Enhanced Dual-Loop Control Strategy Based on SMC for Vienna Rectifier
authors: Hao Ding, Jinrui Xu, Jing Yuan, Shouxiang Li, Zhenbin Zhang
abstract: Although the Vienna rectifier is a promising rectifier topology for broad applications, its high-performance control is susceptible to disturbances such as parameter mismatches and load variations. To enhance the robustness against disturbances while maintaining a low chattering level, this article proposes a novel observer-based terminal sliding mode controller (TSMC) approach for current-loop control. First, after analyzing the inherent chattering and inaccurate voltage vector selection associated with conventional TSMC, we develop an error-based fixed-time supertwisting extended state observer (FTST-EESO) and integrated within the TSMC framework. This integration effectively improves the dynamic response, suppresses chattering, eliminates zero-crossing current distortion, and ensures balanced neutral-point voltage. Second, recognizing the limited robustness of conventional proportional-integral (PI) controller in voltage-loop control, we propose a hybrid control strategy termed the barrier function-based adaptive-gain supertwisting algorithm (BAGSTA). By constructing adaptive control gains based on positive-definite barrier function (PBF) and synthesizing the merits of PI controller and the supertwisting algorithm (STA), BAGSTA enables the system to flexibly configure the controller under diverse operating conditions, exhibiting high performance and enhanced the robustness in both steady-state and transient operations. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed scheme is validated through experimental results.

title: Evolutionary Neural Architecture Search for Physics-Informed Neural Networks with Variable-Length Designs_supp1-3676860.pdf
authors: Jiao Liu
abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) are a promising approach for solving partial differential equations (PDEs), but practical tuning remains largely handcrafted and costly, especially in variable-length search spaces where candidate architectures differ in depth and width. We present Evo-NAS-PINN, an evolutionary neural architecture search framework that addresses both the high per-candidate evaluation cost and the challenges posed by variable-length designs. Evo-NAS-PINN follows a two-stage scheme in which the last layer is cast as a convex regression solved in closed form via a gradient-free, physics-informed pseudo-inverse, while only the representation layers undergo a small number of gradient steps, greatly reducing evaluation cost during search. To explore variable-length designs efficiently, we introduce a self-attention-based surrogate-assisted evolutionary algorithm that encodes each architecture as a token sequence, uses attention to summarize sequences of different lengths, and provides uncertainty-aware guidance to the evolutionary loop. Across diverse PDE families, Evo-NAS-PINN delivers consistently better performance with only tens to a few hundred of optimization steps, surpassing strong PINN baselines that require prolonged training. Beyond efficiency, it generalizes robustly to previously unseen settings without additional data or retraining, and extensive experiments demonstrate its effectiveness and scalability for physics-informed architecture discovery.

title: MiniV-Bat: A Micro Bi-Copter UAV With Bias-CoG Stability and Optimized Flight Endurance
authors: Yinglun Liu, Yubing Bao, Yang Guo, Zhulin Wang, Xiang He
abstract: Bi-copter UAVs offer greater endurance potential compared to quadrotors but suffer from nonminimum phase (NMP) dynamics and center of gravity (CoG) sensitivity. This article presents MiniV-Bat, a 233.7 g bi-copter with a novel rotor disk-based control framework that simultaneously addresses both challenges. The controller eliminates NMP behavior by redefining the control objective to the rotor-disk plane while incorporating feedforward compensation for unknown CoG biases. Co-optimization of mechanical design and control enables exceptional performance: maximum 74-minute hover endurance–a record for sub-250 g bi-copters–with demonstrated stability under 45<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> body tilts from CoG displacement and robust disturbance rejection.

title: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
authors: N/A

title: Privacy-Preserving Multi-Modal Object Fusion for Connected Autonomous Vehicles: Resilience Against Malicious Third-Party Attacks
authors: Renwan Bi, Jinbo Xiong, Xu Yang, Yuanyuan Zhang, Zhiqiang Ruan, Junfeng Tian, Xun Yi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Connected autonomous vehicles (CAVs) utilize multi-modal sensors, such as LiDAR and high-definition cameras, to collect diverse types of sensing data. Fusing object detection information from these two modalities facilitates more accurate environmental perception. In this context, lightweight secret sharing techniques are employed to protect information privacy, enabling further calculation while effectively alleviating the computational resource constraints of CAVs. Meanwhile, such techniques require an additional third-party to generate some necessary random numbers. Addressing the challenges of privacy disclosure of multi-modal object information and the reliability of random numbers, we propose a malicious third-party-resistant privacy-preserving multi-modal object fusion model, termed MPOF. First, we develop a series of secure computation protocols that do not rely on time-consuming cryptographic primitives, including secure multiplication, secure sharing conversion, and secure comparison. Leveraging the idea of sacrificial verification, we can effectively detect malicious behavior by the third-party during the random number generation process. Second, we construct a secure object bounding-box matching module based on arithmetic secret sharing (ASS), enabling similarity calculation and matching of bounding-boxes between point cloud and image modalities. Additionally, we design a secure object score fusion module that achieves fusion and updating through secure implementations of convolution, ReLU, and Maxout operations. Detailed theoretical analysis and experimental results demonstrate that, compared to secure computation protocols using homomorphic encryption for random number generation, the proposed protocols reduce computational overhead by five orders of magnitude. Furthermore, the MPOF model constructed by integrating these protocols is secure, accurate, and efficient.

title: IEEE Feedback
authors: N/A

title: On the security of Sequential Multi-signer Ring Signature for Secure Medical Data Sharing in IoMT
authors: Jianhong Zhang, Chuming Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: In the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT), transmitted medical data contain sensitive patient information. To protect patient identity privacy and address the adaptability limitations of traditional ring signatures, Xu et al. proposed the anonymous sequential multi-signature ring signature (ASMR) to enable secure medical data sharing in the IoMT (IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security, DOI 10.1109/TIFS.2025.3574959). Although Xu et al. claim that the ASMR scheme is secure, our analysis demonstrates that it is insecure, as it is existentially and universally forgeable, allowing any entity to generate a valid ring signature for arbitrary messages. Following the presentation of the attack, we analyze the causes of these vulnerabilities and propose corresponding countermeasures.

title: LMAC-Net: A Lightweight Multimodal Attention Compression Network for Edge-Oriented IoT Intrusion Detection
authors: Weizhi Gao, Mingshu He, Miao He
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of IoT devices has increased network heterogeneity and complexity, posing challenges for accurate and real-time intrusion detection under resource constraints. To address this, we propose LMAC-Net, a lightweight multimodal attention compression network designed for edgeoriented IoT intrusion detection. LMAC-Net integrates four complementary modalities-header, temporal, payload, and contextual features-through a gated confidence-uncertainty fusion mechanism that adaptively balances modal contributions and suppresses noise. A bidirectional LSTM captures temporal dependencies, while a structured column pruning algorithm eliminates redundant parameters to reduce computational overhead. Experiments on CICEVSE2024, CIC-IoT 2023, MQTT-IoT-IDS2020, CIC-IDS2017, and a self-collected IoT-AD-Set demonstrate that LMAC-Net achieves high detection accuracy with significantly lower complexity and inference latency, making it well-suited for deployment in intelligent IoT edge environments.

title: Fusion of MFCC Features and CNN Architectures for Multi-Dataset Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Narinder Kaur, Neha Dhiman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Emotion identification from speech is essential for enhancing human-computer interaction, allowing systems to react with contextual empathy. This article presents a convolutional neural network (CNN)-based system that uses Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients (MFCCs) as input characteristics to categorise speech patterns according to emotions. Our approach employs the spatial structure of MFCCs to inherently discern patterns through deep convolutional layers, rather than relying on handcrafted features and basic classifications. We developed and enhanced a CNN architecture, subsequently utilising typical emotional speech datasets to evaluate its efficacy. Our experimental data indicate that our model significantly outperforms standard classification approaches. This demonstrates that deep learning is an effective method for detecting nuanced emotional signals in speech.

title: ICSPED 2026 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)

title: Indian Sign Language Recognition using YoloV5 on Custom Dataset
authors: Aayush Gautam, Aarya Aishwaryam, Satwinder Kaur, Garima Joshi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Hand gesture recognition systems serve as a vital role for lessening the communication gap between people who can hear and the deaf communities. The YOLO-V5 object detection model, coupled with a custom dataset of Indian alphabets, has been employed in this paper to recommend an improved method for the Indian hand gesture alphabet detection. By generating a wide range of sign language alphabet signs with enhanced variability and reliability, our approach tackles the limitations of earlier datasets. The significance of this unique dataset curation in computer vision techniques for user convenience is illustrated by this method, which yields considerable accuracy gains over baseline methods. Based on our experimental results, our YOLO-V5 achieves overall accuracy of 92%. This research enhances real-time sign language recognition systems and highlights the significant impact of superior datasets on accurate performance.

title: Research on Traffic Sign Recognition Under Partial Occlusion Based on Deep Learning
authors: Zhanfei Cao, Ling Xu, Cheng Yao, Xiaoling Cheng, Weiping Zhu, Yu Wang, Dongfei Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This study addresses the challenge of traffic sign recognition under partial occlusion by proposing an occlusion-aware lightweight convolutional neural network, CNN_FV. A self-constructed dataset containing 4,440 images across 15 categories was built, with dynamic occlusion augmentation as well as geometric and photometric transformations applied to enhance generalization. A pyramid-based multi-level feature decoupling mechanism combined with channel attention is introduced to strengthen the extraction of critical non-occluded regions. The model is trained using cross-entropy loss and stochastic gradient descent, with early stopping to optimize convergence. Experimental results demonstrate that CNN_FV achieves an accuracy of 98.5% on a heavily occluded test set while maintaining only about 1.2M parameters, ensuring real-time performance and suitability for in-vehicle deployment. Furthermore, a graphical user interface was developed for visualization, verifying the model's effectiveness on real-world occluded images.

title: Cognitive Implications of Chronic Pain in Individuals with Primary Knee Osteoarthritis
authors: Mazira Mohamad Ghazali, Samhani Ismail, Muhammad Rajaei Ahmad, Jafri Malin Abdullah, Mohd Hanifah Jusoh, Kamaruddin Ibrahim
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Knee osteoarthritis (KOA) is a common degenerative joint disease, characterized by chronic pain that includes sensory and emotional dimensions. This chronic pain not only causes physical disability but also has the potential to affect cognitive function. Although chronic pain has been associated with cognitive changes in other chronic pain conditions, the evidence regarding KOA is still inconclusive and the specific cognitive effects associated with KOA pain remain poorly studied. This study aims to investigate the relationship between cognitive performance and demographic, pain and clinical variables in primary KOA compared with healthy controls. A cross-sectional study was conducted with 31 primary KOA patients, and 31 healthy controls completed the Wechsler Adult Intelligence Scale Fourth Edition (WAIS-IV) assessments for Perceptual Reasoning Index (PRI), Working Memory Index (WMI), and Processing Speed Index (PSI). While pain intensity and quality were evaluated using the Numerical Rating Scale (NRS) and Short-Form McGill Pain Questionnaire-2 (SF-MPQ2). Multiple linear regression and correlation analyses were carried out to identify predictors of cognitive performance. No significant differences between groups were found between PRI, WMI, or PSI <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p>0.05)$</tex>. In KOA, continuous pain correlated with affective <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{r}=0.621)$</tex>, intermittent <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{r}=0.470)$</tex>, and neuropathic <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{r}=0.385)(\mathrm{p}<0.05)$</tex>. Education level predicted all cognitive domain <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p \leq 0.003)$</tex>, while gender predicted WMI and PSI (with a bias towards females), and age negatively predicted WMI. Higher NRS scores were associated with lower PRI. Body mass index (BMI) was positively correlated with pain severity and KOA severity but not with cognitive domains. These findings suggest that KOA-related pain may selectively affect reasoning ability without causing widespread cognitive impairment. Interventions targeted at addressing sensory and emotional components have the potential to help maintain cognitive performance and improve quality of life.

title: SAP-CORA: Security-Aware PPO for Cooperative Resource Allocation in IoV Networks
authors: Yifei Li, Xiaohan Ge, Liangbin Yang, Jing Bai
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This paper tackles the challenges of multi-node collaboration, service continuity, and multi-objective optimization in highly dynamic Internet of vehicles (IoV) network resource allocation through the Security-Aware PPO for Cooperative Resource Allocation (SAP-CORA) framework. The framework introduces three core innovations: first, a three-stage service transmission model spanning uplink, processing, and downlink phases to strengthen multi-node coordination; second, a securitylevel mapping system quantifying security enhancement costs between nodes and services; third, a reinforcement learning architecture based on proximal policy optimization (PPO) incorporating a joint latency-security cost reward function. Experimental results confirm the algorithm's superiority over baseline methods in all key metrics including average reward, service success rate, average latency, and security cost.

title: A Short-Term Wind Power Prediction Method Based on the Optimized Hybrid Model
authors: Xiwang Li, Xiao Han, Xin Liu, Sentai An
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: To improve the accuracy of short-term wind power forecasting, a hybrid prediction model (BiLSTM-KAN) combining a bidirectional long short-term memory network (BiLSTM) and a Kolmogorov-Arnold network (KAN) based on Bayesian optimization (BO) is proposed. This model leverages the bidirectional encoding structure of BiLSTM to fully exploit the bidirectional spatiotemporal features of multivariate time series data, such as historical wind power and wind speed. A KAN network is introduced to replace the fully connected layer, leveraging its dynamic nonlinear mapping capabilities to adaptively weight the high-dimensional features extracted by the BiLSTM. Experimental results show that the proposed model outperforms single LSTM and BiLSTM models, as well as the CNN-BiLSTM model and the BiLSTM-KAN model without BO in terms of key prediction metrics, such as the coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{\mathrm{2}}$</tex>), mean absolute error (MAE), and root mean square error (RMSE).

title: Dynamic Model Selection for Energy-Efficient AI Systems in Healthcare
authors: Neha Nahariya
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The Dynamic Model Selection (DMS) is a method for the energy efficiency of Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems in the healthcare sector. The DMS framework ensures the best trade-off between the precision of the diagnosis and the power consumed. The PTB-XL electrocardiography (ECG) dataset is work for the experiments, three distinct hierarchical models—Tiny, Medium, and Full Networks—are trained, and a reinforcement learning policy is included for adaptive inference. The study of the experiments shows that the suggested framework achieves an overall accuracy of 93.4% while reducing the average power consumption by nearly 60% when compared with the static Deep Learning (DL) models. The technology maintains its clinical sensitivity above 92% and provides real-time processing on both GPU and edge devices. The findings indicate that DMS is a promising path towards green, interpretable, and energy-efficient healthcare AI, and make it possible to achieve reliable diagnostics in different computational and clinical settings.

title: Causal Inference and Uncertainty Quantification for Counterfactual Analysis of Injury Severity in Multi-Vehicle Collisions
authors: Philip F Balyagati, Abdul Ngereza, Emmanuel Kidando, Ntemi Masanja, Caroline Bikuba
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Road crashes continue to pose significant public health concerns, prompting transportation agencies to prioritize strategies for improving roadway safety and mitigating crash impacts. Traditional approaches to modeling highway safety data often rely on regression-based methods to identify significant factors associated with injury severity. However, these methods primarily capture associations rather than causal effects, limiting their ability to evaluate the effectiveness of interventions. This study introduces a Bayesian causal inference framework to advance safety analysis by capturing underlying causal relationships on crash injuries. Using data from Ohio interstates, the framework assesses the effects of roadway, environmental, and behavioral factors on injury severity, with crash type modeled as a mediating variable. The average causal impacts and their associated uncertainties are estimated using the highest density interval (HDI). The study findings reveal that alcohol involvement and multi-vehicle crashes are key contributors to the increased risk of injuries and fatalities. Road geometry and vehicle speed exhibit both direct and mediated effects through crash type, while lighting conditions and the presence of work zones show uncertain impacts based on HDI. The results offer valuable insights for transportation safety policy, emphasizing the importance of targeting both direct and indirect pathways in crash injury prevention strategies.

title: ComboS: Combining State Measurement with DR-Sketch for Adaptive Heavy Hitter Detection
authors: Deyu Zhao, Guang Cheng, Yuyu Zhao
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Given that many of the events that lead to the disruption of networks have characteristics of the heavy hitter such as DDoS attack and elephant flows, accurate and flexible heavy hitter detection is indispensable to network operations. However, most of the existing methods for heavy hitter detection suffer from two drawbacks: lack of adaptiveness and imbalance between accuracy and generality. We present ComboS, a flexible network measurement framework for heavy hitter detection. ComboS introduces the network state measurement to guide task transition, and designs DR-Sketch which can adaptively change the measurement contents according to the tasks based on FPGA dynamic reconfigurable pipeline. We further propose a mechanism for filtering and optimizing measurement results based on an accurate table, which helps ComboS improve the measurement accuracy with less memory usage. The implementation of ComboS in FPGA is evaluated in a real network testbed, and the experiments indicate that ComboS can adaptively react to different kinds of heavy hitters rapidly. Besides, we experimentally find the balance between memory usage and state operation time, and verify that ComboS can reduce the detection error rate of heavy hitters to less than 1% with fewer hardware resources and higher throughput.

title: Evaluating User Experience (UX) Testing on User Interface of a Visual Decision Support System (VDSS)
authors: Ahmad Faiz Bin Ghazali
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: User interface (UI) is an important component in a visual decision support system (VDSS). Information can be presented using various techniques. The article presents the findings of user experience (UX) testing of a VDSS in crime investigation, which examines the involvement with human aspects in the development and evaluation of a system. The implication is that the evaluation of the proposed VDSS with domains experts can be concluded that the human element is an important, perhaps critical, for the management of UX in a VDSS. Results from the evaluation of UX in a VDSS with domain experts show that, in scale of 1 to 10, score 9.25 is achieved on the VDSS potential and score 8.75 for data representation, thus signify high acceptability of the proposed UI in a VDSS.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Gamified E-Marketplace for Sustainable E-Waste Collection
authors: Nurin Damia Sopian, Nor Hasnul Azirah Abdul Hamid, Wan Khairul Anuar Wan Abd Manan, Mazita Mokhtar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: Electronic waste (e-waste) disposal remains a global challenge, with limited public participation and fragmented collection systems in Malaysia. This paper presents Recycle Heroes, a gamified e-marketplace designed to enhance e-waste collection and user engagement in Selangor. The platform integrates points, leaderboards, and avatar customization with Jakob Nielsen's usability heuristics to ensure an intuitive user experience. Developed using an Adapted Waterfall System Development Life Cycle and implemented with a PHP-Laravel and MySQL architecture, the system supports consumers, collectors, and administrators through a centralized interface. Usability evaluation using the System Usability Scale produced a score of 69.13, while task-based testing recorded 86% task success and 78% efficiency, indicating generally effective and user-friendly performance. Expert heuristic evaluation highlighted strengths in consistency, aesthetics, and system visibility, alongside major issues in error prevention and recovery. Future work includes mobile deployment, enhanced gamification strategies, and integration of real-time analytics to scale nationwide adoption.

title: A Lightweight Design of True Random Number Generator Based on Superparamagnetic Tunnel Junction
authors: You Wang, Chaoyue Zhang, Yefan Xu, Yu Gong, Shouzhong Peng, Yue Zhang, Yijun Cui, Weiqiang Liu
abstract: With the rapid development of artificial intelligence (AI), a variety of attack techniques have emerged, threatening the data security. In this background, energy-efficient lightweight security primitives are required to guarantee the security of the Internet of Things (IoT). The conventional silicon-based true random number generator (TRNG) suffers from design complexity, high power consumption, and limited throughput, making it unsuitable for low-power and high-performance applications. Magnetic tunnel junctions (MTJs) can be used as a random source due to the random switching behaviors. However, most of the existing MTJ-based TRNG designs rely on reset–write–read operations, which limit throughput and energy efficiency. In this work, we propose a high-throughput TRNG based on the superparamagnetic tunnel junction (SMTJ). By reducing the thermal stability factor, SMTJ undergoes spontaneous random switching driven by thermal fluctuation, eliminating the need for costly reset and write operations. A compact spin-orbit torque (SOT)-controlled SMTJ model is developed and integrated by using the <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">nand</small>-SPIN structure, achieving a TRNG design with low power, high throughput, and high randomness.

title: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
authors: N/A

title: AI-Enabled Orbital Trajectory Optimization for Low-Earth Satellites: A Hybrid Deep Learning Approach
authors: Apurva, D.Vetrithangam
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Hybrid deep learning architectures have become effective instruments for simulating nonlinear orbital dynamics in Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) systems. This paper suggests a hybrid framework that uses Artificial Intelligence (AI) to combine Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Long-Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for accurate orbital trajectory optimization. The model uses Two-Line Element (TLE) data from the NORAD/CelesTrak General Perturbations (GP) catalogue and the Simplified General Perturbations 4 (SGP4) model to make normalised Cartesian position vectors. The CNN layers find spatial correlations in orbital sequences, and the LSTM layers find long-term temporal dependencies. Experimental evaluation shows that the suggested model has an overall prediction accuracy of 96.4%, with a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.021 km and a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 0.94%. The model also makes calculations more efficient, with an energy-performance ratio 2.5 times higher than baseline methods and an average inference time of 8.6 milliseconds per prediction step. Combining physics-based orbital parameters with data-driven learning makes it possible to make reliable, understandable, and energy-efficient predictions about trajectories. The results show that the framework can be used for real-time satellite navigation, avoiding collisions, and smart mission planning in future autonomous LEO operations.

title: Understanding the Mechanism of Discretionary Lane-Changing Behavior Based on Cumulative Prospect Theory and Decision Tree Model
authors: Wenbin Yao, Waner Li, Sheng Jin, Xinyi Shen, Jiaqi Zeng, Chunqin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Discretionary lane changing is one of the most important behaviors in vehicle dynamics. The analysis of discretionary lane changing can provide support for human-like autonomous driving system and microscopic traffic simulation. The rule based discretionary lane change decision-making model has the advantages of good interpretability and being in line with human intuitiveness, but the performance of the rule based models are relatively poor. The learning-based discretionary lane changing decision-making models perform better in lane changing analysis than that of the rule based models, while they require a large amount of training data and have poor interpretability. This study proposes a framework for discretionary lane changing decision-making based on cumulative prospect theory and decision tree model, and uses genetic algorithm to calibrate the parameters of the framework. The framework proposed in this study has good interpretability for discretionary lane changing behavior similar to rule-based models, and it can achieve good lane changing prediction performance. The Next Generation Simulation (NGSIM) dataset is used to validate the framework proposed in this study. The results show that the framework proposed in this study can achieve better performance of discretionary lane change prediction than the analysis model based on decision tree and the analysis model based on cumulative prospect theory. When predicting discretionary lane changing behavior 2–0 seconds in advance, the accuracy of the framework in the test set can reach 85.6%.

title: MetaMLIR: Meta-Learning With Multilevel Integration for Motion Pattern Recognition of Maneuvering Targets
authors: Zhenzhen Ma, Wei An, Yuan Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Pattern recognition of maneuvering target is challenging due to its rapidly changing patterns and uncertainties. Short-term trajectories are essential for capturing dynamic motion changes. However, existing deep-learning based methods rely on long-term trajectories to extract more comprehensive information, making them less effective with limited data. In this paper, we focus on recognizing short-term trajectories and propose a multilevel integrated framework that narrows the latent search space. Our approach employs a two-level classification with meta-learning (MetaMLIR) to recognize target motion patterns by learning multivariate features from noisy observations. Specifically, the first-level classification generates preliminary scores derived from multiple heterogeneous classifiers with a balanced integration strategy. The second-level classification uses logistic regression with meta-learning to share knowledge from the first-level outputs and provide the final classification result for each motion behavior. Additionally, we develop a target motion trajectory dataset (namely, TMT) to conduct comprehensive performance evaluation. Experimental results on our self-developed TMT dataset and the real-world NCLT dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves enhanced motion-pattern recognition performance across both non-pattern-switching and pattern-switching scenarios.

title: Integrating Voltage Stability into the Static Security Region: A Unified Analytical Framework
authors: Rafael Alberto García Tinoco, João Alberto Passos Filho, Jonathan Muñoz Tabora, Maximiliano Martínez
abstract: The increasing penetration of distributed and renewable generation has driven modern power systems closer to their technical limits, making voltage instability a key threat to grid reliability and reducing the operational margin for preventive control and generation redispatch. Traditional steady-state security tools, such as the Static Security Region (SSR), map feasible operating points under steady-state constraints but do not quantify proximity to voltage collapse. This paper proposes an enhanced SSR that integrates a fast, analytically derived Voltage Stability Index (VSI) based on a Thevenin equivalent and load-impedance characteristics, embedding stability-margin information directly into SSR nomograms. The resulting visualization jointly depicts feasibility and voltage-stability robustness, enabling screening and ranking of dispatch points beyond voltage-magnitude compliance. Case studies on the IEEE 9-bus and New England 39-bus systems show that the VSI-based SSR highlights dispatch-dependent low-margin regions that can occur before conventional thermal or voltage limits are reached. Overall, the method provides an efficient, interpretable tool for steady-state security assessment and preventive decision support.

title: Secure and Efficient Keyword Search over Encrypted Graphs with Trusted Hardware
authors: Qiuhao Wang, Xu Yang, Saiyu Qi, Yiwei Liu, Hongguang Zhao, Ke Li, Wenjia Zhao
abstract: Graph databases are increasingly outsourced to cloud servers for efficient management of entity relationships, yet this raises significant privacy concerns. Although PeGraph, the state-of-the-art scheme, supports encrypted conjunctive search over the graph, it suffers from limitations such as heavy client burden (requiring data users to maintain secret keys and generate extensive search tokens securely), high computational cost, result pattern leakage, and an inability to handle updates and verification. To address these issues, we propose SecGraph, a system that leverages an SGX-enabled cloud server to offload key protection and computation into a trusted enclave. We further design an SGX-aware data structure, LDCF-encoded XSet, based on a Logarithmic Dynamic Cuckoo Filter, to enable efficient plaintext computation within constrained trusted memory while mitigating pattern leakage. Additionally, we introduce Twin-TSet to support dynamic graph updates while ensuring forward and backward privacy. To support verifiable search, we propose VSec-Graph, which employs a procedure-oriented verification method to efficiently validate all data structures inside the enclave, avoiding costly local verification. Security analyses demonstrate the security properties of both schemes. Experimental results show that SecGraph achieves up to 208× faster search than PeGraph, while VSecGraph reduces verification time by 103× compared to VCQ.

title: Enabling Robust Radar Signal Deinterleaving via Multimodal and Continual Learning
authors: Qilin Yang, Xiangmao Chang, Jun Huang, Jingjing Gu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Realistic radar deployments operate in dense, dynamic electromagnetic environments where pulses from multiple emitters overlap in time and frequency, and the active emitter set changes as radars are activated, reconfigured, or repositioned. As a foundational step in radar signal processing, radar signal deinterleaving (RSD) faces fundamental challenges in such environments due to obscured emitter identities and the demand for real-time adaptivity. In this paper, we propose a robust deinterleaving method that integrates multimodal encoders with class-incremental learning (CIL), enabling accurate emitter identification with high precision, strong generalization, and adaptivity in evolving electromagnetic environments. Our approach jointly models intrapulse and interpulse features by transforming each radar pulse into a spectrogram and a structured descriptor set, which are encoded using pretrained backbones and then fused into a shared embedding space via contrastive learning. To support continual emitter learning at low memory and computational costs, we introduce a lightweight CIL module that integrates pseudo-feature replay, asymmetric supervised contrastive loss, and a cosine classifier with learnable scaling. On a simulated dataset built from real emitter parameters, our method maintains stable deinterleaving performance as new emitters gradually emerge under continual class expansion.

title: Stacked Intelligent Metasurfaces-based Joint Source-Channel Coding for Image Transmissions
authors: Inhyeok Choi, Shun Kojima
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: This paper proposes a joint source–channel coding (JSCC) framework based on a stacked intelligent metasurface (SIM)-enabled electromagnetic neural network (EMNN) with residual connections for image reconstruction over wireless channels. A residual connection is embedded in the multi-layer SIM architecture to alleviate the vanishing gradient problem inherent in deep EMNNs. Building on this structure, we formulate a joint optimization problem that minimizes the reconstruction error and devise a training algorithm that jointly updates the amplitudes and phases of the elements in both the transmitter and receiver SIMs via error backpropagation grounded in a physical wave-propagation model. Simulation results on the MNIST dataset show that the proposed residual SIM architecture enables high-quality image reconstruction even under low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions and significantly outperforms a standard SIM design without residual connections, demonstrating enhanced robustness against channel noise.

title: Predictive Human Trajectory Classification in Industrial Settings with Transfer Learning and Data Augmentation
authors: Even Falkenberg Langås, Atle Aalerud, Svein Olav Nyberg, Muhammad Hamza Zafar, Daniel Hagen, Filippo Sanfilippo
abstract: The successful realisation of Industry 5.0 relies on seamless collaboration between humans and robots in shared workspaces. This paper investigates predictive human trajectory classification (HTC) in a factory setting to enhance safety, productivity, and adaptability in human-machine environments. Using a state-of-the-art light detection and ranging (LiDAR) system with reflective mirrors to extend the field of view, motion data are collected across a 26 m × 20 m indoor industrial floor. Human trajectories are classified to predict workstation transitions using a stacked long-short term memory (LSTM) network. To address the challenges of limited real-world data, transfer learning is applied by first training the model on synthetically generated trajectories. The paper compares a traditional stochastic method, specifically the Ornstein-Uhlenbeck (OU) process, with a modern learning based method, represented by a timeseries generative adversarial network (TimeGAN). Further on, the algorithm is fine-tuned on the real-world dataset. The comparison shows that in data-scarce scenarios, OU outperforms TimeGAN in most metrics. The LSTM model trained on OU-based data and real-world data shows an overall accuracy gain of 4.8 percentage points compared to training on real-world data only. It is able to classify the goal workstation three seconds in advance with an accuracy of 95.6%, demonstrating the potential for predictive models to improve workplace operations by reducing stall time, optimising workstation readiness, and reducing the need for traditional safety cages.

title: Respiratory Infection Detection Using Cough Pattern Analysis
authors: K. Prithivi, S. Gomathy, K. Nithya Pooja, M. Mohamed Hashim, B. Santhosh
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The Early Respiratory Disease Detection System employs voice analysis via deep learning to provide a novel, efficient and accessible way to diagnose respiratory disorders that is non-invasive. To initiate the system, a comprehensive audio dataset is compiled to reflect a variety of respiratory diseases, ages, and demographics. Using the Librosa python library, the dataset is analyzed to extract key acoustic features including Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs), spectral contrast and chroma features indicating unique patterns of breath and cough sound. Each audio sample is coupled with a label indicating the corresponding respiratory condition to enable supervised learning. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is subsequently established and trained on the extracted feature set to complicate patterns between sound characteristics and respiratory patterns. The trained model can either classify in real time or batch and can isolate healthy states from diseased respiratory states. An independent test dataset is employed to assess the accuracy of the model and to guide future enhancement for improved reliability and generalization. By taking advantage of CNNs feature-learning advantages, this approach provides a scalable, low cost and reliable diagnostic solution in low-resource or remote healthcare settings. Continuous monitoring, retraining and clinical validation promote consistency and flexibility in a variety of populations and respiratory conditions over time.

title: Scalable and Interpretable Engineering Solutions for Fake Account Detection using GNNs and FactorVAE
authors: L. Selvam, Ramya S, S. Balamurugan, J. Relin Francis Raj, A. Vegi Fernando, S.P. Lavanya
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The proliferation of social media has introduced complex cybersecurity challenges, particularly due to the widespread presence of fake accounts that manipulate information flow, compromise network integrity, and undermine user trust. Detecting such accounts requires scalable and intelligent engineering solutions capable of analyzing multi-modal data at large scale. This paper presents a hybrid deep learning framework for fake account detection, integrating textual analysis, disentangled representation learning, and graph-based network modeling. Textual content is encoded using RoBERTa embeddings to capture semantic and stylistic features, while FactorVAE disentangles latent factors representing content, style, and intent. Network interactions are modeled through Graph Attention Networks (GAT), capturing relational and structural patterns within social networks. A hybrid MLP-GNN classifier fuses textual and network embeddings to enable high-accuracy detection across large-scale datasets. The framework incorporates explainable AI techniques, including SHAP values and attention maps, to provide interpretable predictions and enhance system reliability. Experimental evaluation demonstrates improved detection accuracy, robustness to adversarial and synthetic accounts, and generalization across multiple social media platforms. By enabling reliable identification of malicious accounts, this approach strengthens cybersecurity measures and ensures safer access to the internet. The proposed system establishes a scalable, interpretable, and efficient engineering solution for fake account detection, providing a benchmark framework for securing social media networks and protecting user interactions in online environments.

title: Enhanced Air Quality Prediction Using a Two-Stage XGBoost and LSTM Hybrid Architecture
authors: Rinisri R, Ramya K, Revathi M, Jennifer A
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The issue of air pollution forecasting has gained urgency due to the accelerated urbanization, industrial processes and climatic nature of fluctuation in the behavior of pollutants. While traditional machine learning and deep learning approaches demonstrate promising results, their major limitations still lie in either capturing nonlinear feature relationships or long-term temporal dependencies. This work presents an integrated XGBoost-LSTM hybrid for air quality prediction, combining gradient boosting for learning feature interactions with recurrent neural networks for temporal sequence modeling. The approach encompasses data preprocessing, feature engineering, residual learning, and multi-stage prediction. Experimental results show the supremacy of the hybrid model over the respective single models by reducing the RMSE to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.48 \mu ~\mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex>, MAE to 4.82 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu \mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex>, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> accuracy of 0.96. The system also maintains stable performance over multiple days and can thus be considered for real-time air quality monitoring and decision-support systems.

title: Comparative Analysis of Naive Bayes and Support Vector Machine Algorithms for Automated Health Status Classification
authors: Chandni Manral, Amit Mittal, Anant Deogaonkar, Kimsy Gulhane, Siddharth Joshi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The use of machine learning in healthcare is really important because it helps doctors find health problems early and manage patients better. In this study, we just tried to find out which model is better from another at knowing if someone's healthy or not. Is it the Naive Bayes (NB) or the Support Vector Machine (SVM). We took data from 800 patients so we can test, which included things like their age, BMI, blood pressure, glucose, insulin, and at last their cholesterol levels too. All the job, like making the data, testing the models, and seeing the results, was done in MATLAB. After checking the results, we get to know that the Naive Bayes model did pretty well than the other model which was Support Vector Machine. The Naive Bayes model got about 97.5 % accuracy, and the SVM was close with 96.25%. So yeah, NB worked a bit better as compared to the model. It basically shows that even simple models like Naïve Bayes can be super useful for helping doctors make better and faster choices then the Support Vector Machine.

title: Research on Fault Diagnosis for Variable-Speed Bearings Using VG and GAF Algorithms
authors: Xingxing Wang, Dagao Zhang, Jianxiong Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Most existing bearing fault diagnosis methods are designed for faults under a single speed, making them poorly suited to the practical scenario where bearing speeds dynamically change during operation. Deep learning and image processing techniques have demonstrated excellent performance across various fields; meanwhile, 2D images contain far richer information than 1D signals. To address this, this paper proposes a variable-speed bearing fault diagnosis model based on the Visibility Graph (VG) and Gramian Angular Field (GAF) algorithms. First, the VG and GAF algorithms are used to encode 1D bearing signals into vg-gadf-gasf three-channel images. These images are then fed into a Multi-convolution-branch Convolutional Neural Network (MulCNN) for diagnosis. To enhance the network’s ability to distinguish homogeneous fault features under variable speeds in the high-dimensional feature space, a cross-entropy loss function constrained by feature space distance is proposed for diagnostic tasks. In the 10-class variable-speed diagnosis task on the CWRU dataset, the proposed method achieves an accuracy rate of 99.89%, showing certain advantages over existing methods. In the test on the XJTU-SY dataset, it attains an accuracy rate of 99.57%, which verifies its favorable robustness and rotational speed adaptability.

title: A QoS-Aware RL-Based Virtual Network Embedding Framework for Wireless Multi-Hop Networks
authors: Yingjie Wang, Liwei Jiang, Chan Wang, Minjian Zhao
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Virtual Network Embedding (VNE), which aims to orchestrate the coexistence of multiple virtual networks on shared resources, encounters significant challenges in Wireless Multi-hop Networks (WMNs) due to the open nature of wireless channels and the complexity of interference, resulting in degraded Quality of Service (QoS). To address this, we propose QRL-VNE, a novel prediction-driven, QoS-aware Reinforcement Learningbased VNE framework. This framework integrates RouteNet-W, a specialized QoS prediction model based on a Message-Passing Neural Network, which accurately predicts end-to-end QoS metrics, including latency and packet loss, in WMNs. QRLVNE leverages these precise predictions to guide both node and link embedding, thereby significantly improving the QoS performance of VNE. Extensive packet-level simulation results in various WMNs on OPNET demonstrate that QRL-VNE significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in reducing end-toend latency and packet loss, while maintaining high performance in terms of acceptance ratio and resource efficiency.

title: AI-Driven Customer Service and Its Effect on Customer Loyalty and Trust in E-Commerce
authors: BNS Hemalatha, Srinivas. D
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The development of Artificial Intelligence (AI) has impacted different industries rapidly, and e-commerce is not the exception. Customer service AI which includes the implementation of chatbots, virtual assistants, and automated recommendation engine can provide customers with the best experience in terms of enhancing customer service efficiency and personalization. In this paper, the authors explore how AIbased customer service can convert customer loyalty and trust in the e-commerce industry. The study examines the correlation between the introduction of AI technologies and the two essential elements loyalty and trust that determine customer retention and customer satisfaction in general.
