Crucially, on-device processing significantly reduces the volume of data transmitted to the cloud, lowering operational costs and enhancing the speed of critical anomaly detection. Our evaluation, based on a case study of an EV bus fleet, demonstrates the tangible performance gains in terms of accuracy, cost reduction, and operational efficiency, showcasing a significant step towards sustainable and intelligent urban transportation.

title: Research on Machine Learning Models for Decision Analysis of UAV Inspections Based on Weather Warnings
authors: Chengming Jin, Donghui Tong, Zhenxian Yao, Xi Sun, Jianhang Sun, Shengfu Yan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address the static feature bias and insufficient single-feature information in traditional models for UAV inspection decisions under weather warnings, this study proposes a machine-learning framework-driven regression (DRL) model that integrates attention feature fusion and a three-dimensional reward function. Experiments are based on UAV inspection and meteorological data from January 2023 to June 2024 for a provincial power grid (108,600 valid samples, covering five 500 kV lines). Random Forest (RF) and single-feature DRL models are used as comparison models, and their performance is verified through comparative and ablation experiments. Results show that MFF-DRL achieves an overall decision accuracy of 92.3%, an 8.7 percentage point improvement over RF and a 6.5 percentage point improvement over single-feature DRL. The misjudgment rate is as low as 3.1%, with only 1.2% of cases involving misjudgment of risk as safety. The inspection task completion rate is 89.5%, with an average decision time of 0.8 seconds. In severe adverse scenarios <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{V} \geqslant 12 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R} \geqslant 0.7)$</tex>, the accuracy reaches 79.8%, a 10.9 percentage point improvement over RF. Ablation experiments confirm that the attention layer improves overall accuracy by 6.2 percentage points and significantly reduces the number of repeated inspections. While this research effectively optimizes inspection decisions, it lacks adaptability to extreme and rare weather conditions and does not cover multi-UAV collaborative scenarios. Future work aims to improve its practicality through online learning mechanisms and multi-UAV decision-making extensions.

title: Intelligent Personalized Online Learning Platform Leveraging Random Forest and NLP for Enhanced Learner Engagement
authors: P. Bharathi, G. H Vandhana, S. Abirami
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The rapid growth of digital education has highlighted the need for personalized, adaptive, and intelligent online learning systems capable of catering to individual learner needs. Conventional e-learning platforms often provide static content and uniform assessments, resulting in limited engagement, suboptimal learning outcomes, and inadequate support for diverse learner abilities. This paper presents an AI-powered adaptive personalized e-learning and tutorial system that integrates Random Forest and Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to dynamically tailor learning experiences. The system predicts learner performance using Random Forest on structured (quiz scores, engagement metrics) and unstructured (textual feedback, chatbot queries) data, enabling adaptive quiz difficulty, personalized feedback, and recommendation of learning materials. NLP algorithms, including TF-IDF–based feature extraction, extractive content summarization, and rule-based chatbot assistance, further enhance content accessibility, comprehension, and interactive support. Extensive evaluation demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed system. Compared to traditional Decision Tree and Naïve Bayes–based models, the Random Forest–driven platform exhibits higher accuracy (0.928), precision (0.921), recall (0.915), and F1-score (0.918) in predicting learner performance. Adaptive quizzes, content summarization, chatbot assistance, and recommendation modules achieve superior metrics across all parameters, including engagement, learning gain, topic mastery, and response efficiency. System-level performance analysis indicates improved response time, throughput, and resource utilization, while overall system effectiveness confirms significant gains in learner performance, engagement, and adaptive content utilization. The results highlight that the integration of ML and NLP techniques provides a robust, scalable, and intelligent environment that promotes self-paced learning, continuous assessment, and personalized educational support, making it suitable for academic, technical, and professional e-learning applications.

title: Study of traditional and modern methods for diagnosing mycetoma: the case of grain culture - A systematic review
authors: Mor Diagne, Kpetchehoue Merveille Santi Zinsou, Idy Diop, Mass Gning
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Mycetoma is a neglected tropical disease characterized by chronic granulomatous infection caused by bacteria (actinomycetes) or fungi (eumycetes). It mainly affects populations in Sudan, Senegal, and other tropical regions, causing significant medical, social, and economic impacts. Early diagnosis is crucial to limit complications, but remains difficult due to the limitations of current tools. Grain culture remains the gold standard method, although it is slow ($\mathbf{4}-\mathbf{6}$ weeks on average), resource-intensive, and dependent on specialist expertise. Molecular approaches, such as PCR, have improved accuracy (95%), but their accessibility is limited in low-resource countries. At the same time, recent advances in artificial intelligence, particularly the use of convolutional neural networks for histopathological image analysis, have shown accuracies greater than 90%, significantly reducing interpretation time. This review provides a structured classification and critical analysis of traditional methods (culture, histopathology, molecular tests) and intelligent approaches based on AI. We highlight their comparative performance, advantages, limitations, and prospects for integration in resource-limited settings. The objective is to systematically evaluate the added value of AI solutions compared to conventional methods in order to strengthen diagnostic care and reduce the impact of mycetoma on endemic populations.

title: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering Information for Authors
authors: N/A

title: Investigation of Forward Blocking Characteristics of Buried-Gate Field-Stop Barrier-Type Thyristor
authors: Guoyang Li, Hairong Li, Yucheng Li, Guangbin Zhang, Xiangyou Zeng, Zhixiong Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: This paper focuses on a theoretical analysis of the enhancement in overall electric field intensity and forward blocking characteristics of the Field-Stop Barrier-Type Thyristor (FS-BTH) compared to the conventional BTH. Based on Poisson's equation under the depletion approximation, analytical models are established for the P+N- abrupt junction of the conventional BTH and the PIN structure of the FS-BTH. The theoretical analysis indicates that the conventional BTH exhibits a triangular electric field distribution under lightly doped substrate conditions, where the maximum electric field intensity and blocking voltage are linearly limited by the drift region thickness. In contrast, the FS-BTH modulates the electric field profile from triangular to trapezoidal (quadrilateral) through the field-stop layer, significantly increasing the integral area of the electric field without increasing device thickness. An analytical expression incorporating the punch-through factor is derived. This formula quantitatively demonstrates how the FS-BTH achieves significant gains in overall electric field intensity and breakdown voltage for a fixed drift region thickness by "squaring" the field distribution. TCAD simulation results validate the accuracy of this theoretical model, confirming the physical superiority of the field-stop structure in optimizing the voltage-sustaining capability of power semiconductor devices.

title: Research on Optimization Algorithm of Transmission Line Inspection Path Based on UAV and Satellite Cooperation
authors: Jinhu Liu, Lin Chen, Shuntao Huang, Xiao Zeng, Danbing Liu, Di Li, Yadi Wang
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In this paper, in order to solve the problem that the demand of transmission line inspection increases rapidly and the traditional inspection method is inefficient, an optimization algorithm of transmission line inspection path based on UAV and satellite cooperation is proposed. The algorithm determines the distribution of towers and potential risk areas through large-scale monitoring data provided by satellites, and UAV conducts local detailed investigation based on this information to identify equipment defects and accurately locate fault points. A task allocation mechanism including satellite global monitoring and UAV local detailed investigation is studied and constructed, and a task priority model based on historical fault data is established. At the same time, combining the advantages of Genetic Algorithm (GA) and Ant Colony Optimization Algorithm (ACO), a hybrid optimization algorithm is designed, and a dynamic weight adjustment mechanism is introduced to balance the conflict among path length, energy consumption and coverage. The experimental results show that the algorithm is superior to the traditional GA and pure ACO algorithm in path length shortening rate, high-risk task coverage rate and energy consumption stability, which significantly improves the inspection efficiency and intelligence level, and provides strong support for the efficient implementation of transmission line inspection.

title: A large model API response quality prediction model based on least squares vector machine and SHAP interpretability analysis
authors: Haorui Gao, Wenjun Zeng, Jingtao Zhang, Youqing Liang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Large model apis have become the core infrastructure for enterprises to build AI applications and achieve intelligent interaction of AI agents. The response quality of large model apis directly affects user experience and business efficiency. Constructing models that can predict response quality in advance is a key requirement for the efficient application of large model apis. This paper proposes a regression model that integrates least squares vector Machine (LS-SVM) and SHAP interpretability analysis, providing a solution that takes into account both high accuracy and high interpretability for the prediction of API response quality of large models. The study first conducted a correlation analysis, and then carried out experiments with seven models, namely Decision tree, Random Forest, Adaboost, Extratrees, CatBoost, LightGBM, and XGBoost, as controls. From the perspective of evaluation indicators, the comprehensive prediction performance of the proposed model (Our model) is significantly better than that of most control models, and only some indicators are close to Extratrees. In terms of error indicators, this model performs outstandingly: The MSE is 0.918. Lower than 1.551 of Decision tree, 1.17 of Random Forest, 1.108 of Adaboost, 1.171 of CatBoost, 1.218 of LightGBM and 1.231 of XGBoost It is only slightly lower than Extratrees' 0.973; The RMSE is 0.958, which is lower than that of most models and smaller than Extratrees' 0.987. The MAE is 0.784, which is the lowest value among all models and is superior to Extratrees' 0.803 and other models. The MAPE is 11.722, nearly on par with Extratrees' 11.743 and lower than the rest of the models. The model's explanatory power index R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> is 0.404, which is slightly lower than Extratrees' 0.415 but higher than the other five models, indicating a strong ability to explain data variations. Overall, this model is at an excellent level in terms of error control and explanatory power. Its research results can provide reliable technical support for the quality control of enterprise large model API responses, help improve the stability of AI applications and business efficiency, and promote the efficient implementation of large model apis in actual scenarios.

title: Joint Delay–Energy Optimization of an 8-Bit Carry Lookahead Adder using Logical Effort and Voltage Scaling
authors: Qianyu Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: This paper proposes a critical-path-oriented delay–energy optimization framework for an 8-bit carry lookahead adder (CLA). Instead of optimizing the entire circuit, the optimization problem is reorganized around the dominant carry propagation path that determines most of the circuit delay. This reorganization reduces modeling complexity while preserving the main performance and energy bottleneck of the CLA. Based on this idea, a simplified and physically consistent delay–energy model is constructed using logical effort theory. The effects of diffusion capacitance and gate capacitance between adjacent stages are explicitly separated, which allows transistor sizing to be directly linked to both delay and energy. Supply voltage scaling is modeled independently from transistor sizing, and the two are jointly optimized under realistic device constraints with a strict sizing mapping rule to ensure physical realizability and consistency with SPICE simulation. Using this unified framework, the delay–energy trade-off on the critical path is analyzed and explained. The results show that aggressive minimum-delay design leads to diminishing returns, where small delay reductions cause a significant increase in energy consumption. In contrast, relaxing the delay constraint enables significant energy savings. Although only the critical path of an 8-bit CLA is optimized, the proposed framework captures the dominant delay and energy behavior of the circuit, providing clear design insights for early-stage optimization of CLAs and other CMOS arithmetic circuits.

title: Design and Analysis of a High-Sensitivity Photonic Crystal Pressure Sensor with Hexagonal Ring Resonator
authors: Ranjitha Krishna R, Usha Desai, Santhosh Kumar K B, Sushma N, Indira Bahaddur, P C Srikanth
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and performance analysis of a compact and efficient pressure sensor using a two-dimensional photonic crystal (2D-PC) structure integrated with a hexagonal ring resonator. The proposed sensor uses two quasi-waveguides coupled to a hexagonal ring cavity to detect pressure variations via refractive-index modulation. The optical behavior is modeled and simulated using the Finite-Difference Time-Domain (FDTD) method. Key performance metrics resonant wavelength shift, quality factor (Q), normalized output power, and sensitivity are analyzed. Simulation results show a resonant wavelength near 1.5708 µm with a full width at half maximum (FWHM) ≈ 0.06 nm, resulting in a high Q ≈ 2.6×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup>. The sensor exhibits a sensitivity of 1.35 nm/MPa over the evaluated pressure range (0–6 MPa) with an approximately linear wavelength shift, confirming the device’s reliability for pressure measurement. Compact size, high spectral resolution, and compatibility with integrated photonic circuits make this design suitable for biomedical, industrial, and environmental monitoring applications.

title: Design and Implementation of a Novel FPGA-based Secure Electronic Voting System using Fingerprint Authentication
authors: Y. Chalapathi Rao, Doti Giribabu, Harini Nallanagula, Bandla Soumya
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The design and implementation of hardware fingerprint based EVM on Zynq-7000 FPGA is presented. The objective of designing this system is to provide security and transparency in the process of voting with fast and reliable operation. As we know, there are many EVMs in the market, but the authentication is manual or not very stringent. This causes several issues like multiple and illegal voting. To address this problem, we implemented biometric authentication on the FPGA device. The FPGA’s Block RAM saves the fingerprint data as .mem files after conversion into binary bitstreams. This allows for on-chip authentication and the fingerprint data matching occurs inside the hardware. The design is entirely coded in Verilog HDL. The operations of fingerprint matching, storing of votes and displaying of results are carried out by using various modules. After the design synthesis on the practical device only authenticated user can vote. No duplicate entries are allowed thus all the data is saved perfectly. The voter registration procedure was confirmed to have correct response and stable output at the display unit. As a result, the design has proven to function reliably as a tamper resistant voting machine. Moreover, the findings reveal the implementation of FPGAs.

title: Molecular Simulation Study on the Influence of Different Pyrolysis Products in Oil-Paper Insulation Systems on Insulation Failure Risk
authors: Yifan Zhong, Qiulin Chen, Ran Zhuo, Sicheng Zhao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: In oil-immersed transformers, the thermal stability of oil-paper insulation systems directly impacts the equipment's insulation lifespan and operational safety. When system temperatures rise, oil-paper materials undergo pyrolysis, generating gases such as water vapor and hydrogen. These microscopic molecular behaviors determine the risk of localized electric field distortion and bubble discharge. This study employs molecular dynamics simulations to construct mineral oil models incorporating water molecules and hydrogen molecules. It systematically analyzes the spatial distribution, aggregation characteristics, and migration dynamics of both molecules under temperature-rising conditions. Results indicate that as temperature increases, water molecules readily form clusters through hydrogen bonding interactions. This reduces dielectric homogeneity within the oil, heightening the likelihood of bubble nucleation and partial discharge. Conversely, hydrogen molecules remain dispersed and diffusive, exerting a weaker disruptive effect on insulation uniformity. This study reveals the distinct microscopic behaviors of different pyrolysis gases within transformer insulating oil, providing theoretical foundations for understanding thermal failure mechanisms in oil-paper insulation systems and improving thermal stability designs.

title: Bio-Inspired Hexacell Oval Patch Sensor for Non-Invasive Blood Glucose Sensing
authors: Wansong Gao, Wei Chen, Abhishek Kandwal, Pawan Kumar, Qifeng Lu, Yizhe Ding
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)
abstract: This work proposes an innovative sensor based on bioinspired hexacell structure mimicking certain vascular networks for non-invasive blood glucose sensing and monitoring. The design has been verified using full wave EM 3D simulations and numerically to analyze sensitivity features. The sensor has been tested by varying dielectric constants from 2.2 to 11.9 in order to analyze the shifts in frequencies that can provide sensitivity. Variations have also been introduced over the same material with glucose overlays that can further support the validations for sensitivity factor. The optimizations resulted in the fact that for the proposed sensor design, Rogers RT-duroid 5880 is the most effective dielectric material for the current work. The patch micro-cavities strengthen near-field coupling and achieve a 50Ω impedance match for this dielectric in the operating frequencies with and without glucose. Radiation efficiency stays above 82% from 9-11 GHz, and the radiation pattern shows 10.2 dBi gain, narrow beamwidth and low side-lobes. After glucose loading, the resonance shifts markedly, exhibiting the high sensitivity. Near-field hotspots are localised at patch edges and the feed gap, creating a steep gradient that can detect even minute glucose variations. The sensor offers high sensitivity, high efficiency and biosafety, providing a viable electromagnetic solution for non-invasive, real-time glucose monitoring.

title: Strict One-Shot Prototypical Networks with CosFace Pretraining for Strata Identification in Shield Tunneling
authors: Kuanfeng Fu, Xiaoming Shao, Chao Zheng, Jinjin Zhang, Tianming Zhan
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Accurate real-time identification of geological strata is critical for safe and efficient shield tunneling, yet labeled machine-operation data are typically scarce, imbalanced, and noisy. We propose a strict one-shot metric-learning framework that couples CosFace-supervised pretraining with a deployment-aligned prototypical inference pipeline. In Stage A, a lightweight multilayer perceptron (MLP) encoder is trained on the train split using a large-margin cosine classifier to carve a discriminative embedding space. In Stage B, we enforce strict one-shot: for each stratum, a single support sample selected as the medoid in the learned embedding space is fixed and reused throughout episodic fine-tuning, validation, and testing. Episodes employ class-balanced query sampling with per-epoch usage caps and replacement fallback to mitigate degeneracy under label scarcity. In inference, we compare queries only to the fixed train supports; a scalar cosine temperature is calibrated on the validation split to sharpen decision boundaries without leaking information. The entire pipeline operates on a single table with a fixed 70/10/20 split and Min–Max normalization fit on training data only, making the protocol simple and reproducible. In a metro shield data set, the proposed method achieves 90.79% accuracy and 90.05% macro-F1 on the held-out test split, improving over full-supervised MLP and ProtoNet baselines by roughly 2–3 percentage points while retaining a strict one-shot constraint and robust minority-class behavior.

title: Investigation of Three-Phase Tertiary Winding for Integrated Self-Excitation in Dump Trucks
authors: Mohammad Sarvi-Maraghi, Omid Khanzadeh, Abolfazl Vahedi, Mahdi Abolghasemi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Transportation costs of mineral products have a significant impact on their final price; therefore, the reliability of the diesel-electric propulsion systems of mining dump trucks is of special importance, whose most critical subsystem is the alternator excitation system. Given the space and weight constraints in these vehicles, integrated excitation systems are of considerable interest. In this paper, the windings employed in such systems on a 3.8kVA alternator have been studied, and to overcome the limitations of previous structures, a three-phase tertiary winding has been proposed. Two-dimensional FEM results show that, without increasing the machine volume and with a slot fill factor of 0.4, the required excitation power can be supplied by this winding. Moreover, the output-voltage THD equals 2.44%, indicating that the alternator’s output-voltage quality is preserved in the presence of this winding. These results indicate that an integrated self-excitation system with a three-phase tertiary winding is a compact and industrial solution for mining dump trucks.

title: Zero-Shot Material Transfer Methods for Complex Geometries: DepthAnythingV2 and Dual-Module Enhancement
authors: Bo Niu, Liang Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address issues such as depth discontinuities and texture misalignment that frequently occur in complex geometric scenes within the Zero-shot Material Transfer (ZeST) framework, we propose an improved method for zero-shot material transfer. First, we replace the Dense Prediction Transformer (DPT) depth estimator in the original framework with DepthAnythingV2 to obtain more stable geometric priors, significantly reducing depth discontinuities in irregular objects and cross-category scenes. Building upon this foundation, we design a Depth Feature Alignment Module (DFAM) to mitigate discrepancies between depth geometry and visual features in the semantic space, achieving coordinated fusion of geometry and texture through cross-attention mechanisms. Concurrently, we introduce a Depth Refinement Module (DRM) that enhances depth edge continuity and accuracy by leveraging local structure encoding and detail optimization. Embedding these modules into a ControlNet-guided diffusion-based image inpainting workflow enables precise mapping of object materials. Experimental results demonstrate that our method outperforms the ZeST framework in material consistency and structural fidelity across multiple complex object categories. It also achieves comprehensive improvements in quantitative metrics such as Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), Learned Perceptual Image Patch Similarity (LPIPS), and Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (CLIP). - Image Pre-training (CLIP) models. Requiring no additional training beyond fine-tuning lightweight modules, this method demonstrates strong practicality for tasks like digital asset generation and virtual scene material reconstruction.

title: End-Side License Plate Detection Model Based on Improved YOLOv8s with Depth-Separable CNNs
authors: Junhong Li, Chengxin Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Aiming at the limitations of existing license plate detection algorithms in terms of detection accuracy and model lightweighting, as well as the insufficient extraction of color features, this paper proposes an end-side license plate detection model based on lightweight YOLOv8s with depth-separable convolutional neural network (CNN). In the license plate region detection stage, the number of output channels of the convolutional layer in the YOLOv8s backbone network and detection head are halved by combining migration learning and parameter fine-tuning strategies to optimize its feature extraction capability. In the license plate information parsing stage, a multi-task framework is used to extract robust features through a depth-separable convolutional backbone network, which are input into the character recognition sub-network and the color classification sub-network, respectively. The character recognition sub-network generates the logarithmic probability distribution of the character sequence through pooling, convolution and Logsoftmax, and decodes it in combination with the CTC loss function to recognize the license plate text. The color classification sub-network generates feature vectors by convolution, batch normalization, ReLU activation and global average pooling to discriminate license plate color. The experimental results show that, relative to YOLOv8n, the computation of the model proposed in this paper increases by only 0.03 GFLOPs, while the average mAP@0.5 rises by 4.3% to 79.2%, and the average mAP@[0.5:0.9] increases by 4.7% to 76.6%.

title: Research on anomaly detection and failure prediction model of lithium iron phosphate battery cluster based on consistency feature extraction
authors: Shuo Zhang, Wenbin Sun, Shikai Ye, Wenxian Lin
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Aiming at the application of lithium iron phosphate battery (LFP) in energy storage system, a battery cluster anomaly detection and failure prediction model (CFE-ADFP) based on consistency feature extraction is proposed. In view of the fact that the inconsistency of individual performance in the battery cluster may lead to the efficiency decline and safety accidents of the energy storage system, this study aims to ensure the safe operation of the energy storage system and reduce the life cycle cost through real-time anomaly detection and failure prediction. In this paper, the basic principle of phase consistency detection algorithm is adopted, and a multi-dimensional feature fusion and task coupling learning model is constructed by combining accurate estimation of state of charge (SOC) and data-driven health state evaluation method. Through the consistency index system of dynamic weight time domain characteristics and graph structure characteristics, the model enhances the quantitative ability of early anomaly identification and degradation synchronization between monomers. At the same time, a dual-branch multi-task learning framework is designed, which uses 1D-CNN and attention mechanism to capture the abnormal patterns of time series respectively, and models the capacity degradation trajectory based on Wiener process. The experimental results show that the CFE-ADFP model is superior to the existing methods in anomaly detection and failure prediction, which provides an effective technical means for the health management of lithium iron phosphate batteries.

title:
Distributivity and conditional distributivity of uninorm over a noncontinuous t-norm T
                    <sub>A</sub>
                    with applications in ensemble classifiers
authors: Liteng Zheng, Gang Li
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: This paper investigates the (conditional) distributivity of uninorms over a noncontinuous t-norm, denoted as T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</inf>. Extending prior work on continuous t-norms, we demonstrate that these two properties are not equivalent in this case. This theoretical finding deepens our understanding of uninorm behavior and enriches fuzzy algebraic theory. Furthermore, it may provide a novel pathway for constructing highly interpretable and robust ensemble classifiers, as the principles of operator distributivity can inform the design of optimized aggregation rules to reconcile conflicts among multiple classification outcomes.

title: From Data Freshness to Model Updates: An AoI-Driven Client Scheduling Approach
authors: Qiran Chen, Wen Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: In real-time federated learning (FL), local training does not necessarily start after all client information has been fully collected. In Internet of Things (IoT) networks, the rates at which devices generate or upload data are inherently heterogeneous, leading to asynchronous and uneven information arrivals. We propose a client scheduling framework aware of both age of information (AoI) and data usage that jointly captures the staleness of client models and their buffered data utilization. Building on this metric, we design two scheduling strategies—one emphasizing efficiency and the other emphasizing long-term balance—to guide client selection under communication resource constraints. Extensive simulations demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly improves the designed score compared to baseline scheduling strategies, making it suitable for real-time and resource-constrained IoT FL scenarios.

title: Adaptive Risk-Aware Ride Assignment (ARARA) Algorithm to Improve Efficiency to Lower Cancellation Rates in Bengaluru
authors: Sheetal, A Alli, S Deepa, Jitha Janardhanan, N Vasantha Kumari, F Rosita Kamala
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Ride cancellations on urban mobility platforms like Rapido, OLA, Uber and other service provide platforms are negatively impacting user experience, driver earnings, and platform efficiency due to high cancellation of rides. This study addresses the challenge by developing a machine learning based adaptive userride matching algorithm that is trained on real world ride dataset from Bengaluru. The dataset includes features such as ride time, source, destination, distance, fare, payment method, and ride status. Through data preprocessing and feature engineering, key patterns influencing ride cancellations are identified. A classification model is developed to predict the likelihood of cancellation before ride assignment by using few Machine learning models among various model XGBoost and Logistic Regression outperformed with nearly $\mathbf{9 0 \%}$ accuracy. Later to enhance the performance in allocation based on cancellation prediction the ARARA algorithm suggests that reallocates rides dynamically based on cancellation risk using inference and assignment Algorithm. Experimental results shows that how to reduce cancellation rates and improved accuracy by choosing best allocation based on top three best captains for allocation to optimize chances of cancellation. This framework can be integrated by ride platforms to enhance service reliability and optimize fleet efficiency.

title: Adaptive Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Networks for Real-Time Bitcoin Prediction and Automated Trading
authors: Vignesh M, Uday Akash E M, A.Nithya
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: In this paper, we develop an end-to-end traffic- adaptive deep learning system capable of predicting Bitcoin prices in real-time and executing automated trading based on the policies of extremely volatile cryptocurrency markets. The suggested system combines several new extended neural paradigms in order to solve the issues of temporal dynamics, relations among assets, multidimensional feature representation and low-latency functionality. Particularly, Adaptive Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Networks evolving correlations between Bitcoin and other related financial indicators and Hypercomplex Neural Networks that utilize quaternion representations of both OHLCV features (i.e. gain access to geometric dependencies between them). The Neural Ordinary Differential Equations as well as Continuous-time market dynamics, are developed to be sensitive to change of rotation and abnormal timing characteristics. A Dual-Path Spiking Neural Network additionally guarantees energy-efficient inference with low latency that can be deployed to real-time. The predictive forecasting is connected to an automated executing engine over the Zerodha Kite API so that forecasts can be translated into actual trading directly. Experimental analysis of historical Bitcoin data shows that it can better predict future price movements, has shorter inference latency, or better trading performance than standard statistical models and deep learning baseline models. The findings indicate the strength, extendability, and feasibility of the suggested scheme of intelligent cryptocurrency trading in dynamic market settings.

title: Computer Vision Technologies for Image Object Classification
authors: Karimov Sardor Ilkhom Ugli, Abduqodirova Mohizoda Ilxomidin Qizi, Abdukadirova Gulbakhor Xomidjon Qizi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: this paper presents modern approaches to object analysis and image classification using deep learning architectures VGG19, U-Net, and MobileNet. The features of each model are examined: high accuracy of feature extraction in VGG19, efficiency of image segmentation in U-Net, as well as compactness and computational resource optimization in MobileNet. A comparative analysis of model performance on open datasets was conducted, including Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-score metrics. The results showed that the combined application of these architectures allows achieving a balance between classification accuracy, processing speed, and computational efficiency. The study demonstrates the prospects of using hybrid solutions for tasks in medicine, agriculture, video surveillance systems, and intelligent environmental monitoring.

title: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Path Planning Based on the MCSD-GWO Algorithm
authors: Sifan Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: In response to the unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) path planning problem in mountainous battlefield environments, this paper proposes a novel algorithm called Multi-objective Clustering and Step-Driven Grey Wolf Optimizer (MCSD-GWO). By incorporating the idea of dynamic multi-objective clustering initialization, the algorithm enhances population diversity. Additionally, a step-driven dynamic weight factor is introduced to balance the algorithm’s global exploration and local exploitation capabilities, thereby avoiding premature convergence into local optima and improving convergence speed. The simulation results validate that the proposed algorithm is characterized by superior optimization performance, rapid convergence, and high stability.

title: Machine Learning-Based Enhanced Telematics Data Simulation for Predictive Maintenance in E-Mobility Fleet Operations
authors: Akash Ambulkar, Sammit J. Deshpande
abstract: Predictive maintenance is a cornerstone for reliable e-mobility fleet management, yet acquiring sufficient failure-mode data in real fleets is both expensive and time-consuming. This paper introduces a machine learning-enhanced simulation pipeline to generate synthetic telematics datasets that mimic real-world electric vehicle operational patterns and component degradation. A generative adversarial network (GAN) is trained on limited real-world battery, motor, and sensor data from telematics units to produce diverse, physically plausible trajectories of state-of-health indicators. The simulated data populates a digital twin of an EV fleet, upon which gradient boosting and recurrent neural network models are evaluated for anomaly detection and remaining useful life (RUL) prediction. In comparison to purely heuristic simulations, our approach improves predictive maintenance scheduling accuracy by 28 % and reduces false-positive warnings by 40 %. Results indicate that ML-driven synthetic data generation can accelerate the development of reliable maintenance algorithms, ultimately enhancing fleet uptime and reducing operational costs.

title: Research on Energy Efficiency Modeling and Control Algorithm of 220kV Fully Enclosed Isolation Switch for Green and Low Carbon Development
authors: Yuandong Wang, Yang Cao, Guoyan Zhang, Haiming Li, Heguang Song
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In order to respond to the green and low-carbon development strategy and promote the intelligent operation and management of high-voltage equipment, this paper focuses on the energy efficiency optimization of 220kV fully enclosed disconnector, and studies the modeling and control algorithm. Aiming at the problems of poor adaptability and large fluctuation of energy consumption of traditional control strategy under complex working conditions, an intelligent control method combining equipment characteristics and deep Q network (DQN) is proposed. Firstly, based on the electrical characteristics and operation mechanism, the energy efficiency model of hybrid drive is constructed. Then, a DKN control strategy suitable for the operating characteristics of high-voltage equipment is designed to realize the dynamic adjustment of switching action and the improvement of energy efficiency. The experimental results show that under the same operating conditions, the average single operation energy consumption of disconnector is reduced by about 8.6%, the fluctuation range of contact resistance is obviously narrowed, and the stability of response time is also improved after adopting DKN control strategy. The prediction error of the model is controlled within 5%, which has good engineering application potential. Introducing Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) into the control of high-voltage switchgear can effectively improve operating efficiency and reduce carbon emissions, and provide a feasible technical path for the green transformation of power system.

title: A Hierarchical Approach to Modeling and Controlling Complex Systems
authors: Oksana Porubay, Isamiddin Siddikov, Nilufar Abdulhamidova, Ozodbek Rakhmonov
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: The article discusses the task of constructing effective models for automated control of large complex systems, in particular, autonomous systems (AS). The main focus is on the problem of the impossibility of direct use of functional transformations due to the high dimensionality and uncertainty of the processes described. The paper proposes a hierarchical approach to modeling based on dividing the general ASIS model into a macro model of the process and a micro model of the system. The macro model describes the relationships between goals and operations at the level of the global trajectory of the system’s functioning, while the micro model reflects the interaction of individual modules and elements that implement these goals. Oriented multigraphs of goals and modular networks are introduced as elementary model structures, which allow for automatic model synthesis, take into account the factor of human participation in the control loop, and ensure adaptation to changing external conditions. The proposed approach provides the possibility of a multi-level approximation to optimal control actions, simplifies the solution of optimization problems, and opens up prospects for application in artificial intelligence and intelligent control systems.

title: MS-DeepLab: A Lightweight Semantic Segmentation Model for Autonomous Driving
authors: Yong Ai, Shengfu Mei, Shasha Tian, Jin Xie
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Semantic segmentation is a core technology for pixel-level object recognition in autonomous driving environmental perception, and its performance directly impacts the decision reliability and operational safety of autonomous driving systems. Most mainstream high-precision semantic segmentation models face issues of excessive parameters and low inference speeds, which prevent them from meeting the dual requirements of "lightweight deployment" and "high-precision segmentation" in autonomous driving scenarios. To tackle this challenge, this paper proposes a lightweight improved model based on DeepLabv3+, named MS-DeepLab. Specifically, we adopt MobileNetv2 to replace the original feature extraction backbone of DeepLabv3+ for lightweight compression. We also design a multidimensional shallow feature extraction mechanism and introduce a Multi-scale Convolutional Attention Module (MSCAM) to enhance feature representation capability. Finally, we construct a hybrid loss function by combining CE_Loss and Focal_Loss to mitigate the imbalance between positive and negative samples, thereby further improving segmentation accuracy. Experimental results on the Cityscapes dataset demonstrate that MS-DeepLab has only 3.14MB parameters while achieving a segmentation accuracy of 72.98% mIoU and an inference speed of 58.09 FPS. Compared with the original DeepLabv3+, the proposed model maintains a relatively high level of accuracy, while delivering an 86.90% parameter compression and an impressive 146.14% increase in inference speed. These improvements render it more compatible with the real-time deployment requirements of autonomous driving scenarios.

title: DORA: Dynamic O-RAN Resource Allocation for Multi-Slice 5G Networks
authors: Alireza Ebrahimi Dorcheh, Tolunay Seyfi, Fatemeh Afghah
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: The fifth generation (5G) of wireless networks must simultaneously support heterogeneous service categories, including Ultra-Reliable Low-Latency Communications (URLLC), enhanced Mobile Broadband (eMBB), and massive Machine-Type Communications (mMTC), each with distinct Quality of Service (QoS) requirements. Meeting these demands under limited spectrum resources requires adaptive and standards-compliant radio resource management. We present DORA (Dynamic O-RAN Resource Allocation), a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) framework for dynamic slice-level Physical Resource Block (PRB) allocation in Open RAN. DORA employs a PPO-based RL agent to allocate PRBs across URLLC, eMBB, and mMTC slices based on observed traffic demands and channel conditions. Intra-slice PRB scheduling is handled deterministically via round-robin among active UEs, simplifying control complexity and improving training stability. Unlike prior work, DORA supports online training and adapts continuously to evolving traffic patterns and cross-slice contention. Implemented in the standards-compliant OpenAirInterface (OAI) RAN stack and designed for deployment as an O-RAN xApp, DORA integrates seamlessly with RAN Intelligent Controllers (RICs). Extensive evaluation under congested regimes shows that DORA outperforms three non-learning baselines and a DQN agent, achieving lower URLLC latency, higher eMBB throughput with fewer SLA violations, and broader mMTC coverage without starving high-priority slices. To our knowledge, this is the first fully online DRL framework for adaptive, slice-aware PRB allocation in O-RAN.

title: A Network Vulnerability Classification Method Based on CVSS
authors: Yizhou Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: In the field of information security, vulnerability assessment, classification, and management constitute core tasks for cybersecurity. Traditional vulnerability scoring and classification methods rely on manual analysis and expert review. This paper proposes an automated vulnerability assessment methodology based on large natural language processing models, which leverages deep learning techniques to achieve intelligent evaluation of the Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS). Compared to traditional expert–driven evaluation approaches, this method efficiently parses massive security text data through natural language processing. Specifically, the research innovatively integrates stemming and lemmatization techniques to significantly enhance the model's semantic comprehension of vulnerability description features. Experimental results demonstrate the model's superior assessment performance in cross–dataset tests, validating the effectiveness of deep learning-based automated vulnerability assessment approaches. Experimental results indicate that this model achieves a maximum F1 Score of 96.2% on vulnerability datasets, best reducing error by 12.94% compared to the traditional classification model.

title: Research on RAG Enhanced End-to-end Intelligent Reasoning Algorithm for Real-time Decision-making
authors: Zhenming Deng, Wen Xiong, Haichuan Zhang, Fei Chen, Wenrui Chen, Xing Wang, Lu Yan, Lei Lu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In this paper, an enhanced end-to-end intelligent reasoning algorithm based on RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) for real-time decision-making is proposed to meet the high requirements of AI system in real-time decision-making scenarios. Through the framework of dynamic knowledge fusion, the algorithm realizes the deep fusion of retrieval and generation by using multi-modal attention gating mechanism, dynamically adjusts the intensity of external knowledge injection, ensures the priority adoption of new knowledge, and automatically attenuates old knowledge. At the same time, the iterative reasoning mechanism based on finite state automata is designed, and the state transition is handled by LSTM model to realize multi-hop logical reasoning, and the reward shaping mechanism is introduced to optimize the reasoning process. In addition, the dual-objective loss function is used to realize the deep collaboration of retrieval, reasoning and generation modules, and knowledge distillation technology is introduced to improve the deployment feasibility and response efficiency of the system in real-time decision-making scenarios. The experimental results show that the algorithm is significantly superior to the traditional RAG method in several key indicators, with the response time reduced to 1200±50ms, the HotpotQA F1 value increased to 73.9%, the accuracy of financial risk control reached 93.6%, and the accuracy of emergency dispatch reached 89.8%, which greatly improved the accuracy of multi-hop reasoning and complex decision-making while ensuring real-time. The case study further verifies its advantages in financial risk control and emergency dispatch scenarios, and shows stronger dynamic decision-making ability.

title: Research on Hidden Danger Prediction of Power Transformer Based on TabTransformer
authors: Xi Song
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: The safe operation of power transformer is very important to the stability of power system. DGA (Dissolved Gas Analysis) is an important means to detect its hidden dangers, but the traditional methods have some limitations, such as single feature extraction, rigid threshold and low diagnostic accuracy. Therefore, this paper proposes a transformer hidden danger prediction model (TabTransformer-DGA) based on TAB Transformer. The model combines eight kinds of gas concentrations, three operating parameters and two auxiliary features to construct a multi-dimensional tabular data set, which breaks through the limitation of traditional input. The complex nonlinear relationship between features is dynamically captured by TabTransformer's self-attention mechanism, and the expression ability of diverse feature interaction patterns is enhanced by multi-head attention mechanism. Design a visualization method of multi-head attention weight, calculate the comprehensive importance score of each feature and visualize it as a heat map to improve the interpretability of the model. The experiment is based on 35,240 DGA monitoring records of a provincial company of State Grid. The results show that TabTransformer-DGA is superior to IEC 60599 three-ratio method and XGBoost method in accuracy (93.6%), macro F1 value (0.901) and composite fault recall rate (91.8%), especially in composite fault diagnosis. The model can realize high-precision and interpretable transformer hidden danger prediction, and provide support for improving the intelligent level of transformer operation and maintenance.

title: Domain-Aware Federated Unlearning With Adaptive Multi-Resolution Models for Resource-Constrained IoT Networks
authors: Hai Anh Tran, Abdelhamid Mellouk
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: The proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in distributed environments introduces new challenges for intelligent, privacy-preserving, and scalable learning systems. In such resource-constrained and heterogeneous networks, ensuring efficient collaboration across devices while respecting user privacy-especially when data must be revoked-remains an open problem. We propose FedAMP-FU, a hierarchical federated learning framework that integrates two key innovations: (i) Adaptive Multi-Resolution Models (AMRM) for efficient model scaling across devices with diverse capabilities, and (ii) Partial Forgetting via Attention Masking (PFAM), a lightweight unlearning mechanism that removes user contributions without retraining. Domain-level aggregators manage resource-aware updates and execute unlearning when needed. Experiments on real-world IoT datasets show that FedAMP-FU reduces communication overhead by up to 45 % and achieves unlearning in just 15 seconds while maintaining over 88 % accuracy and low inference latency. These results demonstrate its promise for efficient, privacy-aware learning in next-generation IoT systems.

title: An Adaptive Dual-domain Feature Alignment Network for All-Day City Image Analysis
authors: Ying Qian, Huayi Li, Pan Wang, Zihao Fu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: The vision of smart cities relies on robust, all-day automated image analysis systems to enhance urban management and public security. However, severe illumination variations between day and night present a significant challenge for tasks such as vehicle re-identification, where the same vehicle can appear drastically different due to lighting changes and nighttime glare. This impedes the reliability of continuous urban image analytics. To address this issue, we propose an Adaptive Dual-domain Feature Alignment Network (ADFA-Net). Our approach aligns vehicle features across time domains by learning illumination-invariant representations. ADFA-Net employs a dual-stream architecture with dedicated branches for processing day and night images. Each branch incorporates a glare suppression module to enhance feature quality. Crucially, features from both streams are adaptively fused at multiple semantic levels to construct a coherent and aligned feature space. Extensive evaluations on the cross-day-night benchmarks DN-348 and DN-Wild demonstrate the effectiveness of our model. On DN-348, ADFA-Net achieves 48.7% mAP and 72.2% Rank-1 accuracy. On the more challenging DN-Wild dataset, it attains 41.4% mAP and 51.0% Rank-1 accuracy.

title: Task-Centric Model Transfer for Federated Learning Over the Edge–Cloud Continuum
authors: Swapnil Sadashiv Shinde, Carlo Fischione
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Edge-based, task-centric Federated Learning (FL) enabled by Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) and the Internet of Things (IoT) is essential to meeting the intelligent service demands of 6 G networks. However, training task-specific FL models on resource-constrained devices remains a major challenge. This paper proposes a task-centric FL framework that leverages model transfer across distributed edge-cloud servers to enhance training efficiency and reduce overall latency. We model the end-to-end latency of FL, including model discovery, data routing, and training, and formulate a constrained optimization problem to minimize total latency through selective transfer of pre-trained FL models. Furthermore, we develop a maximum search budget-based solution that balances model search and transfer overhead with training performance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly reduces FL latency compared to traditional solutions, providing a practical foundation for efficient FL over the edge-cloud continuum.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)

title: Research on a Rapid Gemstone Authenticity Identification Method Based on Hyperspectral Imaging and Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Jiarui Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: To address the problems of traditional identification of olivine authenticity relying on experience, being time-consuming and having limited accuracy, this paper proposes a rapid identification method based on hyperspectral imaging and convolutional neural network (CNN). Firstly, a hyperspectral dataset containing 500 natural olivine samples, 500 synthetic olivine samples, and 300 natural and 300 dyed olivine samples was constructed. Principal component analysis (PCA) was used for preprocessing to reduce the data dimension and suppress noise. Then, a lightweight CNN model was designed to achieve the collaborative extraction of spectral and spatial features and authenticity classification. Experimental results show that the identification accuracy of natural and synthetic olivine is 98.9%, and the identification accuracy of natural and dyed olivine is 96.8%. The single-sample identification time is only 0.45 seconds. Compared with the traditional hyperspectral + SVM method, the accuracy is improved by 6.9%, the time consumption is reduced by 60.3%, and the model parameter quantity is reduced by 40% compared to ResNet18. The inference speed is increased by 32%, and the accuracy fluctuation is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 2.1 {\%}$</tex> under illumination disturbance and slight contamination scenarios. The research provides a high-precision and high-efficiency technical solution for the intelligent identification of olivine.

title: Research on an Ecological Benefit Calculation Model for Mine Restoration Based on Deep Learning and DEA
authors: Cen Wang, Zhiyu Liu, Xue Li, Linlin Yang, Shichang Wang, Jiwen Ge, Xinwen Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Traditional evaluations of mine restoration benefits often rely on qualitative methods, which makes it difficult to achieve quantitative and dynamic comparisons across projects. To address this limitation, this paper proposes an ecological benefit calculation model for mine restoration that integrates deep learning with Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA). First, deep learning techniques are employed to automatically extract and predict ecological indicators from multi-source data such as remote sensing imagery, soil monitoring records, and biodiversity surveys. Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are applied to obtain refined vegetation coverage indices, while recurrent neural networks (RNNs) are used to capture temporal variations in soil physicochemical properties and ecosystem service values. These outputs, together with traditional inputs (funding, labor, land reclamation area, and technical equipment), form a high-dimensional input-output dataset. Next, CCR and BCC DEA models are introduced to compute the relative efficiency of each restoration unit, identify input redundancies and output shortfalls, and provide targeted improvement directions. An empirical study of the Hashotan open-pit coal mine restoration project in Inner Mongolia demonstrates that the deep learning-enhanced DEA model achieves more accurate and discriminative efficiency evaluations compared to traditional DEA, while effectively handling data gaps and uncertainties. The results highlight that deep learning significantly improves the quality of ecological indicators, thereby strengthening DEA's ability to differentiate restoration performance. Finally, sensitivity and robustness analyses confirm the model's stability. This study offers a novel methodological framework that combines artificial intelligence with efficiency analysis, providing scientific evidence and practical references for refined mine restoration management and ecological decision-making.

title: Stock Price Movement of Reliance using ML Models
authors: Diksha, Simrandeep Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: In the ever evolving world of stock market it is important to discuss the price forecasting, challenges impact and trend of stock price. This study delves the application of machine learning(ML) as a tool or technique to predict the short term movement of the stock price by using the technical indicators and historical data. Here, we choose Reliance data, which is one of the actively traded stocks by using ML models. We have used supervised learning techniques, i.e. Logistic Regression, Random Forest and XGBoost to asses the predictive performance of the Market. Among all the three modals Logistic Regression archives the highest accuracy of 52.77%. The purchasing and selling of stock can be recommended using ML driven prediction models to achieve high precision.

title: Research on 5G-Enabled Immersive Remote Control Drone Method
authors: Lingjun Xu, Jing Li, Tao Lv, Hongfa Li, Peng Gu, Xiaoyan Jiang, Qingyu Cai, Wenhui Ma
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: With the rapid development of the low-altitude economy expanding drone applications across sectors, existing remote-control solutions face critical limitations in visual environmental awareness, interconnected control capacity, and operational efficiency. This paper proposes a pioneering 5G panoramic remote-control drone system integrating 5G connectivity, VR immersion, AI analytics, and cloud-edge collaboration technologies, where the synergistic deployment of 8K ultra-HD VR video streaming, weak-network-resistant algorithms, 5G dual-SIM redundant links, intelligent gimbal coordination, and AI video recognition achieves comprehensive immersive perception, real-time flight control, and efficient autonomous operations. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system achieves sub- 300 ms end-to-end latency for 4K@60fps VR video transmission. Under challenging network conditions including 50% packet loss in 5G networks or persistent 4G connectivity, compressed VR streams maintain uninterrupted playback, ensuring seamless panoramic remote-control operability. Implementation in substation remote inspection scenarios yields significant improvements: fault repair time reduced from 3.5 to 2.1 days, per-expert troubleshooting capacity increased by 59%, and travel expenditures decreased by 46%. This solution establishes a novel paradigm for drone teleoperation in complex environments, pioneering 5G-immersive remote control while advancing development in the low-altitude economy sector.

title: A Sim-to-Real Few-Shot Object Detection Framework for Multi-Class Industrial Part Grasping
authors: Beibei Li, Yating He, Liang Qu, Miao Jing
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: To address the critical challenges of costly data acquisition and limited adaptability for vision-guided robots in multi-class manufacturing, this paper proposes an efficient Sim-to-Real few-shot object detection framework. This approach enables accurate detection in real-world environments using only a small amount of synthetic data. We introduce an enhanced model, YOLO-DC, which innovatively integrates a DynamicATSS label assignment strategy with a CBAM attention mechanism. This synergistic design creates a positive feedback loop, effectively enhancing feature extraction and generalization to bridge the “reality gap” under data-constrained conditions. Extensive experiments on our self-constructed SimGrasp-IP5 dataset validate our approach. Results show that YOLO-DC achieves a state-of-the-art mAP@.5:.95 of 0.799, demonstrating remarkable superiority over established models like YOLOv5n and YOLOv7-tiny (with performance gains of 17% and 70%, respectively), in addition to outperforming its YOLOv8n baseline. These findings confirm the proposed framework's potential as a low-cost, efficient, and scalable solution for industrial robotic vision systems.

title: Keynote Speakers
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)

title: Federated Multimodal Deep Learning Framework with Adaptive Feature Selection for Early Diagnosis and Personalized Treatment of Cardiac Diseases
authors: G Mahender, Kabita Thaoroijam
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Early identification is key for efficient treatment and management of heart disease, which remains to be a running cause of mortality globally. Unfortunately, the clinical use of present diagnostic methods is limited by their often-low accuracy, which is a result of the misintegration of diverse health data. Federated learning (FL) provides a structure for building local models across organizations while protecting sensitive information. This study presents a new method for predicting the occurrence of cardiovascular illness by utilizing the advanced deep learning (DL) model, which integrates the advantages of both deep-neural-networks and tree-based models. We use above architecture to improve data processing in federated systems and use the UCI-Heart-Disease datasets in our study. The federated averaging approach was used to securely aggregate model modernizes among contestants in horizontal federated learning. With the use of smart contracts, blockchain technology was able to automate governance while simultaneously increasing transparency and accountability. After 50 epochs, The proposed approach successfully balanced privacy and performance, according to the experimental data, with a score of $\mathbf{1. 5 9 4}$ for balanced metrics, an accuracy(Acc) of 0.932, and an epsilon value of 6.755. A strong demonstration of accuracy was achieved by the model after only 10 iterations on aggregated data, demonstrating the advantages of integrating data from several sources. By integrating deep learning approach with blockchain, this study offers a scalable, privacy-preserving method for anticipating cardiac problems, which helps to solve important problems in healthcare while also guaranteeing the integrity of data.

title: The Research of Online Screening Method for Steady-State Operating Condition Data in Power Units
authors: Li Yuanyuan, Wang Xinshuo, Zhang Shaojie, Zhao Zhenning, Li Jinjing, Dong Zhaomin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: During the operation of thermal power units, a vast amount of operational data can be collected. However, due to the inherent dynamic characteristics and strong coupling effects among parameters-where variables interact with each other while exhibiting time delays-only steady-state data are considered valid for analysis. To efficiently identify and extract meaningful steady-state data, accurately assess unit performance, and support energy efficiency evaluation and optimization initiatives, this study proposes a universal online data screening method. The approach evaluates unit operating conditions by analyzing the coefficient of variation (CV) of main steam flow and pressure, along with the relative deviation of main steam temperature. This enables rapid detection of steadystate operating periods and corresponding datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively identifies operational segments with load fluctuation coefficients consistently below 0.5%, proving its reliability. This methodology significantly reduces manual workload for plant operators and provides critical technical support for energy efficiency improvement programs.

title: Research on Environmental Temperature Prediction Model Based on Improved Random Forest Algorithm
authors: Delong Li, Ling Li, Yawen Liu, Shirui Tan
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Environmental temperature data are mostly timeseries data, and their changes are influenced by factors such as wind speed, humidity, and sunshine duration. The nonstationary and nonlinear characteristics of these data make it difficult for traditional time-series learning models to capture the deep underlying features within the data. Therefore, an improved random forest temperature prediction model is proposed. The model employs trigonometric function encoding to transform month, date, and day of the week into continuous periodic features. Additionally, it innovatively uses the Hampel filter instead of the simple threshold method to robustly identify and correct outliers. The model is used to fit and predict the daily average temperature. The experiment uses meteorological data from the Doklam meteorological station as an example. The predicted data show a trend that is basically consistent with the actual observations, achieving high-precision prediction. To further illustrate the accuracy and reliability of the random forest model, the prediction results are compared with those of the Seasonal Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (SARIMA) model. The results show that the random forest prediction model has the smallest error and the best regression fitting effect, and it can be applied to the prediction of environmental temperature.

title: Optimization of Resource Scheduling in Digital Avatar Education Systems Using Distributed Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Zhengchen Yu, Cen Gao, Weixing Lu, Shuaichen Wan
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: With the acceleration of digital transformation in education, digital human education systems face resource scheduling challenges under high-concurrency scenarios while providing immersive learning experiences [1]. This paper proposes a dynamic resource scheduling framework based on Distributed Deep Reinforcement Learning (D-DRL) [2]. By constructing a multi-dimensional state space (node load vector + task feature matrix) and a composite action space (resource allocation/task migration/bandwidth control), combined with a Pareto-optimal reward function, intelligent scheduling of GPU core resources is achieved [3]. The system adopts a four-layer microservices architecture, deploys a D-DRL decision engine at the resource scheduling layer, integrates an LSTM prediction model at the load balancing layer, implements digital human interaction agents in the microservices cluster, and uses a MySQL+Redis hybrid architecture at the data storage layer. Experimental results show that under 5000 concurrent users, the system achieves 81.2% GPU utilization and 178ms P95 latency, with SLA compliance reaching 92.7%, which is 26.5% higher than the Kubernetes HPA solution. This research provides an efficient and reliable resource scheduling solution for digital transformation in education while offering technical references for other high-concurrency intelligent service systems.

title: Annual Power Flow Scenario Generation for Hybrid AC/DC Receiving-End Provincial Grids Based on Production Simulation
authors: Yifei Pan, Shuolin Zhang, Yue Yu, Changgang Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: In response to the challenges faced in annual power flow scenario generation for AC/DC hybrid receiving-end grids with high-penetration renewable energy integration— such as the difficulty in quantifying dynamic interactions of cross-regional power transmission, low efficiency in long-term data processing, and insufficient convergence stability of power flow—this paper proposes a simulation method based on hierarchical modeling and dynamic per-unit curve mapping. First, a hierarchical model based on balanced partitions and tie-line snapshots is constructed to achieve coordinated representation of external AC power, DC interconnections, and provincial power sources. Second, a dynamic per-unit curve scaling method for load/renewable energy and a unit output adjustment algorithm are designed to establish an 8,760-hour continuous power flow snapshot generation framework. Finally, a dynamic compensation strategy for external power is proposed to optimize power flow convergence. Using Simulation Toolkit for Electrical Power Systems (STEPS) and Power Energy Balance Software (PEBL), the proposed method is validated on an ultra-high-voltage (UHV) AC/DC hybrid receiving-end provincial grid. The results demonstrate that the proposed method improves the efficiency of power flow scenario generation in AC/DC hybrid receiving-end grids, and the generated power flow snapshot data supports the verification of provincial power grid planning schemes.

title: Water Pollution Source Spatial Localization Algorithm Based on Attention Mechanism
authors: Guangkai Wang, Bairu Qu, Yaodong Lei, Yulin Sun, Zhong Deng, Bo Li, Yang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Inaccurate spatial localization of pollution sources in shallow geothermal energy development in Longkou City can lead to environmental risks. This study proposes an attention-based spatial localization algorithm for water pollution sources. By integrating hydrogeological parameters, development activity characteristics, and historical pollution data, a multi-source heterogeneous dataset is constructed. A framework coupling a multi-head self-attention mechanism with a graph convolutional network (GCN) is then employed to achieve multi-scale feature perception of pollution source diffusion paths. Experimental results demonstrate that the algorithm achieves a maximum localization error of 31.7 meters and a 94.7% confidence level in fault zone regions, significantly improving the accuracy and interpretability of pollution source localization in complex environments.

title: Construction of General Representation Model and Cross-domain Migration Method Based on Multi-modal Comparative Learning
authors: Qianhui Yu, Guihua Lin, Zhe Zhang, Xudong Zhang, Zhiming Zhang, Jintao Cui, Xiaoxi Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: In order to solve the core problems of modal heterogeneity, semantic gap and model performance degradation in multi-modal data applications, this paper proposes a framework of Dynamic Domain-aware Multimodal Contrastive Learning (DD MCL), aiming at constructing a general multi-modal representation model and realizing efficient cross-domain migration. The framework is optimized through three stages: pre-training, self-adaptation and joint optimization. In the pre-training stage, Modality Attention Weight Network (MAWN) is introduced to dynamically fuse modal features, and the modes are initially aligned with standard comparison loss. In the adaptive phase, Domain-Aware Contrastive Loss (DACL) is activated for unlabeled target domain data, balancing the contrastive learning intensity between the source and target domains through dynamic weight coefficients to alleviate distribution differences between domains; In the joint optimization stage, a Memory Replay Mechanism (MRM) is introduced to construct a source domain sample "memory bank" to avoid catastrophic forgetting of the model when adapting to the target domain. At the same time, the weighted sum of source domain contrastive loss, target domain contrastive loss, and memory replay loss is used as the overall loss function to optimize the model. In the experiment, MS-COCO is used as the source domain data set, cartoon illustration data set CIBR and medical image report data set MIMIC-CXR are used as the target domain data set, ViT-B/16 and RoBERTa-base are used as encoders, and Recall@1, Recall@5 and Recall@10 of cross-modal retrieval tasks are used as evaluation indexes. The results show that the Recall@1 index of image-to-text (I2T) and text-to-image (T2I) retrieval tasks of DDMCL on CIBR and MIMIC-CXR reaches 45.7 and 43.9, respectively, which is significantly better than the baseline models such as CLIP and ALBEF. Ablation experiments verify that the core components of MAWN, DACL and MRM contribute significantly to the performance, and the model performance is the best when the domain sensing weight β is in the range of 0.6-0.8. The research shows that DDMCL can effectively realize cross-modal semantic fusion and efficient cross-domain migration without target domain labeling, and improve the generalization ability and application efficiency of the model in complex and data-scarce scenarios.

title: IoT-Enabled Distributed Smart Farming System using Lora
authors: Pushparani M K, Abaychandrasurya J K, Chandrashekhar P C, Chennakeshavashree D R, Pradeep U Naik
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This paper presents an IoT-enabled distributed smart farming system that enhances agricultural productivity through automation, real-time monitoring, and data-driven decision-making. The system integrates multi-depth soil moisture, temperature, humidity, pH, nutrient (NPK), and environmental sensors with LoRa-based long-range communication to provide reliable, low-power connectivity across large farms. A central Arduino Mega acts as the controller, aggregating data from distributed nodes and executing rule-based logic for irrigation, safety, and environmental management. Data is transmitted via GSM to a cloud dashboard, where farmers can monitor field conditions, analyze historical trends, and remotely control actuators. Automated irrigation ensures precise water delivery, reducing wastage while promoting root health. Safety modules monitor electrical parameters and environmental hazards, providing rapid fail-safe responses. Field deployment demonstrated >95% communication reliability over 2 km and up to 45% water savings compared to timer-based irrigation. The system's modular, solar-powered design ensures scalability, sustainability, and adaptability. Future integration of machine learning models, such as LSTM networks, will enable predictive irrigation scheduling and proactive equipment maintenance.

title: A Low-Latency Framework for Channel Estimation and Reconfiguration
authors: Jinyi Hu, Lili Chen, Jing Yang, Wenbin Sun, Haochen Liu, Ling Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) have emerged as a key enabler for future mobile networks by facilitating highly controllable and energy-efficient wireless environments. However, conventional implementations rely on acquiring full Channel State Information (CSI), a process that incurs significant latency and overhead-especially in scenarios involving user mobility. This bottleneck poses a major obstacle to achieving real-time, high-gain beamforming. Motivated by this challenge, we pose a fundamental question: Can we bypass high-dimensional channel estimation by directly sensing low-dimensional user angles to enable real-time beamforming? We provide an affirmative answer by introducing a novel lowlatency framework that transforms the problem from channel estimation to parametric angle sensing. Leveraging a Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE) approach, the framework enables rapid and accurate user angle detection for real-time RIS configuration. To overcome the computational burden typically associated with MLE, we introduce a high-precision algorithm augmented with a coarse-to-fine search strategy, significantly reducing complexity while maintaining estimation accuracy.
