Moreover, we establish a complete sensing-to-communication performance validation loop that quantitatively links angle sensing accuracy to communication quality. Extensive simulations show that our framework achieves communication rates nearly matching the theoretical upper bound under perfect CSI and consistently outperforms conventional methods-particularly in highly dynamic scenarios with high user mobility and low Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR).

title: Research on Credit Card Risk Prediction Using an LSTM-TCN Hybrid Model
authors: Shiyao Dai
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: To tackle the extreme class imbalance and dynamic temporal dependencies inherent in credit card transaction data, this study introduces a hybrid architecture integrating Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. The proposed framework employs TCN to extract localized temporal features and capture long-range dependencies within transaction sequences. It further incorporates the gating mechanisms of LSTM to strengthen the modeling of temporal dynamics, while an attention layer is embedded to adaptively weight critical risk-related features. Evaluations conducted on benchmark credit card fraud datasets demonstrate that the model attains near-optimal performance across evaluation metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, substantially outperforming conventional logistic regression baselines. These results confirm the framework's robust fraud detection capability and strong generalizability in complex financial time-series scenarios.

title: Bnu Student Visual Interactive Behaviour Dataset
authors: Minglin Hong, Bo Sun, Jun He, Li Yuan, Tore Hoel, Yinghui Zhang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: With the rapid progress of computer vision and video-based action recognition, automated classroom behavior analysis has become increasingly important for smart education. Existing research, however, mainly targets individual actions in generic settings and lacks systematic modeling of classroom interactions, which involve multiple participants and strong temporal dependencies. To address this gap, we construct Beijing Normal University Student Visual Interactive Behaviour Dataset, the first real-world classroom video dataset featuring a three-level annotation scheme (behavior-subsequence-interaction sequence). This design captures both macro-teaching contexts and microindividual behaviors, enabling hierarchical analysis of teacherstudent dynamics. The dataset includes 114 interaction sequences, 332 subsequences, and 4,838 annotated behaviors covering nine behavior types and four teaching scenarios. Benchmark experiments on ARG, DECOMPL, and MLP-AIR models reveal substantial performance degradation, confirming the dataset's complexity and value as a new benchmark for classroom interaction recognition and intelligent education research.

title: OBE Learning Effectiveness Prediction Model Based on AI Algorithms in Curriculum System Reform
authors: Donghua Zheng, Lizhu Ye, Lina Dai, Weirong Xiu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: To construct an Attention Hybrid Neural Network (AHNN) integrating static and dynamic data, we employed a three-tiered knowledge-ability-quality indicator system to predict the achievement levels of PI1.1-1.3 for 865 undergraduates in their fourth semester. Inputting 12dimensional static data, 36-dimensional weekly behavioral data, and 10-dimensional course grades, the macro-average accuracy reached 92.7 %, surpassing LSTM by 2.5 percentage points, with a recall rate exceeding 90% for the “early warning” level. SHAP attribution revealed the highest weighting for behavioral inflection points during weeks 6-8, which has been leveraged for real-time intervention strategy recommendations.

title: LSSA-BP-Based Resource Expenditure Prediction Model for Renewable Energy Projects
authors: Beibei Wang, Zhong Xiao, Hongyan Qian, Huaiwu Peng, Yong He, Hangyu Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: Key quantitative metrics (e.g., resource expenditure) of renewable energy projects exhibit significant nonlinearity and uncertainty due to multifaceted influences from policy, technology, and market dynamics. Traditional prediction methods often yield limited accuracy. This study proposes a novel prediction model for key quantitative metrics by integrating a Levy flight-enhanced Sparrow Search Algorithm (LSSA) with a Backpropagation Neural Network (BPNN). The Levy flight strategy strengthens the global optimization capability of SSA, effectively mitigating BPNN's inherent limitations of local optima convergence and slow convergence rate. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed LSSA-BP model achieves significantly higher prediction accuracy for key quantitative metrics in renewable energy projects compared to conventional BPNN and standard SSA-BP models, providing an advanced modeling framework for project planning and construction.

title: Circulating Current Suppression in Modular Multilevel Converters via Vector Resonance Control Approach
authors: Xianao Wang, Cong Liu, Yang Xue, Chen Qi, Jing Zhang, Wenliang Zhao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: The circulating currents present in Modular Multilevel Converter (MMC) adversely affect the waveform quality of the arm currents. A novel Vector Resonance (VR) control approach is developed in this paper to suppress the second-harmonic circulating current in MMC. Firstly, the topology of the MMC is introduced. Secondly, the mathematical expression for circulating currents is derived and a detailed analysis of its components is conducted. Subsequently, the circulating current suppression strategy utilizing the VR controller is elaborated in detail, accompanied by the presentation of the overall MMC control diagram. Finally, the MMC simulation model based on VR controller is established by using MATLAB/Simulink. Before adding the VR controller, the bridge-arm current contains significant even harmonics, with a Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) as high as 19.81%. After adding the circulating current suppressor, harmonic components are significantly reduced, and the THD is lowered to 1.48%.

title: Research on Distributed Regional Protection of Distribution Network Using Multi-Terminal Electrical Quantities and Intelligent Algorithms
authors: Zhang Bin, Sun Chunshan, Wei Guangming, Du Ruifeng
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of distributed generation in the distribution network, its output fluctuation and fault characteristics make the traditional overcurrent protection face severe challenges in terms of sensitivity, selectivity and adaptability. In order to cope with the protection problems caused by distributed generation access, this paper focuses on the research progress and practical path of regional protection technology, systematically analyzes its hierarchical distributed architecture design and cooperative protection strategy, and focuses on the dynamic threshold differential protection criterion adapted to distributed generation access, the unmeasurable branch current compensation method, and the multi-dimensional information fusion fault location technology that integrates multi-terminal electrical quantities and intelligent algorithms. Finally, the feasibility and effectiveness of regional protection in distribution network with high proportion of distributed generation are verified by practical cases, which provides a systematic reference for the construction of highly reliable and adaptive active distribution network protection system.

title: Research on Optimization of Steady-State Simulation Model of Fuel Cell System Based on Deep Learning
authors: Chen Fengyue, Wang Xia, Feng Zenglei, Jiang Chengcheng, Wang Jinsheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This study aims to improve the performance of steady-state simulation model of fuel cell system by using deep learning technology. Firstly, the working principle and traditional model of fuel cell are deeply analyzed, and deep learning algorithm (such as neural network) is introduced to optimize its accuracy and adaptability. Through the subject experiment, multi-parameter data such as working temperature and gas partial pressure under different working conditions are collected. After cleaning and normalization preprocessing, a model based on deep learning is constructed. We need to select mean square error loss function and Adam optimizer when training model. The optimal model structure was determined after several iterations and tuning. The results show that: the mean square error (MSE) is 0.075; mean absolute error (MAE) is 0.23; determination coefficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2})$</tex> is 0.8; MSE decreases to 0.03; MAE decreases to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.11; R^{2}$</tex> increases to 0.94. It is shown that the precision and stability of the optimized model can be improved significantly in predicting output performance index. The research results provide powerful tools for fuel cell design and optimization, as well as promote its wide application in new energy field.

title: Research on Vehicle Detection and Tracking Algorithm Based on Improved YOLOv5s
authors: Linxi Xie, Xinlu Dong, Xiaonian Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional object detection algorithms in vehicle detection and tracking—such as low real-time performance, limited accuracy, and susceptibility to occlusion leading to missed or incorrect detections—this paper proposes an improved YOLOv5s-based algorithm. The enhancements include the integration of the Convolutional Block Attention Module to enable more precise feature extraction, re-clustering of anchor boxes using the K-means++ algorithm for better localization, and the introduction of the Adaptive Spatial Feature Fusion mechanism and a multi-branch Receptive Field Block to enlarge the receptive field and mitigate information loss. Furthermore, ConvNeXt is employed as a lightweight backbone network to maintain detection accuracy while reducing computational complexity. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly improves both detection accuracy and real-time performance. Additionally, issues such as missed and incorrect detections caused by occlusion are effectively alleviated. Compared to traditional network models, the proposed method exhibits superior performance in practical applications.

title: Current Sensor Fault Identification and Evaluation for Lithium-ion Battery Packs
authors: Jinghan Zhao, Qifan Yang, Jianing Ren
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: The current sensor is the core part of the battery pack, which is critical to reliable battery pack operation. Current sensor faults consist of a variety of modes, of which drift and gain are two typical modes. Aiming at the problem that the drift and gain modes are difficult to distinguish and quantitatively evaluate, this article proposes a method based on model fusion. First, the fault signatures of the drift and gain modes are analyzed. Second, two models, called as the drift model and gain model, respectively, are established with the cell voltage as input and current as output. The gap between the two models lies in the differential representation of the observation equations, where a novel fault parameter is adopted to describe fault signatures. Then, a model fusion-based method is designed based on the matching between fault current and models. At last, the validity of the method is experimentally verified.

title: A Differentiable Bi-Objective Framework for Risk-Aware Distribution-System Repair Scheduling Using IEEE-33 Feeder and NOAA Storm Data
authors: Ling-Feng Chiang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: We propose SoftSchedule, a differentiable dualobjective distribution-system repair scheduling framework that integrates real disaster data with standard feeder test systems. Using the IEEE-33 bus feeder as the base network and NOAA Storm Events 2022 as the hazard source, county-level records are mapped to buses and converted into temporal hazard images via edge-midpoint rasterization, providing localized environmental features for candidate faulted components. Service supply is approximated by reachability-weighted connectivity; subtree load sums are used as estimates, with exact recomputed for the top-ranked edges to improve label quality. Training jointly minimizes the area ∫ ( 1− Q(t))  and T95 (time to reach 95% service). The former is optimized via a Gumbel–Sinkhorn– based differentiable sorting that produces a continuous surrogate of execution order, combined with time-discount weights to approximate area; the latter uses a smooth hinge proxy to encourage early attainment, plus a Q(t) smoothness regularizer to suppress curve jitter. A pairwise rank-distillation term based on v/t strengthens learning of relative order. At inference, instead of computationally heavy MPC, we introduce a risk-aware beam search targeting both area and T95, which fuses standardized signals from dQ, job durations, and neural scores to produce robust, real-time repair sequences. Experiments show that SoftSchedule achieves low area while significantly improving the early part of the service curve and T95, outperforming a greedy v/baseline in time efficiency and risk suppression. Overall, SoftSchedule organically combines real external hazard data, standard test systems, differentiable sorting, and lightweight inference to deliver a reproducible pipeline—from data-driven scenario generation and end-to-end learning to online decision-making—providing a concrete baseline and extensible framework for distribution-grid resilience restoration research and practice.

title: Optimization Strategy for Cooperative Harmonic Management of Multi-Inverter Distributed Power Supplies Based on Dynamic Weight Regulation
authors: Dingdong Wei, Xiuzhi Liu, Tao Sui, Xianghua Peng
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: The growing integration of distributed energy resources and nonlinear loads in microgrids has exacerbated harmonic distortion challenges. To balance limited inverter capacity and compensation effectiveness, this paper proposes a Sigmoid Dynamic Weight Adjustment Harmonic Compensation Optimization (SDW-HCO) strategy. The method monitors the remaining inverter capacity in real-time, allocates harmonic current limits based on local voltage ratios, and dynamically adjusts objective function weights using sigmoid functions of voltage deviation and current utilization. A genetic algorithm then jointly selects inverters and current commands. Simulation results demonstrate that SDW-HCO reduces the THD at the target node from 7.71% to 3.05%. Moreover, by activating a third inverter under tight capacity constraints, it maintains the overall network THD below 4%, complying with standards while preserving system stability and safety margins.

title: Electrochemical behavior of zinc ions on carbon nanotube/chitosan derivative sensors
authors: Funeng Tan
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: In this paper, carbon nanotube/chitosan, carbon nanotube/O-hydroxypropyl chitosan (MWNT/HPCHS), and carbon nanotube/o-hydroxypropyl n-octyl chitosan sensors were prepared by drop coating using chitosan and its derivatives as dispersants. The electrochemical behavior of zinc ions on the prepared sensors was investigated using cyclic voltammetry and linear scanning voltammetry. The results show: Zinc ions have good electrochemical response on the hydroxypropyl carbon nanotube/chitosan sensor. When the zinc ion concentration is 10 to 25 μmol/L, the linear relationship is good, the linear equation is I(A)=-3.02235 + 88367.26531c(mol/L), and the correlation coefficient is 0.99375. The detection limit reaches 1×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-6</sup>mol/L, providing a new method for the detection of trace zinc ions.

title: Development of an Off-line Visual Detection Device for Nozzle Atomization Effect
authors: Jiamin Lei, Jianfeng Chen, Jiacheng Ou, Airong Ling, Yinru He, Liming Lin, Weitao Zhou, Zhenye Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: In this paper, a nozzle atomization effect detection device is developed to solve the problems of low detection efficiency and long adjustment time in tobacco shred process. It has the following features: offline detection, visual analysis and intelligent control. Through the integration of modular design, image processing technology and intelligent PID control system, accurate quantification and rapid adjustment of atomization parameters are achieved. Practical application shows that the device shortens the detection and adjustment time from the original 131.18 minutes to 21.09 minutes, improving efficiency by 522%, providing an innovative solution for process quality control in the tobacco industry.

title: Profit-Driven Multi-Item Data Dissemination Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning in Internet of Vehicles
authors: Guangxiong Jin, Guangchao Chen, Shujuan Wang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Data dissemination plays a vital role in providing value-added services which enhance traffic safety and efficiency in the Internet of Vehicles (IoVs). However, major obstacles exist due to the limited communication resources and harsh transmission environment in IoVs. Moreover, existing work often assume the data/service providers offer their data/service for free, which weaken the practicability of these work. This paper focuses on the profit-driven multi-item data dissemination in IoVs. An incentive mechanism is established between RoadSide Units (RSUs) and vehicles, where vehicles are allowed to compete for receiving better service from RSUs, through a bidding strategy which dynamically adjusts a vehicle's bidding price for a certain data item, based on multiple characteristics that affect the transmission performance. RSUs obtain the payments from vehicles as a compensation for the resource consumed in responding them. The efficient multi-item data dissemination problem is modeled as the optimization of RSU's long-term revenue. A Double Deep Q Network (DDQN) based scheduling algorithm is proposed which implements intelligent data scheduling decisions. Simulation results show that the proposed strategy achieves significant performance improvement in terms of RSU's revenue, successful response ratio and completion delay under varied environment, which justifies the effectiveness and efficiency of this work.

title: Entropy based Intuitionistic Fuzzy Weighted Least Square Twin Support Vector Machine
authors: Nauman Ansary, Niher Ranjan Das, Q. M. Danish Lohani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Imbalanced datasets are difficult to handle in classification problems, where some classes have significantly fewer samples than others. Support Vector Machines (SVMs) are known for their strong performance in many areas, but they struggle with imbalanced and noisy data as they treat all training samples equally, which causes the model to favour the majority class when forming the decision boundary. Although fuzzy SVM variants like entropy based fuzzy SVM (EFSVM) and intuitionistic fuzzy twin SVM (IFTSVM) reduce the impact of noise and outliers by assigning fuzzy membership values, they rely heavily on distance-based assumptions and assume uniform uncertainty across the data. These methods compute membership and non-membership values based on the centres of the data. In this paper, we propose a new entropy based IFW-LSTSVM (EIFW-LSTSVM) that combines intuitionistic fuzzy theory with local entropy to handle class imbalance better. It assigns full membership to minority class samples and reduces it for the uncertain majority class. This dual approach enhances the model performance by focusing more on the minority class while reducing the influence of noisy majority samples, leading to a more balanced and reliable decision boundary. Moreover, to validate the performance of the proposed method, experiments were conducted on 14 imbalanced datasets from the KEEL repos-itory, comparing it with several recent and widely recognized SVM variants. For statistical validation of these findings, the Friedman test followed by the Dunn-Bonferroni post-hoc test was applied at a 95% confidence level. The results indicate that the proposed method exhibits significantly superior performance compared to existing state-of-the-art methods.

title: A Novel Blockchain-Based Secure Voting System with End-to-End Verifiability and Privacy
authors: Moorthy Agoramoorthy
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Current electronic voting infrastructure continues to be plagued by security, transparency and voter privacy concerns, in both large and remote elections. In order to cope with these issues, this paper introduces a blockchain-based voting system which fulfills end-to-end verifiability and maintains ballot secrecy. The system proposed uses a permissioned blockchain system along with Byzantine Fault Tolerant (BFT) consensus protocol to guarantee data integrity and resilience to faults when facing partially adversarial conditions. The combination of homomorphic encryption of tallying encrypted votes and zero-knowledge proofs of voter eligibility and validation of ballots, without disclosing the content of the vote, results in vote confidentiality and auditability. Smart contracts facilitate the process of vote validation and aggregation making it publically auditable without trusting third parties. Simulated workload performance evaluation suggests that, under regular operating conditions, the system has a verification accuracy greater than 98 % and has an average processing time and computational overhead that are lower than those of the corresponding blockchain-based voting systems under realistic operating conditions. The given framework is planned to assist with the real-time auditing and ensure privacy assurances. Besides this, the paper also addresses the practicability of post-quantum cryptographic primitives and cross-chain mechanisms as further improvements to ensure enhancement of long-term security and scalability.

title: Blockchain-Secured Transformer-Based Federated Learning for Adaptive DDoS Detection in IoT Networks
authors: Nilamadhab Mishra, Vakati Latha Sree, Boya Pravalika, Chilamakuru Bhanu Teja
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The rapid expansion of Internet of Things (IoT) infrastructures has increased susceptibility to Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks, necessitating decentralized, secure, and adaptive detection frameworks. Although federated learning (FL) is a privacy-preserving form of distributed intelligence, the current FL-based intrusion detection systems have weaknesses that include vulnerability to model poisoning, high-communication cost, and the inability to work with nonIID data distributions. Moreover, the traditional CNN-based models are incapable of capturing the changing time trends of attack behaviors completely. In order to overcome these drawbacks, we come up with a study by introducing a Blockchain-Secured Transformer-Based Federated Learning (BTF-FL) system to overcome adaptive DDoS detection in heterogeneous IoT settings. The proposed architecture combines a Transformer-based learning of long sequences representation and blockchain-driven secure aggregation, to provide immutability and verifiable model updates. Gradient compression and personalization techniques that are communication-efficient, further help to improve scalability. The use of CICIDS2017, BoT-IoT and TON-IoT data sets under the non-IID conditions, shows that the technique performs better, with 99.12% detection accuracy, reduced communication overhead 55.4% and an enhancement in resistance to poisoning attacks 43%. The findings confirm that BTF-FL is a secure, scalable and high-performance technology that can be deployed in the next-generation IoT security.

title: Research on a Deep Learning-Based Intelligent Recommendation Algorithm for Broadcast and Television Content
authors: Xiaoli Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This paper designs a broadcast and television content recommendation algorithm (DFFRA) based on deep feature fusion. This algorithm employs a three-layer cascade architecture to achieve precise recommendations through feature extraction, dynamic fusion, and interest prediction. The feature extraction layer processes multimodal content features and user behavior sequences in parallel. The feature fusion layer innovatively designs an attention-gated fusion unit. The recommendation prediction layer outputs user interest scores. Experiments on the TVRec-2024 dataset show that, when K=10, DFFRA achieves an accuracy of 82.3%, a recall of 78.5%, an F1 score of 80.3%, and a MAE of 0.62. These improvements are 4.2%, 3.8%, 4.0%, and 0.08, respectively, compared to the next-best algorithm, AttnRec, with the lowest standard deviation (<0.7). Compared to the traditional CF algorithm, DFFRA achieves a 15.6% improvement in accuracy and 8.9% and 6.5% improvement in F1 score over CNNRec and LSTMRec, respectively. Stability tests show that when the training data is reduced by 50%, the F1 score of DFFRA only decreases by 3.2%. Training time increases linearly with the number of users, validating its advantages in data-sparse and large-scale user scenarios.

title: Semantic Review and Contextual Feature Integration in Multi-Armed Bandit for Yelp Recommendation
authors: Xiaowen Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In Yelp merchant recommendation, a semantic-enhanced Contextual Multi-armed slot machine model (SE-Contextual MAB) was proposed to solve the balance problem of “exploring unknown merchants” and “exploiting known high-quality merchants”, as well as the shortcomings of traditional recommendation algorithms that ignore the fine-grained semantic preferences of reviews and the coarse-grained contextual features. This model firstly extracted from Yelp data set user - merchant context features (such as time, location, merchants property) and comment on semantic characteristics (such as theme emotion, user preferences keywords), realize double dimension through attention weighted feature fusion; Secondly, based on the Linear Upper Confidence Bound (LinUCB) framework, a dynamic exploration strategy was designed to increase the exploration weight of new merchants to alleviate the cold start problem. Finally, a semantic consistency penalty was introduced to optimize the reward function to improve the recommendation matching accuracy. The experiment is based on Yelp Academic Dataset, and the results show that: With random recommendations, collaborative filtering (CF), the traditional LinUCB compared to the baseline models such as the accumulation of SE - Contextual MAB reduced by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$23.6 \% \sim 41.2 \%$</tex>, the new merchant click-through rates rise by 35.8%, the average user semantic matching degree is 0.72. The effectiveness of the model in exploration-exploitation balance and personalized recommendation is verified.

title: A Spatio-Temporal Correlation and GAN Based Preprocessing Scheme for Missing Data in Short-Term Power Load Forecasting Systems
authors: Leiming Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: This paper proposes a two-stage data restoration scheme that integrates spatiotemporal correlation analysis (STCA) and conditional generative adversarial network (CGAN) to address the problem of data loss caused by sensor failures and communication interruptions in short-term power load forecasting. Firstly, explore the collaborative fluctuation patterns of adjacent regional load data through spatiotemporal correlation matrices; secondly, design a CGAN model to generate fill values that comply with physical constraints, ensuring that the statistical properties of the repaired data are consistent with the true distribution. Based on actual load data from 10 regions of a certain power grid, verification shows that in the scenario of random missing rate of 40% and continuous missing for 72 hours, the prediction error (MAE=10.9MW) of the repaired data in this scheme is significantly lower than that of traditional methods (KNN: MAE=16.7MW; MF: MAE=16.5MW), and the prediction accuracy is improved by 22.7% after repairing. This physical constraint module can ensure that the repaired data conforms to the operating rules of the power grid and avoid scheduling risks caused by "pseudo repair".

title: Real-Time Defect Classification at the Edge: A Lightweight CNN Approach for Industrial-Scale Applications
authors: Yuanyuan Liu, Xiaoxue Yang, Haijiang Li, Jiahao Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: We introduce a lightweight CNN-based framework for real-time classification of surface defects and efficient deployment on industrial edge devices. The method was evaluated on the NEU-CLS dataset using several compact convolutional architectures. The GhostNet-based variant delivered the most balanced performance across accuracy, efficiency, and inference speed. For practical validation, the trained model was exported to ONNX format and deployed on a Raspberry Pi 4B, achieving stable real-time inference with hardware costs under 100 USD. The deployment workflow integrates preprocessing, streamlined inference, and result output, making it suitable for quality control scenarios. Visual outcomes and classification consistency across multiple defect types demonstrate the model's generalization and reliability. Designed for resource-constrained environments, this solution offers a deployable path toward high-precision defect recognition in embedded industrial applications.

title: Phase-Field Simulation of Insulation Breakdown Evolution in Composite Insulators Considering the Interphase of GFRP
authors: Xiaoyu Yi, Cuiying Sun, Jiaxin Zhang, Yixin Wang, Yuming Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: The interfacial region within the GFRP core rod of composite insulators acts as a structurally weak zone where initial defects readily form, thereby triggering subsequent insulation degradation. To elucidate the mechanism by which the interfacial phase influences the axial evolution of insulation damage in composite insulators, this study employs atomic force microscopy (AFM) to measure and characterize the interfacial phase inside the GFRP material. A phase-field-based finite-element simulation model incorporating the interfacial phase is then developed to investigate insulationdamage evolution in GFRP. Using this model, we analyze the effects of electric-field orientation, glass-fiber distribution, and the relative permittivity of the interfacial phase on the characteristics of electrical-tree propagation within the material. The results indicate that the interfacial phase introduces gradients or non-uniformities in the electrical properties of the GFRP material, which in turn modify its electrical breakdown behavior. Under the combined influence of material anisotropy, relative permittivity, and the critical breakdown field, electrical trees propagate more rapidly parallel to the fibers than perpendicular to them. As the relative permittivity of the interfacial phase increases, the rate at which electrical trees grow toward the interface also accelerates, and the electrostatic energy becomes increasingly concentrated in branches approaching the interfacial region. The findings of this study provide a foundational basis for further investigations into the decay-like degradation mechanisms of composite insulators and contribute to enhancing their long-term operational reliability.

title: Handover Control for Sliced UAV Networks Based on Distributed DDQN
authors: Feng Yang, Meng Li, Ruizhe Yang, Suyu Lv, Enchang Sun, Pengbo Si
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: The integration of end-to-end network slices (NSs) into unpiloted aerial vehicle (UAV) networks to provide differentiated services based on ground users' (GUs') quality of service (QoS) requirements has attracted widespread attention. In a sliced UAV network, due to the dual mobility of UAVs and GUs, as well as the triple association of GU-UAV-NS, handover becomes an important and challenging issue for maintaining GUs' communication performance. This paper proposes a decentralized handover decision process, thereby reducing communication overhead. In this process, each GU independently determines its handover decisions using local information. The decentralized handover decision process is then formulated as a decentralized Markov decision process (DEC-MDP). The service revenue, handover overhead, and outage cost are incorporated into the reward function of the DEC-MDP. Finally, a distributed double deep Q-network (D-DDQN) scheme is designed to maximize the total reward for all GUs, thereby achieving the optimization objective of satisfying GUs' QoS requirements while avoiding frequent handovers. The simulation results confirm that the D-DDQN algorithm converges stably. Moreover, it achieves the highest QoS satisfaction for GUs with the fewest handovers compared with the benchmark algorithms.

title: The Evolution of Chaotic Cryptography: A Comparative Study of Fixed Versus ML-Optimized Parameters
authors: Nishil Jain, Samrudhi Patil, Sarvesh Patil, Preeti Godabole, Divyang Jadav
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: With the increasing deployment of biometric authentication, ensuring iris images against unauthorized access is crucial. This paper presents a comparative study between the traditional hybrid chaotic encryption approach, which combines Henon and Logistic maps by taking fixed parameters, and the machine learning-integrated approach in which XGBoost regression models are used to predict optimal parameters (a, b, r) for every image. In this regard, the conventional scheme has yielded a correlation of 0.01778 between adjacent pixels of the encrypted image, an encrypted entropy of 7.9816, and perfect reconstruction with PSNR = ∞ and SSIM = 1.0000. The ML-integrated approach further reduced the correlation to 0.001761, increased the entropy to 7.9921, and improved the histogram uniformity from 0.9991 to 1.0000 as compared to the results with default parameters. Moreover, the integrated Random Forest-based attack detector resulted in an accuracy of 94.05%, hence further enhancing robustness. Though both approaches have ensured zero reconstruction error, the ML-assisted scheme guaranteed higher unpredictability, increased key space, and superior statistical performance. These results confirm that the proposed ML-driven hybrid chaotic encryption offers enhanced security, adaptability, and robustness against various cryptographic attacks and becomes a stronger contender in protecting biometric data for high-security applications.

title: ADMET-X: Machine Learning Framework for Predicting Pharmacokinetic and Toxicological Drug Properties
authors: Rohith Reddy G K, Sheik Arshad Ibrahim, Sayed Jahangir Ali, Thirumurugan M
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: High attrition rates in drug discovery are frequently driven by unfavourable absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion, and toxicity (ADMET) properties, leading to late-stage failures despite promising efficacy. To address this challenge, we present ADMET-X, a biologically grounded and interpretable machine learning framework for comprehensive in silico prediction of pharmacokinetic and toxicological endpoints. ADMET-X integrates chemically meaningful physicochemical descriptors and structural fingerprints with ensemble learning models, including Random Forest and XGBoost, to capture complex structure–property relationships while preserving interpretability. The framework was evaluated on 31 benchmark datasets obtained from the Therapeutics Data Commons, spanning absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion, and toxicity endpoints, using standardized training, validation, and test splits. Model performance was assessed using ROC–AUC and accuracy for classification tasks and RMSE and coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) for regression tasks. ADMET-X achieved strong predictive performance across multiple endpoints, with ROC–AUC values exceeding 0.90 for key absorption, metabolism, and toxicity predictions, and competitive regression accuracy for physicochemical properties. Unlike black-box deep learning approaches, ADMET-X provides mechanistic insight through feature importance analysis, enabling transparent interpretation of predictions. Overall, the proposed framework offers a scalable, reproducible, and interpretable platform for early-stage ADMET screening and rational drug candidate prioritization.

title: Forensic Sketch-based Face Recognition & Retrieval System using Deep Learning
authors: Janhavi Nandish, Rifa Noorain, BiBi Sadiya, Ayman Shariff, Mohammed Taj Khan M.F
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Forensic sketch-based face recognition is a challenging problem due to the significant domain gap between hand-drawn sketches and real facial photographs. Traditional manual matching approaches are time-consuming, subjective, and prone to human bias, limiting their effectiveness in modern law enforcement investigations. This paper presents an AI-driven forensic sketch-based face recognition and retrieval system using deep learning to automate and enhance suspect identification. The proposed system employs a fine-tuned InsightFace model for robust facial embedding extraction and integrates FAISS-based similarity indexing to enable efficient large-scale retrieval. A unified processing pipeline performs face detection, embedding generation, and similarity matching to retrieve the top-ranked candidates with confidence scores. Experimental evaluation on paired sketch–photo datasets demonstrates an accuracy of 82–88% with sub-second response times, achieving a 12% improvement over traditional manual methods. The system further incorporates gender-based filtering, batch processing, and real-time analytics through a user-friendly web interface. Scalability analysis confirms reliable performance with increasing database size, while ethical and legal considerations are addressed to ensure responsible deployment. The proposed solution offers a practical, scalable, and deployable framework for modern forensic investigations.

title: A Comparative Study of Deep Learning Models for Photo-to-Sketch Recognition with CAT Preprocessing
authors: Philomina Princiya Mascarenhas, Sannidhan M S, Aashna Mathias, Dabis Camero, Jason Elroy Martis
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: In recent years, facial recognition systems have become widely used in many applications, especially in biometrics and law enforcement. Advances in the field have led to the development of pre-trained deep learning models that achieve good accuracy even when training data is limited. Earlier studies have shown that these models perform well on original photographs but struggle when the input comes from other domains such as sketches. In crime investigations, officers often have only the suspect's sketch, and using pre-trained models directly on such sketches gives low accuracy because important texture and lighting details are missing. To address this issue, our study applies a Contour Accentuation Technique (CAT) to enhance the texture information in sketches. This improves the clarity of sketch features and supports better learning by deep learning models. To evaluate the effectiveness of CAT, we conduct a comparative study using multiple recognition models with and without CAT. The models are tested under a unified setup with cross-validation. The results show that the use of CAT improves face recognition performance, with an average accuracy gain of 7.7%. The working model of our paper is found in https://github.com/Princiya1990/faceRecognition.

title: Intelligent Identification of Power Outage Events and Prediction of Impact Range by Integrating XGBoost and Graph Neural Network
authors: Bo Peng, Ruiping Pan, Yanyan Li, Jun Fu, Meicui Qin
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Current power grid outage identification and impact assessment still face challenges such as fragmented data sources, fragmented customer demand information, and interference from invalid outage data. These issues lead to inaccurate outage assessment results, making it difficult to meet the high standards of smart grid monitoring, early warning, and customer service. This paper proposes a modeling approach that integrates multi-source outage data with intelligent models to construct a framework for outage assessment and prediction based on extreme gradient boosting (XGBoost) and graph neural networks (GNNs). First, a multi-source data aggregation analysis is conducted, linking 95598 customer complaints with data from the Collection 2.0 and Marketing 2.0 systems, as well as marketing meter outage and restoration data, to form a unified dataset covering line outages, single distribution transformers, and substation outages. A power outage analysis model is then constructed to automatically remove invalid data such as short-term outages, terminal failures, and duplicate reports. Parameters are optimized based on provincial power outage analysis rules and inter-provincial differences. A GNN is then used to extract topological dependencies and global features. The embedded vectors and business features are then input into XGBoost to identify effective power outage events and quantify their impact. Statistical analysis is performed across dimensions such as province, city, county, station, substation, and user type. Experimental results show that this method achieves 94.8% accuracy in identifying effective power outages and reduces the average error in predicting the impact range to 1.7%, demonstrating significant advantages in both effectiveness and adaptability.

title: Adaptive Multi-Energy State XRF Spectral Fusion and PSO-Wavelet Enhancement Algorithm for Trace Element Detection of Atmospheric Particulate Matter
authors: Mohai Yue, Qi Zhang, Feng Tian, Lan Rao, Fu Wang, Yun Teng, Zipeng Wang, Tongkang Dong
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To overcome the interference caused by complex filter membrane backgrounds in energy-dispersive X-ray fluorescence (ED-XRF) analysis of trace elements in atmospheric particulate matter, this study proposes an adaptive spectral enhancement method based on multi-energy-state XRF fusion and particle swarm optimized wavelet processing. A three-energy-state XRF simulation framework is developed that accounts for nonlinear energy calibration, energy correlation effects, and filter membrane background contributions. Simulation results show that the proposed method improves both the signal-to-noise ratio and the detection limit by more than 11% compared with the PSO-Savitzky-Golay approach. The method is further validated using quartz and Teflon air filter samples, with experimental results in close agreement with theoretical predictions.

title: Design and Implementation of a Real-Time Multi-Source Heterogeneous Data Processing Engine for Shooting Sports Scoring Systems
authors: Guangkuo Wang, JunMing Liu, Yulong Li, Zhikai Mi
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: The fragmentation of scoring data formats across different shooting equipment manufacturers presents a significant challenge for building a unified national shooting sports management platform in China. This paper presents the design and implementation of a cloud-based shooting score data management system, with a focus on its core component: a realtime, multi-source heterogeneous data processing engine. The engine addresses the dual challenges of data heterogeneity and low-latency requirements through a three-pronged approach: a highly abstracted unified data model that normalizes diverse scoring formats from both domestic and international vendors into a consistent JSON schema; an extensible parsing framework based on the Template Method pattern, which efficiently maps 146 distinct competition scenarios into 31 parsing templates for maintainability and scalability; and a high-performance real-time messaging system utilizing WebSocket, thread pools, and concurrent data structures to ensure millisecond-level score updates for live broadcasting. The system has been successfully implemented and demonstrates robust performance in handling the complex, real-time data integration needs of major shooting events.

title: Deep Cross-Modal Learning and Multi-Modal Feature Fusion for Automated Detection of Breast Cancer From Mammography and Ultrasound Images
authors: Mummireddigari Santhi, M. Sunil Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Breast cancer is one of the most important health issues affecting women today, and it needs to be detected quickly. However, as both mammography and ultrasonography have limitations, relying solely on one imaging modality could significantly reduce accuracy. To address these problems, this study presents a Deep Cross-Modal Feature Fusion Network (DCM-FFN) that leverages the complementary and simultaneous use of comprehensive structural information from mammography and soft tissues represented in ultrasound images. To achieve more efficient fusion of features obtained from ResNet50 on mammography images and EfficientNet-B4 on ultrasonic images, a gated fusion module and bidirectional cross-attention are used, and report successful implementation and testing on an RSNA Mammography and Breast Ultrasound Images dataset after careful preprocessing and augmentation. From experiments, it shows that DCM-FFN significantly outperformed single- and multi-modal methods with an accuracy, precision, recall, F 1 -score, and AUC value of 96.87 %, 0.97, 0.96, 0.96, and 0.98, respectively. Moreover, Grad-CAM interpretation on DCM-FFN clearly verifies that it focuses on more meaningful regions when making predictions. The presented model appears more reliable and interpretable and holds great promise for adoption as an interpretable AI method for breast cancer diagnosis.

title: Multimodal Wear Prediction Framework for Heritage Staircases Using Monte Carlo Simulation and Bayesian Inference
authors: Zihao Li, Mengxin Hu, Man Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This paper proposes a simulation-based computational framework for predicting material wear in heritage staircases, integrating Monte Carlo methods and Bayesian inference. Ultrasonic sensing is first employed for efficient, non-destructive data collection. Then, based on Archard's wear model, a statistical wear-depth correlation is established and enhanced using a Gaussian-distributed travel preference model and finite element mesh-based wear calculation. To infer aging and degradation characteristics, a Monte Carlo sampling approach is used in conjunction with Bayesian posterior estimation. Grid-based wear distribution visualization further supports material traceability and regional wear analysis. Experimental results demonstrate the model's ability to accurately capture wear progression patterns under diverse usage scenarios. This framework provides a scalable and data-efficient solution for architectural conservation, offering quantitative decision support for long-term building preservation strategies.

title: Implementation of a GSM-Based Real-Time Water Level Monitoring and Alert System
authors: Rohini M, Anbalagan P, Logesh E
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Absence of real time monitoring of storage tanks and reservoirs also poses a challenge in efficient water resource management which in most cases leads to wastage, overflow, and shortage of water. The main issue is the fact that they rely on the traditional manual checking systems, which are timeconsuming, labor-intensive, and have high chances of mistakes made by the human factor, and finally result in inefficient use of the water resources. In order to overcome the above challenge, the identified research presents an automatic GSM-based water level monitoring system that is able to measure and relay information regarding the water levels in an accurate and remote fashion. It uses ultrasonic detectors to monitor the actual water level and uses a GSM communication device to directly send updates on the status to a mobile phone of the user via SMS. The method allows prompt notifications and in case of emergency conditions like overflow or drastically low water, one can respond straight away. The system fits well in both domestic and industrial setup in which supervision and timely intervention are necessary in order to prevent inefficiencies in operation. The freshness of this solution is that, intelligent sensing technology has been integrate with GSM to create a low energy consuming, inexpensive, and dependable remote monitoring system. The proposed system improves the control of water distribution, reduces the wastage of resources, and promotes sustainable management of water through the automation of the measure and reporting process.

title: Copyright and Reprint Permission
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Social Network Systems (AISNS)

title: Research on Efficient Path Planning Algorithm for UAV in Power Equipment Inspection
authors: Bei Li, Zhicong Dong, Yulin Zhu, Lingbo Liu, Yujie Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This paper proposes a path planning method that integrates reinforcement learning and improved ant colony algorithm. By constructing a mathematical model of inspection scenes that includes factors such as terrain, weather, and equipment distribution, accurate path planning in complex environments is achieved. In the reinforcement learning framework, the state space is defined by the position of the UAV, the equipment number, etc., the flight direction adjustment, etc. is used as the action space, and the reward function is constructed in combination with the path length, inspection efficiency, etc.; at the same time, the pheromone update and search strategy of the ant colony algorithm are improved to achieve complementary advantages. On the MATLAB simulation platform, multiple groups of scenes with terrains such as mountains and plains, high, medium, and low density equipment distribution, and meteorological conditions such as sunny days and strong winds are set up, and compared with Dijkstra algorithm, traditional ant colony algorithm, etc. The results show that in mountainous terrain, the path length of the proposed algorithm is 25.3% shorter than that of the Dijkstra algorithm, and the inspection time is reduced by 27.8%; in the scenario of high-density distribution of equipment, the path planning success rate reaches 96.1%, which is 21.3% higher than that of the traditional ant colony algorithm; the average convergence speed of the algorithm is 35.6% faster than that of the traditional algorithm, which effectively verifies the efficiency and reliability of the algorithm in power inspection path planning.

title: A Mobile Dust-Proof Integrated Solution for GIS Equipment Maintenance in Various Scenarios
authors: Fugao Gong, Kai Zhao, Li Wang, Zuopeng Zhang, Lijiang Chen, Xuefeng Liu, Linglong Tan, Wei Li, Heng Guo, Yang Bai
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of power grid scale, gas-insulated switchgear (GIS) has become increasingly prevalent in power systems, where its operational reliability directly impacts grid safety. This paper proposes a comprehensive solution to address challenges in outdoor GIS maintenance, including complex and variable environmental conditions and limited effectiveness of traditional dust-proof measures. Through systematic analysis of existing dust control shortcomings, we designed a modular dust-proof system based on the principles of “zoned dust prevention, modular adaptability, and intelligent control.” The solution features a zoned dust-proof framework, adjustable modular dust-proof sheds, accordion-style dust covers, and an integrated intelligent environmental control system. Practical applications demonstrate that this approach can reduce dust concentrations in critical areas below standard thresholds, significantly improve maintenance environment cleanliness, effectively lower equipment failure rates, while substantially enhancing maintenance efficiency and reducing dust-proofing costs. The solution holds significant potential for widespread application and promotion.

title: EcoSentiNet: A Multi-Objective Deep Reinforcement Learning Model for Energy-Optimized Sentiment Classification
authors: Udhayakumar. J, Gururama Senthilvel. P, V. R. Vimal, T. Suresh Balakrishnan, Moorthy Agoramoorthy
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The growing computational requirements of transformer-based sentiment analysis models have intensified the energy efficiency and sustainability concern especially in the mobile, Internet of Things, and edge computing systems. In order to overcome this issue, the paper presents EcoSentiNet, which is a multi-objective Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) model that aims at energy-efficient sentiment classification. The EcoSentiNet is balanced with accuracy, latency and power consumption dynamically by varying the depth of neural network, computation granularity, and the timing of an inference. The framework uses a DRL controller that is a policy gradient based and learns the best inference strategies in different hardware and workload conditions. In contrast to the classical static models, EcoSentiNet is able to continuously assess the energy-performance trade-offs via the reward scheme that combines both accuracy and energy cost, guaranteeing the inference quality over long periods of time under limited energy budgets. The experiments on the benchmark datasets, including IMDb, Twitter Sentiment140, and Amazon Reviews, demonstrate that EcoSentiNet can reduce energy usage and minimize latency significantly (40 and 25 percent, respectively) without further deteriorating the accuracy in relation to the traditional deep sentiment models. The suggested framework proves sustainability of the NLP in terms of intelligent energy-performance co-optimization and moves the field forward to the next stage of Green AI real-time sentiment comprehension.

title: Joint CFO and Channel Estimation for OTFS System in LEO Satellite Communications
authors: Tingguang Zhang, Kegang Pan, Aijun Liu, Xiaohu Liang, Xin Lin, Chen Han
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite communication systems, previous research usually overlooks the impact of carrier frequency offset (CFO) during the channel estimation process of the orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS) modulation. In this letter, we investigate the joint CFO and channel estimation for OTFS modulation in LEO satellite communication scenarios. Firstly, the joint estimation model for the OTFS system is formulated. Based on this model, we can obtain the joint CFO and channel estimation expression from the joint probability density function of the received samples. Then, we design the objective function for the joint estimation of OTFS. By maximizing the objective function, a joint CFO and channel estimator is proposed and its computational complexity is analyzed. To improve the channel estimation accuracy, we further design the joint estimator architecture enhanced by deep learning. Under the LEO channel, simulation results demonstrate that the proposed joint estimator outperforms the conventional approaches in both CFO estimation performance and channel estimation performance for the OTFS system.

title: Enhancing Resilience of Complex Networks Against Node Attack: A Constraint Reinforcement Learning Approach for Edge Addition
authors: Yangyang Li, Yunjun Lu, Kebin Chen, Kaishen Zeng, Jian Cheng, Wenlu Zhou
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Ensuring the resilience of complex networks against structural failures and targeted attacks is a critical challenge in modern network science. Adding edges between nodes enhances redundancy and alternative pathways in the network, thereby improving its ability to withstand and recover from structural disruptions. Traditional edge addition strategies often fail to adaptively optimize multiple resilience metrics simultaneously, leading to suboptimal network performance. In this paper, we propose a Constraint Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning (CMORL) method for enhancing network resilience through strategic edge addition. Our approach incorporates multiple objectives—maximizing the size of the largest connected component, improving algebraic connectivity, and preserving node degree distributions—into a unified reinforcement learning paradigm with constraint-based action selection and constraintbased reward function. The designed composite reward functions guide the algorithm to learn balanced and effective restoration policies under various attack scenarios. Extensive experiments on synthetic and real-world network topologies demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms existing baseline strategies.

title: A Real-Time Deep Learning Solution for Helmetless Rider Detection in Urban Traffic
authors: Archana S, Vishesha R, Janani C, Praveen K, Rajan C
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Road accidents involving two-wheeler riders remain a critical concern worldwide, with a significant number of fatalities caused by the neglect of wearing helmets. Manual surveillance to enforce helmet regulations is inefficient and prone to human oversight. This study presents a smart helmet detection framework that employs deep learning-based real-time object recognition to identify riders not wearing helmets. The proposed approach utilises the YOLOv8 (You Only Look Once, version 8) model to precisely detect riders, helmets, and motorcycles from both live video feeds and static imagery. By analysing the spatial relationship between detected helmets and riders, the system classifies whether a rider is helmeted or helmetless. The experimental results demonstrate high detection accuracy and efficient frame processing, proving the model's capability for real-time traffic monitoring. The developed system can be integrated into smart city infrastructure to assist traffic authorities in improving road safety and ensuring law enforcement compliance.

title: Deep Learning-Based Uncertainty-Driven Robust Time Series Forecasting for Backend Service Metrics
authors: Sijia Li, Chengda Xu, Chi Zhang, Bolin Chen, Zizhao Zhang, Zixiao Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: This study addresses the challenge of forecasting backend service metrics in highly dynamic and disturbed environments and proposes a robust time series prediction framework based on uncertainty estimation. The method first applies a multi-scale encoding structure to decompose and represent service metric sequences, allowing the model to capture mixed characteristics of trends, periodic patterns, and noise disturbances. It then constructs a cross-sequence dependency modeling module to describe structural coupling among multiple metrics so that the model can identify implicit interaction patterns across different service nodes in backend systems. On this basis, the framework outputs both the mean and variance of future metrics through distributed prediction heads, enabling joint characterization of predictions and their associated risk ranges. A noise response mechanism is incorporated to enhance model stability under anomalous disturbances. To improve the controllability of the overall prediction process, a risk adjustment module is designed to transform uncertainty signals into actionable adjustment factors, allowing the model to maintain consistent outputs in high volatility environments. The experiments evaluate hyperparameter sensitivity, environmental sensitivity, and data sensitivity. The results show that the proposed framework maintains high stability and robustness under multiple source disturbances and effectively suppresses error propagation caused by anomalous samples, structural changes, and system scaling. By jointly modeling point predictions and risk estimation, the study verifies the effectiveness of an uncertainty-driven robust forecasting framework for backend metric management and system reliability, providing a scalable technical path for data-driven analysis in complex systems.

title: System Design of a Smart Food and E-Commerce Platform with Performance Optimization
authors: J. Gandhimathi Scanya, G. Jayalakshmi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The increasing demand for advanced digital food services necessitates optimized system designs that provide scalability, efficiency, and high operational dependability. This research outlines the System Design of a Smart Food and ECommerce Platform, including Performance Optimization, constructed with a GitHub-based Comprehensive Food Database as the principal modelling resource. The dataset contains a comprehensive collection of structured food information, with 203,086 food entries, which include 193,369 products from 96 restaurants, accompanied by detailed nutritional and category features, allowing realistic system simulation and intelligent processing. The proposed architecture incorporates automated order management, predictive inventory control, and performance-oriented resource allocation algorithms. System optimization is attained by parameter tweaking, including a learning rate of 0.001, a batch size of 32, cache allocation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 1 2 ~ M B}$</tex>, and load-balancing criteria established at 75 % CPU utilization. Experimental validation of performance assessment revealed a system-level operational accuracy of 97.85%, an average response latency of 120 ms, and a throughput capacity of 1,200 transactions per minute at peak load circumstances. These findings affirm that performance indicators are products of system assessment rather than intrinsic characteristics of the dataset. The suggested platform offers a scalable, performance-oriented solution for advanced smart food and e-commerce settings, improving service efficiency and user experience through sophisticated system architecture.

title: A Cross-Domain Pulmonary Nodule Detection Algorithm Based on MSF-YOLOv11
authors: Mengqing Zhan
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: Lung cancer is the leading cause of cancer-related deaths worldwide, and its early screening critically depends on the accurate detection of pulmonary nodules. To address the limitations of current deep learning models, including inadequate multi-scale adaptability and high false positive rates, this study proposes an improved detection framework, MSF-YOLOv11, which is based on multi-scale spatial pyramid pooling and feature fusion enhancements to the YOLOv11 model. The spatial pyramid pooling module improves the model's ability to detect objects of varying sizes through multi-scale pooling operations, while the feature fusion module enhances the understanding of multi-scale information by integrating features from different layers. Experiments on the LUNA16 dataset demonstrate that the improved YOLOv11 model achieves a 4.9% increase in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{m A P}$</tex> @ 50 compared to the baseline. To mitigate overfitting to a single dataset, a cross-domain training strategy is introduced by combining LUNA16 and the TianChi dataset, resulting in a significant improvement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 2. 3 \%}$</tex> in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{m A P} {@} 50$</tex>, reaching 0.946. Experimental results indicate that the proposed model outperforms YOLOv8 in the detection of subtle pulmonary nodules, with the MSPP and feature fusion modules significantly improving the detection of small nodules. Moreover, cross-domain training effectively enhances the model's generalization capability.

title: Data-Driven Air Quality Prediction System using Machine Learning
authors: Thirumalaivasan R, Vijay Raj B, P. Uma Maheswari
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Air pollution has been a global issue due to the fact that air pollution is detrimental to the human health, the ecosystem, and the sustainable city development. It is not an easy task since it is hard to predict the quality of air with high precision, thus being unable to provide timely decision support and implement an efficient intervention to cut pollution. One of the solutions, in this project, is proposed as a machine learning-based air quality forecasting platform using the historical data and environmental information. Its task is to create a scalable data-driven platform that would be able to foresee the level of pollution, identify the reasons, and provide practical information to facilitate prevention and reduction measures. A proposed system will include preprocessing, data features creation and testing of a selection of machine learning algorithms to identify the most accurate model. The predictive analytics allow users to provide environmental parameters and get predictions, based on a Django built web interface. The newty of this system is the association of sophisticated predictive modelling with a simple-to-install platform to assist in the establishment of public consciousness and allow data-driven resilience.

title: A Comprehensive Review Paper on Smart Attendance System Using Machine Learning
authors: Satakratu Chakraborty, Piyush Anand, Khushi Kumari, Sweta Kumari, Saksham Satnalika, Akash Pundir
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: In our modern age, monitoring attendance in educational institutions is very crucial. When done manually, it becomes more difficult. In this paper, many existing smart attendance systems using machine learning or artificial intelligence are briefly reviewed. The analysis reveals a clear methodological evolution from efficient classical approaches like Haar Cascades and Local Binary Pattern Histograms (LBPH), to high-accuracy deep learning paradigms utilizing Convolutional Neural Networks like VGG16, FaceNet and DenseNet. These improvements show how technology has steadily improved the accuracy, speed and automation of attendance monitoring. In addition to that, this review also discusses the future scope of smart attendance technologies. With ongoing advancements in artificial intelligence models, hardware capabilities and automation tools, next generation technologies are expected to become even more reliable, faster and easier to utilize across educational environments. This review aims to support continuous research in cultivating more structured and user-friendly attendance solutions.

title: A Dynamic Voltage Balancing Method for Series-connected SiC MOSFET Power Module Based on External Drain-gate Compensation Capacitor
authors: Xindong Xiang, Jianwei Lv, Zexiang Zheng, Cai Chen, Xinwei Dai, Yifan Zhang, Jiaxin Liu, Jiajia Guan, Yong Kang
abstract: The dynamic voltage imbalance between series connected SiC MOSFETs will cause imbalanced losses and reduce voltage capacity. The existing voltage-balancing methods introduce additional losses and make the circuit design more complex and difficult to implement. This article proposes a dynamic voltage-balancing method using external drain-gate compensation capacitors, which leads to almost no additional losses, maintains a simple circuit structure, and is easy to implement. An analytical voltage-sharing model is established. The impact paths and degrees of different parasitic parameters on the imbalanced voltage are obtained. The key parameters that induce the imbalance voltages have been identified. Based on the model's conclusions, this article presents a voltage-balancing method using external drain-gate compensation capacitors. Then, a power module with three MOSFETs in series is designed, fabricated, and optimized. Finally, a 1500 V/45 A double-pulse test platform is established to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. The experimental results showed that the imbalance voltages of MOSFETs were reduced by more than 90% after the optimization. Additionally, pulse experiments conducted under various operating conditions further demonstrated the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title:
Comprehensive Analysis of Highly Efficient La
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    NiMnO
                    <sub>6</sub>
                    Double Perovskite Solar Cell: Towards Lead-Free Sustainable Photovoltaics
authors: Md Sadullah, Akash Kumar Pradhan
abstract: Lead-free double perovskite materials, such as La2NiMnO6 (LNMO), have emerged as promising alternatives to toxic lead-based perovskites due to their non-toxicity, favorable band gap alignment with CH3NH3PbI3, and promising performance. Despite their potential, the optimization of lead-free double perovskite solar cells (DPSCs) remains underexplored. This study presents a numerical approach to model and optimize the performance of an environment-friendly DPSC with an FTO/ETL/LNMO/HTL structure. In this work, a comprehensive optimization process is carried out to select the most suitable electron transport layer (ETL), hole transport layer (HTL), and back metal contact (BMC) for enhanced device performance. Among the various candidates, ZnO (ETL) and CBTS (HTL) are identified as optimal choices. Furthermore, the impact of absorber layer thickness, doping density, and defect density on the overall device efficiency is thoroughly analyzed. Device optimization identified an absorber thickness of 1200 nm, acceptor doping of 5×1015 cm−3, and low defect density of 1014 cm−3 as critical for maximizing charge generation while minimizing recombination losses. Additionally, optimized resistive parameters (Rs ∼ 0.5 Ω · cm2, Rsh ∼ 105 Ω · cm2) further improved the device performance. The proposed DPSC structure, after all optimizations, achieves a remarkable power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 20.95%, with an open circuit voltage (Voc) of 0.7892 V, short circuit current (Jsc) of 31.53 mA/cm2, and fill factor (FF) of 84.18%, representing an idealized efficiency ceiling for LNMO-based oxide DPSC. Additionally, the effects of working temperature and parasitic resistance on device performance are evaluated. The results are compared with earlier experimental and theoretical studies, demonstrating the superior performance of the optimized DPSC. This work introduces a stable, non-toxic, and highly efficient DPSC model, offering a promising pathway toward sustainable and eco-friendly energy solutions.

title: Beyond Borders: Why Africa Matters for a Geospatially Connected Planet
authors: Abimbola Atijosan
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Magazine
abstract: Africa plays a crucial role in understanding Earth’s interconnected systems. Its geospatial data, observational networks, and growing scientific expertise inform global climate, environmental, and geophysical knowledge. Strengthening African capacity, satellite programs, and collaborative research ensures more accurate and actionable insights while promoting equity. By partnering with African institutions, the world can better monitor, predict, and respond to environmental change, making Africa central to the planet’s sustainable future.

title: Characteristics of Automatic Offset and PID Controllers for IPDT Plant Models
authors: Mikulas Huba, Damir Vrancic, Mingwei Sun, Pavol Bistak
abstract: The article examines approximations of dead-time inversion proposed for integrator plus dead-time process models managed by generalized proportional-integral-derivative (PID) and automatic offset controllers (AOC) with derivatives of increasing order. The proposed controllers are tuned using the multiple real dominant pole method and are implemented and made robust with low-pass filters, which also help attenuate measurement noise. PID controllers have traditionally been the most popular, but nevertheless, closed loop performance, as measured by integral of absolute error or total variation, can be increased by factors reaching two to three orders of magnitude using AOC. The range of performance achievable with both approaches, along with the popular SIMC and iSIMC controllers, can be assessed by studying speed-effort and speed-wobbling charts. These charts clearly demonstrate the advantages of controllers that include second-order derivatives. Two additional time-domain charts are then used to characterize the impact of the controller derivative order on closed-loop performance for a fixed equivalent delay of the controller filter and for a fixed speed of setpoint responses. The same charts are also used to illustrate the performance of PID and AOC when controlling a nonlinear thermal process in real time. In this context, AOC proves to be a much less sensitive and much more flexible and effective alternative to PID controllers, interesting even to carry-out start-up of experiments with PID controllers.

title: Deep Learning-Based Regional Electricity Demand Prediction Using Smart Meter Data in Sri Lanka
authors: N.M.R.K. Jayathilaka, D.J.S. Warnasooriya, Kopisankar Nagarajah, T. Mukunthan, S. Jalini, V. Logeeshan, Chathura Wanigasekara
abstract: Precise short-term forecasting of regional and consumer level electricity demand is vital for energy management, especially in countries such as Sri Lanka, where significant energy challenges are faced. However, the increasing availability of smart meters’ data, existing models often lack regional specificity and do not adequately integrate external factors such as weather. This study proposes a robust deep learning framework for the prediction of real-time residential demand in Sri Lanka. A 3.5-year smart meter dataset was utilized, with 15-minute intervals, and combined with weather data. The energy demand being heavily dependent on human behavior, which is difficult to capture but it is significantly influenced by external environmental conditions. The methodology included advanced preprocessing and a generalized feature engineering pipeline, enhancing its adaptability to new regional contexts. Six models based on LSTM and GRU architectures were evaluated using incremental transfer learning strategy. Where models were trained on the Paris dataset and fine-tuned on Sri Lankan data. The analysis revealed different strengths of the model, with LSTM capturing long-term trends, GRU responding to short-term changes, and CNN-hybrids detecting sudden spikes. The GRU with the hybrid BPNN was identified as optimal, achieving the highest R² score of 0.8217 by combining the efficiency of GRU with the capacity of BPNN to model complex nonlinearities. These results were derived using datasets in which anomalies were intentionally retained to preserve real-world operational behaviour. The results confirm the potential of the framework to improve grid operational efficiency and support the development of sustainable energy systems.

title: Bunch Compression Monitoring in Longitudinal Electron Bunch Diagnostics Using Zero-bias Schottky Diode and AlGaAs/GaAs TeraFET Direct THz Detectors
authors: Rahul Yadav, Michael Kuntzsch, Ulf Lehnert, Sascha Preu, Andreas Penirschke
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology
abstract: Electron bunch compression is a crucial technique for generating high-brilliance, high-peak-current electron beams in third-generation and upcoming fourth-generation accelerator-based light sources. Bunch compression monitors (BCM) are required for non-invasive, shot-to-shot monitoring of the electron bunch compression state, allowing for precise beam characterization to optimize machine configuration and provide valuable real-time feedback to beamline scientists or directly to the machine, e.g., via a feedback loop, ultimately enhancing the overall operation and performance of the free electron laser (FEL) facility.
