In this work, two promising room-temperature operable BCMs have been investigated: zero-bias Schottky diodes (ZBSD) and AlGaAs/GaAs transistor-based (TeraFET) direct THz detectors. The application-oriented characterization of developed technologies was carried out at the ELBE facility located at the Helmholtz-Zentrum Dresden-Rossendorf in Germany. The electron bunch charge sweep was performed from 18 pC to 220 pC. Based on the simulation, the shortest bunch length is estimated to be below 0.5 ps. We estimate the minimum detectable bunch charge of the ZBSD detector to be in the single-digit pC range for single-bunch measurements. Both ZBSD and TeraFET detectors detect bunch charges non-destructively. Bunch compression was compared with a commercially available power meter based on a thermal detection principle, showing that the ZBSD is considerably more sensitive but saturates earlier. At the same time, the TeraFET is excellently suited for a high bunch charge of a few 100 pC with little saturation. The novelty of the proposed technologies lies in: (1) combined ZBSD and TeraFET provides a solution for BCMs in a wide range of bunch charges, and (2) their capability to have intrinsic response time on ps-scale, contrary to the ms to μs-scale response time of the conventionally used thermal-based power meters. To the best of the author's knowledge, TeraFETs are tested for the first time in the BCM application. These detectors are expected to be commissioned at free-electron laser facilities in the near future.

title: Fast Switching Quasi-Inversion-Channel β-Ga₂O₃ UMOSFET
authors: Huidong Yao, Xuanze Zhou, Qi Liu, Jingbo Zhou, Man Hoi Wong, Guangwei Xu, Shu Yang, Shibing Long
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive investigation into the dynamic switching characteristics of a vertical β-Ga₂O₃ UMOSFET. A 400-mA vertical quasi-inversion-channel β-Ga₂O₃ UMOSFET fabricated using a self-aligned process was demonstrated with a specific on-resistance of 13.2 mΩ·cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and a threshold voltage of 2.9 V. Through double-pulse testing at bus voltages up to 200 V, the device presents excellent switching speed, achieving an outstanding turn-on time of 10.6 ns and a turn-off time of 73.4 ns. In addition, inter-terminal capacitances are characterized and used to calculate the switching charges, including total gate charge of 1.37 nC and Miller charge of 0.26 nC, which can account for the high switching speed. Device figure of merits (FOMs) and switching frequency were then derived. Our β-Ga₂O₃ UMOSFETs exhibit superior switching FOMs when compared with commercial Si power transistors of similar current rating. These results offer the critical insights into the dynamic performance of β-Ga₂O₃ UMOSFETs, confirming their significant potential for next generation power electronics.

title: Multi-granularity Adversarial Generation Integrated Consistency Representation for Chest Low-Contrast-Enhanced CT Synthesis
authors: Lin Zhao, Shangwen Yang, Dianlin Hu, Zhan Wu, Huazhong Shu, Chunfeng Yang, Jean-Louis Coatrieux, Yang Chen
abstract: Contrast-enhanced CT (CECT) is essential for clinical evaluation of vessel structures and function. However, high contrast agent dose increases the risk of renal injury. Reducing contrast agent dose decreases the contrast between vessels and surrounding tissues, which complicates diagnosis. Despite their potential in CECT synthesis, existing methods often suffer from edge unclarity, contrast anomalies, and texture distortion, limiting their clinical applicability. This paper proposes a novel Multi-granularity Adversarial Generation Integrated Consistency Representation (MAGIC) for high-quality synthesis from low-contrast-enhanced CT to clinical usable CECT. MAGIC addresses current problems through four innovations: 1) Multi-Granularity Refined Booster (MRB) introduces contextual refinement and cross granularity boosting mechanisms for mining the multi-granularity contextual information to enhance feature representation capability, thus improving tissue edge clarity. 2) Supervised Contrast Enhancement Module (SCEM) imbues MAGIC with the ability to enhance tissue contrast, which leverages supervised images to adaptively adjust the contrast information of soft tissue structures and vessels, effectively overcoming the challenge of contrast anomalies. 3) Hierarchical Harmonized Consistency Representation (HHCR) utilizes domain consistency to construct a novel auxiliary loss for harmonizing the semantic and content relationships of multi-level hierarchical features to improve tissue texture performance, ensuring accurate restoration of real textures. 4) Dual-path Dynamic Collaborative Discriminator (DDCD) is designed with complementary strategies and injects content priors to dynamically collaborate the discrimination process, thereby comprehensively evaluating the fidelity of the synthesized results. Qualitative and quantitative results demonstrate that MAGIC significantly outperforms existing methods in edge clarity, image contrast, and texture restoration, underscoring its substantial clinical potential.

title: Optimization and Performance Improvement of Multisensor Piezoelectric Balance Assembly Method Based on a Complex Super-Statical Determinate Structure_supp1-3667884.pdf
authors: Yuzhe Tian
abstract: Multisensor piezoelectric balances are increasingly used in wind tunnel tests requiring high dynamic performance, due to their flexible structure, easy fabrication, high stiffness, and excellent dynamic performance. However, superimposed manufacturing and assembly errors degrade their force-measuring performance. To address this issue, this article proposes a novel assembly method for such balances based on a complex statically indeterminate structure. The method controls the preload of connecting bolts using a digital torque wrench and optimizes sensor height via three approaches, leveraging coordinate measuring machine data and copper sheets’ high plasticity. Mechanical and mathematical models were established, followed by simulations to analyze error impacts, and a six-component force calibration system was built for static/dynamic tests. Results show that placing copper sheets on sensors’ top/bottom (with uniform bolt preload) significantly reduces force imbalance; top-placed sheets improve static performance. Natural frequency tests confirm no negative impact on dynamic performance, verifying the method’s effectiveness and feasibility.

title: The Rise of AI-Generated Anime Avatars: Trends, Challenges, and Opportunities
authors: Fernanda Miyuki Yamada, João Paulo Gois, Hiroki Takahashi
abstract: The rise of 3-D anime-style avatars in gaming, virtual reality, and other digital media has driven significant interest in automated generation methods capable of capturing their distinctive visual characteristics. These include stylized proportions, expressive features, and nonphotorealistic rendering. This article reviews the advancements and challenges in using deep learning in 3-D anime-style avatar generation. We analyze the strengths and limitations of these methods in capturing the aesthetics of anime characters and supporting customization and animation. In addition, we identify and discuss open problems in the field, such as difficulties in resolution and detail preservation, and constraints regarding the animation of hair and loose clothing. This article aims to provide a comprehensive overview of the current state-of-the-art and identify promising research directions for advancing 3-D anime-style avatar generation.

title: Analysis High Isolation Features of CP MIMO Array based DRA for mm-wave 5G Applications
authors: Ashutosh Gupta, Ankit Pandit, Sanjeev Kumar Gupta, Laxmi Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Human society in the twenty-first century is mostly dependent on wireless technology, whether it is for long-distance communication like deep space radio communications or short-distance communication like Bluetooth. Similar to a person's eyes and ears, antennas are essential parts of this wireless communication system. Over the past few decades, antenna technology has advanced steadily due to the vast and ever-changing range of tasks that antennas undertake in the communication industry. The suggested DRA array incorporates a number of isolation augmentation strategies, such as orthogonal mode excitation, spatial and polarization variety, and structural decoupling approaches, to overcome mutual coupling problems that frequently occur in compact MIMO systems. Envelope Correlation Coefficient (ECC), Diversity Gain (DG), and S-parameter measurements are used to assess isolation performance throughout the operational frequency range. The results show that the planned CP MIMO DRA array performs exceptionally well in terms of diversity, with an isolation of better than –25 dB and an ECC < 0.01. This work sheds light on how inter-element spacing, DRA material selection, and CP contribute to improved isolation. Furthermore, the design is evaluated experimentally using manufactured prototypes and confirmed through full-wave electromagnetic simulation using HFSS/CST. The results confirm that in mm-wave 5 G applications, the suggested CP MIMO DRA array is ideally suited for small, high-data-rate, and low-interference communication systems.

title: Enhanced Optical Character Recognition Through Statistical and Neural Network-Based Handwriting Character Recognition Framework
authors: Shruthi S, Rashmi V, Megha A, Vijaya Kumar H R, Swetha G, Raghu N
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Handwritten character recognition (HCR) has numerous applications, including postal address interpretation, bank check processing, and document digitization. This paper presents a comprehensive framework for handwritten character recognition using statistical methods and artificial neural networks (ANNs) to address challenges such as nonlinearity and stylistic variations. The study evaluates traditional methods, including template matching, structural pattern recognition, and graphical approaches, against the proposed Support Vector Machine (SVM)-based model integrated with machine learning techniques. The proposed framework demonstrates high recognition accuracy, achieving 91% on a diverse dataset of handwritten characters, including stylistic letters and digits. Extensive testing with datasets such as MNIST and IAM highlights the robustness and adaptability of the approach. Future research directions are discussed, emphasizing improvements in dataset diversity and scalability for broader real-world applications.

title: Results Announced for the 2026 RAS AdCom Election
authors: N/A

title: SSE-Net: Towards Low-Power-Consumption Spiking Neural Network for Monaural Speech Enhancement
authors: Enrui Liu, Andong Li, Cunhang Fan, Chengshi Zheng, Jiangyan Yi, Ruibo Fu, Xinhui Li, Jian Zhou, Zhao Lv
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: Speech enhancement (SE) as a front-end task, especially in edge device deployment, requires low complexity and power consumption. However, deploying most artificial neural network (ANN)-based SE models while ensuring performance is challenging, particularly with the rapid development of large models in recent years. Spiking neural networks (SNNs) have shown potential in reducing power consumption. However, SNN-based SE models face two main challenges: on one hand, directly converting an ANN model to a SNN model introduces redundant modules, leading to difficulties in model training and information mismatch; on the other hand, the discrete binary activation and complex spatio-temporal dynamics of SNNs often result in information loss. To address these challenges, this paper proposes the SNN Speech Enhancement Network (SSE-Net). Unlike conventional approaches that convert ANNs to SNNs, all modules in SSE-Net are specifically designed for spike signal characteristics. Furthermore, the method innovatively develops a spiking feature extraction group that simultaneously converts information into spike signals while extracting original speech features, thereby effectively reducing information loss while significantly lowering both power consumption and computational complexity. The proposed method further designs information transformation blocks to convert spike signals back into continuous signals, fine-tuning and supplementing the speech information. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed model achieves state of the art (SOTA) performance compared to the best SNN-SE models, with a 62% reduction in power proxy and 11% reduction in power cost, it also demonstrates strong competitiveness when compared with ANN-based SE models.

title: MediaPipe-Driven Real-Time Hand Gesture Recognition: An Embedded AI Framework for Adaptive Human-Computer Synergy
authors: Ishwarkumar Nirale, Mohammad Ziaullah, Ravi Hosamani
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Hand gesture recognition is revolutionizing human-computer interaction (HCI) by enabling seamless, touch-free control in assistive technologies, immersive gaming, and intelligent environments. This paper introduces a real-time gesture recognition framework powered by MediaPipe, utilizing a single RGB camera to accurately detect and classify hand gestures. The system employs a hybrid two-stage architecture combining a lightweight palm detector with a deep learning-based hand landmark estimation model, ensuring high-precision and low-latency performance. Experimental results demonstrate an impressive accuracy of 98.2% and a real-time processing speed of 40 frames per second (FPS), confirming its suitability for mobile and embedded platforms. While the system demonstrates reliable performance, it exhibits limitations when subjected to challenges such as hand occlusions, illumination variations, and diverse hand orientations. Future work will focus on adaptive AI models to mitigate these limitations, multimodal sensor integration, and deployment in real-world dynamic environments. This research underscores the viability of vision-based, AI-enhanced gesture recognition as a scalable and efficient solution for next-generation human-computer synergy.

title: Federated Learning with Differential Privacy in IoT Intrusion Detection
authors: Mamta Rawat, Gaurav Singal
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The significant rise in the deployment of IoT devices has led to the expansion of the attack surface for attackers, making intrusion detection a critical part of IoT security. However, using traditional centralized IDS approaches lacks data privacy, scalability and incurs higher communication overhead, especially in resource-constrained environments. This paper proposes a privacy-aware intrusion detection framework using FL along with Differential Privacy (DP) to train a model without sharing raw data. We have used a benchmark dataset to evaluate the effect of privacy mechanisms on model accuracy. Results obtained demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves competitive detection performance while safeguarding sensitive data, making it suitable for deployment in distributed IoT networks. Finally, we discuss the trade-offs between privacy and accuracy, highlighting potential directions for future work in secure and scalable intrusion detection.

title: Low-Power FPGA-Based Accelerator for Alphanumeric Character Recognition
authors: Arun Kumar K, Dr.S.Navaneethan, Rishany Samlin S J, Sanjay E
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The very problem of digital pattern recognition is Alphanumeric recognition, which finds applications in digitizing a document, as well as automated identity recognition. Classic neural networks are precise, computationally expensive, necessitating a large memory space and floating-point multiplication mathematical operations, and do not lend themselves to digital implementation on low-power hardware. Binary Neural Networks (BNNs) reduce computational complexity by representing weights and activations in binary form (+1/-1 or 1/0). This conversion allows multiplication and accumulation operations to be replaced with XNOR and population count (pop count) operations, which are simple to implement in digital logic. This project proposes a low-power FPGA-based BNN system for alphanumeric recognition. The system integrates preprocessing, dataset preparation, weight binarization, and a custom FPGA accelerator implementing parallel XNOR-pop count operations with pipelining to optimize latency, area, and power. Experimental results show high recognition accuracy (~95%), low inference latency, and significant reduction in power consumption, demonstrating the feasibility of VLSI-oriented FPGA design for real-time alphanumeric recognition.

title: Intelligent Green Aviation Transportation via Space Solar Power: Past, Present and Future
authors: Shu Ting Goh, Seyed A. Zekavat, Ricardo Pereira, Nuno Borges Carvalho, Alessandra Costanzo, John Olds, Si-ping Gao
publish: IEEE Journal on Wireless Power Technologies
abstract:
The commitment of the International Air Transport Association to achieve net-zero carbon emissions by 2050, in tandem with increasing orders for all-electric aircraft (AEA), has accelerated the transition towards fully electric transportation. This paper introduces Space Solar Power (SSP) as a pivotal green transportation technology, focusing on mid-air recharging (MAR) for AEAs via microwave power transmission (MPT) from a SSP constellation. While SSP-AEA MAR promises a potential green air-transportation solution, several critical challenges must be addressed, including solar cell efficiency, high implementation cost, complex antenna and rectenna designs, power amplifier efficiency, complex beamforming and tracking process, and regulatory constraints.

This paper examines the evolving landscape of SSP-enabling key technologies, such as solar energy harvesting units, high-efficiency DC-to-RF converters, and advanced antenna and rectenna arrays. The roles of artificial intelligence (AI) and smart materials are highlighted for optimizing design and integration processes, beam tracking and control, and autonomous operation across both space and air segments. In addition, this paper discusses the administrative and operational challenges, including the frequency of regulatory management and projected system cost. This paper identifies key technological areas requiring further advancement to support robust AEA MAR deployment

title: Integrating Multi-Level Features of Pathological Images and Pathway Features for cancer Survival Prediction
authors: Xinyue Xu, Wei Peng, Wei Dai, Xiaodong Fu, Li Liu, Lijun Liu, Shikang Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Accurate cancer survival prediction is crucial for personalized precision medicine. However, existing survival analysis methods based on Whole Slide Images typically rely on single-magnification image patch features, neglecting key multi-magnification level information in histopathological images and failing to effectively integrate biological features such as pathways. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel multi-instance learning model called HiMulti, which improves survival prediction accuracy by deeply fusing multi-level pathological image features and pathway features. HiMulti first constructs WSI into multiple multi-level pyramid-structured regions of interest. Then, it utilizes improved Mamba-Inspired Linear Attention model(iMILA) and linear attention Transformer to capture intra-level micro-texture details and inter-level macro-structural interactions, respectively. Simultaneously, the model incorporates pathway features based on RNA-Seq and generates multimodal and unimodal attention weights through a novel dual-branch attention mechanism to accurately select key image regions with the highest prognostic value. Finally, patient-level survival prediction is achieved by aggregating these key region features. Experiments on the TCGA-LUAD, BRCA, and BLCA datasets show that HiMulti's C-Index is significantly improved by 4.45% to 21.01% compared to state-of-the-art weakly supervised methods, and visualization results further confirm the model's superiority.

title: A Shielded Planar Loop Magnetic Field Probe in PCB Technology for EMC Measurements and Calibration Method
authors: Mario Filipašić, Martin Dadić
abstract: This article presents a multiturn design and characterization of a shielded planar loop magnetic field probe developed in printed circuit board (PCB) technology. Due to its compact structure and PCB-based design on a four-layer FR-4 substrate, the probe offers a cost-effective alternative to commercial magnetic field probes, while maintaining sufficient sensitivity, shielding, and frequency range of operation. The simulation and analytical results are validated with experimental measurements. Furthermore, a calibration setup consisting of Helmholtz coils and standard probe (antenna) method is shown and validated. Finally, a shielding effectiveness measurement method in Helmholtz coils is proposed and validated by measurements in a TEM cell.

title: Erratum to “ LC Repeater-Inspired Battery-Free, Chipless, Flexible Body Sensor Network With Lightweight Reader”
authors: En-Zhu Lyu, Yen-Ling Sung, Dong-Yu Hsu, Guan-Lin Su, Ting-Wei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement

title: Design and Verification of IE5+ Ultra Efficiency Line-start Permanent Magnet Assisted Synchronous Reluctance Motor
authors: Bin Chen, Yong Xiao, Jinfei Shi, Yusheng Hu, Ying Li, Liyi Li
abstract: Line-start permanent magnet assisted synchronous reluctance motor (LS-PMaSynRM) fully utilizes both permanent magnet torque and reluctance torque to enhance output capability, it has the advantages of low cost and high efficiency. Firstly, the theory and characteristic of LS-PMaSynRM during starting and steady-state operation are introduced, providing theoretical guidance for motor design. Secondly, based on the neural network surrogate model and the second-generation genetic algorithm, a multi-parameter, multi-objective optimization design is conducted on the rotor structural, the optimization goal is to improve the torque and efficiency of the motor, reduce torque ripple and material cost. The optimized LS-PMaSynRM can achieve IE5+ ultra-high efficiency. Again, the characteristics of various torques generated by the LS-PMaSynRM during the starting process were studied, and the reasons for poor starting capability were analyzed. Under the condition that has the least impact on efficiency, the starting capability was improved by optimizing the conductor bars. The starting inertia of the optimized LS-PMaSynRM has increased by 4 times, and the starting load torque has increased by 2.73 times. Finally, the starting and steady-state performance of the optimized LS-PMaSynRM were verified through prototype manufacturing and testing.

title: Subcultural Symbols and Climate Action: Visual Storytelling Strategies on Bilibili Short Videos
authors: Wang Yuhan, Yue Gailing
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: As the issue of climate change grows increasingly severe, enhancing public attention and action becomes paramount. This study selects 397 high-engagement videos from Bilibili between 2020 and 2023 as research subjects, conducting systematic coding and analysis from the perspectives of symbolic representation and user engagement behavior. The findings reveal that hard-core science popularization climate change short videos, leveraging extension symbols such as data visualization and expert interpretation, combined with mythic symbols like “technology worship” and “rescue narrative,” excel in boosting user attention, memory retention, and sharing willingness. Creative climate change short videos utilize subcultural symbols, such as “second dimension,” to lower cognitive barriers, effectively attracting Generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Z$</tex> users, but excessive entertainment may diminish the long-term value of the content. Documentary and policy-related climate change short videos lack emotional resonance symbols, resulting in relatively fewer interactive behaviors. In the creation of climate-related short videos, creators should balance scientific accuracy with accessibility, considering both entertaining expressions and indepth information transmission. Platforms need to optimize algorithms, break the “information cocoon,” and promote the dissemination of diverse content to enhance the influence and effectiveness of climate communication.

title: Integrated Multi Stage Transformer (IMST) model for Crop Yield Prediction
authors: M. Vamshi Reddy, M. Ananya Reddy, M. Akhil Kumar, M. Rama Chandra Rao, M.A. Jabbar
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: Predicting crop yield is a crucial factor in precision agriculture, especially under the conditions of increasing environmental uncertainty and climatic variability. This interest in predicting crop yield increased the necessity to develop advanced machine learning and dl models to refine the capabilities of accurate and efficient crop yield prediction. Existing models fail to handle real-time data and multiple types of data like weather conditions and IoT sensor readings. So it is crucial for us to implement a better crop yield prediction framework that can work well in all scenarios. In this paper, an Integrated Multi Stage Transformer (IMST) framework is discussed that uses an advanced Deep Learning (DL) approach. This IMST model uses a self-attention mechanism that captures complex spatial and temporal dependencies. The dataset contains different data like weather conditions, IoT sensor readings. The model is implemented and their performance is assessed using several evaluation metrics like accuracy, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and F1 score. The transformer model results with an accuracy of 94.3%. It addresses the difficulties commonly found in real-world applications and exhibits good performance with real-time data.

title: Design and Simulation of a Miniaturized Circular Patch Antenna for Biomedical Application
authors: Regina S, Vasuki S, Ramya S, Kanmani M
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: The research study designs and develops a new generation circular patch antenna system which specifically suits biomedical applications within the 2.4 GHz frequency frequency range. A next-generation circular patch antenna exists as a lightweight fabrication on FR-4 substrate material having 1.6 mm thickness while its relative permittivity amounts to 4.4. The circular pattern design enables small size which makes this antenna suitable for medical devices requiring implantation or wearability. The performance examination involved detailed analysis which focused on optimizing the antenna through evaluations of return loss together with gain and directivity metrics. The proposed design delivers superior performance which reaches -20.87 dB return loss combined with 2.03 dBi gain and 5.94 dBi directivity. The antenna received its design specifications and simulation evaluation through CST software for its optimal utilization in biomedical applications.

title: Target Recognition Based on Haptic Information
authors: Tong Su, Jiannan Lin, Nian He
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Traditional target recognition methods primarily rely on visual information. However, in visually constrained environments, the role of haptic perception becomes particularly critical. Extracting useful information accurately and efficiently from complex haptic data remains a challenge in current scientific and technological research. To address this issue, a novel haptic recognition method is proposed in this paper. An enhanced convolutional neural network architecture ResTactSENet is introduced, which integrates Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) modules and residual connections. This model is designed to achieve precise analysis and accurate recognition of haptic information. The SE module is employed to adaptively recalibrate channel-wise feature responses, enhancing the emphasis on salient features and improving representational capacity. Meanwhile, residual connections are utilized to alleviate the vanishing gradient problem, thereby facilitating the training of deeper networks and improving their efficiency in handling complex haptic data analysis tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed ResTactSENet model achieves superior performance in classifying complex spatio-temporal haptic signals.

title: High Precision IOT Front End Acquisition System
authors: Yulong Liu, Bo Li, Jiaxin Shen, Siyuan Xue, Bowei Hou
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: In order to meet the requirements of the front-end of the Internet of things for multi-sensor high-precision synchronous acquisition and low-power transmission, this paper designs a multi board cascade acquisition system based on FPGA. The system takes the front end multi board scalable architecture as the core, and realizes the high consistency capture and reliable transmission of multi-channel data through the slot allocation and multi board synchronization mechanism. The test results show that in the process of 500 Hz to 2000 Hz signal acquisition, the data phase and amplitude of each channel are consistent, and the packet loss rate of continuous transmission is 0. The system has high precision and good scalability, and is suitable for the application of multi-sensor data acquisition in the front end of the Internet of things.

title: Design and Development of an E-Commerce Website and Mobile App for Silk Testing Services
authors: Vaibhavi Katate, Akash Katate, Santosh P, Meharchand, Nivedita S, Periyasamy S, Manikandan J
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Central Silk Board offers several comprehensive silk testing services through its seventeen centers located all over India. In order to provide wide publicity of the silk testing services to stake holders and increase revenue generation from silk testing services, an attempt is made to design and develop an E-commerce website for all the silk testing services available with Central Silk Board. After the successful evaluation and usage of the website, a Mobile App version of the same E-commerce website is also developed. The proposed website development comprises of front end and back end development with payment gateway API and delivery integrated to the website. Details about the differences in requirements for proposed E-commerce website over other E-commerce websites developed is also reported in the paper.

title: Ensuring Reproducibility in Stream Processing with Blockchain Technologies
authors: Niaz Mohammad Ramaki, Florian Schintke
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Ensuring reproducibility in streaming data analytics remains a complex challenge due to factors such as nondeterministic event ordering, schema drift, and state management inconsistencies. Existing stream processing frameworks, including Apache Kafka and Flink, emphasize real-time performance but lack mechanisms for verifiable and consistent outcomes. We explore how blockchain technologies can enhance reproducibility by leveraging their immutability, distributed consensus, and auditability. We propose a conceptual integration model in which blockchain is used to record metadata, hash event sequences, and checkpoint processing states. A prototype implementation demonstrates window-level checkpointing to support reproducibility validation. While integrating high-speed streaming platforms with slower blockchain systems introduces performance tradeoffs, we propose scalable techniques to mitigate this overhead. With this blockchain-backed reproducibility framework scientific transparency, data governance, and trust in real-time analytics can be improved.

title: Balancing Interpretability and Performance in Credit Card Fraud Detection Using Machine Learning Techniques with SMOTE and Logistic Regression
authors: Modina Jahan Priya, Hasan Ahammad
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Credit card fraud detection is a critical issue in modern financial systems, with challenges arising from the imbalanced nature of transaction datasets, where fraudulent activities account for only a small fraction. This paper proposes an enhanced fraud detection system by combining Logistic Regression with Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) to address class imbalance, improve model performance, and detect fraudulent transactions more effectively. SMOTE, which generates synthetic samples from the minority class, helps to improve the recall and precision of the Logistic Regression model. The results are evaluated using standard metrics - accuracy, precision, recall and AUC. The results show a significant improvement in detecting fraudulent transactions. Similar works like those of IEEE have also shown the benefits of SMOTE in fraud detection, thus providing further justification for the use of this technique. The results show that the combination of logistic regression and SMOTE makes it possible to successfully detect fraud in imbalanced datasets, thus providing a solution to the requirements needed in the monetary organizations in the real world.

title: Distribution Network Planning and Reliability Collaborative Optimization Considering Distributed New Energy
authors: Xiaobo Wang, Xiaoyuan Chen, Jing Mao, Xingxing Zhou
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: With the large-scale access of distributed new energy, the power flow distribution of distribution network has changed significantly, and the system uncertainty has increased, which has brought many challenges to the planning and operation of distribution network, such as voltage fluctuation and reliability decline. In order to meet these challenges, this paper proposes a two-stage collaborative optimization model, which realizes the efficient operation of power system through the close cooperation of planning and operation stages. In the planning stage, optimize the grid structure, line capacity and the location and constant capacity of distributed generation (DG); The operation stage focuses on distributed energy output scheduling, energy storage charging and discharging strategy and demand response measures. By constructing a multi-objective optimization function, the total cost is minimized and the system reliability is maximized, and various constraints are considered, including DG's permeability constraint, capacity upper limit constraint, dynamic power balance constraint and voltage security constraint. The improved NSGA-II algorithm combined with Benders decomposition is used to accelerate the solution, which ensures the feasibility and efficiency of the optimization scheme. The effectiveness of the proposed method is verified by the actual case analysis of coastal industrial parks. After optimization, the reliability of the system is significantly improved, SAIDI and SAIFI are reduced by 60%, and the voltage qualification rate is increased to 98%. The total life cycle cost is reduced by 5%, and the abandonment rate of new energy is reduced by 8%, which shows that the collaborative optimization method has obvious advantages in improving the economy and reliability of distribution network, and provides new ideas and methods for distribution network planning under distributed new energy access.
